songs.subject: Write a song about my cat John.
songs.lyrics:
John on the windowsill, watching the world go by
Tail like a question mark against the morning sky
You don't care about my problems, you don't care about my day
You just want your dinner and a sunny place to lay

Oh John, my little shadow on the floor
King of every empty cardboard box and so much more
You rule this house like neon in the night
Oh John, oh John, everything's alright

Knocking cups off tables just to watch them fall
Nine whole lives of mischief and you've lived through them all

Oh John, my little shadow on the floor
King of every empty cardboard box and so much more
songs.lyrics_en:
John on the windowsill, watching the world go by
Tail like a question mark against the morning sky
You don't care about my problems, you don't care about my day
You just want your dinner and a sunny place to lay

Oh John, my little shadow on the floor
King of every empty cardboard box and so much more
You rule this house like neon in the night
Oh John, oh John, everything's alright

Knocking cups off tables just to watch them fall
Nine whole lives of mischief and you've lived through them all

Oh John, my little shadow on the floor
King of every empty cardboard box and so much more

songs.subject: Write a song about a rainy Tuesday.
songs.lyrics:
Grey light through the window, coffee going cold
Another rainy Tuesday, same story being told
The whole world keeps on turning while I'm frozen in my chair
Watching shadows on the ceiling, raindrops everywhere

It's a rainy Tuesday, washing me away
Echoes of the weekend fading into grey
But I'll rise above the storm, I'll find my light
On this rainy Tuesday I will be alright

Drop by drop, the hours fall
I'm still standing through it all
songs.lyrics_en:
Grey light through the window, coffee going cold
Another rainy Tuesday, same story being told
The whole world keeps on turning while I'm frozen in my chair
Watching shadows on the ceiling, raindrops everywhere

It's a rainy Tuesday, washing me away
Echoes of the weekend fading into grey
But I'll rise above the storm, I'll find my light
On this rainy Tuesday I will be alright

Drop by drop, the hours fall
I'm still standing through it all

songs.subject: Write a song about my morning coffee.
songs.lyrics:
First light hits the kitchen, kettle starts to scream
Pour it black and bitter, wake me from the dream
The steam is like a whisper, rising to the sky
One sip and I'm a phoenix, watch this soul revive

Morning coffee, burning like a fire in my veins
You're the only thing that keeps me sane
Liquid gold, my midnight turning into day
Morning coffee, never go away
songs.lyrics_en:
First light hits the kitchen, kettle starts to scream
Pour it black and bitter, wake me from the dream
The steam is like a whisper, rising to the sky
One sip and I'm a phoenix, watch this soul revive

Morning coffee, burning like a fire in my veins
You're the only thing that keeps me sane
Liquid gold, my midnight turning into day
Morning coffee, never go away

songs.subject: Write a song about missing the last bus home.
songs.lyrics:
Taillights disappearing down the avenue
Stood there in the neon glow with nothing left to do
The city's cold and endless, the night is wearing thin
Just me and all these shadows and the state that I am in

I missed the last bus home, now I'm walking all alone
Every streetlight is a ghost of someone I used to know
Miles to go before I sleep, secrets that I cannot keep
I missed the last bus home tonight
songs.lyrics_en:
Taillights disappearing down the avenue
Stood there in the neon glow with nothing left to do
The city's cold and endless, the night is wearing thin
Just me and all these shadows and the state that I am in

I missed the last bus home, now I'm walking all alone
Every streetlight is a ghost of someone I used to know
Miles to go before I sleep, secrets that I cannot keep
I missed the last bus home tonight

songs.subject: Write a song about my grandmother's kitchen.
songs.lyrics:
Flour on the counter, sunlight on the tiles
Grandmother's hands are moving, and I can see her smile
The radio is playing something old and sweet
The smell of bread and cinnamon, the warmth beneath my feet

In grandmother's kitchen, time stands still
Love baked into every windowsill
Memories like embers, glowing soft and gold
In grandmother's kitchen, I'll never grow old

She's gone now but the recipe remains
Her love still echoes through these windowpanes
songs.lyrics_en:
Flour on the counter, sunlight on the tiles
Grandmother's hands are moving, and I can see her smile
The radio is playing something old and sweet
The smell of bread and cinnamon, the warmth beneath my feet

In grandmother's kitchen, time stands still
Love baked into every windowsill
Memories like embers, glowing soft and gold
In grandmother's kitchen, I'll never grow old

She's gone now but the recipe remains
Her love still echoes through these windowpanes

songs.subject: Park stranger I mistook for an old friend; talked an hour, never got their name.
songs.lyrics:
Saw your back across the green and called a name I knew
Sure as Sunday it was Daniel from the class of '92
But you turned around a stranger with a kinder, rounder face
And I should've just apologized and given you your space

But we talked until the sprinklers came on
'Bout your sister, 'bout the job you just quit
An hour of a stranger and then you were gone
And I never even asked your name — that's it

You knew nothing of my history and I knew none of yours
That's maybe why the talking came so easy, felt so pure
Now you're just a Tuesday and a bench I'll always see
A whole life passing through a name that never came to be
songs.lyrics_en:
Saw your back across the green and called a name I knew
Sure as Sunday it was Daniel from the class of '92
But you turned around a stranger with a kinder, rounder face
And I should've just apologized and given you your space

But we talked until the sprinklers came on
'Bout your sister, 'bout the job you just quit
An hour of a stranger and then you were gone
And I never even asked your name — that's it

You knew nothing of my history and I knew none of yours

Now you're just a Tuesday and a bench I'll always see
A whole life passing through a name that never came to be

songs.subject: Car broke down at midnight; a trucker helped and left before thanks.
songs.lyrics:
Mile marker nineteen, the engine gave its last
Midnight on the mountain and the cold came on so fast
Then headlights in the mirror, a big rig pulling slow
A stranger with a thermos and a wire and nowhere to go but home

He fixed it with his hands and a half a cup of coffee
Didn't ask my name, didn't want a dime
Gone before I turned around to thank him for the kindness
Some angels drive a diesel late at night

I still don't know your face, I never caught your plate
But I pull over now for strangers — I learned that from you that day
songs.lyrics_en:
Mile marker nineteen, the engine gave its last
Midnight on the mountain and the cold came on so fast
Then headlights in the mirror, a big rig pulling slow
A stranger with a thermos and a wire and nowhere to go but home

He fixed it with his hands and a half a cup of coffee
Didn't ask my name, didn't want a dime
Gone before I turned around to thank him for the kindness
Some angels drive a diesel late at night

I still don't know your face, I never caught your plate
But I pull over now for strangers — I learned that from you that day

songs.subject: Father taught me to fish; he's gone; took my daughter to the same lake with his rod.
songs.lyrics:
Seven years old and a tangle in the line
He never raised his voice, he just said "take your time"
The same green water, the same flat morning light
I brought your granddaughter, Dad — I think we did it right

Your old rod in her little hands
A reel that still remembers how you taught a boy to stand
And the lake holds everything it ever held
I cast it out and felt you in the line, I felt you, I felt you still

She caught a little sunfish and she shrieked and let it go
You'd have laughed the way you used to, low and slow
Three generations standing on a dock that's going grey
I finally understand the things you couldn't say

Seven years old and a tangle in the line
He never raised his voice, he just said "take your time"
The same green water, the same flat morning light
I brought your granddaughter, Dad — I think we did it right

Your old rod in her little hands
A reel that still remembers how you taught a boy to stand
And the lake holds everything it ever held
I cast it out and felt you in the line, I felt you, I felt you still

She caught a little sunfish and she shrieked and let it go
You'd have laughed the way you used to, low and slow
Three generations standing on a dock that's going grey
I finally understand the things you couldn't say

songs.subject: New city, quit the job, ate alone nightly; the diner waitress saved me a booth.
songs.lyrics:
New town, new badge, lasted ninety days
Didn't know a single soul in all that concrete haze
Found a corner diner with a flickering sign
Ordered the same special, table for one at nine

But Donna saved my booth by the window every night
"The usual?" — two words that felt like light
I quit the job I came here for, I'm still not sure I'm free
But somebody in this whole town kept a seat for me

She never asked my story and I never told her mine
Just coffee and a kindness in a quiet little line
I'm leaving in the morning, left her twenty on the tray
And a note that said the booth was the best part of my day

New town, new badge, lasted ninety days
Didn't know a single soul in all that concrete haze
Found a corner diner with a flickering sign
Ordered the same special, table for one at nine

songs.subject: Found mother's old letters to someone before father; chose not to read past the first.
songs.lyrics:
A shoebox in the attic tied with garden string
Your handwriting younger than the mother I'd been seeing
Addressed to someone "dearest" that I never knew you'd known
Dated three years early — before Dad, when you were on your own

So we folded up the first one and we put the lid back on
Some doors aren't ours to open even after you are gone
You were a whole person once before you were my mom
And we left you that one secret in the dark up where it belongs

I don't need to know his name
I just love you more for the life I never knew you came from

Addressed to someone "dearest" that I never knew you'd known
Dated three years early — before Dad, when you were on your own

You were a whole person once before you were my mom
And we left you that one secret in the dark up where it belongs

I don't need to know his name
I just love you more for the life I never knew you came from

songs.subject: People reflect our unspoken expectations; we meet our prediction of them, not them.
songs.lyrics:
I built you out of everyone who left
A wanted poster sketched from my regret
You said good morning, I heard something else
A man can drown inside a thing he tells himself

I keep meeting the idea of you
The draft I wrote before you ever spoke
You're a stranger in a coat I sewed
And I call it love, this argument I hold

You ordered tea, I read it as goodbye
Turns out you just don't sleep well, that's the why
The version in my head won't take a seat
It eats the actual you across the table where we eat

What if I set the cardboard cutout down
And let the real one walk into the room
No script, no part for you to play
Just two strangers learning what the other one would say

What if I set the cardboard cutout down
And let the real one walk into the room
No script, no part for you to play
Just two strangers learning what the other one would say

songs.subject: Quiet grief of outgrowing a version of yourself others still love and expect.
songs.lyrics:
They keep the photo on the fridge, the one where I was loud
The funny one, the easy one, the favorite in the crowd
I packed him in a suitcase about four winters back
But everybody sets a place for the guy who's not coming back

And I can't tell them quietly
That I buried who they're waiting for
He was good, he made them laugh
But I can't be him anymore

My mother asks where'd all the jokes go, did the city make me cold
I don't know how to tell a woman she's been mourning something old
It's nobody's fault, it's just a tide
You can love a person and the ghost they used to be inside

So I do the voice at Christmas, I perform him for a while
Then I drive back to my real life and let him go a mile at a time

You can love a person and the ghost they used to be inside

So I do the voice at Christmas, I perform him for a while
Then I drive back to my real life and let him go a mile at a time

songs.subject: A city remembers you through small rituals you leave behind, after you've gone.
songs.lyrics:
The barista on Fifth still makes it without the sugar
A muscle in her wrist that I taught her by mistake
There's a bench that holds the shape of every Thursday lunch I took
And a crosswalk that still counts down to a step I'll never make

The city keeps me like a habit
In the hands of people I forgot to know
I'm a route the buses run at seven
A small warm dent in the places that I go

The bodega cat still waits beside a door I used to open
The locksmith saved my spare key in a drawer he'll never check
You don't get a statue, you get nobody noticing
That the morning has a hole in it the shape of your old neck

I moved away, I didn't die
But a version of me still buys the paper, still says hi

There's a bench that holds the shape of every Thursday lunch I took

I moved away, I didn't die

songs.subject: Realizing your parents were improvising; the authority you feared was fear in a bigger coat.
songs.lyrics:
I found the bank statements behind the good china
Red ink in the year I thought we had it made
The man who seemed carved from the mountain in the kitchen
Was a kid in a tie who was terrified and brave

You were guessing the whole time
You just guessed it in a deeper voice
The hand on the wheel was shaking
And you drove us anyway, no choice

The rules I broke my back against were duct tape on a panic
The curfew was a prayer dressed up to look like steel
Now I've got a kid who thinks I'm certain
And I'm reading the same map upside down, learning how it feels

So thank you for the trembling you hid so well from me
I'll shake behind a bigger coat so mine grows up feeling free
songs.lyrics_en:
I found the bank statements behind the good china
Red ink in the year I thought we had it made
The man who seemed carved from the mountain in the kitchen
Was a kid in a tie who was terrified and brave

You were guessing the whole time
You just guessed it in a deeper voice
The hand on the wheel was shaking
And you drove us anyway, no choice

The rules I broke my back against were duct tape on a panic
The curfew was a prayer dressed up to look like steel
Now I've got a kid who thinks I'm certain
And I'm reading the same map upside down, learning how it feels

So thank you for the trembling you hid so well from me
I'll shake behind a bigger coat so mine grows up feeling free

songs.subject: The strange intimacy of strangers on a night train, each a life you'll never know.
songs.lyrics:
The woman reading Russian has a ring she keeps on turning
The kid asleep on someone's coat that isn't his to keep
We're close enough to count each other's breathing in the carriage
And we'll never trade a word — just borrow forty miles of sleep

For one hour we're a country
Strangers stacked against the dark
Headed somewhere none of us is from
With the same rain on the glass, the same spark

The conductor knows our tickets but he doesn't know our reasons
The drunk man near the doorway sang one verse then lost the rest
I'll forget you by the platform, you'll forget me by the morning
But right now your shoulder's warm and we are briefly, oddly blessed

Then the brakes, the names of stations
And the country comes apart
Everybody takes their suitcase
And the small unspoken thing we shared in the dark

The kid asleep on someone's coat that isn't his to keep
We're close enough to count each other's breathing in the carriage
And we'll never trade a word — just borrow forty miles of sleep

songs.subject: gospel / an ex you still think about
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning I put on my church dress
Ironed it twice like mama taught me right
My little girl is braiding up her hair
And I'm pretending that my heart is fine

I lift my hands up in the sanctuary
I let the choir wash me clean again
But somewhere between the prayer and the offering
Your face comes drifting through my mind like sin

Lord knows I'm trying, Lord knows I'm faithful
I got a child to raise and bills to pay
I built this life up from the broken pieces
So why do I still call your name when I pray

I never asked you to be my forever
Just needed more than what you chose to give
My daughter's watching how her mama handles
Every heartache, teaching her to live

Lord knows I'm trying, Lord knows I'm faithful
I got a child to raise and bills to pay
I built this life up from the broken pieces
So why do I still call your name when I pray

She asked me once if she would ever meet you
I told her God provides what children need
I held it together till she fell asleep
Then cried into the laundry on my knees

I know your grace is real I know it covers
The lonely Tuesday nights I almost called
I know my worth is not wrapped up in whether
A man who left me loved me after all

Lord knows I'm trying, Lord knows I'm faithful
I got a child to raise and bills to pay
I built this life up from the broken pieces
So why do I still call your name when I pray

Lord I am trying, Lord I am faithful
Take this old longing, take this weight
I built this life up from the broken pieces
Help me stop calling his name when I pray
Help me stop calling his name when I pray

songs.prompt:
Write 4 completely different full-length songs, one per request below. Each song must be complete, like a real radio song: at least three verses and a repeated chorus. For each, write only the song lyrics -- no titles, no section labels like Verse or Chorus, no commentary. Separate each song with a line that is exactly:
@@@NEXT-SONG@@@

1. A gospel song about an ex you still think about. from a single mother's point of view
2. A hip-hop song about getting sober. like a diary entry
3. A americana song about a new baby. from a bartender's point of view
4. A alt rock song about falling in love at a party. from someone who just got dumped

songs.subject: hip-hop / getting sober
songs.lyrics:
Woke up on a Thursday couldn't tell you what month
Looked in the mirror like who is this front
Empty bottles lined up like they keeping score
Counted fourteen on the bathroom floor

Sat down on the cold tile, phone in my hand
Thought about calling somebody who'd understand
But everybody I burned either moved or forgot
So I just sat there in everything I got

This is day one, writing it down
Nobody watching, nobody around
Shaking a little but I'm still alive
This is what it looks like when you choose to survive
Day one

Afternoon I made myself some eggs
Noticed how bad I was shaking in my legs
Turned the TV off cause everything's too loud
Couldn't tell the silence from the noise of the crowd

Wrote my sister's name then crossed it out again
Wrote it back because I needed to begin
Somewhere outside a dog was going crazy
Everything in my chest was thick and hazy

This is day one, writing it down
Nobody watching, nobody around
Shaking a little but I'm still alive
This is what it looks like when you choose to survive
Day one

I lost the apartment on Meridian Street
Lost the job at the warehouse in the summer heat
Lost two years I can't get back again
Almost lost the part of me that holds a pen

But I'm writing this down cause somebody said to
Write it all down even when it hurts you
So here's the truth inside a spiral notebook
I was disappearing and I finally looked

This is day one, writing it down
Nobody watching, nobody around
Shaking a little but I'm still alive
This is what it looks like when you choose to survive

Went to bed at nine with a glass of water
Thought about the man I used to be before it
Thought maybe that man's still somewhere underneath
Fell asleep and actually slept
Actually slept
Day one
Day one

songs.subject: americana / a new baby
songs.lyrics:
Last call came and went around two
I was wiping down the bar when Jimmy walks through
Big old grin and a box of cigars
Says she's here, she's here, go look at the stars

I've seen a lot of things from behind this wood
Broken hearts and celebrations, more bad than good
Men who lost everything on a Friday night
But Jimmy's hands were shaking with pure delight

And I thought about what it means to be new
To come in clean with nothing to undo
Eight pounds something and a whole life ahead
While her daddy's buying rounds and I'm being fed

All these stories that people bring through my door
I pour the whiskey but I'm always wanting more
Something real to hold onto at the end of the night
And Jimmy's little girl just walked into the light

Regulars came in the next afternoon
Somebody brought a card, somebody brought balloons
Old Carl who never smiles bought a round for the house
Said it for all of us without raising his voice

I've mopped these floors ten thousand times or more
Heard confessions that would shake you to your core
Watched people toast to everything they've lost
But today we're toasting something that it didn't cost

And I thought about what it means to be new
To come in clean with nothing to undo
Eight pounds something and a whole life ahead
While her daddy's buying rounds and I'm being fed

All these stories that people bring through my door
I pour the whiskey but I'm always wanting more
Something real to hold onto at the end of the night
And Jimmy's little girl just walked into the light

I drove home slow down the county road
Stars were out and I lightened my load
Thought about calling my own daddy up
Tell him I get it now, I finally grew up

Cause I've been serving sorrow in a pint glass
Watching people chase a future through their past
But something about a name not yet worn thin
Makes me want to be better than I've been

And I thought about what it means to be new
To come in clean with nothing to undo
Eight pounds something and a whole life ahead
While her daddy's buying rounds and I'm being fed

All these stories that people bring through my door
I pour the whiskey but I'm always wanting more
Something real to hold onto at the end of the night
And Jimmy's little girl just walked into the light
Jimmy's little girl just walked into the light

But Jimmy's hands were shaking with pure delight

To come in clean with nothing to undo
Eight pounds something and a whole life ahead

All these stories that people bring through my door
I pour the whiskey but I'm always wanting more
Something real to hold onto at the end of the night
And Jimmy's little girl just walked into the light

Regulars came in the next afternoon
Somebody brought a card, somebody brought balloons

And I thought about what it means to be new
To come in clean with nothing to undo
Eight pounds something and a whole life ahead
While her daddy's buying rounds and I'm being fed

All these stories that people bring through my door
I pour the whiskey but I'm always wanting more
Something real to hold onto at the end of the night
And Jimmy's little girl just walked into the light

I drove home slow down the county road
Stars were out and I lightened my load
Thought about calling my own daddy up
Tell him I get it now, I finally grew up

Cause I've been serving sorrow in a pint glass
Watching people chase a future through their past
But something about a name not yet worn thin
Makes me want to be better than I've been

songs.subject: alt rock / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
She left on a Sunday with half of my records
The good ones, the ones that I played when we met
I showed up here broken just looking for volume
Something loud enough to make me forget

Somebody's kitchen, red cups everywhere
Coat on a stranger's coat on the stair
I was about to leave I was reaching for nothing
When you laughed at something and turned in the air

And I felt it go sideways
Everything tilting the wrong kind of right
My chest doing something it hasn't done lately
I don't even know your name yet tonight

I don't know your name yet and I don't even care
I'm still bleeding out but you're already there
I came here destroyed and the room was just noise
But you cut right through it like signal through static
I'm not ready, I'm wrecked, I'm a mess in a jacket
And I think I'm falling, I think I'm falling

We talked by the window for longer than either
Of us could pretend was a casual thing
You said you liked the band on my shirt and I lied
Said yeah they're my favorite, whatever that means

But you saw through it laughed said you've never heard them
I said okay you got me and you said okay good
And something about being caught in a small lie
Made me feel more honest than I thought I could

And I felt it go sideways
Everything tilting the wrong kind of right
My chest doing something it hasn't done lately
I don't even know your name yet tonight

I'm still bleeding out but you're already there

I'm not ready, I'm wrecked, I'm a mess in a jacket
And I think I'm falling, I think I'm falling

She took the tenderness when she took the records
Packed it in boxes and drove it away
I told myself I was done with the whole thing
Done with the hoping and done with the ache

But here in this kitchen with bad overhead lighting
And someone's old playlist bleeding through the wall
I'm looking at you and I'm terrified honestly
Cause I didn't come here to feel this at all

And I felt it go sideways
Everything tilting the wrong kind of right
My chest doing something it hasn't done lately
You just told me your name and it wrecked me tonight

I know your name now and I'm already gone
I'm still bleeding out but I want to be wrong
About all the reasons I shouldn't be here
You cut right through it like signal through static
I'm not ready, I'm wrecked, I'm a mess in a jacket
And I think I'm falling
God I think I'm falling
I know I'm falling

songs.subject: alt rock / quitting your job
songs.lyrics:
Thirty-seven years I punched that clock
Hung my jacket on the same cold hook
Watched the young ones come and go like a flock
And nobody ever asked me for a second look

My hands are rough as the underside of stone
My back don't straighten like it used to do
Spent half my life inside that place alone
And half pretending that I liked it too

So I'm walking out, walking out today
Leaving this badge on the foreman's desk
I got nothing left that I need to say
Done wearing myself like a second-best

The fluorescent lights were making me blind
The meetings were a kind of living death
I kept leaving little pieces of my mind
Between the parking lot and my own front step

My daughter said, Dad, you look so tired
My wife said, honey, you're not even here
I used to think that I was something hired
Now I think I was just living out of fear

So I'm walking out, walking out today
Leaving this badge on the foreman's desk
I got nothing left that I need to say
Done wearing myself like a second-best

There's a garden out back that needs my hands
There's a fishing rod I haven't touched in years
There's a version of me that understands
That a man can disappear in the daily gears

I don't need their watch, their golden lies
Don't need the cake and the hollow applause
I just need to look up at open skies
And stop pretending I believe in their cause

So I'm walking out, walking out today
Leaving this badge on the foreman's desk
I got nothing left that I need to say
Done wearing myself like a second-best

They'll hire someone younger, someone cheap
Someone who still believes the posters on the wall
And I'll be home by three and fast asleep
And I won't feel like nothing at all

I won't feel like nothing at all

songs.subject: americana / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
The terminal is filling up with strangers carrying their lives
In bags above their heads and rolling suitcases and wives
And kids who can't sit still and grandmas crying soft and low
And here I am just standing here with nowhere left to go

Your gate is seventeen, they said it's boarding pretty soon
I keep checking all the signs like I might see a different room
You're pulling out your phone and acting like the words won't come
And all I want to do is beg you baby please don't run

These airport goodbyes are the cruelest kind
When you hand back a heart and you get on a flight
I'm watching your back disappear down the line
And I'm falling apart in the terminal light
In the terminal light

I told myself I'd hold it like a stone behind my ribs
Not let you see me shaking in the fluorescent glow
But every overhead announcement cuts me like it is
The world just going on not knowing what I know

You checked your bag, you said it was too heavy for the plane
I think about the weight of everything we couldn't carry
I think about the drive back in the February rain
The exit ramp, the empty seat, the silence we will marry

These airport goodbyes are the cruelest kind
When you hand back a heart and you get on a flight
I'm watching your back disappear down the line
And I'm falling apart in the terminal light
In the terminal light

There's a coffee shop with people laughing over lattes by the gate
And a little boy who's waving at the planes out on the tarmac
And somewhere in this ordinary Tuesday running late
Is the moment that I'll spend my whole life trying to get back

You turned around just once before you crossed the TSA
And I lifted up my hand like something slow and underwater
And you were gone before the gesture finished what it had to say
And I stood there with my hand up like a fool, like a soldier

These airport goodbyes are the cruelest kind
When you hand back a heart and you get on a flight
I'm watching your back disappear down the line
And I'm falling apart in the terminal light

I drove home through the rain like I was made of dirty glass
I put your number in my phone under a different name
I told myself that this was real and everything would pass
But I'm standing in arrivals waiting on a different plane

songs.subject: rock / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I found the letter in a box I hadn't touched in years
Between a photograph of us and something I'd forgot
The handwriting was yours and so were all the tears
That fell across the page in every syllable you wrote

You said you didn't want to carry this alone
You said you hoped that someday I'd be free
And all those years I kept that anger like a stone
I never stopped to think what it was costing me

I'm letting go of the fire I've been holding
I'm putting down the wreckage of the blame
Forgiveness isn't saying nothing happened
It's choosing not to burn inside the flame
I'm not doing this for you anymore
I'm doing this so I can breathe again
I'm walking through this dark into a different kind of morning
And I am letting go

I built a wall around the year that broke us both
Made every new beginning pay the debt
I swore on every version of a worthless oath
That I would never let myself forget

But the man I was becoming in that darkness
Was someone I don't want to recognize
I was clenching something rotten in the hardness
Of a heart that used to open to the sky

I'm letting go of the fire I've been holding
I'm putting down the wreckage of the blame
Forgiveness isn't saying nothing happened
It's choosing not to burn inside the flame
I'm not doing this for you anymore
I'm doing this so I can breathe again
I'm walking through this dark into a different kind of morning
And I am letting go

It doesn't happen like a river or a flood
It comes in quiet inches in the middle of the night
It comes when you stop tasting all the iron and the blood
And something small and stubborn leans toward light

I said your name out loud without the venom
And nothing fell apart and nothing burned
The silence after felt like an open window
The kind of thing that has to be unlearned

I'm putting down the wreckage of the blame

It's choosing not to burn inside the flame

And I am letting go

songs.subject: hip-hop / insomnia
songs.lyrics:
Yo, it's 2 AM and my ceiling's got my eyes
Can't shut my brain down no matter how I try
School tomorrow, seventh period, I'll be dead
But right now I got a thousand thoughts inside my head

Scrolling through my phone just to give my mind a task
Putting on a show that I've already seen the last
Mom keeps saying sleep is how you grow up right
But she don't know I've been fighting this since every night

Can't sleep, can't sleep, my brain won't let me go
Eyes wide open watching shadows on the wall below
Counting sheep got old around the hundredth try
Can't sleep, can't sleep, and I don't even know why

It started back in eighth grade with the tests and grades
Now I lie here in the dark and watch the hours fade
Three AM the house is quiet everyone's at rest
And I'm in here wide awake just feeling like a mess

Put my headphones in and let the music fill the space
Trying not to think about tomorrow's tired face
My homework's done, my bag is packed, I got no reason left
But anxiety don't need a reason, steals my breath

Can't sleep, can't sleep, my brain won't let me go
Eyes wide open watching shadows on the wall below
Counting sheep got old around the hundredth try
Can't sleep, can't sleep, and I don't even know why

Yo I Googled all the tricks, the melatonin too
Tried the lavender and chamomile and breathing through
Told the school counselor and she said reduce your stress
But stress is like the uniform I wear, I can't undress

Four AM the birds start up before the sun
And I know I lost the battle before it begun
I'll drag myself to first period half alive
Running on an energy drink just trying to survive

Can't sleep, can't sleep, my brain won't let me go
Eyes wide open watching shadows on the wall below
Counting sheep got old around the hundredth try
Can't sleep, can't sleep, and I don't even know why

Can't sleep, no rest, just me and all this noise
Lost inside my own head, drowning in the void
Maybe tomorrow night will finally be the one
But I've been saying that since long before the sun

songs.subject: lo-fi / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Three dots appeared at half past nine
Thought maybe you were checking in, thought maybe you were fine
Then the message came through quiet, seven words or so
Didn't even call me, just decided I should know

Read it once and then I read it again
Tried to find a reason hiding deep between the lines
Screen went blurry, had to put my phone face down
Sat there in the silence in my bedroom in the dark

Hey it's not you baby it's just me
Couldn't even say it to my face apparently
Just a paragraph and then you left me on read
Now I'm staring at the ceiling from my bed

Told my friend about it and she said you weren't worth it
Said you always had a way of making things uncertain
But she wasn't there the night we stayed up till the morning
Talking about everything and nothing and forever

Deleted then undeleted every photo twice
Tried to find the moment where it all stopped feeling right
Your hoodie's on the chair and I don't know what to do
Leave it there or fold it up or pretend I never knew

Hey it's not you baby it's just me
Couldn't even say it to my face apparently
Just a paragraph and then you left me on read
Now I'm staring at the ceiling from my bed

I made a playlist that I'll probably never play
Wrote a long response and then I threw the whole thing away
Sixteen and I know that this ain't the end of things
But it feels like it tonight beneath these quiet strings

Rain outside the window kind of soft and low
Somewhere in the city you're asleep and I don't know
If you even checked to see if I replied at all
Probably not, probably moved on, probably had a ball

Hey it's not you baby it's just me
Couldn't even say it to my face apparently
Just a paragraph and then you left me on read
Now I'm staring at the ceiling from my bed

Phone screen fades to black and I let it go
Nothing left to read and nothing left to know
Still the words are sitting somewhere in my chest
Guess I'll feel it fully once I finally get some rest

For each, write only the song lyrics -- no titles, no section labels like Verse or Chorus, no commentary. Separate each song with a line that is exactly:
@@@NEXT-SONG@@@

1. A hip-hop song about insomnia. from a teenager's point of view
2. A lo-fi song about a breakup text. from a teenager's point of view
3. A R&B song about falling in love at a party.
4. A punk song about a city at 3am. from a single mother's point of view

songs.subject: R&B / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
Walked in Friday night when the music was loud
Found you standing by the speaker in a beautiful crowd
Didn't know your name but something pulled me near
Like the bass line wrote a story I was dying to hear

You were laughing at something that your friend had said
Gold light from the kitchen catching sparks around your head
I pretended I was looking for a drink or the door
But I was really trying to work my way across the floor

Now I'm falling, falling slow
Into something warm I didn't know
Every song they play feels like it's ours tonight
I'm falling, can't stop falling
Heart wide open, arms out, stalling
Never thought a party'd feel this right

We talked in the kitchen for an hour or two
Found out you read the same books, got the same view
You said you don't usually do this kind of thing
I said me neither but I felt something ring

Swapped numbers by the back door while the party peaked
You laughed and said I seemed like what I'd been all week
I said that doesn't make sense and you said I know
Then somehow in that nonsense something started to grow

Now I'm falling, falling slow
Into something warm I didn't know
Every song they play feels like it's ours tonight
I'm falling, can't stop falling
Heart wide open, arms out, stalling
Never thought a party'd feel this right

Everybody's dancing but I only see you
The room could empty out and I'd still have my view
Your eyes keep finding mine across the crowded space
And every single time I feel my heart pick up the pace

I don't believe in signs but this feels close enough
I don't believe in quick things but this don't feel rushed
Like the universe decided on a Friday night
To put you in the doorway of the pale gold light

Now I'm falling, falling slow
Into something warm I didn't know
Every song they play feels like it's ours tonight
I'm falling, can't stop falling
Heart wide open, arms out, stalling
Never thought a party'd feel this right

Last song of the night and we're still here standing close
You smile at me and that's when I know
I'm gone, I'm done, I'm all the way in
And I'd do every single night again to find you where I've been

songs.subject: punk / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
City's dead but it ain't quiet, three AM alive
Dragging myself home from a double shift drive
Heels on concrete, street light flicker, bag on my back
Two miles from the babysitter, no time to slack

Corner store is locked up but the light's still on
Drunk kids on the stoop singing some forgotten song
They don't see me, I'm invisible, a woman and a ghost
Just another body in the city I love most

This city doesn't sleep it just pretends
Graffiti on the shutters, broken glass around the bends
I know every crack in every sidewalk tile
Been walking this same street for every desperate mile

Three AM the city shows its teeth and scars
Neon signs reflected in the hoods of parked up cars
I carried you nine months through this concrete cold
Three AM the city's ours alone

Passed the place where Danny used to run his crew
Now it's a vegan restaurant with an Instagram view
Progress or erasure, I don't know anymore
Still I keep my head down, keep my feet toward the door

Sirens in the distance, getting closer then they fade
Another night in progress, another mess that's been made
I think about my daughter sleeping safe inside
And I dig a little deeper for the last of my stride

This city doesn't sleep it just pretends
Graffiti on the shutters, broken glass around the bends
I know every crack in every sidewalk tile
Been walking this same street for every desperate mile

Three AM the city shows its teeth and scars
Neon signs reflected in the hoods of parked up cars
I carried you nine months through this concrete cold
Three AM the city's ours alone

Nobody asked me what I want for this place
Nobody asked the women at this grinding pace
We hold the whole thing up at three and four and five
We are the reason half this city stays alive

Almost at the door now, key between my fingers tight
This ain't fear it's habit from a thousand other nights
Check the lock, climb the stairs, peek inside her room
Little face asleep and breathing cuts right through the gloom

This city doesn't sleep it just pretends
Graffiti on the shutters, broken glass around the bends
I know every crack in every sidewalk tile
Been walking this same street for every desperate mile

Three AM the city shows its teeth and scars
Neon signs reflected in the hoods of parked up cars
I carried you nine months through this concrete cold
Three AM the city's ours, three AM we hold

songs.subject: reggae / a first apartment
songs.lyrics:
Cardboard boxes on the floor
Moving in through a broken door
This place is mine now, all mine alone
Guess I'm building a brand new home
She took the pictures off the wall
Left her perfume in the hall
But the sunlight hits different here
In this empty room I'll find my way clear

Rise up, rise up in the morning light
Cook my own food, sleep on my own side
No more waiting for a call that won't come through
Just me and these four walls starting something new
Rise up, rise up let the island wind blow
Water the plant she left, watch it grow
This little apartment gonna set me free
Finally got a space that's just for me

Mattress on the floor, that's alright
Gonna burn some incense through the night
Neighbors playing music down below
Learning all the rhythms as the good vibes flow
Fridge is almost empty, rent is due
But the view from this window pulls me through
Watching all the city lights come alive
Reminding me a man can still survive

Rise up, rise up in the morning light
Cook my own food, sleep on my own side
No more waiting for a call that won't come through
Just me and these four walls starting something new
Rise up, rise up let the island wind blow
Water the plant she left, watch it grow
This little apartment gonna set me free
Finally got a space that's just for me

She said I'd never make it on my own
Said I needed her just to feel at home
But I'm painting these walls a color bright
Learning how to sleep alone at night
Every crack and corner telling me
That broken hearts can still walk free
And though the loneliness comes around
In this little place I'm finally found

Rise up, rise up in the morning light
Cook my own food, sleep on my own side
No more waiting for a call that won't come through
Just me and these four walls starting something new
Rise up, rise up let the island wind blow
Water the plant she left, watch it grow
This little apartment gonna set me free
Finally got a space that's just for me

Yeah, rise up, rise up
This broken heart gonna heal in time
Rise up, rise up
This little apartment, it's mine, all mine

songs.subject: hip-hop / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
Terminal C, gate forty-four
Checked your bags twice, checked your bags once more
Four hours early cause you couldn't sleep
Making promises we're trying to keep
Coffee going cold on a plastic chair
Watching strangers move through the heavy air
Every flight board flipping to a new destination
Every minute closer to our separation

This is the part that nobody warned me about
When the words get tangled and won't come out
When the hug lasts longer than it ever should
When you'd give anything to be misunderstood
Cause understood means walking through that door
Understood means I don't see your face no more
At the airport, at the airport
This is where the real goodbyes live
At the airport, at the airport
Taking more than I know how to give

Security line moving way too fast
Holding onto something that won't last
You got your boarding pass gripped in your hand
And I'm standing here trying to understand
How a gate can be the whole world's edge
How a person walks right off a ledge
Into a plane that carries them away
While the ones who love them have to stay

This is the part that nobody warned me about
When the words get tangled and won't come out
When the hug lasts longer than it ever should
When you'd give anything to be misunderstood
Cause understood means walking through that door
Understood means I don't see your face no more
At the airport, at the airport
This is where the real goodbyes live
At the airport, at the airport
Taking more than I know how to give

We said we'd call we said we'd text
We said we'd figure out what comes next
Made a joke about the airline food
Tried to keep the whole thing light the mood
But your eyes were doing something else
Putting feelings on a higher shelf
And I watched you go through that security frame
And the whole world looked completely different and the same

Took your jacket, you forgot it on the seat
Held it all the way back to my street
Smelled like you still, hung it by the door
Airports hit different than they did before
Every takeoff sound cuts through the room
Every contrail slicing through the blue
I don't go to terminals no more
Can't stand watching people walk through that door

This is the part that nobody warned me about
When the words get tangled and won't come out
When the hug lasts longer than it ever should
When you'd give anything to be misunderstood
Cause understood means walking through that door
Understood means I don't see your face no more
At the airport, at the airport
This is where the real goodbyes live
At the airport, at the airport
Taking more than I know how to give

Gate forty-four, gate forty-four
I still drive past but I don't stop no more
Gate forty-four, gate forty-four
Some goodbyes stay open like a door

songs.subject: hip-hop / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
Sixty-three dollars till the fifteenth, yeah
Ramen on the stove but she's sitting right here
Light bill overdue, phone plan prepaid
But she laughing at something I said and I swear
I forgot about the numbers I forgot about the math
When she smiles like that everything goes off track
I'm the richest man in a poor man's shoes
Baby I got everything, I got you

Can't afford the fancy restaurant scene
But I cook for her like a straight up king
Candles from the dollar store still burn bright
Table for two every Thursday night
She don't care about the car I drive
Says she loves the way I come alive
When I'm talking bout my dreams out loud
Baby makes me feel like I own a crowd

Broke but I'm happy, happy, happy
Pockets empty but my heart stay stacked
Broke but I'm happy, happy, happy
Got everything I need and it ain't on a rack
They say money's the root but love is the tree
And she the only thing that's growing in me
Broke but I'm happy, yeah I'm happy
Cause she loves me and she loves me free

Landlord knocked again on the door
She hid behind it on the kitchen floor
We both laughing trying not to make a sound
Best game I ever played when she's around
Split the last few dollars at the store
She let me pick the cereal, I loved her more
That's a moment that no bank account buys
That's a moment that no interest rate describes

Watching other couples throwing money around
She lace her fingers in mine without a sound
Like she telling me that this is more than enough
And honestly right now I believe it, I do
Cause when I look at her I don't see what's missing
I see everything that matters in the everything I'm living
She the dividend, she the compound gain
She the only thing that make the struggle not a strain

Broke but I'm happy, happy, happy
Pockets empty but my heart stay stacked
Broke but I'm happy, happy, happy
Got everything I need and it ain't on a rack
They say money's the root but love is the tree
And she the only thing that's growing in me
Broke but I'm happy, yeah I'm happy
Cause she loves me and she loves me free

One day the money gonna catch up to the dream
Gonna give her everything and more than it seems
But right now tonight with sixty-three to my name
And her head on my shoulder I would not change a thing
Not a single digit, not a single dime
Not a single second of this barely-making-it time
Cause we young and we hungry and we got something real
And no amount of money ever taught me how to feel

Broke but I'm happy, happy, happy
Pockets empty but my heart stay stacked
Broke but I'm happy, happy, happy
Got everything I need and it ain't on a rack
They say money's the root but love is the tree
And she the only thing that's growing in me

Cause she loves me and she loves me free

songs.subject: alt rock / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Screen lit up at 2 AM
Seven words I read and then I read again
No punctuation, no explanation given
Just a door slamming shut on something living
I was half asleep, now I'm fully awake
Trying to count how many ways a person breaks
Cause there's a way you break when someone leaves the room
And there's a way you break when it comes through bluetooth

You couldn't look me in the face to do it
Couldn't even call, no you just typed right through it
Sent it like a file, sent it like a form
Like I was something that needed to be informed
I keep reading it over, over, over again
Trying to find the moment that it started to end
Was it something I said was it something I did
Or did you just grow tired of the life that we lived

I hate your lowercase letters
I hate the way you spelled it out
I hate that I can see you read mine
I hate what this is all about
I hate the timestamp, I hate the thread
I hate the words still living in my head
Two blue checks, you left me on read
You left me on read, you left me on read

I tried to call, it went to voicemail straight
You blocked me or you let it ring, I can't keep this straight
I showed my friend the message, she said say nothing back
But my fingers kept returning to that open chat
Three hours I watched the cursor blink and blink
Trying to find the language for the way I sink
Every word I typed felt wrong, I deleted them all
Cause how do you respond to a wrecking ball

We had a Tuesday thing, we had a grocery store
We had a side of the bed, we don't have it anymore
All reduced to pixels on a five-inch screen
The ugliest ending that I've ever seen
I deserved a conversation, I deserved your eyes
I deserved the courtesy of real goodbyes
Not a notification, not a bubble gray
Not a screenshot that my friends will see someday

I hate your lowercase letters
I hate the way you spelled it out
I hate that I can see you read mine
I hate what this is all about
I hate the timestamp, I hate the thread
I hate the words still living in my head
Two blue checks, you left me on read
You left me on read, you left me on read

Three weeks later and it's still sitting there
Scrolled back up to it again, yeah I know it isn't fair
To keep returning to the scene of something dead
Keep pressing on the bruise, keep rereading what you said
I should delete the thread, I should delete the thread
But I keep it like a wound that I pick instead
Some part of me still hoping that there's something missed
Some explanation hiding in that cold dismiss

But the screen stays dark, no typing bubble shows
No second message coming, everybody knows
You said what you had to say in seven words or less
And I'm still here deciphering the emptiness
I turned my read receipts off, first thing I changed
Cause nobody deserves to know that you've been rearranged
Nobody deserves to watch the dots disappear
Nobody deserves to end up here

I hate your lowercase letters
I hate the way you spelled it out
I hate that I can see you read mine
I hate what this is all about
I hate the timestamp, I hate the thread
I hate the words still living in my head
Two blue checks, you left me on read
You left me on read, you left me on read

Left me on read
Left me on read
Somewhere between sent and delivered I lost everything
Left me on read

Screen lit up at 2 AM
Seven words I read and then I read again
No punctuation, no explanation given
Just a door slamming shut on something living
I was half asleep, now I'm fully awake
Trying to count how many ways a person breaks
Cause there's a way you break when someone leaves the room
And there's a way you break when it comes through bluetooth

You couldn't look me in the face to do it
Couldn't even call, no you just typed right through it
Sent it like a file, sent it like a form
Like I was something that needed to be informed
I keep reading it over, over, over again
Trying to find the moment that it started to end
Was it something I said was it something I did

But my fingers kept returning to that open chat
Three hours I watched the cursor blink and blink
Trying to find the language for the way I sink
Every word I typed felt wrong, I deleted them all
Cause how do you respond to a wrecking ball

We had a Tuesday thing, we had a grocery store
We had a side of the bed, we don't have it anymore
All reduced to pixels on a five-inch screen
The ugliest ending that I've ever seen
I deserved a conversation, I deserved your eyes
I deserved the courtesy of real goodbyes
Not a notification, not a bubble gray
Not a screenshot that my friends will see someday

songs.subject: punk / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
Cracked the window, left your smell behind
Safety pins and photographs, your ghost is on my mind
The city swallowed you like it swallows everything
Now I'm here with broken strings and all this suffering

You said you'd write, you said you'd call
You said a lot of things before you let yourself fall
Into somewhere new, somewhere I can't follow
And every Tuesday night just feels completely hollow

I don't want your sympathy
I don't want your sorry face
I want you back on my front steps
I want you back in this place
I'm screaming at the ceiling fan
I'm kicking at the wall
I don't want your postcard letters
I don't want nothing at all

Threw your hoodie in a box beneath the bed
Pulled it out at 3am and wore it instead
The television's broken and the landlord doesn't care
And everything is loud except when I want noise up there

We used to tear this neighborhood completely apart
Now I'm just a stupid kid with a stupid broken heart
The show last Friday wasn't half of what it used to be
Without you shoving to the front and screaming next to me

I don't want your sympathy
I don't want your sorry face
I want you back on my front steps
I want you back in this place
I'm screaming at the ceiling fan
I'm kicking at the wall
I don't want your postcard letters
I don't want nothing at all

So go ahead and find yourself
Go ahead and shine
Just know that every mile you put between us
Costs me every time
Go ahead and love it there
Go ahead and stay
I'll be here in the same old wreckage
Rotting where you left me anyway

I don't want your sympathy
I don't want your sorry face
I want you back on my front steps
I want you back in this place
I'm screaming at the ceiling fan
I'm kicking at the wall
I don't want your postcard letters
I want you back is all
I want you back is all
I want you back is all

songs.subject: trap / working a dead-end job
songs.lyrics:
Twelve hour shift and my feet done bled
Pour another drink, smile, nod my head
These ones tippin' nothin', act like I'm they friend
Clock says 2 AM, this cycle never end
Empty glasses stackin' up like all my debt
Every single night the same, you ain't seen nothing yet
Manager come talkin' 'bout I gotta stay late
Another thirty dollars, yeah that's my fate

Pourin' liquor for the rich while I'm barely alive
Got a second job waiting when I leave at five
They laughing and they toasting, celebrating life
I'm wiping down this counter with a plastic knife
Dead end, dead end, this bar my prison cell
Smile pretty for the customers, don't let 'em tell
That behind these eyes I'm drowning in the well
Dead end, dead end, I got a story to tell

Aye, Tuesday night and it's slow up in here
Only drunk is Bobby Ray been nursing the same beer
He talking 'bout his glory days, I've heard it all before
My back is killing me, my hands are cracked and sore
Tips split four ways barely cover my light
I got a daughter at the house need shoes before Friday night
Degree collecting dust up on my bedroom shelf
Corporate said they hiring, lied to me myself

Pourin' liquor for the rich while I'm barely alive
Got a second job waiting when I leave at five
They laughing and they toasting, celebrating life
I'm wiping down this counter with a plastic knife
Dead end, dead end, this bar my prison cell
Smile pretty for the customers, don't let 'em tell
That behind these eyes I'm drowning in the well
Dead end, dead end, I got a story to tell

They want me smilin' when they disrespect
Want me bouncin' back with nothing but respect
Bachelorette party spillin' on my shoes
Acting like my dignity is theirs to use
Owner driving foreign cars and buying boats
I'm counting quarters in my coat
This system got me locked in a rotation
Serving people's celebrations
While I'm dying slow, no vacation

Last call ringing and I'm mopping up the floor
Counting all my ones and sliding out the door
Sun coming up and I gotta do it again
This is what they mean when they say a dead end
Nobody seeing me, nobody knowing my name
Just another body keeping up the same game
I swear tomorrow I'ma send that application
But tonight I'm just a ghost in this location

Pourin' liquor for the rich while I'm barely alive
Got a second job waiting when I leave at five
They laughing and they toasting, celebrating life
I'm wiping down this counter with a plastic knife
Dead end, dead end, this bar my prison cell
Smile pretty for the customers, don't let 'em tell
That behind these eyes I'm drowning in the well
Dead end, dead end, I got a story to tell
Dead end, dead end
I got a story to tell

songs.subject: country / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
The moving truck came early on a Saturday in June
You had boxes stacked up taller than the afternoon
I stood there on the driveway like a fool without the words
Just the sound of cardboard scraping and the morning birds

Your mama cried and hugged me said take care of yourself
I smiled like I wasn't dying on the inside like hell
You came out in that sundress with your hair tied back
And I knew the whole ride over I wasn't getting that back

Honey I still reach for you on the cold side of the bed
I still talk to you in the mornings like you haven't gone ahead
To some better place, some bigger sky, some life I couldn't give
But lord these Georgia nights are the hardest ones to live
Without you, without you
Every firefly's just taunting me with what we used to know
Without you, without you
The whole county feels like winter and you took away the glow

I drive by the diner where you always got the pie
The girl behind the counter got your color eyes
I had to pull the truck over out on Miller Road
Set there in the gravel underneath my heavy load

Everybody tells me time will do what time will do
But nobody in this whole town ever loved you like I do
And your new address sits folded in my wallet like a prayer
That I'm too scared and too stubborn and too broken down to share

Honey I still reach for you on the cold side of the bed
I still talk to you in the mornings like you haven't gone ahead
To some better place, some bigger sky, some life I couldn't give
But lord these Georgia nights are the hardest ones to live
Without you, without you
Every firefly's just taunting me with what we used to know
Without you, without you
The whole county feels like winter and you took away the glow

Maybe you found somebody there who deserves you right
Maybe you're dancing in some city bar on a Friday night
Maybe the mountains out there treat you better than I could
Maybe leaving was the right thing, maybe leaving was good

But I still got your garden in the backyard growing wild
And I still got the picture of you laughing like a child
Taken out on Caldwell Creek the summer we turned twenty-two
And I ain't got the heart to pull those flowers out, honey
They still reach toward the sun the way I still reach toward you

Honey I still reach for you on the cold side of the bed
I still talk to you in the mornings like you haven't gone ahead
To some better place, some bigger sky, some life I couldn't give
But lord these Georgia nights are the hardest ones to live
Without you, without you
Every firefly's just taunting me with what we used to know
Without you, without you
The whole county feels like winter and you took away the glow
You took away the glow
Lord you took away the glow

songs.subject: ballad / faith
songs.lyrics:
I have stood at the edge of everything I knew
When the road went dark and disappeared from view
When the names I called out echoed back to me
And the ground beneath my feet was all I couldn't see

I have held the hands of people going home
I have been the only one left standing alone
In a room where all the answers turned to dust
And the only thing remaining there was trust

Something holds me when the holding makes no sense
Something fills the hollows when the grief is too immense
I have called it by a hundred different names
But it burns inside me still the same
Like a candle in a cathedral, like a fire in the rain
Unbroken, undiminished, through the sorrow and the pain
It was there before I found it, it will be there when I'm through
This is what I know of faith, this is what has carried me to you

I was twenty years old when my father's body failed
When the doctors turned their eyes away and all the prayers went stale
I sat beside him in a room that smelled of time
And I said I don't believe in this but let him know he's mine

Something lifted in that moment, something changed the air
Like a warmth that had no reason and a grace beyond compare
He opened up his eyes once more and found my face
And I felt it then, I felt it then, I felt it fill that place

Something holds me when the holding makes no sense
Something fills the hollows when the grief is too immense
I have called it by a hundred different names
But it burns inside me still the same
Like a candle in a cathedral, like a fire in the rain
Unbroken, undiminished, through the sorrow and the pain
It was there before I found it, it will be there when I'm through
This is what I know of faith, this is what has carried me to you

I am not the kind who wears it on my sleeve
I am not the kind with easy answers to believe
I have doubted in the darkness, I have cursed the sky
I have laid down in the silence asking God to tell me why

And the silence wasn't empty, though it gave me nothing clear
It was full of something ancient, something steady, something near
Not a voice, not an answer, not a vision or a sign
Just a presence like a river running parallel to mine

Something holds me when the holding makes no sense
Something fills the hollows when the grief is too immense
I have called it by a hundred different names
But it burns inside me still the same
Like a candle in a cathedral, like a fire in the rain
Unbroken, undiminished, through the sorrow and the pain
It was there before I found it, it will be there when I'm through
This is what I know of faith, this is what has carried me to you

So I'll walk into tomorrow with the questions still in hand
With the things I cannot prove and the things I don't understand
With the fear and with the wonder both wrapped up in one breath
With the mystery of living and the certainty of death

And whatever waits beyond it, I will meet it unafraid
Because something has been with me in every choice I've made
Through the broken and the beautiful, the lost and the reclaimed
Through the fire I walked through and came out still the same

Something holds me when the holding makes no sense
Something fills the hollows when the grief is too immense
I have called it by a hundred different names
But it burns inside me still the same
Like a candle in a cathedral, like a fire in the rain
Unbroken, undiminished, through the sorrow and the pain
It was there before I found it, it will be there when I'm through

songs.subject: synthpop / a new baby
songs.lyrics:
You arrived like a signal breaking through the static
I never knew my heart could tune to something automatic
Your fingers curl like tiny satellites in space
And every time you breathe I see the future on your face

I've been running on a frequency of one
But now the whole transmission has begun
You changed the wavelength of the life I knew
Every single pulse inside me beats for you

You are the neon light that fills the darkest room
You are the synthesizer playing out our tune
I never knew that love could feel this brand new
Little miracle, my whole world is you

songs.subject: R&B / the ocean
songs.lyrics:
Last call was forty minutes ago and the stools are all empty
I'm wiping down the mahogany thinking about the plenty
Of stories that get told in here, the salt and the sorrow
But nothing hits me harder than the ocean come tomorrow
See I close up every Saturday and drive down to the shore
And something about standing there just opens up a door

The ocean she don't ask me how my shift was, how I'm feeling
She just rolls up on the shoreline with that low and heavy dealing
She's been pouring out her troubles since before the world had names
And I respect a thing that gives without expecting anything

She's deep enough to drown in and wide enough to save you
She'll take your worst confession and she'll never once betray you
I been slinging gin and tonic, pouring out the rye
But the ocean is the only bartender I trust besides my own two eyes
She pours it to the brim every single time
She pours it to the brim every single time

I get it, you know, the way she works, she listens without talking
The way she pulls things out of you while you just keep on walking
Along her edge, I've seen grown men just crumble at her feet
And she swallows every tear they drop and still the current keeps
I've heard a thousand secrets standing six feet from the rail
But the ones I told the water are the ones beyond the pale

songs.subject: hip-hop / moving to a new city
songs.lyrics:
October 14th, pulled in with a U-Haul and a prayer
Two suitcases, a box fan, and a mattress without a frame
Didn't know a single person in a city of eight million
Felt like dropping a grain of sand into the Pacific Ocean
Sat on the floor of my new apartment eating gas station sushi
And I wrote this down so I'd remember how it really felt

New streets don't know my name yet and I don't know theirs
Still learning which train to take and how to climb these stairs
GPS on everything, still getting lost
Paying rent on a dream and counting up the cost
But I wrote it all down in the margins of a map
Left the old life folded up and ain't going back

Ain't going back, ain't going back
New city got my future and I ain't going back

November 3rd, I found a coffee shop that keeps late hours
The barista learned my order by the second week, small victories
Got a temp job downtown filing papers for a firm
Spent my lunch breaks walking blocks just trying to learn the grid
The loneliness is loud, man, it's louder than the traffic
But loneliness is honest and I wrote that down too

New streets don't know my name yet and I don't know theirs
Still learning which train to take and how to climb these stairs
GPS on everything, still getting lost
Paying rent on a dream and counting up the cost
But I wrote it all down in the margins of a map
Left the old life folded up and ain't going back

Ain't going back, ain't going back
New city got my future and I ain't going back

December 19th, I cried watching the old neighborhood on Google Maps
Zoomed into my mama's street and sat there for an hour
Then I closed the laptop and I cooked my first real meal in the kitchen
And I ate it standing up because I still don't have a table
But the window showed the skyline and the skyline didn't care
About where I came from, only where I'm trying to get to

January 2nd, new entry, different handwriting almost
Like the city's starting to rewrite the person holding the pen
Met some people at a show, exchanged some numbers, made some plans
Small things that feel enormous when you built them out of nothing
Found a park I like, found a route that feels like mine
Wrote it down so I'd remember that surviving took its time

New streets are learning my name now and I'm learning theirs
I know which train to take and I can climb these stairs
Don't need the GPS on every single block
Starting to feel the rhythm and I'm starting not to stop
Still writing it all down in the margins of a map
Left the old life folded up and I ain't going back

Ain't going back, ain't going back
New city got my future and I ain't going back
Ain't going back, ain't going back
Wrote every hard day down and I ain't going back

This city don't owe me nothing
And I don't owe it fear
Just writing down the story
Of how I ended up here

songs.subject: disco / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
Quarter to twelve and the room is still humming
The candles are burning low
I've been waiting all evening for something to happen
And then came the phone, and the glow

That little light blinking alive on the table
Lit up the whole darkened room
I reached for it slow like I knew it was magic
Beneath the big face of the moon

Ring ring ring through the midnight
Somebody calling my name through the night
Ring ring ring through the midnight
Dancing alone in the telephone light
The night came alive when you called me at midnight
Ring ring ring ring ring

I picked it up laughing not knowing the reason
Your voice came in warm on the line
You said you'd been thinking of me since the evening
And honey I said I'd been thinking of you the whole time

We talked about everything, nothing, and somewhere
Between all the spaces we share
The disco ball turning in some far imagination
Was scattering light through the air

Ring ring ring through the midnight
Somebody calling my name through the night
Ring ring ring through the midnight
Dancing alone in the telephone light
The night came alive when you called me at midnight
Ring ring ring ring ring

So I'm spinning around in the kitchen in socks now
The linoleum shining like stars
I'm holding the phone like it's all that I need now
Forgetting how lonely we are

The music from somewhere is creeping in under the door
And I'm dancing like nobody's watching
Except for your voice on the line at the midnight hour
Telling me keep on, keep on, keep on

Ring ring ring through the midnight
Somebody calling my name through the night
Ring ring ring through the midnight
Dancing alone in the telephone light
The night came alive when you called me at midnight
Ring ring ring ring ring

The clock rolls past twelve now and neither of us moves
We're breathing in sync on the line
Two people alone on the separate sides of the city
Inventing a Saturday night

So let the phone ring through the midnight
Let somebody call out your name in the night
Let the phone ring through the midnight
Dancing alone in the telephone light
The night comes alive when you call me at midnight
Ring ring ring ring ring

Ring ring, don't stop now
Ring ring, don't stop now
Ring ring through the midnight
Ring ring through the midnight
Don't ever stop calling my name

songs.subject: americana / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
Mama's crying in the kitchen again
Daddy's got his good boots on
My sister's twisting flowers in her hair
In the mirror where she's been since dawn
I'm sitting on the porch steps in my suit
That itches at the collar and the sleeves
Everybody's acting like today
Is bigger than anything I've seen

And maybe it is, I don't know yet
I'm just sixteen watching grown folks cry
Uncle Tommy's got a flask behind the barn
Grandma's singing some old lullaby
The oak trees don't look different than before
The gravel driveway's still the same old gray
But everybody swears that something's changed
Just because my sister's walking away

She used to drive me down to Miller's Creek
And let me ride up on the tailgate edge
She used to sneak me half her Halloween
And talk me back from every nervous ledge
Now she's standing in a dress like something
I don't have the words to say
And the boy beside her looks at her
Like she hung every star in the Milky Way

And maybe that's enough, I don't know yet
I'm just sixteen trying hard to see
Why a good thing ending feels like loss
Even when it's good as good can be
The preacher's talking 'bout forever now
The fiddle's starting slow then finding its way
And I'm crying and I don't know why
I'm just sixteen on my sister's wedding day

Later on they're dancing on the lawn
The fireflies are coming out to play
Somebody's laughing over by the pond
The little kids are chasing every stray
I watch her dancing with our daddy now
His big hand trembling on her back
And I think I understand a little more
About the things in life you can't take back

Yeah maybe I get it, just a little bit
I'm just sixteen but something's clear tonight
Some things are worth the ache inside your chest
Some hellos deserve a long goodbye
The moon is hanging low and heavy now
The lanterns strung between the trees all sway
And I'll remember every single thing
About the night of my sister's wedding day

songs.subject: indie folk / a funeral
songs.lyrics:
I didn't know what to wear so I wore black
I didn't know what to say so I said nothing
The drive there took forty minutes on the dot
I counted every exit sign like something
I needed to hold onto, some kind of math
To keep me tethered to the ordinary world
Because the ordinary world felt far away
From where I was, from where we were

The flowers on the altar were too bright
The kind of yellow that belongs in summer
Not in a room where people hold their breath
And try to cry quiet so the others
Don't feel obliged to comfort them just yet
I held my program and I read your name
Three times just to feel the letters out
Three times like a song or like a game

I wrote this down tonight because I had to
I wrote this down so I don't lose the edges
The way the light came sideways through the glass
The way grief sits on people like a sediment
I wrote this down because you were my friend
And I don't want to smooth it into something
Pretty and digestible and clean
I want it jagged, I want it to mean

Your mother wore your favorite color
Somebody told me that and I believed it
A soft kind of green like early spring
And she was steady, God, the way she breathed it
All in and out and stood and thanked the room
I don't know where that kind of courage grows
I wanted to tell her you were everything
But I just shook her hand and said I know

I wrote this down tonight because I had to
I wrote this down so I don't lose the edges
The way the light came sideways through the glass
The way grief sits on people like a sediment
I wrote this down because you were my friend
And I don't want to smooth it into something
Pretty and digestible and clean
I want it jagged, I want it to mean

Outside after, in the parking lot
The clouds were doing something complicated
Everybody gathered in their little groups
And I stood between them, sort of weighted
Down in both directions, wanting in
To every circle and to none at all
I drove home with the radio turned off
And all the windows down despite the fall

There's a thing about a gathering like that
Where everyone arrives to say the same
Important thing in their own separate way
And all of it is true and none of it contains
Enough, not even close to enough
To hold the size of what the room was for
I keep returning to your laugh instead
The one that sounded like a stuck screen door

I wrote this down tonight because I had to
I wrote this down so I don't lose the edges
The way the light came sideways through the glass
The way grief sits on people like a sediment
I wrote this down because you were my friend
And I don't want to smooth it into something
Pretty and digestible and clean
I want it jagged, I want it to mean

I want it jagged
I want it to mean
I want it honest
And I want it seen

songs.subject: bluegrass / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I been writing this down since half past eight
With a cup of cold coffee and the morning light late
Coming through the window where the curtain's gone thin
And I'm thinking about the town that I was raised in
Cedar Street still runs down to the mill
Where my daddy lost his hearing and the paycheck still
Came every Friday folded in his hand
Thirty years of faithful to that land

I remember the smell of the hardware store
Old Mr. Calloway and his wooden floor
And the way the bell above the door would ring
Like it was announcing something worth announcing
The water tower painted blue and white
Somebody's name up there from ninety-five
I don't know if it's still there, I don't know
Been a while since I had a reason to go

This is my town in the back of my mind
Scrawled in the margins of a different kind
Of life than the one I thought I'd have
I got out and I thought that would be glad
But the creek behind the Baptist church still runs
Through every dream I have under different suns
And I write it down to remember the whole thing
Cedar Street and the mill and everything

Becky Harrison had a horse named Lou
Kept her in the field by the old depot
We used to feed her apples after school
Before the depot closed and Becky moved to Duluth
The grain elevator standing tall
I used to think it was the biggest thing of all
Turns out it was, in its own way still
Bigger than most things I've since fulfilled

Fourth of July they'd shut the main road down
Everybody in the county came to town
Hot dogs and the Rotary Club's parade
And I'd stay out till every firefly faded
Mama'd let us sleep out in the yard
Those nights that felt like they weren't trying hard
Just soft and long and easy as a breath
I was too young then to know to hold it

This is my town in the back of my mind
Scrawled in the margins of a different kind
Of life than the one I thought I'd have
I got out and I thought that would be glad
But the creek behind the Baptist church still runs
Through every dream I have under different suns
And I write it down to remember the whole thing
Cedar Street and the mill and everything

I ain't gonna lie and say it was all good
Winters were hard and people misunderstood
Each other more than they ever let on
Some of us couldn't wait to be gone
And I was one of them with my bag packed tight
Took the highway out on a Tuesday night
Didn't look back but I looked back in my sleep
Found it there in the places that I keep

The school gymnasium with the leaky roof
The way the truth in a small town was never proof
Of anything you said, it just was known
The way you know the walk from school to home
I carry it like a stone that's smooth and warm
Worn down by the years since I was born
And every now and then I'll take it out
Turn it in my hand and know what I'm about

And I write it down to remember the whole thing
Cedar Street and the mill and everything

Cedar Street and the mill and everything
