songs.subject: lo-fi / driving with the windows down
songs.lyrics:
Warm asphalt rolling underneath my wheels
The radio is crackling something old and real
Summer heat is bending up the road ahead
Got nowhere to be until the sun is dead

Wind is tangling fingers through my hair
Every traffic light turns green like it don't care
Coffee going cold inside the cupholder there
Just me and all this open amber air

I been running from the city for a while
Letting every mile stretch into a smile
The engine hums a song I almost know
Windows down and letting myself go

Dusty fields are bleeding into gold
The afternoon is turning sweet and old
A hawk is drawing circles up above
Drifting like a half-remembered love

Convenience store and parking lot at three
Bought a soda and a paperback I'll never read
Back inside the seat that knows my shape
This is not a plan this is an escape

I been running from the city for a while
Letting every mile stretch into a smile
The engine hums a song I almost know
Windows down and letting myself go

The long way home is sometimes all you need
A little wind a little summer and a little speed
No destination saved inside my phone
Just the feeling that I'm never quite alone

When the highway bends I bend along
When the signal fades I make my own song
Arm hanging out and catching all the heat
This old world feels good from this old seat

I been running from the city for a while
Letting every mile stretch into a smile
The engine hums a song I almost know
Windows down and letting myself go

Windows down and letting myself go
Nowhere fast and taking it real slow

songs.subject: disco / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Friday night I'm dressed and ready at the door
Wearing all the things you always said you'd adore
Candles on the table and the good wine poured
And then my phone lit up and lord

A little bubble with your name in blue
Four lines long and every word cut through
No phone call no flowers no face to face
Just a text that put me in my place

Oh oh oh but I'm still in love with you
Oh oh oh what am I supposed to do
You sent a text to end it all tonight
While I was standing underneath the disco light
Oh oh oh I read it three times through
Oh oh oh the whole floor fell into
How do you just type those words and send
How do you let a text message be the end

The mirror ball is spinning overhead
Throwing light on everything you said
People dancing all around me in the glow
And I got nowhere left to go

I keep the screen lit up against my chest
Pressing all your sentences against
The sequins on the dress you used to love
While this whole room is rising up above

Oh oh oh but I'm still in love with you
Oh oh oh what am I supposed to do
You sent a text to end it all tonight
While I was standing underneath the disco light
Oh oh oh I read it three times through
Oh oh oh the whole floor fell into
How do you just type those words and send
How do you let a text message be the end

The bass is heavy and the lights are low
And everyone around me doesn't know
That I just lost the best thing in my life
Between a dinner reservation and a strobe light

I want to call you up and make you see
That four lines in a phone cannot erase what we
Built up over all those tender years
Honey I am standing here in tears

The DJ doesn't know he's playing our song
Every beat reminding me of where it went wrong
I'm dancing by myself in this sequin dress
Still reading your goodbye like it's a test

Oh oh oh but I'm still in love with you
Oh oh oh what am I supposed to do
You sent a text to end it all tonight
While I was standing underneath the disco light
Oh oh oh I read it three times through
Oh oh oh the whole floor fell into
How do you just type those words and send
How do you let a text message be the end

Oh let a text be the end
No you don't call
Oh you don't show up at all
Just a little blue bubble
And I'm standing here in trouble
Still so in love with you

songs.subject: blues / getting sober
songs.lyrics:
Woke up on a Tuesday with the shakes again
Bottle on the nightstand like my oldest friend
Mirror on the wall showed me something new
A man I didn't recognize was staring through

Been doing this dance since nineteen eighty-three
The whiskey and the darkness keeping time with me
But something cracked this morning like a window pane
I put the bottle down and felt the sun and rain

Lord I'm putting it down putting it down today
Gonna walk a crooked line but I'm walking anyway
I got thirty years of trouble in these hands of mine
But I'm putting it down putting it down this time

Called my brother Jimmy up at half past eight
Said I need your help before it gets too late
He didn't say I told you so he just said come on
Said we'll sit together till the worst is gone

The coffee at the church hall tasted thin and bad
But every face around that table known what I had
Told my story out loud for the very first time
Lord it's hard to count the years you threw away on wine

Lord I'm putting it down putting it down today
Gonna walk a crooked line but I'm walking anyway
I got thirty years of trouble in these hands of mine
But I'm putting it down putting it down this time

Yeah the body wants what the body wants it's true
And the mind plays tricks when the afternoon comes through
Four o'clock's the meanest hour I ever knew
When the shadows get long and the old voice speaks to you

But I got a little chip that's made of plastic white
And I hold it in my pocket through the restless night
Thirty days ain't nothing but it's something too
Every sober morning is a sky brand new

Lord I'm putting it down putting it down today
Gonna walk a crooked line but I'm walking anyway
I got thirty years of trouble in these hands of mine
But I'm putting it down putting it down this time

I went out to see my daughter and her little boy
He grabbed my sober finger with that whole hand joy
I thought about the years I missed and let that hurt
Pressed my face down in the smell of clean dirt

Gonna be an honest man if it takes my whole life
Gonna call my daddy's grave and call my former wife
Tell them all the things the bottle had me swallowing
This old dog is healing and he's following

Lord I'm putting it down putting it down today
Gonna walk a crooked line but I'm walking anyway
I got thirty years of trouble in these hands of mine
But I'm putting it down putting it down
Yeah I'm putting it down
Lord I'm putting it down this time

songs.subject: pop / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I held onto every word you said
Like broken glass inside my head
Replayed the fight a thousand times at night
Convinced that I was always right

But I've been carrying this weight so long
My shoulders ache from holding on
Maybe I don't have to understand
To finally let go of your hand

I'm learning how to breathe again
I'm choosing you over the anger
It doesn't mean I'm saying it was fine
But I'm drawing a new line
We're just kids trying to figure out
What forgiveness is all about
I'm not doing this for you
I'm doing this so I can move
I'm learning how to breathe again

I used to think that letting go
Meant saying that you won the show
But my therapist and my mom both say
That holding grudges makes you pay

I deleted all the screenshots saved
The evidence of how you misbehaved
Not because it never even happened
But because my heart needs to be open

I'm learning how to breathe again
I'm choosing you over the anger
It doesn't mean I'm saying it was fine
But I'm drawing a new line
We're just kids trying to figure out
What forgiveness is all about
I'm not doing this for you
I'm doing this so I can move
I'm learning how to breathe again

Maybe next year we'll sit in the same lunch room
Maybe we won't and that's okay too
Forgiveness doesn't mean we go back to before
It just means I'm not at war anymore

But I'm drawing a new line
We're just kids trying to figure out

songs.subject: blues / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning, reached for the telephone
Said I woke up this morning, reached out for the telephone
Then I remembered, daddy, you been gone so long, gone so long from home

Used to call you every Sunday, seven on the dot
Said I called you every Sunday, seven on the dot
Now Sunday just a graveyard for everything I got

Oh lord, the house is quiet, quiet as a stone
Yeah the house is quiet, quiet as a cold cold stone
Seventy-three years old now and I never felt so alone

Your old hat still hangs there on the kitchen door
Your old hat still hangs there on the kitchen door
I can't move it, daddy, can't move it, can't move it no more

I found your harmonica underneath the bed
Found your old harmonica underneath the bed
Still smells like the tobacco and the life you led

Oh lord, the house is quiet, quiet as a stone
Yeah the house is quiet, quiet as a cold cold stone
Seventy-three years old now and I never felt so alone

They say it gets easier, give it enough time
Everybody tells me, son, give it enough time
But time's the very thing I'm running low on, and that ain't no crime

I see your eyes in my reflection, looking back at me
See your eyes in the mirror, daddy, looking back at me
Funny how a man becomes the thing he used to see

Oh lord, the house is quiet, quiet as a stone
Yeah the house is quiet, quiet as a cold cold stone
Seventy-three years old now and I never felt so alone

I'll be seeing you, old man, I'll be seeing you soon
Yeah I'll be seeing you, daddy, seeing you real soon
Save me a place beside you underneath that big old moon

Oh lord, the house is quiet, quiet as a stone
Yeah the house is quiet, quiet as a cold cold stone
Seventy-three years old now, but I'm finally coming home

songs.subject: disco / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Stepping out on Friday night
Neon signs and traffic lights
Every corner got a story, every block a different beat
The smell of pizza and the summer heat

I grew up on these very streets
Riding bikes past old concrete
Grandma's house on the corner there
The barbershop with the spinning chair

This is where my heart was made
This is where I learned to play
Every pothole every crack
Every time I go I come right back
Oh oh oh this city's in my bloodstream
Oh oh oh it's better than a daydream
Put your hands up if you know this place
There's no other town I'd rather embrace
This is where my heart was made

The diner open all night long
The jukebox playing our old songs
The mayor's face on every sign

songs.lyrics:

songs.subject: ballad / quitting your job
songs.lyrics:
Forty years I walked through that same front door
Hung my coat on the same old nail like a thousand times before
Sat down at the same damn desk with the same damn view
Did the same damn things that men like me were supposed to do

Now my hands are shaking writing out this letter
And my eyes ain't quite as sharp as they used to be
But there's a window in the mountains calling out my name
And I'm finally old enough to set myself free

I'm laying down this burden I been carrying so long
All those years of silence when I should have wrote a song
The coffee's getting cold and the clock is ticking slow
But I'm picking up my hat now and I'm letting myself go
I'm letting myself go

My daddy worked until the day they carried him away
Said a man's worth nothing if he ain't got a place to stay
I believed him for so long I forgot to ask myself
If the life I was living was the life I'd choose myself

There's a garden in the back that's been waiting for my hands
And a fishing hole up north I bought but never planned
My grandkids are getting tall and they're growing much too fast
And I want to be the kind of grandfather that lasts

I'm laying down this burden I been carrying so long
All those years of silence when I should have wrote a song
The coffee's getting cold and the clock is ticking slow
But I'm picking up my hat now and I'm letting myself go
I'm letting myself go

I don't need a gold watch and I don't need a speech
I just need the morning light and a sky within my reach
They'll find somebody younger, somebody with more fire
And I hope they find the thing that sets their own soul higher

Because I found it late but lord I found it true
There's a whole wide world out there I barely wandered through
So I sign my name and fold it, slide it under Johnson's door
And I walk out in the sunshine and I breathe in something more

I'm laying down this burden I been carrying so long
All those years of silence when I should have wrote a song
The coffee's getting cold and the clock is ticking slow
But I'm picking up my hat now and I'm letting myself go

I'm laying down this burden, I'm finally moving on
All those years of quiet and I'm done being strong
The door swings shut behind me and the whole world starts to glow
I'm picking up my hat now and I'm letting myself go
I'm letting myself go
I'm letting myself go

songs.subject: americana / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:
The radio's been playing since about half past nine
Old Hank Williams crackling down the telephone line
The linoleum is worn through in the places that I know
Where Eleanor used to stand when she would kneel down low

I put my hand out to the countertop and find my footing there
And I take a step like maybe she's still standing in the air
And lord I feel ridiculous and lord I feel just fine
Dancing with the ghost of her in the fading supper shine

I'm turning slow in the kitchen alone
With the smell of cornbread warming the stone
She taught me every step I know
I'm turning slow, I'm turning slow

My granddaughter came by on Sunday, saw me there
Said grandpa what are you doing just spinning in the air
I told her I was practicing for when I see her again
She smiled and shook her head and said amen

There's a photo on the refrigerator held by a little clasp
Her hair was red in nineteen sixty, I can almost feel the past
So I shuffle to the left now and I shuffle to the right
And the kitchen fills with something close to light

I'm turning slow in the kitchen alone
With the smell of cornbread warming the stone
She taught me every step I know
I'm turning slow, I'm turning slow

Now my knees don't bend the way they did in sixty-three
And the music on the radio is too young for me
But something in the fiddle string, it cuts right through the years
And I'm twenty-seven years old again with nothing left to fear

I'm turning slow in the kitchen alone
With the smell of cornbread warming the stone
She taught me every step I know

I'm turning slow in this old quiet house
With the sound of her still living in the walls
I don't need nobody watching me now
I'm turning slow, I'm turning slow
I'm turning slow

songs.subject: punk / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
You left your records and you left your key
Left a note that didn't say a damn thing to me
And now I'm standing in the wreckage of the thing we made
With my fists clenched tight and my heart on a grenade

I should be screaming, I should be burning down the door
I should be throwing every single thing across the floor
But something keeps me standing still inside this rage
Like I'm trying to rip a different ending to this page

I forgive you, I forgive you
God it tastes like broken glass going down
I forgive you, I forgive you
Not because you deserve it, not because it's sound
I forgive you because I'm not gonna let you
Live inside my chest for another year
I forgive you, I forgive you
And that's the only way I'm getting out of here

You told your friends I was the problem, told them all your side
I heard it back through Sarah on a Thursday night
Yeah you wrapped it up so pretty, made yourself the saint
While I was choking down the fury and restraint

But I've been carrying around the worst of you for months
Like a stone inside my jacket, like a fist inside my lungs
And I'm done with being heavy, I'm done with being slow
There's a world outside this anger and I'm gonna go

I forgive you, I forgive you
God it tastes like broken glass going down
I forgive you, I forgive you
Not because you deserve it, not because it's sound
I forgive you because I'm not gonna let you
Live inside my chest for another year
I forgive you, I forgive you
And that's the only way I'm getting out of here

This isn't mercy, this is survival
This isn't peace, this is my revival
I'm not doing this for you, I'm doing this for me
I'm kicking down the door and I'm finally free

So take your records and take your key
Take the version of yourself you showed to me
I'm dropping everything you left here in the street
And I'm walking till the anger turns to beat

I forgive you, I forgive you
God it tastes like broken glass going down
I forgive you, I forgive you
Not because you deserve it, not because it's sound
I forgive you because I'm not gonna carry
The weight of what you did for another year
I forgive you, I forgive you
I forgive you and I'm finally out of here

I forgive you
Get out of my head
I forgive you
I'm done

songs.subject: americana / summer ending
songs.lyrics:
The screen door's hanging loose again
The way it always does in late July
But something in the morning air's gone different
Like the season's said its last goodbye

The hay is turning gold along the fenceline
The kids are counting days till school begins
The summer dresses folded in the closet
And the river's running shallow in the bends

Hold on, hold on to what we had out here
The fireflies are fading, but the memories stay clear
The sun is setting earlier, the crickets singing low
Hold on, hold on, don't let the summer go

My daddy used to say the land remembers
Every drought and every flood and every yield
And maybe that's why standing on this front porch
I can feel the turning spinning in the field

The tomatoes on the vine are past their ripeness
The carnival has packed up and moved on
The radio is playing something lonesome
And I'm watching all the daylight getting gone

Hold on, hold on to what we had out here
The fireflies are fading, but the memories stay clear
The sun is setting earlier, the crickets singing low
Hold on, hold on, don't let the summer go

We drove the backroads with the windows open wide
You had your bare feet up on the dashboard
And we swore we'd never let this feeling die
But seasons don't ask permission when they're leaving
And neither did the best parts of our lives

Hold on, hold on to what we had out here
The fireflies are fading, but the memories stay clear
The sun is setting earlier, the crickets singing low
Hold on, hold on, don't let the summer go

The screen door's hanging loose again
And I don't have the heart to fix it now

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / chasing a dream
songs.lyrics:
You left a Tuesday with your boxes and your keys
Said I was too distracted, always chasing something
You said I loved my maybe more than I loved you
And maybe you were right, but I'm still running

So I'm packing up the pieces that you didn't take
And buying a one-way ticket somewhere forward
The ache is just the engine, the rejection just the fuel
And I'm burning every bridge back to the ordinary

I'm gonna make it mean something, all this pain
Gonna write it into something that will last beyond your name
You said I'd never get there, well now I gotta try
I'm chasing down this dream with broken wings but I can fly
I can fly, I can fly

The apartment feels like evidence of failure
Your coffee mug still sitting on the shelf
And every song I wrote was half about you watching
But now I'm writing just for me, for no one else

And yeah it hurts to hear our song on someone's playlist
And yeah I still look up when someone laughs like you
But the stage is lit and empty and it's waiting
And I've got nothing left to lose by seeing this thing through

I'm gonna make it mean something, all this pain
Gonna write it into something that will last beyond your name
You said I'd never get there, well now I gotta try
I'm chasing down this dream with broken wings but I can fly
I can fly, I can fly

They say that heartbreak makes the better artists
That you gotta bleed a little for the song
Well I've been bleeding since that Tuesday in the kitchen
And I've been writing ever since, and writing strong

I don't need you to believe in where I'm going
Don't need your voice inside my head at night
I just need this guitar and this hunger and this highway
And the absolute refusal to give up the fight

I'm gonna make it mean something, all this pain
Gonna write it into something that will last beyond your name
You said I'd never get there, well now I gotta try
I'm chasing down this dream with broken wings but I can fly

And someday when they play my song on every radio
I won't be thinking about you, I'll be thinking about the road
But tonight I'll cry a little, yeah tonight I'll let it sting
And tomorrow I'll be one day closer to my everything
I can fly, I can fly, I can fly

I just need this guitar and this hunger and this highway
And the absolute refusal to give up the fight

I'm gonna make it mean something, all this pain
Gonna write it into something that will last beyond your name
You said I'd never get there, well now I gotta try
I'm chasing down this dream with broken wings but I can fly

And someday when they play my song on every radio
I won't be thinking about you, I'll be thinking about the road
But tonight I'll cry a little, yeah tonight I'll let it sting
And tomorrow I'll be one day closer to my everything
I can fly, I can fly, I can fly

songs.lyrics:
The windows are all crying little rivers down the glass
And the whole world's gone to silver, grey and soft
You're still asleep and tangled in the sheets we never make
And the kettle's starting whistling on the stove

I could stay inside forever when it rains like this
When the outside world forgets that we exist
And the only sound is weather and your breathing and the clock
And the whole wide world is just this little room

Oh rainy Sunday, you're the best day of the week
When there's nowhere we need to be
Oh rainy Sunday, with your lazy afternoon
And the one I love right next to me
Right next to me

songs.subject: R&B / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
Last call on a Wednesday and the bar's almost empty
Just me and the bottles and the hum of the lights
This man slides in on a barstool like he's carrying the ocean
And orders a whiskey with these storm-heavy eyes

I've seen that look before, I know that look by heart
It's the one that comes when something's torn a man apart
He says her name like it's a wound that hasn't closed
I pour the drink and lean in cause that's how this story goes

I said everybody's got a somebody they wronged
Everybody's got a chapter they wish they hadn't written in that song
But holding on to all that weight'll take you down
Set it on the bar, let me pour you something, let it drown
Forgiveness ain't for them, baby, it's for you
It's the thing that finally cuts you loose

He talks for maybe an hour about the ways he failed her
The promises he made and let the highway take
I'm wiping down the counter and I'm nodding and I'm listening
Cause some nights tending bar means tending to the ache

I've heard confessions here that priests will never know
This barstool is a witness box, this bar is the front row
Of every human drama, every reckoning and fall
I keep the bottles full and keep my judgment off the wall

Cause everybody's got a somebody they wronged
Everybody's got a chapter they wish they hadn't written in that song
But holding on to all that weight'll take you down
Set it on the bar, let me pour you something, let it drown
Forgiveness ain't for them, baby, it's for you
It's the thing that finally cuts you loose

Now I've been on that barstool, I know how the other side feels
I've said the words I can't take back, I know how that guilt gets real
And I sat across from someone just like me behind this bar
Who said, son, the only prison is the one you make from scars

So I'm passing it along tonight, it's all that I can give
A little hard-earned wisdom and a drink and permission to live
You don't have to be the sum of everything you broke
Sometimes grace is just the next breath after you let go

Cause everybody's got a somebody they wronged
Everybody's got a chapter they wish they hadn't written in that song
But holding on to all that weight'll take you down
Set it on the bar, let me pour you something, let it drown
Forgiveness ain't for them, baby, it's for you
It's the thing that finally cuts you loose

He left a decent tip and something lighter in his step
And I poured myself two fingers, let it burn
Closed up all the chairs and killed the neon one by one
Cause some nights we all need someone in our turn
Some nights we all need someone in our turn

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / an old photograph
songs.lyrics:
Pulled this box down from the shelf again tonight
Dusty cardboard, faded tape, a busted lid
And there you are in nineteen eighty-nine
Looking young as I am now, just like a kid
You're standing in the backyard, squinting at the sun
Your hair is long, your bell-bottoms are wide
I never knew you looked this much like someone
Who hadn't lost the thing they're trying to hide

Was the world still soft when this was taken
Were you dreaming of a life you hadn't planned
Did you know the roads that you'd be making
Did someone hold the camera or your hand

I trace the white border with my finger
Try to read the version of you I never met
There's something in your eyes that likes to linger
Like a word you know but somehow can't forget
My friends think I'm strange for staying inside
Looking through these plastic sleeves on Friday night
But there's a whole life folded up inside
This square of paper barely four inches wide

Was the world still soft when this was taken
Were you dreaming of a life you hadn't planned
Did you know the roads that you'd be making
Did someone hold the camera or your hand
Did you know that I'd be standing here someday
Trying to find the face behind the frame
Trying to understand the price you paid
Trying to figure out if we're the same

You're younger here than you've ever been to me
A stranger and a mirror all at once
The date is written in a hand I recognize
Your whole life still ahead like open months
I wonder if you smiled when they said cheese
Or if you turned away the way I do
I wonder if you ever thought that someone
Would spend a Friday night just studying you

Was the world still soft when this was taken
Were you dreaming of a life you hadn't planned
Did you know the roads that you'd be making
Did someone hold the camera or your hand
Did you know that I'd be standing here someday
Trying to find the face behind the frame
Trying to understand the price you paid
Trying to figure out if we're the same

I'll put you back inside the box now
Fold the cardboard, press the lid back down
But I'll carry you around inside my ribcage
Like a song I heard in some forgotten town
Like a song I heard in some forgotten town

songs.subject: dream pop / insomnia
songs.lyrics:
Three fifteen and the ceiling won't stop breathing
The shadows on the wall are doing things
I've counted every crack above the window
I've mapped the dark geography of this room
Emma's down the hall and she is sleeping
Her small chest rising easy in the blue
I don't know why the night won't let me follow
I don't know what the silence wants from me

Float me out across the water
Let the current pull me under for a while
I have been awake for so many years now
I have been the one who watches through the night
Float me out beyond the harbor
Where the light is thin and no one needs a thing
Just let me be a body in the darkness
Just let me be a body resting still

I make the tea I've made a hundred evenings
The kettle's hiss is company enough
The kitchen feels like somewhere I've been living
In a life that runs adjacent to the world
I think about the things I didn't finish
The forms, the call, the whatever I forgot
My mind is like a browser with too many tabs open
And I can't find the one I'm looking for

Float me out across the water
Let the current pull me under for a while
I have been awake for so many years now
I have been the one who watches through the night
Float me out beyond the harbor
Where the light is thin and no one needs a thing
Just let me be a body in the darkness
Just let me be a body resting still

There is something almost holy in this hour
When the city drops its voice a few degrees
And I can almost hear the shape of quiet
Pressing gentle up against the glass
I love her more than sleep and that's the problem
I love her more than anything I've lost
But love is not a pillow and I'm tired
And the wanting and the giving weigh the same

Float me out across the water
Let the current pull me under for a while
I have been awake for so many years now
I have been the one who watches through the night
Float me out beyond the harbor
Where the light is thin and no one needs a thing
Just let me be a body in the darkness
Just let me be a body resting still

Four a.m. and Emma makes a small sound
I'm already standing in the doorway
She doesn't even know I never left
She doesn't even know how hard I try
I pull her blanket up and watch her breathing
And something in my chest unclenches slow
Maybe this is all the rest I'm getting
Maybe that's enough, I don't know
Maybe that's enough

songs.subject: hip-hop / moving to a new city
songs.lyrics:
Packed up everything I owned in two big garbage bags
Mama hugged me at the Greyhound, tried to keep her cool
Cousin said the city hits you different, hits you fast
Said you either figure out the tempo or you lose
Watching highway signs replace the ones I memorized
Corn and church and water towers fading in the glass
Sixteen hours listening to playlists and my heartbeat
Fell asleep in Ohio, woke up somewhere past

New city, new concrete under my feet
New city, new strangers I'm about to meet
I don't know the exits or the streets or where to eat
But I can feel it in my chest like a brand new beat
New city, new version of me walking through the door
New city, I left my old self back at the Greyhound store
Brought my name and nothing else and room enough for more
New city

First week I got lost three times and played it off like nothing
Asked a girl at the bodega where to catch the train
She talked fast and pointed left and I went right and still got lost
But eventually I found it, that's the whole refrain
Everybody here is running somewhere or from something
Everybody got headphones and a look that says don't try
But on the fourth day somebody nodded at me on the platform
And I nodded back and something clicked behind my eyes

New city, new concrete under my feet
New city, new strangers I'm about to meet
I don't know the exits or the streets or where to eat
But I can feel it in my chest like a brand new beat
New city, new version of me walking through the door
New city, I left my old self back at the Greyhound store
Brought my name and nothing else and room enough for more
New city

Called my mama on a Sunday from the fire escape
She asked if I was eating and I lied and said I was
She said baby just remember who you are out there
I said I'm still figuring out who that is because
Back home everybody knew my name before I earned it
Knew my daddy knew my drama knew my whole biography
Out here I'm just a face inside a city full of faces
And somehow that is terrifying and it's setting me free

Month two I found a court with guys who play on Saturday
My handles got respect before my name did and that's fine
Found a diner that does breakfast all day every day
And a section of the park that I've decided is all mine
I video-called DeShawn and Marcus, showed them my window
They said bro that looks like somewhere people actually live
I laughed but then I sat with it when the call was over
Said yeah, I think it might be, yeah I think it is

New city, new concrete under my feet
New city, new strangers I'm about to meet
I don't know the exits or the streets or where to eat
But I can feel it in my chest like a brand new beat
New city, new version of me walking through the door
New city, I left my old self back at the Greyhound store
Brought my name and nothing else and room enough for more
New city

I still don't know every block and every shortcut
I still call mama when the loneliness gets loud
But something in me opened up like a window I forgot about
Something in me stands a little different in a crowd
New city
Yeah
New city

songs.subject: trap / getting sober
songs.lyrics:
Used to pop them bottles just to feel the lights come on
Used to need the static just to think that I belonged
Bathroom floor at 3 AM, you calling me your own
Wiped my face and made a promise in the porcelain and chrome
Now my hands are clean but they don't know what clean is for
Now my mind is quiet but it's louder than before
Trading in the numbness for the weight of something real
And I'm learning how to love you when I actually feel

This is what sober feels like, terrifying and awake
Every nerve alive and raw and trembling like a flame
Used to drown it all so I could stand to hear your name
Now I hear it like a bell and I don't look for a way out
This is what sober feels like, this is what you feel like
This is how your hand in mine don't need no chaser
This is what I almost threw away
This is what sober feels like

Sixty days and I called you from outside the meeting
Voice was shaking, said I got my chip, you started crying
Said you'd been awake all night just lighting candles for me
I said I don't deserve the way that you keep trying
But you said that's not the math we're doing anymore
Said we're only counting forward, we ain't counting what it cost
I wrote it down inside the back of my notebook
Trying to memorize the things I almost lost

This is what sober feels like, terrifying and awake
Every nerve alive and raw and trembling like a flame
Used to drown it all so I could stand to hear your name
Now I hear it like a bell and I don't look for a way out
This is what sober feels like, this is what you feel like
This is how your hand in mine don't need no chaser
This is what I almost threw away
This is what sober feels like

Some nights I still drive past the old block slow
Some nights the wanting gets so loud it's like a frequency
But I think about your face and how you looked that morning
When you said I see you trying, that's enough for me
That's enough for me

songs.subject: alt rock / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Saw your name light up the screen at half past two
Didn't know three sentences could cut right through
You said it's not you, it's the space you need to find
But the space you carved was straight through my mind

I read it seven times beneath the bathroom light
Trying to find a reason buried in the white
Between the periods and the carefully placed words
The kind of ending that nobody ever heard

And I'm writing this down so I don't forget
How it feels to lose something through a cold blue screen
Every word you typed a careful silhouette
Of a person saying nothing that they mean
I keep the phone face down now on the kitchen tile
I've been staring at the ceiling for a while
Didn't think that this was how we'd say goodbye
Didn't think you'd let a signal carry why

Tuesday morning and the coffee tastes like rust
Read our old texts like archaeology of us
You were warmer back in October, back in fall
Now you're lowercase letters on a bathroom wall

The dots appeared and disappeared before you sent
I wonder how many drafts you wrote and meant
Did you practice how to leave me on a screen
Did you save a copy somewhere in between

songs.subject: blues / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning, drove you to the gate
Hands on the steering wheel, running ten minutes late
Radio was playing something neither of us heard
We sat in that departure lane and struggled with the words

Baby when the engine idles and the bags are on the curb
When the automatic doors keep opening to the world
And everybody's rushing past with somewhere they belong
How do you say goodbye when goodbye's been too long

Honey I've been loving you through every kind of weather
But loving you and holding you ain't always been together
The terminal is crowded and your flight won't understand
So I'm standing here in January holding your left hand

Baby when the engine idles and the bags are on the curb
When the automatic doors keep opening to the world
And everybody's rushing past with somewhere they belong
How do you say goodbye when goodbye's been too long

You said call me when you land now
I said I always do
But we both know that phone call
Ain't the same as having you

Watched you walk through security and disappear from view
Shoes back on and boarding pass, the crowd just swallowed you
I stood there by the window where the planes were lined up grey
And I tried to find your window as you slowly taxied away

Baby when the engine idles and the bags are on the curb
When the automatic doors keep opening to the world
And everybody's rushing past with somewhere they belong
How do you say goodbye when goodbye's been too long
Lord how do you say goodbye
When goodbye's been too long

Drove home on the highway with the passenger seat bare
Your coffee still half empty and your perfume in the air
The blues don't need a reason when the reason's thirty thousand feet
Just a man and a highway and a whole lot of empty seat

songs.subject: R&B / insomnia
songs.lyrics:
Clock says 3 a.m. and I'm wide awake again
Ceiling got familiar with my stare somewhere around ten
Turned the pillow to the cool side twenty times or more
Mind running laps on a hardwood floor

Tried the lavender, tried the counting sheep
Tried the ocean sounds and the medication sleep
But here I am inside the blue glow of the night
Every thought I own feeling way too bright

Come lay down with me, baby, quiet don't come easy
I been chasing rest and it's running like a river
Come lay down with me, the dark ain't dark enough to free me
From the thinking and the feeling and the shiver
3 a.m. knows all my secrets now
She keeps me company, I don't know how
I just want to close my eyes and finally let go
Come lay down with me and let the nighttime be slow

Wednesday blurring into Thursday blurring into grey
I've been living in the margins of the sleeping and the day
Coffee isn't helping but I'm drinking it like faith
Running on the fumes of a half-remembered place

Eyes heavy as a history I can't put down
Heart beating like it lost something it never found
And the quieter the apartment gets the louder gets my head
I'm the most awake I've ever been inside this bed

I been chasing rest and it's running like a river
Come lay down with me, the dark ain't dark enough to free me

Come lay down with me and let the nighttime be slow

Every word I swallowed and every word I spread
The night expands to hold it all so willingly
Like it was made for every restless part of me

Somewhere out there people sleep like breathing
Somewhere out there rest comes without grieving
I remember sleep the way you miss a place you loved
Somewhere warm and soft and somewhere far enough

Come lay down with me, baby, quiet don't come easy
I been chasing rest and it's running like a river
Come lay down with me, the dark ain't dark enough to free me

songs.subject: bluegrass / the ocean
songs.lyrics:
Never seen the ocean but I heard it calls your name
I traded in my mountains for a chance to feel the rain
You left me with a suitcase and a one-way ticket south
So I drove until the salt air found its way into my mouth

The waves don't care that you are gone, they roll and roll and break
They crash against the shoreline like the promises you make
I'm standing at the water's edge with sand between my toes
Wondering how the tide comes back when everything else goes

Roll on, roll on, you endless gray and blue
I came here with a broken heart, I don't know what to do
The banjo in my chest keeps picking at this pain
Roll on, roll on, wash me clean again
Roll on ocean, roll on

I watched the pelicans sail low across the afternoon
And thought about how you and I once danced beneath the moon
Back home the hollers echo out with what we used to be
But the seagulls here don't know my name and that's some kind of free

The fishermen are pulling in their nets along the dock
They don't ask about a girl with eyes the color of the rocks
They just haul their silver catch and nod and go about their days
I wish that I could trade my grief for their uncomplicated ways

Roll on, roll on, you endless gray and blue
I came here with a broken heart, I don't know what to do
The banjo in my chest keeps picking at this pain
Roll on, roll on, wash me clean again
Roll on ocean, roll on

Maybe I'll stay down here a while and learn to read the tides
Learn to let the current take whatever still resides
Back home my mama's porch light burns through every kind of night
But the ocean's teaching me that sometimes wrong can feel like right

I pressed my palm against a wave and let it pull away
Just like you did, darling, on that cold November day
But the ocean always fills back in and smooths the broken shore
And maybe that means I can fill the place you filled before

Roll on, roll on, you endless gray and blue
I came here with a broken heart, I don't know what to do
The banjo in my chest keeps picking at this pain
Roll on, roll on, wash me clean again
Roll on ocean, roll on

Roll on ocean, roll on
Just roll on

songs.subject: R&B / faith
songs.lyrics:
Nobody told me faith could feel this heavy on your chest
Like carrying a backpack full of questions to a test
Sixteen years of wondering if anybody hears
When I'm crying in my bedroom and I'm drowning in my fears

But something in the morning when the light comes through my blinds
Feels like somebody's answering the questions in my mind
I can't explain the feeling and I know that sounds so wild
But I swear I feel it holding me like I'm somebody's child

I believe, even when the night is long
I believe, even when I don't feel strong
When the world is loud and nothing's going right
Something keeps me breathing through the night
I believe, yeah I believe
Even when I can barely see
I believe

My pastor speaks on Sundays but sometimes the words feel old
And my friends all laugh at church like it's a story that's been told
But then I'm by myself at three a.m. and wide awake
And I feel something steady in the middle of my ache

I'm not the kind of perfect that they talk about in rows
Of people in their Sunday best with everybody's nose
Turned up at all my questions and my secondhand Jordans too
But faith ain't about looking right, it's about pushing through

I believe, even when the night is long
I believe, even when I don't feel strong
When the world is loud and nothing's going right
Something keeps me breathing through the night
I believe, yeah I believe
Even when I can barely see
I believe

Mama lost her job last spring and daddy never stayed
And the lights been cut off twice since then and I was so afraid
But we made it to the other side I don't know exactly how
There's something I can't name but I feel it here right now

All my friends are posting lives that look like magazine
And I'm just trying to figure out the space between
What I feel inside my chest and what the world can see
All I know is something greater's always reaching back for me

I believe, even when the night is long
I believe, even when I don't feel strong
When the world is loud and nothing's going right
Something keeps me breathing through the night
I believe, yeah I believe
Even when I can barely see
I believe

Yeah I believe it
When the darkness wraps around me like a second skin
I feel something light a candle deep within
I can't explain it, can't put it in a line
But something's holding on to me and I'm holding on to mine
I believe
I believe
I believe

songs.subject: trap / working a dead-end job
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at four the manager don't even know my name
Been here since September and it's all the goddamn same
Mop the floor and restock and they cut my hours again
I'm counting every dollar and I'm counting down to ten

Red vest on my back and I'm dying on my feet
Register lane seven and the light above won't beep
Customers be looking through me like I'm made of air
I got a physics test tomorrow and I cannot care

I'm here again, I'm here again
Punching in my code and punching out my pain
I'm here again, I'm here again
Minimum wage minimum and I'm going insane
I'm here again

Manager named Kevin got a clipboard and a stare
Walks around like he invented how to breathe the air
Told me smile more, dress code, and I bit my tongue
I'm seventeen years old and already so wrung

My feet hurt and my back hurt and my eyes hurt too
I spent my whole damn check on groceries for the crew
Mama working doubles and my little brother needs
New shoes for the winter and I'm filling all the needs

I'm here again, I'm here again
Punching in my code and punching out my pain
I'm here again, I'm here again
Minimum wage minimum and I'm going insane
I'm here again

Breakroom got a vending machine that takes your dollar twice
Jayla fell asleep in back and nobody was nice
About it when they wrote her up and sent her home to cry
I been watching all this happen through my seventeen-year eyes

Got a notebook in my locker full of everything I plan
Got a mind that's moving faster than this job thinks that I can
But the rent is due regardless and the lights don't stay on free
So I tie my apron tighter and I'm back on register three

I'm here again, I'm here again
Punching in my code and punching out my pain
I'm here again, I'm here again
Minimum wage minimum and I'm going insane
I'm here again

They don't see us, nah they don't see us

They don't see us, nah they don't see us
But I'm writing down every single thing I'm going to miss
About this place
Nothing
I'm out

Clock out at midnight walking to the bus in the cold
Seventeen years old feeling sixty years old
This ain't forever, this ain't forever, I keep saying it
I'm here again but I won't always be, I won't quit

I'm here again, I'm here again
Punching in my code and punching out my pain
I'm here again, I'm here again
Minimum wage minimum and I'm going insane
I'm here again
But not for long
Not for long

songs.subject: americana / a new baby
songs.lyrics:
Tiny hands reach up through the morning light
Your mama's counting all your fingers, getting everything right
Ten little miracles curled up soft and small
We built this house with crooked nails but darling it won't fall

Your grandfather's eyes are looking back at me
From somewhere down a river none of us can see
The fields are turning green outside the bedroom window pane
And everything I ever lost just wandered home again

Oh little one, oh little one
You came like April after all that rain
Oh little one, oh little one
Nothing in this world will ever look the same
The dusty roads that wore me down
Led me straight to you somehow
Oh little one, you're here right now

Your daddy drove through two states when the call finally came
His hands were shaking at the wheel, he couldn't speak your name
He stood there in the doorway just a trembling kind of man
Then something old inside him broke and something new began

The dogs are sleeping on the porch beneath the summer moon
Your grandma's in the kitchen singing some forgotten tune
The whole world feels like Sunday and like grace and like a hymn
Like a door I'd given up on swinging open from within

Oh little one, oh little one
You came like April after all that rain
Oh little one, oh little one
Nothing in this world will ever look the same
The dusty roads that wore me down
Led me straight to you somehow
Oh little one, you're here right now

We'll teach you how the garden grows and how the seasons turn
We'll show you all the constellations, let you watch them burn
We'll tell you that the broken things are often still worth keeping
And that the bravest thing we do is wake up and keep breathing

Oh little one, oh little one
You came like April after all that rain
Oh little one, oh little one
Nothing in this world will ever look the same
The dusty roads that wore me down
Led me straight to you somehow
Oh little one, you're here right now

Sleep now, sleep now, close your eyes
We'll still be here when morning rises
Sleep now, sleep now, you're our sky
Everything we ever prayed for, you're the answer and the why

songs.subject: hip-hop / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
City don't sleep, she just changes her clothes
Three in the morning and everybody knows
The neon bleeds red on the wet asphalt street
And I'm out here moving to a different heartbeat

Seventeen years and I know every block
The corner store closed but I still hear the lock
My hoodie pulled up against the October cold
This city keeps secrets that'll never get old

Bus shelter empty, just me and the glass
Watching the headlights and letting them pass
My phone at eleven percent and my thoughts running loud
Too young for the bars but too old for the crowd

This is my city at three in the morning
No parents awake, no teachers to warn me
The skyline is honest when nobody's watching
Everything glitters and nothing is stopping
The city and me, we got our own language
Speaking in sirens and steam and in anguish
Three in the morning, I finally feel free
This city don't sleep and she looks just like me

Called up Marcus but Marcus don't answer
The block's got its own kind of rhythm and cadence
There's a woman in heels on the corner of Fifth
And an old man asleep, and I walk past him swift

I think about school and I think about home
I think about how I feel less alone
Out here with the city than inside those walls
Where silence gets heavy and loneliness calls

The bridges are golden above the black water
I'm somebody's son but I'm nobody's order
I'm making my choices before the sun rises
Collecting the city's most beautiful crises

This is my city at three in the morning
No parents awake, no teachers to warn me
The skyline is honest when nobody's watching
Everything glitters and nothing is stopping
The city and me, we got our own language
Speaking in sirens and steam and in anguish
Three in the morning, I finally feel free
This city don't sleep and she looks just like me

Checkout line receipts blowing down Eighth Avenue
Graffiti that's saying the things that are true
A cat on a stoop with the whole world figured out
And me in the middle of all of this doubt

My sneakers are wet from a puddle I caught
And I'm counting the stars between buildings I'm not
Supposed to be under but here I am anyway
The city my classroom before the break of day

Seventeen feeling like forty sometimes
Reading the city between all the lines
One day I'll leave and I'll miss every crack
In the sidewalk that raised me and never looked back

No parents awake, no teachers to warn me
The skyline is honest when nobody's watching
Everything glitters and nothing is stopping

songs.subject: trap / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Phone buzz on the nightstand, middle of the night
Thought it was nothing, thought it might be alright
Picked it up blinking, one eye still asleep
Read through the words and felt the floor underneath

Just disappear, two sentences long
After eight months you can't even call me on the phone
Lowercase letters like I ain't worth the shift key
You spent more time on your Instagram caption than leaving me

Two sentences, period, sent
Everything we built and this is how it went
A breakup text, a breakup text
You couldn't even say it, couldn't face what comes next
Two sentences, period, sent
Screenshot it laughing but I'm crying in the Benz
A breakup text, a breakup text
Deleted my number but you still in my head

Okay, yeah, uh
I read it three times thinking autocorrect lied
Fourth time I read it I was dead inside
Threw my phone across the room but it didn't break
Picked it back up, checked to see if it was fake

Called my boy Dre at like two forty-five
He said pull up, said come on, said you'll survive
Sat in the parking lot, didn't say much
Just stared at the sky where it cuts

You was all in my stories, all in my posts
Now you the person who text-kills what you loved the most
No call, no voice note, not even a DM slide
Just a notification like my feelings stepped aside

Two sentences, period, sent
Everything we built and this is how it went

You the coldest thing my phone has ever seen
But I'm good though, yeah I'm moving on
Put your contact name to Do Not Respond
Block, delete, unfollow, next
But nothing hits different than a breakup text

Hundred missed calls I never sent
Wrote you a paragraph, deleted, never went
Up at four AM rereading everything
Checking if your status changed or anything

This ain't closure, this ain't peace
This is two lines on a screen and a slow release
You said it's not you, it's just timing and stuff
Two sentences to say that you ain't built for this love

songs.subject: punk / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
I got a call on a Wednesday morning
Coffee still hot and the kids were snoring
Voice on the phone said I'm sorry to say
And the whole damn world just fell away

She raised me up on secondhand hope
Taught me how to fight and how to cope
Now I'm standing at this kitchen sink
Trying to find the words I think

You were the safety net I never saw
You were the reason I don't quit at all
Fifty-three years old and gone too fast
How was I supposed to know that Sunday was the last
I got three kids and a broken chest
And I'm doing it alone like I always did best
But nobody told me it would feel like this
Nobody told me how much I'd miss

Showing up, just showing up
Every school play and every rough cut
Every bad date and every bad break
You were the one thing that I didn't fake

The landlord's calling, the youngest is sick
The car needs something and I'm running on fumes and grit
I keep reaching for the phone to tell you how the day went
And then I remember and I don't know where the time went

You were the safety net I never saw
You were the reason I don't quit at all
Fifty-three years old and gone too fast
How was I supposed to know that Sunday was the last
I got three kids and a broken chest
And I'm doing it alone like I always did best
But nobody told me it would feel like this
Nobody told me how much I'd miss

Your old coat is still hanging by the door
I can't move it, can't look at it, can't ignore
The way it smells like menthol and like years
Like every time you showed up drying all my tears

I am so angry and I am so wrecked
I am so tired and I'm not done yet
I got bills to pay and lunches to make
And a grief so loud that it keeps me awake

They say be strong like I got another choice
They say she'd want you to, I can still hear her voice
Saying baby you were born to outlast every hard thing
But she forgot to tell me how to do it without her in my corner

You were the safety net I never saw
You were the reason I don't quit at all
Fifty-three years old and gone too fast
How was I supposed to know that Sunday was the last
I got three kids and a broken chest
And I'm doing it alone like I always did best
But nobody told me it would feel like this
Nobody told me how much I'd miss

I'm gonna hold it together and I'm gonna fall apart
I'm gonna do both things simultaneously from the start
I'm gonna show my kids what it looks like to survive
I'm gonna honor you by staying fiercely alive

But tonight I'm just a daughter
Tonight I'm just undone
Tonight I'm sitting in the dark before the morning comes
Tonight there's no one older
No one left above
Just me and all this grief and all this stubborn love

You were the safety net I never saw
You were the reason I don't quit at all
I'll be the safety net now, that's what's left
Carrying all of us on this broken chest
I got three kids who need to see me stand
So I'll stand up for them with your invisible hand
Nobody told me how much it'd hurt
Nobody told me

But I think maybe you knew

I'm gonna hold it together and I'm gonna fall apart
I'm gonna do both things simultaneously from the start
I'm gonna show my kids what it looks like to survive
I'm gonna honor you by staying fiercely alive

But tonight I'm just a daughter
Tonight I'm just undone
Tonight I'm sitting in the dark before the morning comes
Tonight there's no one older
No one left above
Just me and all this grief and all this stubborn love

You were the safety net I never saw
You were the reason I don't quit at all
I'll be the safety net now, that's what's left
Carrying all of us on this broken chest
I got three kids who need to see me stand
So I'll stand up for them with your invisible hand
Nobody told me how much it'd hurt
Nobody told me

But I think maybe you knew

songs.subject: ballad / a first apartment
songs.lyrics:
Cardboard boxes stacked against the wall
A secondhand couch that barely fits the hall
The faucet drips a rhythm through the night
But baby this is mine and it feels right

I painted over someone else's life
Covered up the marks and all the strife
The windows let the morning pour on through
I never knew that starting over felt brand new

This cracked and crooked place is my cathedral
Every creaking floorboard tells a story
I pay my own electric, buy my own groceries
And fall asleep beneath my own glory
Beneath my own glory

The neighbors play their music way too loud
And sometimes I just sit here in the crowd
Of silence that belongs to only me
The first time in my life I'm finally free

I learned to cook by burning everything
And dancing in the kitchen suffering
Through smoke alarms and laughing all alone
The echo of my voice inside my own home

This cracked and crooked place is my cathedral
Every creaking floorboard tells a story
I pay my own electric, buy my own groceries
And fall asleep beneath my own glory
Beneath my own glory

Nobody told me lonely could feel proud
Nobody said that quiet gets this loud
When you are the one who holds the key
And every corner here belongs to me

The landlord never fixes what he should
The radiator knocks inside the wood
The shower takes forever to run warm
But I have learned to shelter my own storm

This cracked and crooked place is my cathedral
Every creaking floorboard tells a story
I pay my own electric, buy my own groceries
And fall asleep beneath my own glory

I will hang my pictures where I choose
I will leave the dishes when I lose
The will to be what everybody needs
And grow into myself like patient seeds

This cracked and crooked place is my cathedral
Every creaking floorboard tells a story
I pay my own electric, buy my own groceries
And fall asleep beneath my own glory
Beneath my own glory
Beneath my own glory

songs.subject: R&B / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
I'm counting out my tips at the end of the night
Twenties and fives stacked up in the low light
Rent is due Thursday and my shoes got holes
But I got something money can't control

I slide these drinks across the bar with love
I know every story every heartache every shrug
The lonely ones the loud ones the ones in between
I am the keeper of everything unseen

Pockets ain't heavy but my spirit overflow
Pockets ain't heavy but my spirit overflow
I got enough to smile enough to give enough to know
That rich ain't always something that your bank account can show
Pockets ain't heavy but my spirit overflow

Tuesday night is slow and the bar smells like rain
Old man Chester nursing whiskey for his pain
I pour him one more and I sit and I listen
There are things in this life that no paycheck could glisten

My mama said baby you were made for more
But more don't always mean a bigger score
I am more right here between this wood and brass
Watching people's whole lives fill up a glass

Pockets ain't heavy but my spirit overflow
Pockets ain't heavy but my spirit overflow
I got enough to smile enough to give enough to know
That rich ain't always something that your bank account can show
Pockets ain't heavy but my spirit overflow

Friday night is wild and the music is loud
And I am the still water in the middle of the crowd
The college kids are spending what their parents earned
And I'm serving every lesson that I ever learned

I walk home at two in the morning dark
And the city is breathing and I feel the spark
Of being alive and exactly where I am
Broke and beautiful and not giving a damn

Pockets ain't heavy but my spirit overflow
Pockets ain't heavy but my spirit overflow
I got enough to smile enough to give enough to know
That rich ain't always something that your bank account can show
Pockets ain't heavy but my spirit overflow

Some nights I count the quarters in the tip jar slow

Beyond this bar beyond these calloused hands
But then the music starts and I understand

This floor this rail this brass this light
Has given me a thousand lives on every night
I am wealthy in the ways they don't teach school
The bartender is always the one who's never the fool

Pockets ain't heavy but my spirit overflow

I got enough to smile enough to give enough to know
That rich ain't always something that your bank account can show
Pockets ain't heavy but my spirit overflow

songs.subject: trap / an old photograph
songs.lyrics:
Found it in a shoebox underneath my bed
You were just a baby I was barely out my head
Twenty-two and terrified and trying to stand tall
Before I knew that I would be the one to give you all

Your daddy in the background barely half a smile
I knew right then I'd be walking every single mile
Just me and you against the world before you even spoke
We came up from nothing baby we don't even joke

This photograph this photograph
Tell me where the time go tell me where the time go
This photograph this photograph
I carried us through the storm and I want you to know
Look at us look at us
We were just beginning we were just beginning
Look at us look at us
Against all odds baby we been winning

Nails done never mind the credit card decline
I made sure your lunchbox never showed the bottom line
Worked the double shift and still made it to your play
You thought I was just mama you didn't know the price I pay

Kept that photograph inside my wallet worn
A reminder of the morning my whole life was reborn
Skinny young and scared but holding you so tight
I would fight the world again and never lose the fight

This photograph this photograph
Tell me where the time go tell me where the time go
This photograph this photograph
I carried us through the storm and I want you to know
Look at us look at us
We were just beginning we were just beginning
Look at us look at us
Against all odds baby we been winning

I never let you see me on my knees on the floor
I smiled through the eviction I smiled through every door
That closed up in my face when they saw it was just me
A single mama building what a family could be

Some nights I would hold you and I'd cry real quiet
You sleeping on my chest and I would try to fight it
The feeling that I wasn't gonna be enough
But every morning came and I was still that tough

This photograph this photograph
Tell me where the time go tell me where the time go
This photograph this photograph
I carried us through the storm and I want you to know

Now you tall and laughing with your whole life spread out wide
And I look at that old picture with a fullness deep inside
Couldn't see it then but I can see it now so clear
Everything I sacrificed to get us right here

Look at that young woman barely more than a girl
About to hold her baby and take on the world
Didn't know her strength yet didn't know her power
But she figured it out in every single hour

This photograph this photograph
Tell me where the time go tell me where the time go
This photograph this photograph
I carried us through the storm and I want you to know
Look at us look at us
We were just beginning we were just beginning
Look at us look at us
Against all odds baby we been winning

Look at that photograph

songs.subject: emo / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
Crowded room and the lights are low
I don't know anybody here but you
You're standing by the window and the stereo
Is playing something I never knew
My palms are sweating through my hoodie sleeves
I'm seventeen and terrified of this
You looked at me and I forgot to breathe
I don't know how I'm supposed to exist

Before tonight I had it figured out
Keep my walls up, keep my head down, disappear
But you laughed at something and I felt it shout
Through every single nerve that you were here
And I've been running from this kind of thing
Scared of what it means to let someone in
But you said hey and now I'm following
Every word you say like oxygen

This is not what I planned for
Standing here with my heart on the floor
I don't know what I came here for
But now I only know your name
This is not what I dressed up for
Trying hard not to want you more
I've been keeping my own score
But you went and changed the game

So we slipped out to the backyard past the noise
And talked until the neighbors killed the lights
I told you things I've never told the boys
At school who wouldn't get it right
You said you felt invisible sometimes
Like screaming underwater, no one hears
I said I write it all down in bad rhymes
You laughed and said that's how you face your fears

This is not what I planned for
Standing here with my heart on the floor
I don't know what I came here for
But now I only know your name
This is not what I dressed up for
Trying hard not to want you more
I've been keeping my own score
But you went and changed the game

And maybe I'm a fool for feeling this fast
Maybe it burns out by Monday afternoon
But I swear right now it feels like it could last
Like something that was written on the moon
My phone is dying and the party's gone
And everyone has scattered to their beds
But I'm still here and somehow holding on
To everything you said inside my head

This is not what I planned for
Standing here with my heart on the floor
I don't know what I came here for
But now I only know your name
This is not what I dressed up for
Trying hard not to want you more
I've been keeping my own score
But you went and changed the game

You went and changed the game
And I don't mind
You went and changed the game
And I think I'm fine with that

songs.subject: emo / an old photograph
songs.lyrics:
Cardboard box in the corner of the hall
I found it underneath the winter coats
A square of light inside a world so small
A ghost of someone I can barely quote
Your smile is frozen at a different time
When everything was simpler, softer, true
And looking at you now feels like a crime
Because I don't know how to grieve for you

The colors faded to a yellow-gray
The edges curling soft like they're ashamed
Of all the years that slowly ate away
The life we had before we both got changed
I trace the outline of your jawbone there
The way you tilted toward the afternoon
You didn't know that I was standing near
You didn't know that we'd be over soon

So I'll hold this photograph and fall apart
Let the silence say the things I can't begin
You're a paper wound pressed against my heart
A reminder of the state I'm always in
And the ink has bled but I still see your face
In the background of a summer I can't keep
Every happy thing decays and leaves a trace
And I find them when I cannot fall asleep

I don't know where you are now, if you're well
If you ever think about the years we burned
If you ever have a story left to tell
About the girl who never got what she deserved
I was sixteen in this photo, you were too
We were standing at the edge of something bright
I thought the whole world smelled and looked like you
I thought we'd never have to say goodnight

So I'll hold this photograph and fall apart
Let the silence say the things I can't begin
You're a paper wound pressed against my heart
A reminder of the state I'm always in
And the ink has bled but I still see your face
In the background of a summer I can't keep
Every happy thing decays and leaves a trace
And I find them when I cannot fall asleep

I should put this back inside the box
Seal it shut and shove it out of sight
But instead I sit here watching clocks
And holding on until the morning light
Because letting go means that you really left
And the photograph means something still remains
And maybe that's just grief dressed up as theft
But I'll steal you back in black and white and stains

So I'll hold this photograph and fall apart
Let the silence say the things I can't begin
You're a paper wound pressed against my heart
A reminder of the state I'm always in
And the ink has bled but I still see your face
In the background of a summer I can't keep
Every happy thing decays and leaves a trace
And I find them when I cannot fall asleep

I find them when I cannot fall asleep
And I keep them close and let myself believe

Cardboard box in the corner of the hall
I found it underneath the winter coats
A square of light inside a world so small
A ghost of someone I can barely quote
Your smile is frozen at a different time
When everything was simpler, softer, true
And looking at you now feels like a crime
Because I don't know how to grieve for you

The colors faded to a yellow-gray
The edges curling soft like they're ashamed
Of all the years that slowly ate away
The life we had before we both got changed
I trace the outline of your jawbone there
The way you tilted toward the afternoon
You didn't know that I was standing near
You didn't know that we'd be over soon

So I'll hold this photograph and fall apart
Let the silence say the things I can't begin
You're a paper wound pressed against my heart
A reminder of the state I'm always in
And the ink has bled but I still see your face
In the background of a summer I can't keep
Every happy thing decays and leaves a trace
And I find them when I cannot fall asleep

I don't know where you are now, if you're well
If you ever think about the years we burned
If you ever have a story left to tell
About the girl who never got what she deserved
I was sixteen in this photo, you were too
We were standing at the edge of something bright
I thought the whole world smelled and looked like you
I thought we'd never have to say goodnight

Let the silence say the things I can't begin
You're a paper wound pressed against my heart
A reminder of the state I'm always in
And the ink has bled but I still see your face
In the background of a summer I can't keep
Every happy thing decays and leaves a trace
And I find them when I cannot fall asleep

I should put this back inside the box
Seal it shut and shove it out of sight
But instead I sit here watching clocks
And holding on until the morning light
Because letting go means that you really left
And the photograph means something still remains
And maybe that's just grief dressed up as theft
But I'll steal you back in black and white and stains

So I'll hold this photograph and fall apart
Let the silence say the things I can't begin

songs.subject: alt rock / an old photograph
songs.lyrics:
Picked it up off the dresser this morning
Wasn't looking, wasn't planning to stop
But there she was like some kind of warning
Printed thin on the paper you drop
Nineteen-sixty-something, hard to tell now
The corner's soft, the whole thing's turned tan
I was standing by a rusted old plow
Thinking I was some kind of a man

Forty years or fifty, hard to figure
Time stops meaning what it meant before
My hands back then were thinner and quicker
Now they shake when I open the door
But the kid in that photo don't know it
Doesn't know what the years have in store
Doesn't know how it feels when you blow it
Doesn't know what a life's really for

God I wish I could tell him something
Shout across the decades in between
All that noise and all that running
Toward a future that he's never seen
But the photograph just stares back and holds it
All that light in his eyes like a dare
Every answer this old world withholds
Is already right there in his stare

I remember the field behind the county road
Where we'd ride until the engine quit
We were stupid with the heavy load
Of thinking none of it would ever hit
My father's voice is gone now, twenty years
I'd give this old body just to hear it once
Pour myself a drink to chase the gears
Of a memory that time has made a runt

God I wish I could tell him something
Shout across the decades in between
All that noise and all that running
Toward a future that he's never seen
But the photograph just stares back and holds it
All that light in his eyes like a dare
Every answer this old world withholds
Is already right there in his stare

My wife walked by and saw me standing there
She said what you got, I said just a ghost
She laughed the way she does and touched my hair
And I thought maybe that's what I love most
That she knows me past the photograph
Knows the middle and the messy end
Not the kid but all the aftermath
And she stayed through every single bend

So maybe the old photo's not a wound
Maybe it's just evidence I lived
That the summer comes and summer's ruined
And you spend the rest on what you give
I'll put it back beside the lamp tonight
Where I can see it if I wake at three
Let the young man keep his borrowed light
And the old one drink his morning tea

God I wish I could tell him something
Shout across the decades in between
All that noise and all that running
Toward a future that he's never seen
But the photograph just stares back and holds it
All that light in his eyes like a dare
Every answer this old world withholds
Is already right there in his stare

Is already right there in his stare
Right there
Right there

songs.subject: rock / a childhood home
songs.lyrics:
Drove three hours back to nothing new
Same cracked driveway, same old peeling blue
The shutters hanging just the way they did
When I was someone's son and just a kid
But she's not in my phone now, she's not there
I'm standing in the yard with nowhere where
To put this thing that's heavy in my chest
So I drove here because I knew the rest

The oak tree in the back still leans the same
My father carved a heart with our last name
The wood grew over it but you can feel
The letters underneath like something real
I pressed my hand against the bark and thought
About how certain things can't be untaught
Like how your home remembers every year
And people just evaporate from here

These walls don't care about the words she said
They held me when I couldn't leave my bed
At fifteen with a fever, at eleven scared
These walls were always something solid there
And she could take the good years and the nights
And she could take the arguments and fights
But she can't take the smell of this back room
The wood and dust and history in the gloom

I walked around the side and found the mark
Where I had measured inches in the dark
Each birthday on the doorframe, pencil lines
A tiny kind of history in signs
And somewhere in the measuring I grew
Into the kind of man who trusted you
Who handed you the whole thing, every part
You didn't want it, handed back my heart

These walls don't care about the words she said
They held me when I couldn't leave my bed
At fifteen with a fever, at eleven scared
These walls were always something solid there
And she could take the good years and the nights
And she could take the arguments and fights
But she can't take the smell of this back room
The wood and dust and history in the gloom

The porch light still comes on at half past five
My mother must have set it, still alive
In circuit memory or muscle motion
Some small mechanical devotion
I sat down on the steps and let it wash
That amber light without a single thought of loss
For maybe forty seconds, maybe three
Before the whole thing came back down on me

But even then the porch held what it knew
The way it always held the evening through
When I was young and didn't know that people leave
When I was young and didn't know how to grieve
And I'm not young now but I'm back here on the wood
And the house holds me the way I knew it would
Not fixing anything, not making it alright
Just holding me inside the amber light

These walls don't care about the words she said
They held me when I couldn't leave my bed
At fifteen with a fever, at eleven scared
These walls were always something solid there
And she could take the good years and the nights
And she could take the arguments and fights
But she can't take the smell of this back room
The wood and dust and history in the gloom

She can't take what was mine before she came
She can't take what was mine before she came
The house still knows my name
The house still knows my name

songs.subject: punk / insomnia
songs.lyrics:
Can't close my eyes, the ceiling stares me down
Another night I'm drowning in this town
The clock keeps ticking like a war machine
My brain won't shut up, you know what I mean

I've tried the pills, I've tried the counting sheep
I've tried the tea, I've tried the boring reads
But every time I lay my body flat
My skull explodes and there is no cure for that

I'm wired up, I'm burning out
I'm screaming at the dark without a sound
My mattress is a battlefield tonight
I'm losing every round of this fight

Three in the morning and I'm still awake
My body's tired but my mind won't brake
I'm running on the fumes of yesterday
And everything I push just won't stay

The neighbors sleep like nothing's wrong outside
While I am here just barely staying alive
My eyelids droop but snap right back in place
Like springs that won't give in to any grace

I'm wired up, I'm burning out
I'm screaming at the dark without a sound
My mattress is a battlefield tonight
I'm losing every round of this fight

I don't need your advice about my sleep
I don't need your lavender and your deep
Breathing exercises, counting to ten
I've done all that and I'm back here again

I haven't slept since sometime Tuesday week
My bones are chalk, my future's looking bleak
I'm funny now, I laugh at everything
Delirium is my favorite offering

I'm wired up, I'm burning out
I'm screaming at the dark without a sound
My mattress is a battlefield tonight
I'm losing every round of this fight

I'm wired up, I'm burning out
Too tired to sleep and too awake to drown
My mattress is a battlefield tonight
And I'll be losing every single night
