songs.subject: soul / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The streetlights bleed their orange on the rain
I'm walking slow because I got no place to be again
Your number's in my phone but I won't call
I'm just another shadow on this city's wall

The diners glow like lanterns in the deep
The waitress pours my coffee, doesn't ask me why I weep
She's seen a thousand versions of this face
A person with a hollow where a heart used to be placed

Oh city, hold me gently in your arms tonight
The buses still are running though they're mostly out of sight
The buildings never sleep and neither will I
Just me and all these quiet streets beneath this indigo sky

Somebody left their window cracked and music pours on down
A saxophone is crying and it fills the empty sound
Of everything I thought we were and aren't anymore
I sit down on these courthouse steps and stare out at the store

Where we bought those stupid candles every Sunday afternoon
Where you laughed so hard at nothing underneath the harvest moon
I didn't know that laughing was a thing that we would lose
I didn't know that loving you would leave me with these blues

Oh city, hold me gently in your arms tonight
The buses still are running though they're mostly out of sight
The buildings never sleep and neither will I
Just me and all these quiet streets beneath this indigo sky

There's a man asleep inside a doorway down on Vine
There's a couple tangled up together, doing fine
There's a taxi with no passengers still circling the block
There's a church with all its lights on at a quarter past the clock

And I am just the space between the things that people are
I am just the echo of a song without a bar
I am three am, I am the absence of your name
I am walking through this city and I'll never be the same

Oh city, hold me gently in your arms tonight
The buses still are running though they're mostly out of sight
The buildings never sleep and neither will I
Just me and all these quiet streets beneath this indigo sky

Oh city don't you let me go
I got nowhere left to go
Just breathe me in and breathe me out
Let your rivers take my doubt
Let your lamplight be enough
When love decides to stop being love
When love decides to just give up

songs.subject: trap / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Woke up early with the sunrise on the water tower red
Got my grandmother's voice still ringing softly in my head
Saying boy you come from something, don't you ever lose that ground
This city got a heartbeat and it's the finest kind of sound

The corner store been there since 1989
Mr. Eddie knowing everybody's name and knowing mine
The murals on the overpass still bleeding royal blue
I took a photograph of every single one for you

Baby I wish you could see it like I see it
Every broken thing is beautiful if you know how to read it
I fall in love a thousand times before the afternoon
With the place that made me, honey, I'll be showing you soon

Forty-seventh and the boulevard where summer never left
Where the cookout smoke gets tangled up with every single breath
Where the music leaks from basements onto sidewalks, onto skin
Where everybody knows your name before you even begin

The high school where I learned that I was more than what they said
Where Coach Williams put his hand upon my shoulder, go ahead
Where I wrote my first rap verse inside a notebook full of dreams
This town was never pretty but it held me at the seams

Baby I wish you could see it like I see it
Every broken thing is beautiful if you know how to read it
I fall in love a thousand times before the afternoon
With the place that made me, honey, I'll be showing you soon

They always talking bout escaping, getting out and getting free
But I been to all those places and they don't feel home to me
The cracks inside the sidewalk got my name somewhere inside
The stoplights know my rhythm and the bridges know my stride

My father drove these avenues before I learned to walk
My people filled these churches up with prayer and gospel talk
The winters here are brutal and the summers hit like grace
And I would not trade nothing for this imperfect place

Baby I wish you could see it like I see it
Every broken thing is beautiful if you know how to read it
I fall in love a thousand times before the afternoon
With the place that made me, honey, I'll be showing you soon

I'll take you to the river where we watched the fireworks fall
I'll take you to the diner where they know my usual call
I'll take you to my block and let you feel it in your chest
Why I carry home inside me, why I never could leave this nest

Baby this is where I'm from and this is who I am
Every street a chapter, every face a diagram
Of the love that shaped me, of the roots beneath my feet
This hometown got my whole heart every time these two worlds meet

Baby I wish you could see it like I see it
Every broken thing is beautiful if you know how to read it
I fall in love a thousand times before the afternoon
With the place that made me, baby, I'll be showing you soon
Yeah I'll be showing you soon
I said I'll be showing you soon

songs.subject: alt rock / moving to a new city
songs.lyrics:
Boxes on the sidewalk, rain won't stop
Kids asleep in the back seat, three a.m. clock
I told myself I'd never run from anything
But sometimes running is the bravest thing

New street signs I don't know yet
Apartment smells like someone else's cigarettes
She woke up asking where her bedroom went
I said baby this is where our story starts to bend

And I don't know a single face on this block
But I've been learning how to pick every lock
Spent so long waiting for permission to breathe
Now the only thing behind me is the life I used to need

Grocery store with all the wrong brands
Pizza place that doesn't understand
That we like extra cheese and no bell peppers please
But we'll figure out the menu eventually

Preschool said her teacher's name is Claire
She came home with paint still in her hair
Laughing about something on the playground swings
God I forgot she still believes in simple things

songs.subject: lo-fi / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Woke up to my phone buzzing twice
Didn't even think it was something nice
Rubbed my eyes and read it half asleep
Now I'm staring at the ceiling counting sheep

You said you needed space and time to grow
Said it wasn't me, which means it is, I know
Seven months erased in fourteen words
That's the quietest and the loudest thing I've heard

And I keep reading it again
Like the meaning's gonna change
Like autocorrect just got it wrong
Like you're coming back to say I made a mistake
But the dots aren't blinking anymore
And your last seen was an hour ago
I'm just a kid who doesn't know
How you end things through a screen and then just go

Showed my friend the screenshot on the bus
She said you were never worth the fuss
But she doesn't know you like I thought I did
She doesn't know what it feels like being sixteen and skid

Lunch period I sat beside your locker
Pretended I was only there by accident
You walked by and didn't even pause
That's when I understood what silence does

And I keep reading it again
Like the meaning's gonna change

Like you're coming back to say I made a mistake
But the dots aren't blinking anymore
And your last seen was an hour ago
I'm just a kid who doesn't know
How you end things through a screen and then just go

My mom would say there's other fish
My dad would probably miss the point
I'm lying on my bedroom floor with headphones in
At the age where everything feels like the end

And maybe it's not the end of everything
But it's the end of something real to me
And real things hurt when they break apart
Even a small phone screen can shatter a whole heart

And I keep reading it again
Like the meaning's gonna change
Like autocorrect just got it wrong
Like you're coming back to say I made a mistake
But the dots aren't blinking anymore
And your last seen was an hour ago
I'm just a kid who doesn't know
How you end things through a screen and then just go

I muted you but didn't block
Still checking just in case
Delete the thread or keep it there
I haven't found the courage yet to leave without a trace

songs.subject: ballad / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
She took the car and the savings account
Left a note so brief it barely even counts
Said she needed more than I could give
Funny thing is I forgot how good it feels to live

Ramen on the stove, window cracked for air
Candle I found burning on the stair
My buddy Jake came over with some bread and wine
We sat on the floor and laughed till half past nine

I've got holes in both my shoes
And a heart that's freshly bruised
And I've never been so free
Counting coins for coffee, finding beauty in the small
No safety net but I don't mind the fall
Cause I've never felt more me
And it turns out broke and happy
Is a place I didn't know could be

Canceled every subscription that she liked
Turned the lights down low and rode my bike
Down to the river where nobody goes
Sat there counting all the things nobody knows

That I hate fancy restaurants and ties
That I'd rather watch the stars than television skies
That I sing off-key to songs from ninety-three
And she always said I was too much of me

I've got holes in both my shoes
And a heart that's freshly bruised
And I've never been so free
Counting coins for coffee, finding beauty in the small
No safety net but I don't mind the fall
Cause I've never felt more me
And it turns out broke and happy
Is a place I didn't know could be

Bought a notebook from the dollar store
Started writing down the things worth living for
Warm socks and the way bread smells when it's done
And the feeling of the morning's early sun

She said I had no ambition, no direction home
Maybe she was right or maybe I was born to roam
Maybe poverty of wallet isn't poverty of soul
Maybe all along I just needed to let go

I've got holes in both my shoes
And a heart that's freshly bruised
And I've never been so free
Counting coins for coffee, finding beauty in the small
No safety net but I don't mind the fall
Cause I've never felt more me
And it turns out broke and happy
Is a place I didn't know could be

Some people leave and take the whole room with them
But then the room fills up again with light
And you realize the darkness wasn't permanent
You realize you were hiding from yourself all night

Broke as a joke and grinning anyway
That's where I am and honey that's okay

songs.subject: soul / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I seen a thousand faces at this bar
Some come in broken, some come in from far
But the ones who sit right down on stool number three
Got a look I recognize because it once was me

You want the usual or something stronger tonight
Yeah I can see it written all across your eyes
You don't have to talk, I'll keep your glass filled up
But if you need to speak, well baby I got time enough

Cause I learned forgiveness wasn't something given from above
I found it at the bottom of my own damn cup
It didn't come in church or from somebody telling me it's right
It came on a slow Tuesday night

When I let the thing I was holding finally go
And something in my chest just said okay
And I breathed out ten years of a grudge I had to carry
And Lord have mercy it felt something like grace

You said somebody done you wrong, I hear that
Said they walked away and never once looked back
Said you want to be the bigger person but it burns
That's the fire, darling, that's how healing works

I wiped this bar down forty thousand times
Listened to the same confession wearing different lines
Ain't nobody in this place who hasn't been destroyed
And ain't nobody here who can't be rebuilt from the void

Cause I learned forgiveness wasn't something given from above
I found it at the bottom of my own damn cup
It didn't come in church or from somebody telling me it's right
It came on a slow Tuesday night

When I let the thing I was holding finally go
And something in my chest just said okay
And I breathed out ten years of a grudge I had to carry
And Lord have mercy it felt something like grace

Now I ain't gonna preach because you didn't ask for that
Just gonna pour another and sit with where you're at
But somewhere between the first drink and the last one that you take
You might find the place where anger starts to break

My daddy left when I was nine years old
Took his leather jacket and left me in the cold
Spent twenty years perfecting how to hate a man
Spent twenty years forgetting how to open up my hands

Saw him at a diner, 2009, fall
He looked so small and tired against that wall
And something shifted, some old lock just turned
I walked on over, shook his hand, and learned

That forgiveness wasn't something given from above
I found it at the bottom of my own damn cup
It didn't come in church or from somebody telling me it's right
It came on a slow Tuesday night

When I let the thing I was holding finally go
And something in my chest just said okay
And I breathed out ten years of a grudge I had to carry
And Lord have mercy it felt something like grace

So you sit here long as you need to, honey
Bar's not closing till the last soul finds their way
And if you walk out lighter than you walked in
Well then I done my job tonight, and that's enough to say

songs.subject: rock / starting over
songs.lyrics:
Broken glass beneath my feet tonight
I'm walking out into the fading light
Everything I built came crashing down
Left me with the ashes of this town
But I can feel something moving in my chest
Like a fire starting after all the rest
Has turned to dust and smoke and hollow ground
I'm ready now to turn this thing around

I'm starting over, starting over
Burning down the walls I hid behind
I'm starting over, starting over
Leaving every broken piece behind
Yeah I'm starting over now

The radio still plays our favorite song
I used to think that meant I still belonged
To all the versions of myself I buried deep
The promises I made I couldn't keep
But every scar is just a map of where I've been
And every door that's closed another opens in
This highway stretches out into the dawn
And I'm the only one who can push on

I'm starting over, starting over
Burning down the walls I hid behind
I'm starting over, starting over
Leaving every broken piece behind
Yeah I'm starting over now

I don't need saving, I just need the road
I don't need anyone to carry this load
I'll pick myself up off this dirty floor
And walk straight through that wide-open door

I'm starting over, starting over
Burning down the walls I hid behind
I'm starting over, starting over
Leaving every broken piece behind
I'm starting over, starting over
Nothing left to lose and miles to find
Yeah I'm starting over now
I'm starting over now
Starting over

songs.subject: blues / a childhood home
songs.lyrics:
Went back down to Miller Street last fall
Couldn't even recognize the place at all
The old oak tree we climbed is long since gone
They put a parking structure on the lawn
Front porch where my daddy used to sit
Smoking his pipe in the evening lit
By a yellow bulb above the door
Lord I won't see any of that no more

I got the old house blues, the old house blues
Every room I walked in had a different set of shoes
Got the old house blues, the old house blues
Time don't wait for nothing, and it sure don't wait for you

Mama kept her garden on the side
Roses red and tall with southern pride
She'd be out there every Saturday
Before the heat of Georgia burned the day
I can smell them still sometimes at night
Something in the air just hits me right
And I'm eight years old again it seems
But you can't live your whole life in your dreams

I got the old house blues, the old house blues
Every room I walked in had a different set of shoes
Got the old house blues, the old house blues
Time don't wait for nothing, and it sure don't wait for you

Seventy-three years I been on this earth
Trying to figure out just what it's worth
And the only thing that still makes my chest tight
Is that little house beneath the porch light
My brother and me, we'd catch fireflies
Put them in a jar and watch them rise
Before the world got complicated and cold
Lord what I'd give to be that young and bold

I got the old house blues, the old house blues
Every room I walked in had a different set of shoes
Got the old house blues, the old house blues
Time don't wait for nothing, and it sure don't wait for you
Yeah I got those old house blues
Those low-down, won't-let-go, old house blues

songs.subject: disco / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
Walking in and finding all your stuff is gone
Every drawer is empty and the TV's on
Staring at the ceiling fan at half past two
Thinking how I'm gonna make my way on through
But then the music starts and something in me shifts
Body getting lighter and my spirit lifts
Gonna put my dancing shoes on, hit the floor
Cause I've been crying long enough, I ain't crying more

I forgive you baby, I forgive you now
Gonna spin around this heartbreak and I'll show you how
I forgive you baby, set myself on fire
Dancing on the ashes of our lost desire
Forgive you, yeah I forgive you
Forgive you, watch me let it go
Forgive you, yeah I forgive you

I'll forgive every single thing you did tonight
Cause holding onto anger only poisons what's inside
And I've got way too much to offer and too much damn pride

songs.subject: indie folk / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
Stumbled through the kitchen door
Found you leaning on the wall
You were laughing at a story
I couldn't hear it but I felt the call
Something in the golden light
Something in the way you turned around
Like the whole room lost its volume
And you were the only sound

I handed you a drink you didn't ask for
You smiled and took it anyway
And I thought I'd been to a hundred parties
But I'd never felt this kind of strange
Like the night was holding its breath for something
Like the stars had leaned in close
I've been looking for a long time, darling
But I didn't know till now what for

We sat out on the fire escape
Talking till the music died
You told me about your sister's wedding
I told you things I usually hide
And the city hummed beneath us
Like it was keeping us in tune
I've lived here all my life but honey
It felt like somewhere new

I handed you a drink you didn't ask for
You smiled and took it anyway
And I thought I'd been to a hundred parties
But I'd never felt this kind of strange
Like the night was holding its breath for something

Someone called your name from inside
And we both pretended not to hear
You laughed and I laughed with you
And the whole world just disappeared
I don't believe in much these days
But I believe in what I know
And I know something started here tonight
Wherever it decides to go

I handed you a drink you didn't ask for

And I thought I'd been to a hundred parties

And we stayed until they turned the lights on
And you wrote your number on my hand
I walked home in the cold October
Feeling more like myself than I have in years

songs.subject: country / a childhood home
songs.lyrics:
Dear old house on Miller Road
I drove past you again today
Somebody painted your shutters green
Mama always kept them gray
There's a trampoline in the backyard now
Where Daddy grew his tomatoes tall
And a stranger's name on the mailbox
But your bones still know my name, I know they do

I remember the way the screen door stuck
You had to lift and pull
And how the third step on the staircase creaked
So I learned to skip it coming home past curfew
The kitchen always smelled like coffee
Even in the middle of the night
Mama said the walls absorbed it
All those years of early morning light

I'm not sad, I'm just remembering
The way a thing can hold a life inside
All those birthdays, all those arguments
All those summers, all those goodbyes
You were just a house, I know that
Just some wood and glass and stone
But you were the first place in this whole wide world
That ever felt like home

I carved my name into the cedar tree
I wonder if it's still out back
I used to think that kind of permanent
Was something you could always come back to
But they tell you when you're young
That home is where your people are
They don't tell you that the people move
And the place stays where it was

My brother called me on a Tuesday
Said the Hendersons had sold
Some young couple from the city
Gonna fix it up, or so we're told
And I said good, I hope they love it
I hope their kids run up those stairs
I hope they find the hidey-hole we made
Behind the panel in the hallway, still back there

I'm not sad, I'm just remembering
The way a thing can hold a life inside
All those birthdays, all those arguments
All those summers, all those goodbyes
You were just a house, I know that
Just some wood and glass and stone
But you were the first place in this whole wide world
That ever felt like home

I'd give anything for one more Sunday
Sitting on that porch in August heat
Daddy in his chair, Mama shelling beans
The dog asleep down at our feet
Nothing happening and everything happening
The way it does when you are ten
I didn't know I was collecting it
I didn't know I'd need it all again

I'm not sad, I'm just remembering
The way a thing can hold a life inside
All those birthdays, all those arguments
All those summers, all those goodbyes
You were just a house, I know that
Just some wood and glass and stone
But you were the first place in this whole wide world
That ever felt like home

So I drove past slow and then I drove on
Didn't stop, didn't need to
Some things you keep better from a distance
Some things you just carry with you
Some things you just carry with you

songs.subject: blues / a first apartment
songs.lyrics:
Moved in on a Friday with a borrowed truck
Two boxes and a mattress and a whole lotta luck
Landlord said the heat works most of the time
Charged me two months up front for that peace of mind
Number 4C, third floor walk-up, water stain on the ceiling shaped like a boot
First night I ate crackers sitting on the floor
Man, I felt like something

Yeah I was broke but I was free
In that drafty little room just big enough for me
Radiator banging like it had something to say
Singing its own blues at the end of the day
Oh that first apartment, she was cold and she was thin
But she was mine, Lord, she was mine
She let me in

Cockroaches had squatters rights in the back of the stove
Neighbor down the hall played his music way too low
Not loud, just constant, like a heartbeat through the wall
I learned to sleep to Marvin Gaye down the hall
Shower took eleven minutes to run warm
I stood there every morning counting down
But when the hot water came it came like salvation
Man I hollered like I found something

Yeah I was broke but I was free
In that drafty little room just big enough for me
Radiator banging like it had something to say
Singing its own blues at the end of the day
Oh that first apartment, she was cold and she was thin
But she was mine, Lord, she was mine
She let me in

I hung one picture crooked and left it that way
Cooked my first real meal on a Tuesday
Burned the onions but I ate every bite
Sat by the window watching the street fill up at night
Called my mama, told her I was doing fine
Didn't tell her about the mice behind the wall
Didn't tell her I cried a little the second week
Some things a grown man keeps to himself, that's all

Yeah I was broke but I was free
In that drafty little room just big enough for me
Radiator banging like it had something to say
Singing its own blues at the end of the day
Oh that first apartment, she was cold and she was thin
But she was mine, Lord, she was mine
She let me in

I stayed two years and then I moved on up
Left my name in pencil inside the cabinet above
Whoever you are living there right now
I hope she treats you something like she treated me somehow
A little hard, a little honest, cold in the morning
Hot in July
First place you ever failed and figured it out
First place you ever learned to get by

In that drafty little room just big enough for me

Singing its own blues at the end of the day

She let me in

songs.subject: indie folk / a friend who changed
songs.lyrics:
He used to come in every Thursday
Order the same thing, bourbon neat
Sit at the far end of the bar
And talk to anybody he would meet
Had this laugh that filled the room up
Had this way of making strangers feel like friends
I used to think that some people are just built like that
Open-handed, easy, no pretend

But somewhere in the second year
He started sitting closer to the door
Started watching who came in and out
Like he was keeping some kind of score
The laugh got shorter, eyes got careful
He started asking questions, strange and thin
I kept pouring, kept my mouth shut
That's the job, you just take it in

I've seen a hundred people change at this bar
Some of them for better, some of them for hard
Some just slowly drift like smoke toward the door
And come back different, if they come back at all
I don't ask questions, I just watch the hands
Watch what they do when they think nobody can
I knew him when he was somebody I liked
Now I don't know what he is

His friends stopped coming around October
One by one they just stopped showing up
He never mentioned it, I never asked
Just kept his glass from running dry enough
He'd talk sometimes about a business
Something vague about connections, getting in
I'd nod and wipe the bar down, keep my eyes low
There are things you learn to hear and not to listen

I've seen a hundred people change at this bar
Some of them for better, some of them for hard
Some just slowly drift like smoke toward the door
And come back different, if they come back at all
I don't ask questions, I just watch the hands
Watch what they do when they think nobody can
I knew him when he was somebody I liked
Now I don't know what he is

Last winter he came in with different people
Hard-looking men who didn't meet my eye
Left a tip too big, which means something
When a man tips too big, he's buying something, buying quiet
I've tended bar for going on eleven years
I know the difference between generous and afraid
He looked at me one second longer than he needed
And I looked back and neither of us said

I've seen a hundred people change at this bar
Some of them for better, some of them for hard
Some just slowly drift like smoke toward the door
And come back different, if they come back at all
I don't ask questions, I just watch the hands
Watch what they do when they think nobody can
I knew him when he was somebody I liked
Now I don't know what he is

He hasn't been in since the snow melted
Could be nothing, could be that he moved
Could be something that I'd rather not think on
Too long after I turn off these lights
I still got the glass he left here on a Tuesday
Left without it, left without a word
I washed it out and put it back on the shelf
Funny how you grieve a thing that isn't gone
Funny how you grieve a thing that isn't gone

songs.lyrics:

This town got its hooks in me so deep, Lord, I been here too long

Every corner got a memory, every street got a ghost

The people that I loved the most done left from coast to coast

Water tower's rusting, still got our names spray-painted there
Water tower's rusting, still got our names spray-painted there
I keep driving past it slow, just breathing in that stale air

Oh this hometown, she's a ball and chain wrapped in a lullaby

Can't live with her, can't leave her, just watch the years go by

Mill closed down in eighty-nine, took my daddy's back with it

Now they selling fancy coffee where the union hall once sit

Boys I grew up running with got swallowed by this road

Oh this hometown, she's a ball and chain wrapped in a lullaby

I keep my bags half packed beside the door just in case

songs.subject: trap / a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Black roses on the casket, dressed in all my pain
Everybody crying but me, I'm standing in the rain
They don't know what we were, they don't know our name
I'm the one who loved you hardest, nothing look the same

Ayy, said a prayer but God know I'm barely holding on
Ayy, everybody leaving now but I can't move along
Flowers on your picture, got your fragrance on my shirt
Never knew that loving somebody could produce this kind of hurt

They lowering you down slow, preacher talking 'bout the sky
All I keep on thinking is the last time I caught your eye
You were laughing at my joke, had your hand up on my chest
Now they tucking you in permanent, and baby you look blessed

But I'm not, nah I'm not, I'm falling apart at the seam
They passing me the program, feel like I'm lost in a dream
Your mama hugged me tight, she said you loved me so real
That just broke me open wider, nothing gon' fix what I feel

Ayy, said a prayer but God know I'm barely holding on
Ayy, everybody leaving now but I can't move along
Flowers on your picture, got your fragrance on my shirt
Never knew that loving somebody could produce this kind of hurt

Reception at your auntie house, they got your favorites out
Macaroni, lemon cake, I can't eat, I can't shout
Just sitting in the corner with your cousin, looking at your face
On every single photograph they scattered 'round the place

songs.subject: blues / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
Phone rang at midnight, shook me from my closing rag
Phone rang at midnight, shook me from my closing rag
Been wiping down this bar top, Lord, got my spirits in a bag

Voice on the other end said something 'bout a name I knew
Voice on the other end, whispering a name I knew
Said there'd been some trouble, darling, and I knew that trouble too

See I hear confessions that the priest don't even get to hear
I pull the good stuff for confessions that the priest don't get to hear
Every soul that walks in broken, I'm the one who's always here

Midnight calls don't carry good news, that's the first thing that I learned
Midnight calls don't carry good news, Lord, that's the first thing that I learned
Every happy story's told by daylight, darkness brings what's been deferred

He used to sit at stool number three, every other Friday night
Old man used to sit at stool number three, every other Friday night

Told me once his daughter left him, said he drove her to the door

I hung up and I sat down on the cooler in the back

Thinking 'bout how many times I shoulda walked around that bar's old track

Put my hand on someone's shoulder, say the thing that matters right

Midnight calls don't carry good news, that's the first thing that I learned

Now I open up at four o'clock, the regulars file in

And I look at every single face and wonder where they've been

What they carrying in underneath those ordinary eyes
What they carrying underneath those plain and ordinary eyes
How many of 'em gonna get a midnight call before the sunrise

I keep the bourbon where he liked it, stool stays open now

songs.subject: hip-hop / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
I drove past your old apartment just to feel something real
Lights on in the window, someone else's life, someone else's deal
You been gone six months and two weeks, I been counting every day
She left me on a Tuesday and now you gone, and I got nothing to say

Used to call you when the world got sideways, you'd pick up every time
We'd sit on the phone for nothing, just existing in each other's time
Now you in Atlanta or wherever the dream pulled you to be
And I'm sitting in the same zip code with a hole where you used to be

I miss you like the city misses summer in December
I miss you like a song you can't quite fully remember
You took the best parts of this block when you loaded up that van
And she took the best parts of my heart, and I'm just barely who I am

Your corner spot got a new smoothie place, you would hate that so much
You'd go on for twenty minutes 'bout gentrification and such
I'd pretend to half-listen while really soaking up every word
Now I'm talking to nobody, feel like everything I say's unheard

She said I loved the past more than I loved her standing right there
Maybe she was right, I don't know, I been too numb to be fair
All I know is two people gone from this life in the same cold year
One chose opportunity, one chose to disappear

I miss you like the city misses summer in December
I miss you like a song you can't quite fully remember
You took the best parts of this block when you loaded up that van
And she took the best parts of my heart, and I'm just barely who I am

Remember the night we sat on your roof watching lightning roll in
We said we'd always live close, we meant it, that was genuine
Life don't care about the promises you make at twenty-three
Now you chasing something bigger and she's chasing someone free

I scrolled your page last night, you look happy in your new city light
New friends, new spots, new everything, and that's alright, yeah that's alright
But who's gonna tell me when I'm being stupid 'bout a girl
Who's gonna be the one who actually knows me in this world

She packed a box of my records, left them outside by the stair
Said she kept the ones I loved the most 'cause she knew I would care
Even leaving she was thoughtful, God that makes it worse somehow
I played every single one of them and thought of both of you, right now

I miss you like the city misses summer in December
I miss you like a song you can't quite fully remember
You took the best parts of this block when you loaded up that van
And she took the best parts of my heart, and I'm just barely who I am

So I'm sitting on your old stoop where they keep the packages now
Trying to figure out the next move, trying to figure out how
A person rebuilds the geography of who they are
When everybody that they anchored to has gone so far

I ain't mad at you for leaving, you were always meant for more
I'm just struggling with the silence that comes knocking at my door
Two different kinds of missing living side by side in me
One is pride, one is heartbreak, both are all I got for free

I miss you like the city misses summer in December
I miss you like a song you can't quite fully remember
You took the best parts of this block when you loaded up that van
And she took the best parts of my heart
And I'm just barely
Just barely
Who I am

songs.subject: emo / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
Flowers in my hands but they're turning black
The church is full of faces and there's no turning back
White dress, white lies, white knuckles on the pew
I said I do, I said I do, but did I mean it too

The organ plays a hymn I've never learned
The candles burn for something I have not yet earned
My mother's crying happy but I know the difference
Between tears of joy and tears that hide the evidence

And I am drowning in the aisle
I've been drowning for a while
With rice and ribbons in my hair
And a hollow I can't repair
Everyone is smiling now
And I don't know how
To tell the world the bells they ring
Are ringing for the end of everything

The photographs will come out clean and bright
Two faces pressed together in the golden light
But somewhere in my chest a door is closing shut
And all the flowers in the world can't fill this cut

I kissed you like a promise I can't keep
I signed my name in ink that's running deep
The guests are throwing petals at our feet
And I am falling through the concrete street

And I am drowning in the aisle
I've been drowning for a while
With rice and ribbons in my hair
And a hollow I can't repair
Everyone is smiling now
And I don't know how
To tell the world the bells they ring
Are ringing for the end of everything

So drive me through the dark to somewhere new
I'll wear this white forever if it's true
But somewhere in the back of all this lace
Is a girl who doesn't recognize her face

And maybe love is not a burning thing
Maybe love is just the weight of wearing someone's ring
And maybe I'll believe it in the morning light
Or maybe I'll be crying through the night

And I am drowning in the aisle
I've been drowning for a while
With rice and ribbons in my hair
And a hollow I can't repair
Everyone is smiling now
And I don't know how
To tell the world the bells they ring

songs.subject: rock / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
You left on a Sunday with your boxes in the rain
You said you needed freedom, I just needed you to stay
The door swung shut behind you like a hammer on my chest
And I put your photograph face down with all the rest

I broke a couple glasses just to hear the sound
I drove until the city turned to open ground
I called your name into a field and heard it echo back
And carried all that anger like a stone inside a sack

But I've been thinking hard about the way that I hold grudges
Like they're something worth the weight
I've been thinking hard about the fire I keep burning
And it's only burning me
So I'm laying down the sword tonight
I'm laying down the war
I don't know if you deserve it
But I know what anger's for
It's a room with all the windows shut
And I've been gasping for so long
So I'm letting go, I'm letting go
I'm letting go and moving on

Picking at the edges of the life that we once had
Your sweater's in the hallway and your books are on my shelf
And forgiving you means finally forgiving myself

Because I wasn't easy either, I know I had my walls

And maybe what we made together wasn't built to last

You don't get to watch me rise
This forgiveness isn't a gift to you
It's me opening my eyes

Yeah I wasted months in a courtroom in my head

So I'm walking out the courtroom and I'm throwing out the case
I'm not erasing what you did, I'm just reclaiming space
I'm not saying it was nothing, I'm not saying you were right
I'm just saying I'm done bleeding for a finished fight

songs.subject: ballad / faith
songs.lyrics:
When the morning feels too heavy and the night won't let me rest
When the road I've chosen stretches past the edges of my chest
I reach for something solid in the middle of the dark
And find the same old ember still alive inside my heart

My father used to tell me that the doubting is a part
Of something bigger breathing through the chambers of your art
That faith is not the absence of the fear inside your bones
But walking through the valley knowing you're not walking alone

So I hold on, I hold on
Even when the answers don't arrive
I hold on, I hold on
To the reason I'm alive
When the world turns cold and the walls close in
And everything I've built looks paper thin
I breathe in, I breathe out
I believe without a doubt
That something holds me up when I let go
Something holds me up when I let go

I've stood at the edge of things I couldn't understand
I've felt the ground beneath me turn to water under sand
I've watched the people that I loved get swallowed by the night
And shook my fist at heaven in the absence of the light

But even in the shaking, even in the grief
There was a thread too thin to see but stronger than belief
A pull toward the morning when the darkness made no sense
A hand I couldn't hold but felt in everything since

So I hold on, I hold on
Even when the answers don't arrive
I hold on, I hold on
To the reason I'm alive
When the world turns cold and the walls close in

I believe without a doubt

Something holds me up when I let go

And I have knelt in empty rooms and spoken to the air
And risen with the feeling that the air was listening there
I have sung in broken voices, I have wept in borrowed pews
I have carried faith like water through the long and winding news

Of living, of surviving, of beginning again
Of finding grace in faces, of finding grace in pain
Of learning that the mystery is not a thing to solve
But a river you surrender to and trust enough to follow

So I hold on, I hold on
Even when the answers don't arrive
I hold on, I hold on
To the reason I'm alive
When the world turns cold and the walls close in
And everything I've built looks paper thin
I breathe in, I breathe out
I believe without a doubt
That something holds me up when I let go
Something holds me up when I let go

Something holds me when I'm falling
Something hears me when I'm calling
Through the silence and the distance
There is love, there is love
There is love, there is love
And I hold on
I hold on
I hold on

When the road I've chosen stretches past the edges of my chest
I reach for something solid in the middle of the dark
And find the same old ember still alive inside my heart

But even in the shaking, even in the grief

A pull toward the morning when the darkness made no sense
A hand I couldn't hold but felt in everything since

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:
The coffee's cold and Maya's asleep in the backseat
Got three hundred miles of highway and a prayer beneath my feet
The radio keeps cutting out somewhere past Amarillo
I rolled the window down and let the warm air be my pillow

I left the apartment with two bags and a car seat
Left the life I thought I wanted on that cracked and sunlit street
She don't know what I'm carrying she just clutches her stuffed bear
And I keep both hands on the wheel and I keep my eyes out there

This road is long and the painted lines keep pulling me along
I've been crying since the county line but honey I am strong
Every mile is a maybe and every exit is a choice
And I'm singing to the open sky just to hear my own voice
Just to hear my own voice

Somewhere past the state line there's a town I've never been
My sister's got a pullout couch and a yard with a trampoline
She said come on down we'll figure out the details when you're here
And that was all I needed just to know that someone's near

Maya woke up asking me if we were almost there
I told her we were on an adventure and I braided back her hair
At the gas station in nowhere she found a plastic ring
She said Mama you can have it and I felt everything

This road is long and the painted lines keep pulling me along
I've been crying since the county line but honey I am strong
Every mile is a maybe and every exit is a choice
And I'm singing to the open sky just to hear my own voice
Just to hear my own voice

The sun is going down behind the mountains turning red
And I've been rehearsing who I'm going to be instead
Not the woman in that kitchen not the girl who swallowed no
But the one behind this steering wheel who finally let go

She's sleeping now her little mouth is open to the dark
And I reach back and touch her hand and feel it in my heart
That every broken road I took was leading right to this
This quiet revolution and her small and steady breath

This road is long and the painted lines keep pulling me along
I've been crying since the county line but honey I am strong
Every mile is a maybe and every exit is a choice
And I'm singing to the open sky just to hear my own voice

This road is long and the yellow lines are humming me a song
I don't know what's waiting but I know where I belong
Every mile is a promise and every exit is a door
And I'm finally driving toward the life I'm living for
Just to hear my own voice
Just to hear my own voice

3. A singer-songwriter song about a childhood home. from a single mother's point of view
4. A pop song about starting over. from someone in love

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a childhood home
songs.lyrics:
I drove by the old house on a Tuesday afternoon
The maple tree we planted is taller than the moon
Someone painted the shutters and the porch swing is still there
And I sat out in the car and cried with my hands in my hair

I raised you in those four rooms with the leaky bathroom sink
I taught myself to fix things on the nights I couldn't think
We made a fort out of the couch cushions on the living room floor
And I still hear your little footsteps running to the door

These walls don't know my name anymore but they hold my ghost
In every crooked nail hole in the room I loved the most
I left my fingerprints on every window painted shut
And I still feel the heartbeat of that house deep in my gut
Deep in my gut

Your father's name is somewhere in the grain of the oak floor
I swept around it every day until I swept no more
And the kitchen smelled like coffee every morning come what may
I'd hold you at the counter while the world just went away

The woman who lives there now she hung a wreath upon the door
I wonder if she feels us in the creaking of the floor
I wonder if at three a.m. she hears a lullaby
Some leftover love we couldn't take when we said our goodbyes

These walls don't know my name anymore but they hold my ghost
In every crooked nail hole in the room I loved the most
I left my fingerprints on every window painted shut
And I still feel the heartbeat of that house deep in my gut
Deep in my gut

You were two when we moved in and seven when we left
And I have carried that address like a stone inside my chest
You probably don't remember the way the light came through
But I was standing in that gold light every day just loving you

The maple tree we planted when you came home from the hospital small
Now it shades the whole front yard it covers up the wall
I like to think we left something that the new paint can't erase
Something breathing in the roots something warm about that place

These walls don't know my name anymore but they hold my ghost
In every crooked nail hole in the room I loved the most
I left my fingerprints on every window painted shut
And I still feel the heartbeat of that house deep in my gut

These walls don't know my name anymore
But I know every wall
I know every scratch and story
I know every fall
And the house is just a house now
But the home is still in me
It's in you baby it's in you
And that's where it'll always be
Deep in my gut
Deep in my gut

songs.subject: pop / starting over
songs.lyrics:
I used to think that starting over meant that something had to die
But you came in like morning light and caught me by surprise
I had my boxes packed and labeled I was halfway out the door
And you just smiled across the room and I forgot what I was leaving for

My heart was like a city after all the people left
Just empty streets and echo rooms and not a whole lot left
But you walked in with your laugh like that your ridiculous good laugh
And something in the rubble said okay let's do the math

So let's start over let's burn the map and find a brand new way
Let's start over I don't need yesterday
Every wall I built is just a door I haven't opened yet
And with you beside me I am not afraid to forget
Let's start over let's start over
Let's start over you and me

I cleaned out all my old songs and I threw away my sad
I stopped apologizing for the best I ever had
You said to me last Tuesday eating takeout on the floor
That you had never felt this easy with anyone before

I know the statistics and I know how this could go
I know my own history better than I want to know
But you reach across the table and you take me by the hand
And I am not the person that I was when this began

So let's start over let's burn the map and find a brand new way
Let's start over I don't need yesterday
Every wall I built is just a door I haven't opened yet
And with you beside me I am not afraid to forget
Let's start over let's start over
Let's start over you and me

There's a version of me hiding in the back of my own mind
Saying don't you dare go loving someone leave yourself behind
But I've been listening to that voice for such a long long time
And all it ever gave me was this perfectly safe life

So tonight I choose the danger of a love that feels this true
Tonight I choose the terrifying joy of choosing you
Not the careful calculated version of what love could be
But the wild and laughing messy beautiful reality

Let's start over let's burn the map and find a brand new way
Let's start over I don't need yesterday
Every wall I built is just a door I haven't opened yet
And with you beside me I am not afraid to forget

Let's start over let's start over
Let's rearrange the furniture and paint the bedroom walls
Let's leave our names in wet cement somewhere before the fall
Let's start over I'm not scared I'm not scared anymore
You're the best thing I've walked toward by walking out that door
Let's start over you and me
Let's start over
Let's start over
You and me

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
She walked in at closing, phone in hand and cryin'
I said what'll it be and she said something strong
She showed me the screen, three words and a period
Didn't even call, just typed it out wrong
I poured her a bourbon, kept quiet a moment
Some nights the bar cloth just wipes up the tears
I've seen a hundred endings from behind this counter
Proposals and fights and a lifetime of years

You deserve more than a backlit goodbye
More than a thumbs up or a little blue read
He couldn't even dial, couldn't look you in the eye
Just tapped out your heartbreak and left you to bleed
I'm just a stranger with bottles and barlight
But honey I'm telling you, you'll be alright
Some people leave doors and some people leave windows
And some sorry cowards just leave you on read at night

Last call came around and she'd softened a little
Told me his name, told me how long they'd been
I dried out a glass and I listened real careful
The way that I do when the hour gets thin
She said we were good once, we laughed at the same things
He used to leave notes on the mirror in steam
And now there's a timestamp on everything broken
A seven word sentence and one shattered dream

You deserve more than a backlit goodbye
More than a thumbs up or a little blue read
He couldn't even dial, couldn't look you in the eye
Just tapped out your heartbreak and left you to bleed
I'm just a stranger with bottles and barlight
But honey I'm telling you, you'll be alright
Some people leave doors and some people leave windows
And some sorry cowards just leave you on read at night

I've been tending this bar going on fourteen seasons
Watched people fall apart and watched people heal
The ones who got texted, the ones who got ghosted
The ones who got nothing but learned how to feel
She left me a twenty and tucked in her jacket
Walked out to the cold and the neon and rain
I wiped down the counter and turned off the televisions
And thought about all the ways love can cause pain

songs.subject: dream pop / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
The morning came in silver blue
Light through the curtains touching you
You were sleeping still and I couldn't move
Just breathing in this moment like perfume
The flowers on the windowsill were white
I'd been awake since three or four or five
Watching all the darkness turn to something bright
Feeling like I'd only just arrived

Into this life, into this light
Into your hands where everything is right
The veil is thin between the real and dreams
And I am floating somewhere in between
Today we'll stand beneath a sky of flowers
Today they'll watch us give away our hours
And I will find your eyes across the room
And fall in love the way I always do

The dress was hanging like a ghost of silk
Something borrowed and a little gilt
My mother fixed the clasp with trembling hands
And told me love was more than just a plan
She said it's ordinary Tuesdays too
It's cold feet touching yours at half past two
But looking at you standing in that hall
I felt the ordinary and the all

Into this life, into this light
Into your hands where everything is right
The veil is thin between the real and dreams
And I am floating somewhere in between
Today we'll stand beneath a sky of flowers
Today they'll watch us give away our hours
And I will find your eyes across the room
And fall in love the way I always do

String lights above us like a scattered sky
Music drifting warm and low and wide
You took my hand and everything went slow
A beautiful unraveling below
The words we said were simple and they stayed
Inside my chest like sunlight in a vase
I heard you breathing when you said my name
And nothing ever will be quite the same

Into this life, into this light
Into your hands where everything is right
The veil is thin between the real and dreams
And I am floating somewhere in between
Today we'll stand beneath a sky of flowers
Today they'll watch us give away our hours
And I will find your eyes across the room
And fall in love the way I always do

Fall in love the way I always do
Every single time I look at you

songs.subject: ballad / a friend who changed
songs.lyrics:
She used to call me every Sunday morning
Before the kids were up and coffee cooled
We'd laugh about the neighbors and the nothing
The small and silly things that kept us full
I raised my boy on mac and cheese and movies
Working double shifts at half past four
And she would always leave the light on for me
But somewhere down the line she changed the door

I don't know who you are now in those pictures
All highlighted and filtered into gold
We used to split a six dollar bottle of wine
And talk about the stories we'd been told
I'm not angry, I'm just sitting with the aching
Of a friendship that I can't quite locate
You grew up and out and somewhere I got left here
Holding all the old things at the gate

My son asked me the other day where's Katie
I told him she was busy being new
He nodded like a seven-year-old prophet
And said I hope she still remembers you
I tucked him in and sat beside the window
The street below was quiet as a thought
And I realized I'd been grieving you in pieces
The way you grieve the things that can't be caught

I'm just saying I miss you all the while

Before the years had time to pull us down

1. A singer-songwriter song about a breakup text. from a bartender's point of view

3. A ballad song about a friend who changed. from a single mother's point of view
4. A reggae song about growing up in a small town. like a diary entry

songs.subject: reggae / growing up in a small town
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning to the same roosters calling
Same cracked sidewalk leading down to Mama's gate
Same Mr. Henderson sweeping up his front step
Same old clock above the hardware store running late
This town got one traffic light and two churches
A gas station, a diner, and a dream
I wrote it all down in my notebook by the window
Growing up here taught me what it means

July the fourth we'd ride our bikes to the water
The whole town fit beneath one stretch of sky
Somebody's uncle always burning something
And everybody knowing everybody's alibi
We didn't have the malls or moving pictures
We had the river and the root and the red clay road
I didn't know I was living something precious
I was just a boy beneath a heavy load

But this is where the rhythm found me
This is where the bass line underground me
Every pothole, every Sunday, every heat
This little town put music in my feet
I used to dream of somewhere bigger, somewhere faster
Now I send these words back like a letter home
No matter where the current carries me hereafter
This little town is written in my bone

Sixteen and restless I was watching the highway
Every truck that passed was pulling at my chest
Somebody's cousin left and came back empty
Said the city took his fire and left the rest
But old Miss Vera used to braid her granddaughter's hair
Right there on the porch in the afternoon heat
And the smell of something cooking at the end of summer
Was the most honest thing that I'd ever meet

I filled my notebook up with all the contradictions
The boredom and the beauty side by side
The way this place could hold you like a good thing
And also be the thing you tried to hide
But I was seventeen and stupid and I left it
Packed a bag and didn't look behind me long
And the first thing that I did when I got lonely
Was sit down and write this very song

Because this is where the rhythm found me
This is where the bass line underground me
Every pothole, every Sunday, every heat
This little town put music in my feet
I used to dream of somewhere bigger, somewhere faster
Now I send these words back like a letter home
No matter where the current carries me hereafter
This little town is written in my bone

Now I'm older and I carry it inside me
The way you carry something you can't see
All the voices and the faces and the front porches
All the versions of the person I might be
I come back sometimes and walk the old roads slowly
Say hello to everyone who stayed
And I think about the diary I been keeping
All these years since I was just a kid afraid

This little town, she never asked for nothing
Just gave me all the raw material
I turned it into music, turned it into living
Turned the ordinary into something real
So here's my entry for the day I'm writing
Sitting somewhere far across the sea
Still that boy beside the one-light intersection
Still becoming who I'm trying to be

This is where the rhythm found me
This is where the bass line underground me
Every pothole, every Sunday, every heat
This little town put music in my feet
I used to dream of somewhere bigger, somewhere faster
Now I send these words back like a letter home
No matter where the current carries me hereafter
This little town is written in my bone

This little town is written in my bone

songs.subject: reggae / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Warm wind rolling off the harbor
Salt and smoke in the morning air
Everybody knows your name here
Everybody stops and stares

The market opens up at sunrise
The fisherman brings in his catch
Old Mr. Brown still sells his mangoes
There's a rhythm nobody can match

This is where my heart was planted
This is where my roots run deep
Every stone and every corner
Is a memory I keep
Oh this town raised me up from nothing
Gave me everything I own
Every time I wander far away
My heart still calls me home

The children running through the alleys
The church bells ringing Sunday strong
The elders sitting on their porches
Everybody singing the same song

The rain comes down in the evening
And washes all the heat away
And the people gather round together
To give thanks for another day

This is where my heart was planted
This is where my roots run deep
Every stone and every corner
Is a memory I keep
Oh this town raised me up from nothing
Gave me everything I own
Every time I wander far away
My heart still calls me home

I have traveled to the mountains
I have seen the city lights
But nothing pulls me like this harbour
On a warm and starry night

The drumbeat steady in the distance
The laughter spilling from the yard
Life here ain't always easy
But love here ain't ever hard

This is where my heart was planted
This is where my roots run deep
Every stone and every corner
Is a memory I keep
Oh this town raised me up from nothing
Gave me everything I own
Every time I wander far away
My heart still calls me home

Come back come back to the harbor
Come back come back to the shore
Come back come back to the people
Who love you forever more

songs.subject: hip-hop / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
Seventy-three years and I'm still standing here
Holding a folded flag and fighting back tears
They played the trumpet and they fired the guns
But none of that helped me and none of that numbed
The fact that my father left this world last spring
And I'm still figuring out what that's supposed to mean

I remember calloused hands and Old Spice cologne
A man who worked double shifts just to keep us a home
He never said I love you but he showed up every day
Now I'm standing at his graveside and I don't know what to say
So I just say thank you pop for carrying us through
All my life I been becoming more and more like you

Gone but not forgotten
Roots grow even when the tree falls down
Gone but not forgotten
I still hear your voice when I'm lost and I can't be found
You raised me up from nothing
And now nothing feels the same
Gone but not forgotten
I still carry your name

I got grandkids running round who never heard him speak
And that's the part that tears me up and keeps me up all week
How do I explain a man whose silence said so much
Whose presence filled a room up and whose absence cuts and cuts
I tell them he was hardworking I tell them he was proud
I tell them he was quiet but he loved us very loud

I still pick up the phone sometimes before I remember
His number disconnected since that morning in September
Sixty years of muscle memory don't die overnight
I still want to call him when something don't feel right
And I know that he was old and I know that he was tired
But a son still needs his father like a cold night needs a fire

Gone but not forgotten
Roots grow even when the tree falls down
Gone but not forgotten
I still hear your voice when I'm lost and I can't be found
You raised me up from nothing
And now nothing feels the same
Gone but not forgotten
I still carry your name

I got his pocket watch sitting on my dresser top
Got his Bible and his toolbox and a photo that I got
From nineteen fifty-something when he was just a boy
Before the war before the working before life took his joy
He's smiling in that picture and it breaks my heart in two
To think about who he was before the world got through

And maybe I'll see him on the other side of something
Maybe all this grief is just the price of all that loving
I'm an old man now myself and I can feel the years
My knees ain't what they used to be and neither are these tears
But I'll carry him inside me till I lay my own self down
Till they fold another flag and play that trumpet sound

Gone but not forgotten
Roots grow even when the tree falls down
Gone but not forgotten
I still hear your voice when I'm lost and I can't be found
You raised me up from nothing
And now nothing feels the same
Gone but not forgotten
I still carry your name

Gone but not forgotten
Gone but not forgotten
Pop I still carry your name

songs.subject: bluegrass / a childhood home
songs.lyrics:
I drove past the old place out on Miller Road
The paint is still peeling where it always peeled before
The porch swing is rusted and the gate hangs wrong
And I sat in the driveway for a little too long

Because she said she didn't love me and she walked away
And something pulled me back here where the good things stayed
Back to the kitchen table and the creak of the floor
Back to the only place that ever wanted me more

These old wooden floors knew my footsteps by heart
These fields never told me that I wasn't smart
This back porch held me when the world felt unkind
The childhood home is the peace I can't find

Mama used to hang the laundry out back
The smell of the cedar and the railroad track
We'd chase the fireflies till the night came in deep
And nobody told us there was something to weep

But she told me I was ordinary and she told me I was plain
Said she needed something bigger than this old weather vane
And maybe she was right about the size of what I am
But this old house remembers when I thought I had a plan

These old wooden floors knew my footsteps by heart
These fields never told me that I wasn't smart
This back porch held me when the world felt unkind
The childhood home is the peace I can't find

There's a notch on the door frame from when I was nine
Daddy measured my height and said boy you're doing fine
That notch is still there underneath the peeling white
A little piece of someone who believed things would be right

I ain't nine years old and Daddy's long since gone
And the girl I loved has moved to somewhere carrying on
But something in the timber of these walls knows my name
Something in the hollow of this valley feels the same

The creek still runs behind the old red barn
The hay still catches on the rusted wire and yarn
The mountain in the distance don't care about my pain
And somehow that is comfort and somehow that's a grace

These old wooden floors knew my footsteps by heart
These fields never told me that I wasn't smart
This back porch held me when the world felt unkind
The childhood home is the peace I can't find

These old wooden floors knew my footsteps by heart
These fields never told me that I wasn't smart
This back porch held me when the world felt unkind
Come on home now come on home
You were always one of mine

songs.subject: punk / an ex you still think about
songs.lyrics:
I got a kid asleep down the hall and it's quarter to two
And I'm sitting here in the kitchen and I'm thinking of you
I swore I'd stop doing this I swore I'd let it die
But here I come again crawling back to the same old lie

You were bad for me I know it I knew it back then
But knowing never stopped the wanting from starting again
I got permission slips to sign and lunches left to make
But here I am unraveling for somebody else's sake

You still live in my 3am you still live in my walls
You still live in every stupid song that plays in the halls
I got a life you're not a part of I got a child I love
But you still take up more than the space I got shoved
You away with both hands I locked the door I changed the code
But you still haunt this apartment like a ghost I'm owed

I don't miss your drama and I don't miss your games
I don't miss the way you never once remembered my names
The name of who I was before I had to be strong
The name of who I'd be if I hadn't held on so long

But something in the muscle memory fires at night
When the apartment gets too quiet and the city too bright
And my daughter's just like you sometimes around the eyes
And I hate that I can love her and still despise

The way you left without a suitcase without a word
The way I found out from a stranger it's absurd
That I still give you real estate inside this tired head
When you couldn't even give me an honest word instead

You still live in my 3am you still live in my walls
You still live in every stupid song that plays in the halls
I got a life you're not a part of I got a child I love
But you still take up more than the space I got shoved
You away with both hands I locked the door I changed the code
But you still haunt this apartment like a ghost I'm owed

So here's what I want you to know and hear it loud and clear
I built something worth having and you don't get to be here
I packed her lunch today and I braided up her hair
And I watched her board the school bus with a force beyond compare

And you were never half the person that she is at seven
And you were never anything close to this imperfect heaven
Of spilled cereal and crayon marks and stories before bed
So why am I still writing songs for you inside my head

You still live in my 3am you still live in my walls
You still live in every stupid song that plays in the halls
I got a life you're not a part of I got a child I love
But you still take up more than the space I got shoved
You away with both hands I locked the door I changed the code
But you still haunt this apartment like a ghost I'm owed

We're done we're done we're done
I said it loud enough this time
We're done we're done we're done
You don't get another rhyme
We're done

I don't miss your drama and I don't miss your games
I don't miss the way you never once remembered my names
The name of who I was before I had to be strong
The name of who I'd be if I hadn't held on so long

But something in the muscle memory fires at night
When the apartment gets too quiet and the city too bright
And my daughter's just like you sometimes around the eyes
And I hate that I can love her and still despise

The way you left without a suitcase without a word
The way I found out from a stranger it's absurd
That I still give you real estate inside this tired head
When you couldn't even give me an honest word instead

You still live in my 3am you still live in my walls
You still live in every stupid song that plays in the halls
I got a life you're not a part of I got a child I love
But you still take up more than the space I got shoved
You away with both hands I locked the door I changed the code
But you still haunt this apartment like a ghost I'm owed

So here's what I want you to know and hear it loud and clear
I built something worth having and you don't get to be here
I packed her lunch today and I braided up her hair
And I watched her board the school bus with a force beyond compare

And you were never half the person that she is at seven
And you were never anything close to this imperfect heaven
Of spilled cereal and crayon marks and stories before bed
So why am I still writing songs for you inside my head

You still live in my 3am you still live in my walls
You still live in every stupid song that plays in the halls
I got a life you're not a part of I got a child I love
But you still take up more than the space I got shoved
You away with both hands I locked the door I changed the code
But you still haunt this apartment like a ghost I'm owed

We're done we're done we're done
I said it loud enough this time
We're done we're done we're done
You don't get another rhyme
We're done

songs.subject: rock / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
Glasses lined up on the bar, the morning flight's at nine
She wheeled her suitcase through the door and ordered cheap red wine
Said she'd been coming here for years each time she had to go
I've seen a hundred goodbyes here but this one hurt me so

She left her lipstick on the rim, she left a twenty down
She didn't say a word to me, just pulled her jacket 'round
I watched her walk toward gate fourteen, her carry-on in tow
Another soul I'll never know, another letting go

I pour the drinks and wipe the bar and watch the people leave
This terminal's a church of sorts for everyone who grieves
The pilots order coffee black, the lovers order two
And everyone who sits here's saying something overdue

You leave me here behind this bar, you leave me with your tab
You leave me with the olive pits and everything you had
I'll rinse the glass and set it dry and line it up again
And wait for someone just like you to walk back in

The afternoon crowd trickles in with rolling bags and tears
A soldier and his mother sharing nothing but their fears
She's touching all his buttons like she's counting what she'll miss
He's staring at his boarding pass and she just keeps on this

I've learned to read the faces here, the ones who won't come back
The ones who left something behind and can't afford the cab
The ones who hold the other ones until the very gate
And walk away with hollow chests and eyes that detonate

You leave me here behind this bar, you leave me with your tab
You leave me with the olive pits and everything you had
I'll rinse the glass and set it dry and line it up again
And wait for someone just like you to walk back in

So raise a glass to everyone who's standing at the door
Who kissed somebody hard enough to bruise their bottom lip
Who dragged their whole life through the rain to reach the TSA
Who sat here with a stranger just to breathe before the gate

Another flight is boarding now, the speakers crackle on
I pour myself a soda water, watch another dawn
I've never been the one who leaves, I've always been the bar
Just close enough to hold the weight and never go that far

You leave me here behind this bar, you leave me with your tab
You leave me with the olive pits and everything you had
I'll rinse the glass and set it dry and line it up again
And wait for someone just like you to walk back in
Wait for someone just like you to walk back in

songs.subject: R&B / growing up in a small town
songs.lyrics:
Baby you don't know what it means to grow up where the stoplight blinks
Where everybody knows your name before you even think
Where the Dairy Queen closes at nine and the fields go on forever
And loving you feels like that road, like it's gone on since whenever

I learned your laugh before I learned to drive
You were the reason I thought I'd survive
In a town with one grocery store and the same ten songs on the radio
You were the only frequency I'd ever need to know

We were seventeen and restless on the overpass
Throwing stones at nothing, making futures out of glass
Your mama knew my daddy and our teachers shared a wall
And I loved you in the in-between, I loved you through it all

This small town gave me something that the city never could
It gave me you on Tuesday nights beneath the neighborhood
It gave me porch light evenings and the smell of someone's yard
And growing up was easier because you weren't so far

I know every crack in every sidewalk on your block
I know the sound your screen door makes at seven o'clock
I know the way the water tower looks when storms are rolling in
And I know that I'd go back to nothing just to love you there again

We were seventeen and restless on the overpass
Throwing stones at nothing, making futures out of glass
Your mama knew my daddy and our teachers shared a wall
And I loved you in the in-between, I loved you through it all

People always leave these towns for somewhere that feels real
They chase the lights and lose the things they swear they'll always feel
But I keep coming back to you like summer coming back to June
Keep finding you in everything, you're every afternoon

In the parking lots of churches on a Wednesday evening late
In the high school gym that smells like 2003 and fate
In the faces of the people who look just like who we were
I find you in the small things, baby, that's where you occur

We were seventeen and restless on the overpass
Throwing stones at nothing, making futures out of glass
Your mama knew my daddy and our teachers shared a wall
And I loved you in the in-between, I loved you through it all

This small town gave me something that the city never could
It gave me you on Tuesday nights beneath the neighborhood
It gave me porch light evenings and the smell of someone's yard
And growing up was easier, growing up with you wasn't hard

Wasn't hard, no
Growing up wasn't hard when I had you
In that small town I had you

songs.subject: country / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
She said I love you but I don't love this life
Said she needed more than camping chairs and pocket knives
Left her key on the counter by the sink
Poured myself the last of what I had to drink

The truck needs tires and the rent is three weeks late
Got a dollar sixty-seven and a half a bag of bait
But the sun came up this morning orange and wide
And I'd be lying if I said I haven't smiled

Broke and breathing, still alive
Don't need much to feel like I can thrive
She took the future that I thought I had to keep
But she left me with the morning and the creek
Left me with the porch and the October breeze
Left me with exactly what I need
Yeah I'm broke but honey I am free

Drove out past the county line just to clear my head
Radio was playing every song she said she hated
Rolled the window down and let the cold air in
Found a twenty in my jacket, felt like winning

Stopped and bought a cup of coffee and a bag of chips
Watched the farmer in the field just doing what there is
And I thought about her leaving, thought about the hurt
Then I thought about the sunrise and I thought it first

Broke and breathing, still alive
Don't need much to feel like I can thrive
She took the future that I thought I had to keep
But she left me with the morning and the creek
Left me with the porch and the October breeze
Left me with exactly what I need
Yeah I'm broke but honey I am free

I know the bills are stacked and the phone keeps ringing off
I know my pride is scattered like the leaves the wind just caught
I know she found somebody better, good for her
But better don't always mean it's better for sure

There's something in the nothing that I'm learning how to feel
When you strip away the wanting, what you've got is what is real
And what I've got is timber and a fire in the pit
And two good dogs who love me and I'm grateful for it

Broke and breathing, still alive
Don't need much to feel like I can thrive
She took the future that I thought I had to keep
But she left me with the morning and the creek
Left me with the porch and the October breeze
Left me with exactly what I need

Yeah I'm broke but I'm standing in the sun
Yeah I'm broke but I ain't coming undone
Yeah she's gone but the living's still begun
Yeah I'm broke but honey I am free
Lord I'm broke but honey I am free

songs.subject: gospel / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
I've been pouring drinks since half past four
Watched the flowers come in through that old church door
Watched the groomsmen straighten up their ties
Watched the mothers trying not to cry
But when that music started from the back
I set my bottle down and I stepped back
Because something holy filled this banquet hall
And I was just a man behind a wall

This is God's own doing, this is God's own day
Every single detail was planned before they prayed
The way she walked, the way he broke apart
That was Jesus meeting Jesus in two hearts
I've seen a lot of parties in my time
Poured the liquor, kept the glasses lined
But I have never felt a room like this
Where heaven leaned down low enough to kiss

The pastor spoke about a covenant
About the kind of love that wasn't meant to dent
He talked about the vine, he talked about the grace
I watched that groom reach up and touch her face
And something in my chest I can't explain
Like something old in me got washed by rain
Like I'd been standing outside all my years
And somebody opened up the door right here

This is God's own doing, this is God's own day
Every single detail was planned before they prayed
The way she walked, the way he broke apart
That was Jesus meeting Jesus in two hearts
I've seen a lot of parties in my time
Poured the liquor, kept the glasses lined
But I have never felt a room like this
Where heaven leaned down low enough to kiss

The grandmother was holding someone's hand
The little flower girl didn't understand
But she stood still like even she could feel
That something in that moment was too real
And when they said their vows the whole room held
Its breath like it was waiting to be filled
And then they kissed and everybody rose
And glory broke out right between the rows

I poured the champagne for the first toast made
The father of the bride who stood and prayed
His voice broke twice, he had to stop and breathe
He said Lord thank you, that was all he'd need
And every person in that room said yes
And every person in that room was blessed
I raised my glass behind the bar alone
And thanked whatever brought me here today to witness what I've known

This is God's own doing, this is God's own day
Every single detail was planned before they prayed
The way she walked, the way he broke apart
That was Jesus meeting Jesus in two hearts
I've seen a lot of parties in my time
Poured the liquor, kept the glasses lined
But I have never felt a room like this
Where heaven leaned down low enough to kiss

Where heaven leaned right down and sealed it with a kiss
