songs.subject: hip-hop / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
Bags are heavy and my heart is heavier
Checking in at the counter, this goodbye getting scarier
You said you'd hold it together, now your eyes are glistening
I'm pretending that I'm okay but my hands are trembling

Departure board is flashing, gate number forty-two
Every minute on this clock is a minute without you
Security is calling and I gotta move along
Funny how a terminal can feel like somewhere wrong

We've been through the arguments, the laughter, and the years
Now I'm watching you dissolve behind a wall of tears
Told myself I wouldn't do this, told myself be strong
But the weight of this departure's been here all along

How do you say goodbye at an airport
When the words get lost in the crowd
How do you say goodbye at an airport
When everything you feel is too loud
I keep looking back at your face in the glass
Knowing this moment is moving too fast
How do you say goodbye at an airport
When you don't know how to let go

Pre-check lane is moving and I barely feel my feet
Every stranger here is rushing but I'm frozen in the heat
Got my boarding pass, my passport, got my carry-on bag
But the one thing I can't carry is the grief inside my chest

You texted me a heart, I sent you back the same
Distance hasn't started yet and I already feel the pain
Flight attendants don't know nothing about what's left behind
About the person standing curbside losing half their mind

I remember when we drove here, you were singing to the radio
Now the silence in my earbuds is the only thing I know
Terminal C is calling but I'm stuck at terminal us
Every intercom announcement turns my stomach into dust

How do you say goodbye at an airport
When the words get lost in the crowd
How do you say goodbye at an airport
When everything you feel is too loud
I keep looking back at your face in the glass
Knowing this moment is moving too fast
How do you say goodbye at an airport
When you don't know how to let go

Now I'm sitting at the gate and there's a woman next to me
She's crying on the phone and somehow that's the most I've felt seen
We are scattered broken people at the edge of takeoff now
Making promises to visit that we both know don't know how

Window seat, I always get the window seat for you
Said you liked imagining me watching skies of blue
Wheels up and the city is a memory below
The lights of everything I love are fading in the glow

I will call you when I land, I'll call you every night
I'll fall asleep on video and we'll make this feel right
But right now in this cabin thirty thousand feet above
The only thing I know for certain is I'm flying from my love

How do you say goodbye at an airport
When the words get lost in the crowd
How do you say goodbye at an airport
When everything you feel is too loud
I keep looking back at your face in the glass
Knowing this moment is moving too fast
How do you say goodbye at an airport
When you don't know how to let go

You don't know how to let go
You don't know how to let go
Standing in the terminal, I still don't know how to let go

songs.subject: dream pop / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
Somebody turned the lights down low
Lanterns hanging, music slow
I didn't know that I was lost
Until I saw you cross the room

Lipstick ring on a plastic cup
Someone laughing, volume up
The whole night felt like static noise
Until your eyes made everything bloom

And I don't know what this feeling is
Like standing at the edge of something big
Like the universe just rearranged itself
And left me here without a script
Everything is softer now
The ceiling and the crowd somehow
Are melting into something beautiful
And it's you, it's only you

You were standing by the window light
Hair like a halo, blurring bright
I thought about just walking past
But something pulled me like a tide

My friends were somewhere in the back
My phone was buzzing, I lost track
Of everything that usually matters
When I stepped to your side

And I don't know what this feeling is
Like standing at the edge of something big
Like the universe just rearranged itself
And left me here without a script
Everything is softer now
The ceiling and the crowd somehow
Are melting into something beautiful
And it's you, it's only you

We talked until the music changed three times
You quoted something, I forgot the line
But I laughed anyway because your voice
Was the only sound I wanted near

We found the back porch, sat on the steps
You said you never do stuff like this
I said I know because neither do I
But here we are and it feels clear

Like watercolor bleeding through the night
Like seventeen and terrified and right
Like every song I ever played alone
Was somehow practice for this moment here

And I don't know what this feeling is
Like standing at the edge of something big
Like the universe just rearranged itself
And left me here without a script
Everything is softer now
The ceiling and the crowd somehow
Are melting into something beautiful
And it's you, it's only you

My curfew came and went without a care
You tucked a strand behind your ear
And gave me your number on a folded piece
Of someone's homework from the hall

I walked home underneath the streetlamp glow
The summer air was warm and slow
And I kept reading those seven digits
Like they were written on my soul

And I don't know what this feeling is
Like standing at the edge of something big
Like the universe just rearranged itself
And left me here without a script
Everything is softer now
The ceiling and the crowd somehow
Are melting into something beautiful
And it's you, it's only you

It's you, it's only you
Drifting, drifting into something new
It's you, it's only you
The whole world soft and glowing through

1. A hip-hop song about saying goodbye at an airport.
2. A dream pop song about falling in love at a party. from a teenager's point of view
3. A bluegrass song about a long road trip. from someone in love
4. A trap song about your hometown. from someone in love

songs.subject: bluegrass / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:
Rolled the windows down in Tennessee
Sun was burning gold on you and me
You had your bare feet on the dashboard there
Wildflowers tangled up in your hair

Coffee going cold in the cup holder
Every mile makes me want to hold you closer
The map says we got hours left to go
But darling I would drive forever, don't you know

This road is long and winding, babe
Through mountains and the valleys we have made
Every yellow line beneath these wheels
Is another moment I know how I feel
I love you like the highway loves the sky
Stretching out forever, no goodbye
Keep on rolling through the fading afternoon
Just me and all these miles and you

Stopped for gas in some small Georgia town
Found a diner that was falling down
But the biscuits were like nothing we have known
And we danced a little to the jukebox all alone

You stole all the sugar packets from the table
Laughed so hard you almost lost your ladle
The waitress winked and said you two are something
I tipped her well and kissed you, said we're running

This road is long and winding, babe
Through mountains and the valleys we have made
Every yellow line beneath these wheels
Is another moment I know how I feel
I love you like the highway loves the sky
Stretching out forever, no goodbye
Keep on rolling through the fading afternoon
Just me and all these miles and you

Now the Appalachian ridgeline caught the sunset fire
You were singing something sweet and getting higher
Into harmonies you learned from your old grandma's porch
And I played along by ear just like a torch

Could've stayed in any city we had passed
Could've found a shortcut, could've driven fast
But you said let's take the long way, see the light
And everything about that felt exactly right

This road is long and winding, babe
Through mountains and the valleys we have made
Every yellow line beneath these wheels
Is another moment I know how I feel
I love you like the highway loves the sky
Stretching out forever, no goodbye
Keep on rolling through the fading afternoon
Just me and all these miles and you

Stars came out above the Carolina line
You fell asleep around a quarter to nine
Head against the window, breath fogging up the glass
I turned the music low and let the moment last

I thought about the town where I was raised
About the life I had before these days
About how nothing that I chased back then
Could've led me anywhere as good as this

Pulled into the driveway just past midnight there
Carried you inside without a care
You woke up long enough to say my name
And I knew I'd drive that road all over again

This road is long and winding, babe
Through mountains and the valleys we have made
Every yellow line beneath these wheels
Is another moment I know how I feel
I love you like the highway loves the sky
Stretching out forever, no goodbye
Keep on rolling through the fading afternoon
Just me and all these miles and you

All these miles and you
All these miles and you
Lord I'd drive ten thousand more with you

songs.subject: americana / faith
songs.lyrics:
Dust on the Bible, mud on my boots,
Walked a long way down to find these roots.
Mama lit a candle every Sunday night,
Said son, hold on, there's something worth the fight.

I've been down in the valley where the cold wind blows,
Where the river runs dry and the cotton won't grow.
But I keep my hands folded and my heart turned up,
Like an old tin cup waiting to be filled.

Grace is a slow road through the Georgia pines,
Faith is a lantern swinging down the mine.
I don't always see it but I feel it sure,
Like rain on the hardpan, soaking to the core.

My father never talked much but he worked the ground,
Every seed he planted was a holy sound.
He said the earth don't lie and the sky don't cheat,
And something bigger than us keeps the seasons neat.

Grace is a slow road through the Georgia pines,
Faith is a lantern swinging down the mine.
I don't always see it but I feel it sure,
Like rain on the hardpan, soaking to the core.

I've been angry, Lord, I won't pretend I haven't.
Shook my fist at storms and cursed the dark.
But every time I reached the end of reaching,
Something struck a match inside my heart.

There's a congregation meeting in the hollow,
Old hymns bleeding out through cedar walls.
I don't need the steeple or the silver collection,
Just the sound of something when the evening falls.

Grace is a slow road through the Georgia pines,
Faith is a lantern swinging down the mine.
I don't always see it but I feel it sure,
Like rain on the hardpan, soaking to the core.

So I'll walk this red clay until my legs give out,
Carry my believing like a hand-me-down coat.
Worn through at the elbows, soft around the collar,
Still keeping me warm when the cold nights close.

3. A ballad song about a childhood home.

songs.subject: rock / working a dead-end job
songs.lyrics:
Clock punched in at seven, punch out at five,
Fluorescent lights humming just to keep me alive.
Manager's got that look he saves for Mondays and rain,
I'm pushing the same broom down the same damn lane.

But I got your picture tucked inside my locker door,
Your laughing face that makes this worth it, makes me want more.
I'm grinding gears in neutral, spinning without wheels,
But loving you is the only thing I know that's real.

Dead-end job, dead-end street,
Dead-end everything except the way you make me feel.
I'm running low on reasons, running out of pride,
But I've got you burning like a fire on the other side.

Tony in the stockroom says he's been here twelve years,
Says it creeps up on you quiet like a slow disease.
I told him I ain't staying, I've got somewhere to be,
Got someone waiting at the door who actually sees me.

Dead-end job, dead-end street,
Dead-end everything except the way you make me feel.
I'm running low on reasons, running out of pride,
But I've got you burning like a fire on the other side.

Break room coffee tastes like cigarettes and regret,
I'm counting down the minutes on a busted up clock.
But at 5:01 I'm alive again, I'm running to my car,
And every light turns green between here and where you are.

They can have this uniform, they can have this name tag,
They can have the overtime that breaks your back.
I'm just a man in love with somewhere else to go,
And that makes the hours shorter than they know.

Dead-end job, dead-end street,
Dead-end everything except the way you make me feel.
I'm running low on reasons, running out of pride,
But I've got you burning like a fire on the other side.

So I'll show up tomorrow, I'll show up the day after,
Clock in with my head down, clock out with a smile.
Because loving you's the reason I can take this job for granted,
And hate it just enough to make the rest worthwhile.

Dead-end job, dead-end street,
Dead-end everything except the way you make me feel.
I'm not running low on nothing when I'm running home to you,
You're the only finish line I've ever wanted to.

songs.subject: ballad / a childhood home
songs.lyrics:
The porch light's still on in a photograph,
Mama in the kitchen, daddy's voice down the hall.
The oak tree in the yard with our heights in the bark,
Summer going on forever in the small.

I drove past it last September on my way to somewhere new,
Yellow curtains gone, a stranger's car out front.
The rosebush that she planted still was clinging to the brick,
Holding on like memory does when it must.

Take me back to that old house, let me stand inside the door,
Let me smell the wood and plaster, let me feel the uneven floor.
Let me hear my name called out from two rooms away,
Let me be that small again for one more day.

My sister carved her initials in the bathroom door,
We got in trouble then but now it feels like gold.
Every crack and corner had its own particular sound,
The way the third stair creaked when we were out past cold.

Christmas morning light through those east-facing windows,
Fell across the living room in gold and red.
Nothing since has felt so much like everything was possible,
And nothing permanent had broken yet.

Take me back to that old house, let me stand inside the door,
Let me smell the wood and plaster, let me feel the uneven floor.
Let me hear my name called out from two rooms away,
Let me be that small again for one more day.

You can't go back, I know, I've heard that all my life,
But knowing doesn't stop the ache from sitting in your chest.
The people who made it home are scattered now or gone,
And a house is just a building without the rest.

Still I keep that porch light burning somewhere in my sleep,
Still I wake sometimes and reach for that old ceiling overhead.
Still the smell of rain on that particular concrete drive,
Comes to me on certain days like something that's not dead.

Take me back to that old house, let me stand inside the door,
Let me smell the wood and plaster, let me feel the uneven floor.
Let me hear my name called out from two rooms away,
Let me be that small again for one more day.

Maybe home's not somewhere that exists beyond the mind,
Maybe it's the feeling pressed and kept inside the chest.
But I'd give nearly anything for one slow afternoon,
Back inside that ordinary, irreplaceable nest.

songs.subject: country / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Saw your name light up my screen at half past nine
Thought maybe you were calling just to say you're fine
But the words came rolling in like a winter storm
Said you found somebody else to keep you warm

I read it three times standing in the kitchen light
My coffee going cold just like our last goodnight
Seven years of Sundays fading into blue
All reduced to fourteen words from you

How do you say goodbye with your thumbs
How do you kill a love with a screen that goes numb
I keep staring at the period at the end of the line
Like a door swinging shut on everything that was mine
How do you say goodbye with your thumbs

I drove out to the county road we used to walk
Where the fireflies come up and we'd forget to talk
The fields were full of quiet and the sky was wide
And I talked to you like you were standing right beside

Told the moon about the way you laughed out loud
Told the wind about the dress you wore that made me proud
Said I hope he holds you better than I ever could
Said I hope this hurts him if he don't

How do you say goodbye with your thumbs
How do you kill a love with a screen that goes numb
I keep staring at the period at the end of the line
Like a door swinging shut on everything that was mine
How do you say goodbye with your thumbs

Mama says that time will do what time does best
Daddy says a man just puts it in his chest
My brother poured me whiskey till the bottle bled
But nothing takes away the words you said

Some loves go out in fire, some go out in rain
Mine went out in blue light on a window pane
I still got your contact saved, I don't know why
I just ain't ready yet to let the name go by

How do you say goodbye with your thumbs
How do you kill a love with a screen that goes numb
I keep staring at the period at the end of the line
Like a door swinging shut on everything that was mine
How do you say goodbye with your thumbs
How do you say goodbye with your thumbs

songs.subject: alt rock / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:
Six days out of Denver and my back's a wreck
Got a ring of ginger ale dried around my neck
Pulled the closing shift on Friday, hit the road by four
Left the keys behind the bar and locked the door

The highway's got a pulse when it's dark like this
Like something in the asphalt that you can't dismiss
I been pouring other people's troubles into glass
Now I'm watching every city in the mirror as I pass

Yellow lines and radio static
Somewhere past the mountains going automatic
Hands at ten and two on a wheel that pulls right
Just me and the diesel smoke and the interstate night
Yellow lines and radio static
My whole life's packed in boxes in the back of this thing
Heading toward whatever the next horizon brings
Yellow lines and radio static

Tucson in the morning smelled like rain and rust
A woman at the gas station was reading something just
She looked up like she recognized a face she'd never met
Two strangers in the nowhere that the maps forget

I used to think the regulars were keeping me alive
All their ordinary heartbreak helped the place survive
But you can't live off borrowed stories every night
Eventually you gotta find your own headlights

Yellow lines and radio static
Somewhere past the mountains going automatic
Hands at ten and two on a wheel that pulls right
Just me and the diesel smoke and the interstate night
Yellow lines and radio static
My whole life's packed in boxes in the back of this thing
Heading toward whatever the next horizon brings
Yellow lines and radio static

I've heard confessions at two AM over bourbon neat
I've mopped up every kind of sadness off the concrete
But out here there's nobody sliding down a stool
Nobody needs me to pretend that I am cool

The radio keeps cutting out past Lordsburg
And the silence fills the car up like a medicine
I never knew how loud my own thoughts get
When there's nothing left to drown them out with cigarettes

Outside of El Paso something cracked the sky
Like the universe was trying to say a slow goodbye
To everything I carried and was too tired to name
I pulled off at a rest stop in the dark and rain

Yellow lines and radio static
Somewhere past the mountains going automatic
Hands at ten and two on a wheel that pulls right
Just me and the diesel smoke and the interstate night
Yellow lines and radio static
My whole life's packed in boxes in the back of this thing
Heading toward whatever the next horizon brings
Yellow lines and radio static
Yellow lines
Yellow lines and radio static

songs.prompt:
Write 4 completely different full-length songs, one per request below. Each song must be complete, like a real radio song: at least three verses and a repeated chorus. For each, write only the song lyrics -- no titles, no section labels like Verse or Chorus, no commentary. Separate each song with a line that is exactly:
@@@NEXT-SONG@@@

1. A country song about a breakup text. from someone in love
2. A alt rock song about a long road trip. from a bartender's point of view
3. A americana song about starting over.
4. A emo song about a breakup text. from someone in love

songs.subject: americana / starting over
songs.lyrics:
Sold the house in October when the leaves came down
Packed a single suitcase and I drove out of town
Didn't tell a soul which road I meant to take
Just followed every river for the morning's sake

Found a room above a laundromat in some small place
Where nobody knew my history or face
The radiator knocked all night like it was trying to say
Something about the grace of an ordinary day

I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
With my two good hands and my half-broke heart
I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
Lord knows I'm getting better at the falling apart
I'm starting over

Walked into a diner, took a seat alone
Ordered eggs and coffee like a seed into loam
The waitress had a laugh that sounded like a bell
And I thought for the first time I might be doing well

Called my sister from a payphone just to hear her breathe
She said honey you don't have to go so far to leave
I said sister I'm not running from a single thing
I'm just finally walking toward something

I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
With my two good hands and my half-broke heart
I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
Lord knows I'm getting better at the falling apart
I'm starting over

There's a dignity in burning down the field
Something about the ash that makes the new crop real
I have buried all my versions in the ground before
But this time I am planting something worth growing for

The creek behind the laundromat runs cold and clear
Over rocks that have been patient for a thousand years
And I sat there til the sun fell in the pines
Learning how to read my own new lines

I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
With my two good hands and my half-broke heart
I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
Lord knows I'm getting better at the falling apart

People say that scars are just the skin that learned
I say sometimes you have to watch it all get burned
And stand there in the cinders with your boots undone
And turn your whole face upward to the early sun

I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
I'm starting over
I'm starting over

songs.subject: emo / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
I still have the notification glow burned in my eyes
Three forty seven AM and the end of my life
I thought I knew the shape of you by heart
But I didn't know a text could take a person apart

I read the first line four times til the letters bled
Rearranged the words to mean something else instead
But language doesn't care about the way that I feel
And every reread just made it more real

You sent me a paragraph and called it an end
Like I was a chapter and not everything
You hit send and probably went back to sleep
While I'm coming apart at the seams of me
You sent me a paragraph and called it an end
I'm still typing responses I'll never send
Watching the ellipsis that never arrives
You ended us in lowercase and it's eating me alive

My hands keep shaking like they're trying to find
Something to hold that isn't just inside my mind
The bedroom ceiling's doing nothing for the pain
And autocorrect keeps changing your name

I wrote you twelve replies and deleted every one
What do you say to someone who is already done
I put my phone face down and pressed it to the floor
Like maybe the carpet could absorb what I was feeling for

You sent me a paragraph and called it an end
Like I was a chapter and not everything
You hit send and probably went back to sleep
While I'm coming apart at the seams of me
You sent me a paragraph and called it an end
I'm still typing responses I'll never send
Watching the ellipsis that never arrives
You ended us in lowercase and it's eating me alive

There's something specifically awful about a screen
That makes the words look smaller than they mean
No voice to crack, no eyes to look away
Just fonts and punctuation where a person used to stay

I memorized the timestamp like a scar
Four minutes was how long it took to break my heart
Four minutes and you're probably dreaming now
And I'm learning how to breathe again somehow

And I know I shouldn't but I check again
And I know I shouldn't but I check again
To see if maybe you regret it in
The blue light of the morning creeping in

You sent me a paragraph and called it an end
Like I was a chapter and not everything
You hit send and probably went back to sleep
While I'm coming apart at the seams of me
You sent me a paragraph and called it an end
I'm still typing responses I'll never send
Watching the ellipsis that never arrives
You ended us in lowercase and it's eating me alive
You ended us in lowercase
And it's eating me alive

songs.subject: trap / the ocean
songs.lyrics:
Deep in the water where the currents run cold
Got waves on my wrist and the tide turning gold
They say the sea don't sleep and neither do I
Dripping saltwater diamonds under a midnight sky
Poseidon vibes got me moving in silence
Coral on my neck, yeah the ocean's my alliance
Hundred fathoms deep where the pressure apply
Only real ones swim here, let the weak ones die

Riding every wave like it's money incoming
Sharks in designer, yeah the predators coming
Stay dangerous moving through the aqua marine
Cleanest water flow but this lifestyle mean
Pull up on the shore with the Maybach gleaming
Mermaids on the back, hunnid thousands streaming
Ocean to the crib, yeah the coast is the life
Salt air on the bills, cut through struggle like a knife

Tide coming in, tide coming in, we counting again
Sharks don't stop swimming and neither do men
Blue water money got the currents on lock
Poseidon chains swinging, twelve on the clock
Tide coming in, tide coming in, we counting again
Sharks don't stop swimming and neither do men
Drown out the noise, let the deep water speak
Seven seas grinding every day of the week

Jellyfish whipping through the bioluminescence
My presence on these waters got a godly essence
Underwater empire built on hustle and pain
Anchored to nothing but the cash and the game
Nets full of profit, yeah we pulling it up
Octopus fingers in every pot and every cup
Deepsea operation, navigating the dark
You a little minnow, homie, I'm the great white shark

Submarine living where they cannot reach
No signal no service out beyond the beach
Counting out the blue faces, ocean blue hundreds
Blessed by the water while the broke world wonders
Neptune salute when I pull to the marina
Never seen a setup looking any cleaner
Saltwater in my veins, yeah the sea made me strong
Been swimming in these waters my whole entire life long

Tide coming in, tide coming in, we counting again
Sharks don't stop swimming and neither do men
Blue water money got the currents on lock
Poseidon chains swinging, twelve on the clock
Tide coming in, tide coming in, we counting again
Sharks don't stop swimming and neither do men
Drown out the noise, let the deep water speak
Seven seas grinding every day of the week

Pull up to the reef where the colors go crazy
Only God and the ocean got me, nothing else faze me
Tidal wave lifestyle, yeah we flooding the street
Anchor tatted deep, got the current on repeat
From the shoreline starving to the vessel paid
Everything I built came from every choice I made
Whirlpool spinning but I'm eye of the storm
Cold water raising me, this pressure the norm

Abyssal plain riches, yeah we found the bottom
Every doubt they gave me, I already forgot em
Swordfish slicing through every opposition
Deepsea grinder on a God-level mission
Waves don't stop crashing on the rock and the sand
Built an empire out here with these two bare hands
Ocean told me early, son the water don't lie
Swim harder than they sleeping, touch the limit of the sky

Tide coming in, tide coming in, we counting again
Sharks don't stop swimming and neither do men
Blue water money got the currents on lock
Poseidon chains swinging, twelve on the clock
Tide coming in, tide coming in, we counting again
Sharks don't stop swimming and neither do men
Drown out the noise, let the deep water speak
Seven seas grinding every day of the week

4. A soul song about summer ending. from a single mother's point of view

songs.subject: trap / chasing a dream
songs.lyrics:
Woke up at the bottom but I seen the top
Every single night I pray they cannot make me stop
Notebook full of visions that they called insane
Only thing between me and the win is all this pain
Mama said be careful what you wish upon
I wished upon a fire and I kept the flame on
Nineteen with a hunger and a broken phone
Betting on myself when I was doing this alone

They said the odds were impossible, the doors were sealed
Showed up to the table, yeah I forced them to deal
Every single setback was a setup for something
From nothing to the building, from the silence to something
Late nights in the basement with the ceiling low
Planting every seed that I was dying to grow
Laugh at me again when I'm accepting the award
Everything I chased, yeah I chased it for the Lord

I'm chasing the dream, they said let it go
I'm running to the top where only few men go
They tried to dim the light but they don't know the name
Keep chasing keep running keep burning the flame
I'm chasing the dream, they said let it go
I'm running to the top where only few men go
Nobody gave me nothing, had to take the game
Keep chasing keep running keep burning the flame

Signed the first check, never thought I'd see my name
On something that was built from my struggle and my pain
Two years of rejection, forty letters in the trash
Stayed up in the trenches, came out covered in cash
Every conversation where they counted me out
I took every doubt and turned it to a different route
They sleeping, I'm creeping past the gate they locked
Alarm going off and I still haven't stopped

Dream chasing is a religion, gotta study the word
Every blessing in my future gotta be deferred
Sacrifice the comfort, put the pleasure on pause
I'm building something bigger than their limited laws
Studio at midnight, writing pages til dawn
By the time they noticed I was already gone
Momentum is a monster and I feed it daily
They said I wouldn't make it, homie told them maybe

I'm chasing the dream, they said let it go
I'm running to the top where only few men go
They tried to dim the light but they don't know the name
Keep chasing keep running keep burning the flame
I'm chasing the dream, they said let it go
I'm running to the top where only few men go
Nobody gave me nothing, had to take the game
Keep chasing keep running keep burning the flame

Used to share a mattress on a cold linoleum floor
Promised if I made it I'd go back and give more
Five years of grinding in a city that's cold
Every story worth telling gotta first be lived bold
Vision board collecting every picture and clipping
Now the same ones doubting me been steady lip-reading
Reading off my headlines in the morning with their coffee
Years ago these same ones tried to talk me off me

I remember every name and every closed door
Every time they told me not to come back anymore
Every year of hunger made the victory sweeter
Every extra mile made me so much more of a believer
Dream don't die unless you let it lay down
I had mine in a chokehold, never let it drown
Built it out of silence and a will to survive
Feel the dream inside me every day I'm alive

I'm chasing the dream, they said let it go
I'm running to the top where only few men go
They tried to dim the light but they don't know the name
Keep chasing keep running keep burning the flame
I'm chasing the dream, they said let it go
I'm running to the top where only few men go
Nobody gave me nothing, had to take the game
Keep chasing keep running keep burning the flame

songs.subject: hip-hop / the ocean
songs.lyrics:
Another Friday night and the bar is full
Got the mahogany counter and the overhead pull
Regulars ordering the usual, couples on dates
Meanwhile in my mind all I see is deep blue straights
I been reading about the Pacific on my breaks
Got a picture on my phone of the waves and the wakes
Pouring out the Hennessy and wiping down wood
Dreaming about the ocean like I always knew I should

Two-top wants a martini, five-top wants a round
Boss is on the floor making sure nobody drowns
Tips are decent, conversation's good tonight
Old man Phil talks to me about the fishing life
Says the sea will clean you out from somewhere inside
Says the water takes your problems on a long long ride
I laugh and pour his bourbon and I think to myself
There's a coast line calling me beyond this shelf

I can hear the ocean when the bar goes quiet
I can feel the saltwater in the middle of the riot
Glasses clinking, blender running, orders stacking high
But somewhere in between the pours I watch the water in my mind
I can hear the ocean when the bar goes quiet
I can feel the saltwater in the middle of the riot
Every shift I'm working, every night I stand here breathing
Part of me is already standing on the beach leaving

Last call crowd is loud, somebody spilled a drink
Mopping up the mess and trying hard not to think
About the fact that it's been seven years behind this bar
And the ocean keeps on pulling like a fixed and famous star
I grew up in Ohio where there was no shore
Saw the Gulf of Mexico when I was twenty-four
Something split inside me when I touched that water line
Like the sea had written something deep inside my spine

Closing time I count the drawer and cash out the till
Outside the city's restless but the night is still
Walk to my apartment through the yellow streetlight haze
Close my eyes and picture undulating waves
Swear I smell the brine above the exhaust and the street
Swear I feel the cool wet sand beneath my feet
Every glass of water that I pour across this bar
Takes me to the coastline where my true intentions are

I can hear the ocean when the bar goes quiet
I can feel the saltwater in the middle of the riot
Glasses clinking, blender running, orders stacking high
But somewhere in between the pours I watch the water in my mind
I can hear the ocean when the bar goes quiet
I can feel the saltwater in the middle of the riot
Every shift I'm working, every night I stand here breathing
Part of me is already standing on the beach leaving

Carolina coast line's got my name on a reservation
Saved enough to get there, that's my whole motivation
Every tip and every shift and every weekend double
Every sore back morning, every little bit of trouble
Taking me to somewhere with a different kind of pour
Trading in this whiskey for the sound of the shore
Gonna find a bar out there where the windows face the sea
Gonna mix my drinks in view of what was always calling me

Customer asks for water, I pour it clear and cold
Watch it catch the light and something old and bold
Runs right through my ribcage like a current strong and deep
This feeling that the ocean is a promise I should keep
Someday soon the last shift ends and I untie this apron
Someday soon the ocean and the bartender aren't strangers
Every night I work I'm one night closer to the tide
One long drink of saltwater away from something right

I can hear the ocean when the bar goes quiet
I can feel the saltwater in the middle of the riot

But somewhere in between the pours I watch the water in my mind
I can hear the ocean when the bar goes quiet
I can feel the saltwater in the middle of the riot
Every shift I'm working, every night I stand here breathing
Part of me is already standing on the beach leaving

Meanwhile in my mind all I see is deep blue straights
I been reading about the Pacific on my breaks

Write 4 completely different full-length songs, one per request below. Each song must be complete, like a real radio song: at least three verses and a repeated chorus. For each, write only the song lyrics -- no titles, no section labels like Verse or Chorus, no commentary. Separate each song with a line that is exactly:
@@@NEXT-SONG@@@

1. A trap song about the ocean.
2. A trap song about chasing a dream.
3. A hip-hop song about the ocean. from a bartender's point of view
4. A soul song about summer ending. from a single mother's point of view

songs.subject: soul / summer ending
songs.lyrics:
Backpacks sitting by the door again
New shoes bought with money I had to pretend
I had extra somewhere in between the bills
Summer's almost over and I feel the chills
Not from cold air, more from something true
Another season closing, baby, me and you
Stood out in the yard and watched the fireflies fade
You asked me mama why do good things go away

We made the most of everything this summer gave
Dollar store umbrellas when the weather misbehaved
Ice cream on a Tuesday just because we could
Felt like we were wealthy in a different neighborhood
You slept on the back porch underneath the stars
I watched you from the window counting Saturn and Mars
My little one, you never knew how tight things were
You only saw the magic that I tried hard to stir

Summer always leaves me, like everything I love
But what we built together, nothing's made of what we're made of
I hold you a little tighter when September's in the air
Summer always leaves me but you are always there
I watch the garden wilting where we planted seeds in May
Summer always leaves me but you never go away
You never go away

School clothes on the hanger, list of supplies made
Feeling the weight of August as the light begins to fade
Called my mother crying on a late Tuesday night
She said baby you're doing better than you think you might
Hard to believe her when the heat bill's due again
Hard to believe it when you're stretching to the end
But then you run inside and hug me with those little arms
And every worry quiets, every bell and every alarm

We found a farmers market on the last weekend of July
You picked the yellow sunflowers and you held them to the sky
Said mama these are almost tall as you and laughed
And Lord I nearly broke right down in half
Took your picture by the flowers while the world moved around us
Held it in my chest like something precious finally found us
These are the things they never tell you motherhood contains
The way a child's laughter is the only thing that sustains

Summer always leaves me, like everything I love
But what we built together, nothing's made of what we're made of
I hold you a little tighter when September's in the air
Summer always leaves me but you are always there
I watch the garden wilting where we planted seeds in May
Summer always leaves me but you never go away
You never go away

Single and I'm tired in a way words barely cover
But I would do it all again to be this child's mother
Every sleepless morning, every doubled shift and sacrifice
Every year of figuring out how to make the hard times nice
You don't see the worry that I paste behind my smile
You just see your mama making every single trial
Look like an adventure, look like something we can do
Everything I gave up in my life I gave for you

Evening air is cooler now and school starts Monday morning
Sitting on the porch step, getting one last summer warning
Sun is going amber and it's pulling at my heart
Something always aches in me when seasons fall apart
Feels like more than weather, feels like time itself
Feels like all the summers stacked like books on a shelf
You're a year more older, I'm a year more tired
But God I am so grateful, God I feel inspired

Look at what we carried, look at where we stand
You with your little drawings and your little hand
Reaching up to mine when the evening starts to cool
Baby, you're my summer even on the first day of school
Even when December comes and wraps the world in grey
Even when the flowers all have finally gone away
You are what the summer left behind inside of me
You are the warm thing that no season's taking from me

Summer always leaves me, like everything I love
But what we built together, nothing's made of what we're made of
I hold you a little tighter when September's in the air
Summer always leaves me but you are always there
I watch the garden wilting where we planted seeds in May
Summer always leaves me but you never go away
You never go away
You never go away
Baby you never go away

songs.subject: pop / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning, twenty dollars to my name
Checked my bank account and laughed instead of going insane
Ramen on the stove and a candle burning low
But I got a heart full of something that the rich folks'll never know

I danced around my kitchen in my mismatched socks
Sang into a wooden spoon, forgot about the clock
My friends came over Friday with a bottle of cheap wine
And we talked until the sunrise and everything was fine

Yeah I'm running on empty but I'm running just the same
Got nothing in my wallet but I swear I can't complain
This is my beautiful disaster, my glorious broke-down life
I am richer than I realize when the stars come out at night
So here I am, so here I am
Poor as a penny but I'm laughing while I can
So here I am, so here I am
Writing it all down so I remember who I am

Tuesday I ate cereal for dinner, Tuesday I ate cereal for lunch
I found a five dollar bill inside my jacket and I felt like I'd won a million bucks
Walked to the park because I couldn't afford the bus
Sat on a bench and watched the pigeons making all their fuss

The sun was doing something holy through the city trees
And some old man was feeding birds with absolute pure peace
And I thought about my mother saying baby you'll be fine
And for the first time in a long time I believed her one more time

Yeah I'm running on empty but I'm running just the same
Got nothing in my wallet but I swear I can't complain
This is my beautiful disaster, my glorious broke-down life
I am richer than I realize when the stars come out at night
So here I am, so here I am
Poor as a penny but I'm laughing while I can
So here I am, so here I am
Writing it all down so I remember who I am

Maybe someday I'll have more than I need
A savings account and a matching set of sheets
But right now I got the window and the morning and the breeze
And a notebook full of memories that nobody can repossess from me

So I write it down, I write it down
Every single golden ordinary thing I've found
Write it down, I write it down
So when I have more later I remember being proud

Yeah I'm running on empty but I'm running just the same
Got nothing in my wallet but I swear I can't complain

1. A pop song about being broke but happy. like a diary entry
2. A indie folk song about an ex you still think about. from a bartender's point of view
3. A americana song about driving with the windows down. from someone who just got dumped
4. A dream pop song about a breakup text. from an old man's point of view

songs.subject: indie folk / an ex you still think about
songs.lyrics:
Last call came and went about an hour ago
I'm wiping down the bar top in the amber afterglow
The chairs are upside down now on the tables all in rows
And I pour myself a small one before heading home alone

You used to sit on stool number four every Thursday night
You'd nurse a whiskey sour and you'd read by the low light
You never really talked much but you always tipped me well
And somewhere in between those things I fell beneath your spell

I still cut a lemon wedge at half past nine
Some muscle memory of yours that got tangled up in mine
I still reach for the bottle that you always used to order
And I catch myself and stand there like a fool behind the counter
You're in the walls of this place now, in the wood grain and the smell
Just a ghost behind the bar top in the stories I don't tell

Spring came and you were different, you had someone on your arm
She laughed too loud at everything and meant you no real harm
I made your drink without you asking, set it down without a word
You looked at me a second longer than you should have, or I heard

The whole bar in a whisper that neither of us spoke
And then the jukebox started up and swallowed it in smoke
Some old Fleetwood Mac song rolling soft across the room
And you turned back to her laughing and I turned back to the gloom

I still cut a lemon wedge at half past nine
Some muscle memory of yours that got tangled up in mine
I still reach for the bottle that you always used to order
And I catch myself and stand there like a fool behind the counter
You're in the walls of this place now, in the wood grain and the smell
Just a ghost behind the bar top in the stories I don't tell

I've seen a hundred love affairs begin and fall apart
From this side of the mahogany I've watched them break their hearts
There's something about standing still while everyone else moves
That carves you out a little bit, gives loneliness its grooves

And maybe that's the bargain that you make inside this trade
You hold the space for everyone and hope your own ghosts fade
But yours won't seem to go just yet, they linger in the grain
I see you every Thursday in whoever takes your place

I still cut a lemon wedge at half past nine
Some muscle memory of yours that got tangled up in mine
I still reach for the bottle that you always used to order
And I catch myself and stand there like a fool behind the counter
You're in the walls of this place now, in the wood grain and the smell
Just a ghost behind the bar top in the stories I don't tell
Just a ghost behind the bar top in the stories I don't tell

songs.subject: americana / driving with the windows down
songs.lyrics:
She handed back the key ring in the driveway after dawn
Said she needed something more and then she said that she was gone
I didn't cry until she turned the corner out of sight
And then I got into my truck and drove into the light

Rolled all four windows down and let the June air hit my face
Didn't have a destination just needed open space
The radio was playing something I didn't recognize
Some song about forever and I laughed until I cried

There's something about moving when the world has gone still
Something about the pavement and the green of the hills
The wind don't ask no questions and the road don't keep score
I'm just a man with a tank of gas and a heart that's a little sore
Windows down, wheels turning on this long blacktop
Singing to nobody, ain't ready to stop
Somewhere between the leaving and wherever I land
I'm trying to remember who I am

I passed through Millbrook County where we used to get lost
Found that little diner where we never talked enough
Kept on driving past it, couldn't slow my speed
Some doors are better left the size they are in memory

The fields were gold and endless in that late morning sun
The kind of beautiful that makes you ache for everyone
For every person driving with their windows down today
Trying to put some distance between themselves and yesterday

There's something about moving when the world has gone still
Something about the pavement and the green of the hills
The wind don't ask no questions and the road don't keep score
I'm just a man with a tank of gas and a heart that's a little sore
Windows down, wheels turning on this long blacktop
Singing to nobody, ain't ready to stop
Somewhere between the leaving and wherever I land
I'm trying to remember who I am

I pulled over somewhere south of the state line on a hill
Watched the hawks ride the thermals in that blue country still
And I thought about the version of myself from years ago
Who drove these same back highways letting everything go

Maybe losing's just the price you pay for having loved at all
Maybe every open road begins with someone's fall
I started up the engine and I pointed myself west
Sometimes moving forward's all you've got, so you give it your best

There's something about moving when the world has gone still
Something about the pavement and the green of the hills
The wind don't ask no questions and the road don't keep score
I'm just a man with a tank of gas and a heart that's a little sore
Windows down, wheels turning on this long blacktop
Singing to nobody, ain't ready to stop
Somewhere between the leaving and wherever I land
I'm trying to remember who I am
Yeah somewhere between the leaving and wherever I land
I'm gonna remember who I am

Windows down, wheels turning on this long blacktop
Singing to nobody, ain't ready to stop
Somewhere between the leaving and wherever I land
I'm trying to remember who I am

I pulled over somewhere south of the state line on a hill
Watched the hawks ride the thermals in that blue country still
And I thought about the version of myself from years ago
Who drove these same back highways letting everything go

Maybe losing's just the price you pay for having loved at all
Maybe every open road begins with someone's fall
I started up the engine and I pointed myself west
Sometimes moving forward's all you've got, so you give it your best

There's something about moving when the world has gone still
Something about the pavement and the green of the hills
The wind don't ask no questions and the road don't keep score

songs.subject: dream pop / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
The phone lit up the bedroom like a small cold moon
Three sentences that ended sixty years too soon
My reading glasses on the nightstand, hand a little slow
I read the words three times before I let the meaning go

The children had been grown so long the house had learned the quiet
The garden knew my footsteps and the kettle knew my riot
Seventy-one years of mornings and I thought that I knew grief
But nothing quite prepares you for a message on a screen

Blue light, blue words, blue everything
The whole world went to watercolor, lost its edges in between
Is this how it ends now, in this glowing little box
A love song typed in lowercase without a period at the stop
Blue light, blue words, blue everything
I held the phone against my chest and listened to it ring
Inside my ribs where something warm had lived for all these years
Blue light, blue words, and the old man disappears

I remember telephones attached to kitchen walls
And letters with the cursive and the perfume in the folds
A certain kind of ending had a weight that you could hold
Something you could fold back up and put away and know

But this just stays there glowing patient as the dawn
Doesn't age or yellow, never fades or moves along
I could scroll back up through years of her good morning texts
And find the exact moment that the warmth became the past

Blue light, blue words, blue everything
The whole world went to watercolor, lost its edges in between
Is this how it ends now, in this glowing little box
A love song typed in lowercase without a period at the stop
Blue light, blue words, blue everything
I held the phone against my chest and listened to it ring
Inside my ribs where something warm had lived for all these years
Blue light, blue words, and the old man disappears

My grandson says that everything is different now
That love lives in the current and you can't slow it down
Maybe he is right, maybe I am just old stone
But I was built for something slower, something you could hold

I went out to the garden in the early morning grey
Sat among the things that we had planted in our better days
The roses didn't know yet, still performing for the bees
And I thought maybe beauty doesn't need you to believe

Blue light, blue words, blue everything
The whole world went to watercolor, lost its edges in between
Is this how it ends now, in this glowing little box
A love song typed in lowercase without a period at the stop

songs.subject: reggae / a friend who changed
songs.lyrics:
You used to walk beside me on the morning road
Eyes like sunrise, heart was pure as gold
We shared the same old bench beneath the mango tree
Never thought the tide would wash away what used to be

You used to hum the melody the river sang
You used to be the first one dancing when the steel drum rang
But something in the season turned your eyes away
And the one I knew just slowly slipped into the grey

Where did you go, where did you go
I see your face but I don't see your soul
The love you carried melted in the sun
And now I'm standing here wondering what you've become
Where did you go, where did you go
The roots are still here but the flowers won't grow
You traded in the warmth for something cold
Tell me where did you go

I remember when she used to say my name
Like it was something precious, never just the same
She'd wrap her fingers round my hand and hold on tight
We'd watch the stars burn steady through the Caribbean night

Now her words are strangers wearing borrowed clothes
She looks right through me like the wind that comes and goes
I've been burning incense trying to call her home
But the smoke just rises up and leaves me all alone

Where did you go, where did you go
I see your face but I don't see your soul
The love you carried melted in the sun
And now I'm standing here wondering what you've become
Where did you go, where did you go
The roots are still here but the flowers won't grow
You traded in the warmth for something cold
Tell me where did you go

Maybe love is just a candle in the rain
Maybe holding on too tight is half the pain
I'll keep the memory of who you used to be
And pray the current brings you back to me

I light a fire on the hillside every night
Hoping you'll remember what it felt like to feel right
The drumbeat still is calling through the palms
And I'm leaving all the doors open just in case you come along

Where did you go, where did you go
I see your face but I don't see your soul
The love you carried melted in the sun
And now I'm standing here wondering what you've become
Where did you go, where did you go
The roots are still here but the flowers won't grow
You traded in the warmth for something cold
Tell me where did you go

Tell me where did you go
Tell me where did you go

songs.subject: indie folk / heartbreak
songs.lyrics:
The kitchen light is still the same pale yellow
And your coat still hangs beside the door
I keep convincing myself I'm getting better
Then I find your handwriting in a drawer

Outside the leaves are doing what they always do
Burning gold and letting go without a sound
And I'm sitting here on the floor beside the window
Trying to learn what they know, trying not to drown

Oh I loved you like a house loves all its people
Like the walls just hold the warmth without complaint
And when you left the rooms all changed their meaning
And the rain came in and nothing looked the same

I drove out past the lake where we spent August
The water had that silver look it gets in fall
I thought I'd feel something final there, some closing
But grief doesn't care about the beautiful at all

I bought the bread you liked on a Tuesday
Stood there in the aisle just holding it like a fool
The smallest ordinary things are the sharpest
I'm learning that the hard way, I'm learning like a bruise

Oh I loved you like a house loves all its people
Like the walls just hold the warmth without complaint
And when you left the rooms all changed their meaning
And the rain came in and nothing looked the same

There are people who keep saying give it time now
Like time is something gentle that it gives back what it takes
And maybe they are right and I'll stop reaching
For the shape of you beside me when I wake

But tonight I'm just a body in a dark room
With a playlist that we made in better days
And I'm singing every word just like a prayer now
Asking nothing, just refusing to erase

Oh I loved you like a house loves all its people
Like the walls just hold the warmth without complaint
And when you left the rooms all changed their meaning
And the rain came in and nothing looked the same

Oh the rain came in and nothing looked the same
And I loved you all the same
I loved you all the same

songs.subject: hip-hop / starting over
songs.lyrics:
Dear journal, it's a Tuesday in October
I got the last of my boxes from the old place today
Sat in the parking lot for a while just breathing
Told myself if I cried it would be okay

Thirty-two years old and starting from a zero
That's what it feels like even though I know it's not true
Got a mattress on the floor and a window facing eastward
First time in a long time the morning feels brand new

I wrote my name on the wall behind the bookshelf
Nobody'll see it but I'll know that it is there
Just a little proof that I exist in this space now
Just a quiet claim that says I'm going somewhere

I'm turning the page, I'm writing the first line
Not the ending that I feared but the beginning I designed
I've been burned down to the ground, I've been lower than low
But I found the version of myself I forgot I know
I'm starting over, I'm starting over
Not running from the past, I'm walking toward the open
Every scar is just a map of where I've been
And I'm using it to find out where I'm going

Mama called me when I pulled up with the U-Haul
Said she always knew that I would find my feet
I didn't tell her 'bout the nights I spent not sleeping
Or the version of myself I had to finally beat

I used to let people take up all the volume
Till I couldn't even hear my own voice in the room
Spent so long being quiet, being smaller, being useful
Forgot I had a melody, forgot I had a tune

So I'm sitting on the floor eating Chinese from the container
Writing down the things I want to feel in this new life
Not the things I'm running from or the ghosts that used to haunt me
Just the simple ones, like stillness, peace, and time

I'm turning the page, I'm writing the first line
Not the ending that I feared but the beginning I designed
I've been burned down to the ground, I've been lower than low
But I found the version of myself I forgot I know
I'm starting over, I'm starting over
Not running from the past, I'm walking toward the open
Every scar is just a map of where I've been
And I'm using it to find out where I'm going

Yeah I know it won't be perfect, I know the road gets winding
I know some nights will still find me back in that familiar dark
But I got this empty apartment and this pen and this intention
And for the first time in forever I believe in my own heart

They said I'd never leave, they said I was too broken
They said a lot of things I kept as gospel for too long
But look at me, four walls, my name behind the bookshelf
Thirty-two and finally learning how to be strong

I'm turning the page, I'm writing the first line
Not the ending that I feared but the beginning I designed
I've been burned down to the ground, I've been lower than low
But I found the version of myself I forgot I know
I'm starting over, I'm starting over
Not running from the past, I'm walking toward the open
Every scar is just a map of where I've been
And I'm using it to find out where I'm going

Starting over, starting over
Starting over

songs.subject: soul / a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The church is full of flowers that she never would have chosen
She always said carnations were the saddest kind of bloom
But we filled the whole front altar just because we didn't know her
Not the quiet way she laughed or what she hummed around the room

There's a man in the third pew who is staring at the program
Like if he reads the dates again they'll rearrange themselves to more
And her sister keeps on standing up and sitting down and standing
Like her body can't decide what stillness is even for

So we lay you down easy, we lay you down slow
We're not ready but we're here because we love you and we know
That the space you leave behind you is the shape of something holy
And the grief we carry with us is the weight of being known
We lay you down easy, we lay you down with grace
With the sunlight through the windows falling soft across your face

The reverend says a passage about rivers finding oceans
And I watch the old men nodding like they've heard it all their lives
There's a little girl asleep across her grandmother's lap now
She's too young to understand but she will carry this for life

Somebody brought a photograph they taped beside the casket
From a summer in the mountains, must have been the '89
She is squinting at the camera with her hand above her forehead
And she's laughing at whoever held the other end of time

So we lay you down easy, we lay you down slow
We're not ready but we're here because we love you and we know
That the space you leave behind you is the shape of something holy
And the grief we carry with us is the weight of being known
We lay you down easy, we lay you down with grace
With the sunlight through the windows falling soft across your face

There is no good word for this, no frame that holds it cleanly
No theology that fills the hollow in the chest
We just sit inside the silence and we let it be enormous
And we hold the ones beside us and we try to do the rest

After all the words are spoken and the last car leaves the gravel
There'll be casseroles on counters and a house that echoes loud
And somebody will have to learn to sleep without that breathing
And somebody will look for her in every passing crowd

But tonight we are together and the candles are still burning
And her name is in our mouths like something we refuse to lose
And the music that is playing was the music that she danced to
And we're dancing now ourselves because we don't know what else to do

So we lay you down easy, we lay you down slow
We're not ready but we're here because we love you and we know
That the space you leave behind you is the shape of something holy
And the grief we carry with us is the weight of being known
We lay you down easy, we lay you down with grace
With the sunlight through the windows falling soft across your face

Lay you down easy
Lay you down
We lay you down

songs.subject: pop / growing up in a small town
songs.lyrics:
Scuffed knees on the courthouse steps
Riding bikes till the streetlights came on
Mama called us in for dinner
Now half those kids I grew up with are gone
The water tower still has our names
Spray painted sometime in oh-four
I swear this town gets smaller every summer
But somehow I keep coming back for more

I know every crack in every sidewalk
Every shortcut through the woods behind the school
I know the faces at the Friday night game
I know the gossip and I know the rules
This is where I learned to break my own heart
This is where I first learned how to drive
Diary pages filled with nothing and everything
This is where I first learned to be alive

Saturday nights at the Dairy Freeze
Everybody circling the lot
Dreaming out loud about leaving
Funny how we never actually got
Far enough away to stop missing it
Far enough to not hear those church bells ring
I wrote it all down in a spiral notebook
Every ordinary extraordinary thing

I know every crack in every sidewalk
Every shortcut through the woods behind the school
I know the faces at the Friday night game
I know the gossip and I know the rules
This is where I learned to break my own heart
This is where I first learned how to drive
Diary pages filled with nothing and everything
This is where I first learned to be alive

Now I'm older and I understand
What all that slowness was actually for
Learning how to sit inside a moment
Before the whole world opened up its doors
I keep a photograph beside my bed
The whole grade standing in the summer heat
Small town girl with her whole life ahead
Looking scared and looking incomplete

I know every crack in every sidewalk
Every shortcut through the woods behind the school
I know the faces at the Friday night game
I know the gossip and I know the rules
This is where I learned to break my own heart
This is where I first learned how to drive
Diary pages filled with nothing and everything
This is where I first learned to be alive

Riding bikes till the streetlights came on
Mama called us in for dinner
Now half those kids I grew up with are gone
The water tower still has our names
Spray painted sometime in oh-four
I swear this town gets smaller every summer
But somehow I keep coming back for more

I know every crack in every sidewalk

I know the faces at the Friday night game
I know the gossip and I know the rules
This is where I learned to break my own heart
This is where I first learned how to drive
Diary pages filled with nothing and everything
This is where I first learned to be alive

Everybody circling the lot
Dreaming out loud about leaving
Funny how we never actually got
Far enough away to stop missing it
Far enough to not hear those church bells ring
I wrote it all down in a spiral notebook
Every ordinary extraordinary thing

songs.subject: indie folk / moving to a new city
songs.lyrics:
I packed the car on a Thursday morning
Your handwriting still on the moving boxes
You helped me label every single one of them
Even the ones filled with clocks and locks and
All the things I couldn't throw away
All the things that smell like your perfume
You stood in the driveway waving slowly
And I drove away from everything I knew

New city lights don't know my name yet
Strange streets that don't know where I've been
I'm learning all the coffee shops and corners
But everything reminds me of your skin
I hang our photo on the plaster wall
First thing I do before I unpack plates
The city hums its unfamiliar music
And I love you across four hundred miles of interstate

The landlord showed me how the heating works
The neighbors play guitar on Sunday nights
I found a little market down on Delaney
Where the peaches taste almost right
Almost like the ones from your back garden
Almost like that August afternoon
I'm building something new with both my hands
But every room I build has space for you

New city lights don't know my name yet
Strange streets that don't know where I've been
I'm learning all the coffee shops and corners

I say there's pigeons on a fire escape
And somewhere somebody's cooking rosemary
And you say that sounds like a painting
And I say I wish that you were in the frame
You say I'll come when the leaves are changing
I say I'm counting every day until you came

songs.subject: alt rock / driving with the windows down
songs.lyrics:
Engine turning over in the parking lot
Asphalt shimmering and the radio's hot
Got nowhere particular to be right now
Just this road and this sound and this feeling somehow
Roll the windows down let the whole thing in
Sun on your arm and the noise of the wind
Speakers rattling on the lower end
This is how you lose yourself to find yourself again

Every yellow line is a metronome
Every overpass is the long way home
Singing wrong words loud enough to shake the rearview glass
Going nowhere fast and making it last
Going nowhere fast and making it last

Eighteen wheeler casting a long shadow
Somebody's lawn chair sitting in a fallow
Field beside a house that might be abandoned
Everything out here is something you can't plan on
Construction zone at the edge of the county
Traffic cones and workers and the sky is cloudy
But the clouds are moving and the light comes through
In that way that makes everything feel brand new

Every yellow line is a metronome
Every overpass is the long way home
Singing wrong words loud enough to shake the rearview glass
Going nowhere fast and making it last
Going nowhere fast and making it last

There's something about momentum that untangles
All the knots you tied up from every angle
Every problem sitting on your kitchen table
Starts to feel like something you'll be able
To eventually pick apart and handle
When you're doing ninety past a roadside candle
Memorial wrapped in plastic flowers fading
Reminds you what you've got while you're still made of
Something burning something worth protecting
Something moving forward and correcting

Every yellow line is a metronome
Every overpass is the long way home
Singing wrong words loud enough to shake the rearview glass
Going nowhere fast and making it last

Every exit sign is a life you didn't take
Every open window is a promise you can make

songs.subject: country / a first apartment
songs.lyrics:
Sixty dollars in my checking account
Box spring on the floor of room two fourteen
Your name written on the lease beside my name
First real thing we ever built between
These two people who had nothing figured out
Just a set of keys and a blow up chair
Chinese takeout on the kitchen linoleum
Baby I was rich beyond compare

That apartment with the drafty windows
Where we huddled up in January cold
Where the faucet dripped a steady lullaby
And the carpet had a story to be told
I'd give anything to go back to nothing
Back to everything we were right there
Two kids splitting one electric bill
Baby I was rich beyond compare

We borrowed plates from both our mothers
Hung one picture and called it decoration
You made curtains out of an old bedsheet
I called that love without hesitation
Neighbors fought on Tuesday nights like clockwork
Dog upstairs would pace until we heard the floor
I learned the whole geography of you
In those two hundred and forty square feet and nothing more

That apartment with the drafty windows
Where we huddled up in January cold
Where the faucet dripped a steady lullabi
And the carpet had a story to be told
I'd give anything to go back to nothing
Back to everything we were right there
Two kids splitting one electric bill
Baby I was rich beyond compare

Now we got the house and we got the yard
We got a bed that sits up off the ground
And I love this life we've been building slowly
But sometimes I need to go back to that sound
Of rain on a single-pane window
Of your laugh echoing off walls thin as paper
Of two people who had nothing left to lose
Loving each other harder for it later

That apartment with the drafty windows
Where we huddled up in January cold
Where the faucet dripped a steady lullaby
And the carpet had a story to be told
I'd give anything to go back to nothing
Back to everything we were right there
Two kids splitting one electric bill

Baby I was rich
Lord I was so rich
Baby I was rich beyond compare

songs.subject: soul / summer ending
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning and the air felt thin
Like the season's already packing up to leave again
The pool's still full but nobody's swimming
The sun clocks out a little early every evening

I wrote it down so I wouldn't forget
The way July felt like we had no regrets
Now the kids next door are shopping for new shoes
And I'm sitting on the porch with these late August blues

I watched the fireflies give up on dancing
And the backyard barbecue smoke stopped romancing the sky
The radio's playing all the wrong songs for this feeling
Like summer handed me a note and said goodbye

You know I never loved a thing the way I love this heat
The way it makes you lazy, makes the days incomplete
In the best way, in the slow way
The kind of days you only see when you're not looking

And I'm not ready, Lord I'm not ready
I wrote that in the margins of a Tuesday in my mind
Not ready, no I'm not ready
To watch another summer leave me behind

The garden's turning colors it didn't ask for
The tomatoes got heavy and then fell to the floor
I canned the last of everything worth saving
And grieved a little over all the rest

I remember June like a long deep breath
When everything was possible and nothing faced its death
Not even the roses, not even the late nights
Not even the version of me who still believed in forever

But here I am again with this familiar ache
Counting all the promises the cold is gonna take
The hammock's still up but the rope's getting tired
Just like me, just like this, just like everything I've admired

And I'm not ready, Lord I'm not ready
I wrote that in the margins of a Tuesday in my mind
Not ready, no I'm not ready
To watch another summer leave me behind

So I'll keep the windows open one more week
Pretend I don't feel autumn breathing down my cheek
Write it all down like I always do
In this little book that's just between me and you

Summer, I loved you like I've loved very little
You were the answer in the middle of every riddle I had
You were the sweet part, you were the bare feet
You were the reason I forgot to be sad

And I'm not ready, Lord I'm not ready
I wrote that in the margins of a Tuesday in my mind
Not ready, no I'm not ready
But summer, darling, take your time
Take your time
Take your time

songs.subject: ballad / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
She was standing by the window with the gold light on her face
I wasn't supposed to be there, I was only there by grace
Of a friend who said come on out it'll do you good
Three weeks after she said she didn't love me like she should

And I had built a wall of reasons not to feel a thing
Had the whole defense rehearsed, had everything
Wrapped in cynicism tight as a drum
Then you walked across the room and something came undone

I don't believe in much right now
The last thing I believed in took a bow
And left me standing in an empty hall
I swore I wouldn't let myself feel anything at all

But here you are laughing at something your friend said
And every careful brick I laid is crumbling overhead
I came here broken, came here cold
And I don't know your name yet but I'm already letting go

I told myself the music was just loud
I told myself the feeling was the crowd
The energy, the drinks, the colored lights
Just chemistry, the kind that dies by morning light

But then you looked at me and looked away
The way that means you knew that I was looking, hey
I know that look, I wore it once before
Back when love still felt like something worth reaching for

I don't believe in much right now
The last thing I believed in took a bow
And left me standing in an empty hall
I swore I wouldn't let myself feel anything at all

But here you are laughing at something your friend said
And every careful brick I laid is crumbling overhead
I came here broken, came here cold
And I don't know your name yet but I'm already letting go

God I'm terrified, I want you to know that
If this is the part where I say something smooth I don't know how to do that
She took the blueprint when she left, she took the map
And I've been wandering in the dark trying to find my way back

But there's something about the way you stand
Like you're rooted in something I don't understand
Something patient, something sure
And I haven't felt sure about anything anymore

Not since three weeks and six days ago
Not since everything I thought I knew said it had to go
But here in this room with the bass in my chest
You're making me forget that I was supposed to be a mess

I don't believe in much right now
The last thing I believed in took a bow
And left me standing in an empty hall
I swore I wouldn't let myself feel anything at all

But here you are laughing at something your friend said
And every careful brick I laid is crumbling overhead
I came here broken, came here cold
And I don't know your name yet but I'm already letting go

So if you've got a minute, if you've got a name
I'm the one by the door who doesn't look the same
As he did a month ago, or maybe he does
But something just shifted, something shifted because of you
