songs.subject: rock / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning and I'm walking again
Down the avenue where all the neon signs pretend
That something's still alive in here, that someone's still awake
Just me and all my restlessness and everything at stake

The city breathes different when the crowds go home
There's a kind of conversation in the streets when you're alone
Dumpsters and delivery trucks and a saxophone somewhere
Coming out of a bar on fifth like grief turned into air

I wrote this down tonight so I'd remember how it feels
To be invisible inside a city that reveals
Everything in daylight and hides nothing in the dark
I am just a footnote moving through this lit-up park

These streets know me better than my own front door
I've given them my 3am's, what else is living for
If not this hour, this asphalt, this strange cathedral light
That buzzes from the bodega on the corner every night

Old man with a cart of cans on Houston moving slow
We nodded like we had an understanding long ago
Some silent constitution of the ones who can't sleep well
Who've made their peace with living in between the rings of a bell

A cab goes by with someone in the back who's crying
And I thought about the forty times I've been in that cab trying
To hold it all together on the bridge, on the way home
Three in the morning teaches you that everyone's alone

I wrote this down tonight so I'd remember how it feels
To be invisible inside a city that reveals
Everything in daylight and hides nothing in the dark
I am just a footnote moving through this lit-up park

These streets know me better than my own front door
I've given them my 3am's, what else is living for
If not this hour, this asphalt, this strange cathedral light
That buzzes from the bodega on the corner every night

There's a woman on a fire escape just sitting with a smoke
Not looking at anything, or maybe at the whole
Skyline and the water tower and the antenna on the roof
She looked down once and I looked up and that was all the proof

I needed that the city holds the ones who can't be held
By beds or clocks or people or the stories we have told
Ourselves about belonging, about knowing where we stand
Three in the morning strips all that and shows what's in your hand

And what I've got is this, these blocks, these broken yellow lines
These locked-up storefronts with their handmade cardboard signs
This feeling like the whole world is a humming idle engine
Waiting to become itself again when all the light comes in

I wrote this down tonight so I'd remember how it feels
To be invisible inside a city that reveals
Everything in daylight and hides nothing in the dark
I am just a footnote moving through this lit-up park

These streets know me better than my own front door
I've given them my 3am's, what else is living for
If not this hour, this asphalt, this strange cathedral light
That buzzes from the bodega on the corner every night

So I'll walk until the bakery starts smelling like the dawn
And the pigeons start their conference and the darkness starts to yawn
And the city starts forgetting it was ever soft and mine
Three in the morning, I love you, but you vanish every time
songs.lyrics_en:
Three in the morning and I'm walking again
Down the avenue where all the neon signs pretend
That something's still alive in here, that someone's still awake
Just me and all my restlessness and everything at stake

The city breathes different when the crowds go home
There's a kind of conversation in the streets when you're alone
Dumpsters and delivery trucks and a saxophone somewhere
Coming out of a bar on fifth like grief turned into air

I wrote this down tonight so I'd remember how it feels
To be invisible inside a city that reveals
Everything in daylight and hides nothing in the dark
I am just a footnote moving through this lit-up park

These streets know me better than my own front door
I've given them my 3am's, what else is living for
If not this hour, this asphalt, this strange cathedral light
That buzzes from the bodega on the corner every night

Old man with a cart of cans on Houston moving slow
We nodded like we had an understanding long ago
Some silent constitution of the ones who can't sleep well
Who've made their peace with living in between the rings of a bell

A cab goes by with someone in the back who's crying
And I thought about the forty times I've been in that cab trying
To hold it all together on the bridge, on the way home
Three in the morning teaches you that everyone's alone

I wrote this down tonight so I'd remember how it feels
To be invisible inside a city that reveals
Everything in daylight and hides nothing in the dark
I am just a footnote moving through this lit-up park

These streets know me better than my own front door
I've given them my 3am's, what else is living for
If not this hour, this asphalt, this strange cathedral light
That buzzes from the bodega on the corner every night

There's a woman on a fire escape just sitting with a smoke
Not looking at anything, or maybe at the whole
Skyline and the water tower and the antenna on the roof
She looked down once and I looked up and that was all the proof

I needed that the city holds the ones who can't be held
By beds or clocks or people or the stories we have told
Ourselves about belonging, about knowing where we stand
Three in the morning strips all that and shows what's in your hand

And what I've got is this, these blocks, these broken yellow lines
These locked-up storefronts with their handmade cardboard signs
This feeling like the whole world is a humming idle engine
Waiting to become itself again when all the light comes in

I wrote this down tonight so I'd remember how it feels
To be invisible inside a city that reveals
Everything in daylight and hides nothing in the dark
I am just a footnote moving through this lit-up park

These streets know me better than my own front door
I've given them my 3am's, what else is living for
If not this hour, this asphalt, this strange cathedral light
That buzzes from the bodega on the corner every night

So I'll walk until the bakery starts smelling like the dawn
And the pigeons start their conference and the darkness starts to yawn
And the city starts forgetting it was ever soft and mine
Three in the morning, I love you, but you vanish every time

songs.subject: R&B / chasing a dream
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning with the sun still hiding
Wrote your name across the fog on my window pane
Got this feeling living somewhere deep inside me
Like a fire that the rain just cannot tame
I know they said that I was reaching too far
That the ceiling was as high as I could go
But I been talking to the version of myself
That exists somewhere out there in the glow

I packed my bags last Tuesday, left the nine to five behind me
Told my mama that I loved her, said I had to find me
She cried a little, smiled a little, held me at the door
Said baby don't come home until you're everything and more

I am chasing what they told me wasn't real
Running toward the ache I always feel
Every closed door, every no, every night alone on the floor
I write it down, I write it down
This is my diary, these are my pages
Built on tears and hotel rooms and empty stages
But I believe, Lord I believe
I am chasing what they told me wasn't real

Tuesday night in a city that don't know me
Played a set to seven people and the bartender
But one woman in the front row kept on watching
Like she heard the thing I meant beneath the words I said
After the show she grabbed my hand and told me quietly
I heard that song ten years ago in a dream
Keep going, honey, don't you let this world convince you
That the life inside your chest is not a worthy thing

I been sleeping on a pullout at my cousin's
Working doubles at the diner on the weekends
Sending demos out to inboxes that never answer back
But I tape every rejection to the mirror on my wall
So I remember where I started when I finally get the call

I am chasing what they told me wasn't real
Running toward the ache I always feel
Every closed door, every no, every night alone on the floor
I write it down, I write it down
This is my diary, these are my pages
Built on tears and hotel rooms and empty stages
But I believe, Lord I believe
I am chasing what they told me wasn't real

Some days I question every single choice I made
Wonder if the sacrifice was worth the trade
Wonder if the girl I used to be had the right idea
Staying safe and staying small and staying near
But then the melody comes rolling in like morning
And I remember why I ever started singing
I remember that the dream chose me before I chose it
And I was never really free until I let myself begin

Running toward the ache I always feel
Every closed door, every no, every night alone on the floor
I write it down, I write it down

I am chasing, still chasing
I am chasing what they told me wasn't real

songs.subject: americana / growing up in a small town
songs.lyrics:
I been pouring drinks in this town since I was twenty-two
Watching all the young ones come in flushed with something new
They sit down at my bar with all their restless energy
And I see myself in every single one of them, Lord I do
This town's got a way of holding on to people
Like the mud out on Route 9 after rain
Some leave and swear they'll never once look backward
Most of them come rolling right back down this lane

Old Cooper had a son who went to Nashville
Sent postcards back for three or four good years
Then one November night he walked back through that door there
Sat right where you're sitting, ordered two cold beers
Said the city wasn't cruel, just wasn't interested
Said the noise was loud but no one heard a sound
Said he woke one morning reached for something real
And the realest thing he knew was in this town

We grew up where everybody knows your damage
Where the grain elevator scrapes the only sky we knew
Where the football field's a cathedral on a Friday
And the gossip travels faster than the truth
We grew up in the pockets of each other
Swapping lunches, swapping heartbreaks, swapping blame
And no matter where we scattered to find living
Something always whispers us back to where we came

There's a woman at the end who drinks her coffee
Been coming in since way before my time
Her husband built the water tower, rest his soul now
She touches the window when the church bells chime
She told me once that small means you can see it
Every face that forms the fabric of your world
That in a city you can vanish in the millions
Here you gotta show up as the boy or as the girl

I watched Denny Johnson lose his farm in spring
Watched the whole town show up carrying everything
Casseroles and lumber, envelopes of cash
A community is knowing when to show up fast
I watched two girls get married down on Freemont
Half the town came just to see and some to stare
But by the reception even stubborn hearts were dancing
Because love looks hard to argue with out here

We grew up where everybody knows your damage
Where the grain elevator scrapes the only sky we knew
Where the football field's a cathedral on a Friday
And the gossip travels faster than the truth
We grew up in the pockets of each other
Swapping lunches, swapping heartbreaks, swapping blame
And no matter where we scattered to find living
Something always whispers us back to where we came

Last call comes and I wipe down the counter
Stack the chairs and lock the register tight
Drive home past the same four corners I grew up on
Same old water tower standing in the night
I used to think that I had settled, maybe missed out
On the bigger life that waited past the hill
But watching people wander in and wander outward
I think I got the thing they're looking for still

We grew up where everybody knows your damage
Where the grain elevator scrapes the only sky we knew
Where the football field's a cathedral on a Friday
And the gossip travels faster than the truth
We grew up in the pockets of each other
Swapping lunches, swapping heartbreaks, swapping blame
And no matter where we scattered to find living
This old town will always whisper us back home again

songs.subject: soul / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
Your coffee cup is still here on the counter
I ain't moved it since the morning that you left
There's a ring it made on my good wood table
And I trace it like a prayer with what I've got left
The apartment feels like someone turned the volume
All the way down on a song I used to love
I keep reaching for the phone before I catch myself
Remembering you're living your life up above

Three blocks north is not the same as three blocks
When those three blocks are three states and a sky
I told you go, I told you this was something
I told you baby fly, I told you fly
But telling someone go and watching them go
Are two entirely different kinds of ache
And I am learning that the generous heart
Still bleeds the same, still bleeds the same

Your name is in my mouth on Sunday mornings
I set the table and I only need one chair
But I still make enough for two out of habit
And I stand there like a fool just standing there
The city must be treating you to wonders
New faces at new tables, new night air
I hope you eat, I hope you sleep, I hope you call me
I hope you know I miss you everywhere

I miss you in the Tuesday unremarkable
I miss you in the grocery store at nine
I miss you in the laugh track of the television
I miss you in the bottom of this wine
You didn't go because you stopped loving
You went because the living called your name
And I am proud of you and I am broken
And somehow both of those things are the same

I miss you like a season that has ended
Like the way October holds and then lets go
Like the last warm day before the cold comes settling
You feel it leaving even as it glows
Some people leave and take the air out with them
Leave the rooms all rearranged without a key
And I go around trying to find myself inside here
In the version of this life that's still just me

Your name is in my mouth on Sunday mornings
I set the table and I only need one chair
But I still make enough for two out of habit
And I stand there like a fool just standing there
The city must be treating you to wonders
New faces at new tables, new night air
I hope you eat, I hope you sleep, I hope you call me
I hope you know I miss you everywhere

I played the record that we used to dance to
In the kitchen when the dinner had gone cold
You had a way of making ordinary evenings
Feel like something you'd want written when you're old
I dance alone now and it ain't the same thing
But it's mine, and yours is yours, and that's okay
I just needed you to know across whatever distance
That I hold you in the marrow every day

Your name is in my mouth on Sunday mornings
I set the table and I only need one chair
But I still make enough for two out of habit
And I stand there like a fool just standing there
The city must be treating you to wonders
New faces at new tables, new night air
I hope you eat, I hope you sleep, I hope you call me
You moved away but you still live right here

3. A soul song about missing someone who moved away.
4. A americana song about forgiveness.

songs.subject: americana / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I been carrying this stone since nineteen ninety-three
The summer that you took what wasn't yours to take from me
I built a wall around the field where I used to grow
And told myself that keeping you out meant letting go
But a wall ain't healing, a wall's a monument to pain
And I been maintaining it through every wind and every rain
Spending all my good years being keeper of a grudge
When forgiveness wasn't something I could even barely budge

My daddy used to say the ones who wronged you
Don't lose sleep the way you think they do
That the prison that you build around your anger
Has only got one person locked inside it, and it's you
I argued with him on the porch a hundred summer nights
Said justice meant the wound stayed open to the light
He'd shake his head and pour his coffee and go quiet
And I'd go home and feed the fire of my riot

Forgiveness ain't forgetting what was broken
Ain't saying that the hurt was a lie
It's setting down the stone so both your hands are open
It's choosing your own life, it's learning to fly
I ain't there yet but I can see the road ahead of me
Winding through the fields I used to know
Forgiveness is the river, I been standing on the bank here
And I think it might be time to let it flow

I saw you at the hardware store last October
You looked the same and smaller than my memory
All the years I'd made you into something enormous
Some villain with a shadow over me
You looked up and nodded and I looked away first
Walked the long way around the lumber aisle
And I sat in my truck and cried for twenty minutes
Not from anger, more from something reconciled

Because the person that I am today was forged inside that fire
And I won't say it had to happen, but it happened
And the life I built from broken came out stronger and came higher
Than the life I had before the world got fractured
And you don't get the credit and I don't owe you gratitude
But somewhere in the rubble there was me
And I dug myself out bloody and I planted something new there
And now it's tall enough that I can see

Forgiveness ain't forgetting what was broken
Ain't saying that the hurt was a lie
It's setting down the stone so both your hands are open
It's choosing your own life, it's learning to fly
I ain't there yet but I can see the road ahead of me
Winding through the fields I used to know
Forgiveness is the river, I been standing on the bank here
And I think it might be time to let it flow

There's a version of this story where I carry it to dying
Where I hand it to my children as an heirloom made of spite
Where it colors every morning and it darkens every evening
Where I never once get free of that one night
I don't want that story, I don't want that inheritance
I don't want to be the one who got consumed
I want to walk out into morning without armor on my shoulders
I want to walk into a wide and open room

My mama lit a candle at the church for you last Easter
I didn't know until she told me on the phone
I wanted to be angry and I tried to find the anger
But all I found was tired, and tired felt like home
So I drove out to the river where I used to fish as a boy
Threw a stone and watched the circles widen slow
And something in my chest came loose and fell into the current
And I watched it getting smaller as it went

Forgiveness ain't forgetting what was broken
Ain't saying that the hurt was a lie
It's setting down the stone so both your hands are open
It's choosing your own life, it's learning to fly
I found the road and now my feet are on it finally
Winding through the fields I used to know
Forgiveness is the river and I'm wading in the shallows
And I'm learning, Lord I'm learning, letting go

songs.subject: blues / chasing a dream
songs.lyrics:

Packed my old suitcase with a dollar and a prayer

Some folks laughed at me, said boy there's nothing there

But I keep on walking down this long and dusty road
I keep on walking down this long and dusty road
Carrying this dream like it's the only thing I own

songs.subject: soul / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
Rain on the window, nothing to do
Mom's downstairs watching something on the news
The whole house smells like coffee and quiet
And I'm up here wondering why I feel like crying

It's a rainy Sunday, soul so heavy
The weekend's almost gone and I ain't ready
Texted you twice but you never came through
Rainy Sunday, thinking about you

Pulled out my journal, wrote a half a page
Crossed it all out, I couldn't find the words to say
My playlist keeps shuffling to the sad ones
Guess my phone knows me better than anyone

It's a rainy Sunday, soul so heavy
The weekend's almost gone and I ain't ready
Texted you twice but you never came through
Rainy Sunday, thinking about you

I pressed my forehead against the cold cold glass
Watched a kid ride his bike down the street so fast
Wished I was little, nothing to feel
Now everything's complicated, everything's real

And the gray sky just sits there not giving any answers
And my heart does that thing again, that stumbling kind of stammer
I don't know what I want, I don't know what I need
Just know that Sunday rain does something deep to me

It's a rainy Sunday, soul so heavy
The weekend's almost gone and I ain't ready
Texted you twice but you never came through
Rainy Sunday, thinking about you

Dinner's getting ready, I can smell it from the hall
Guess I'll wash my face and pretend I'm fine after all
But Sunday's got a color and it's somewhere between blue and gray
And I'll carry it around till Monday takes it away

It's a rainy Sunday, soul so heavy
The weekend's almost gone and I ain't ready
Texted you twice but you never came through
Rainy Sunday, still thinking about you
Still thinking about you

songs.subject: punk / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Seventy-two years on this spinning stupid rock
Survived a war, a flood, a heart attack, a stock market drop
I fixed carburetors, I raised three kids alone
And you ended thirty years with a goddamn telephone

A text, a text, you sent a lousy text
Three lines and an emoji and a period at the end
A text, a text, you sent a lousy text
Is this what we've become, is this how the story ends

My hands still work, I can still throw a punch
I'm not too old to flip a table over during lunch
I learned to waltz in 1974
You can't break up by text, what the hell are manners for

A text, a text, you sent a lousy text
Three lines and an emoji and a period at the end
A text, a text, you sent a lousy text
Is this what we've become, is this how the story ends

I showed it to my grandson, he said grandpa that's rough
He said at least she didn't ghost you, I said son that's not enough
He tried to explain to me the way the young folks do things now
I told him get off my porch and take your participation trophies out

A text, a text, you sent a lousy text
Three lines and an emoji and a period at the end
A text, a text, you sent a lousy text
Is this what we've become, is this how the story ends

We danced to Marvin Gaye at the American Legion Hall
You had paint on your left shoe and I didn't mind at all
Now you've got a smartphone and apparently no shame
Thirty years reduced to autocorrect and a digital blame

I'm gonna write you a letter, four pages front and back
I'm gonna use a fountain pen, I want my feelings on the record as a matter of fact
I'm gonna stamp it and address it and I'm gonna walk it to the box
Because some of us still know how to communicate like adults

A text, a text, you sent a lousy text
Three lines and an emoji and a period at the end
A text, a text, you sent a lousy text
Yeah this is what we've become
Yeah this is how the story ends
Yeah this is what we've become
You hit send

songs.subject: lo-fi / summer ending
songs.lyrics:
The lawn chairs are still out but nobody sits in them now
The neighbors took their pool down, don't know exactly when or how
The light is doing that thing where it goes golden by four
And I keep reaching for summer but summer don't live here anymore

The radio's still playing the songs we heard in June
But they sound a little different in an almost-autumn room
Ice cream places closing, school supplies in every store
I keep reaching for summer but summer don't live here anymore

Let it go slowly, let it go slow
The way it fades at the edges is the only way I know
How to hold on to something that was never meant to stay
Summer slipping through my fingers like the end of a long long day

Found an old receipt from that roadside stand we loved
Watermelon, two dollars, a Tuesday, hot enough
The back of the paper's got a little sunscreen smear
Proof that we were happy, proof that summer was here

The windows stay closed now when they used to stay wide open
The morning has a chill that feels like something quietly broken
I wore a sweater yesterday and didn't even think twice
I keep reaching for summer but I'm reaching into ice

Let it go slowly, let it go slow
The way it fades at the edges is the only way I know
How to hold on to something that was never meant to stay
Summer slipping through my fingers like the end of a long long day

There's a stillness in the backyard where the fireflies used to be
The dark comes earlier now and it comes differently
I'm not sad exactly, just a little hollow and full
The way you feel when beautiful things become beautiful and gone

I'll pack the lighter clothes up and I'll put them in the bin
And I'll tell myself that summer's just around the bend again
But tonight I want to sit here where the last warm air remains
And breathe it in before the season turns and quietly explains

That nothing gold stays golden, nothing bright stays bright
That every easy afternoon will give itself to night
And the thing about the ending is it makes the middle shine
So here's to every barefoot day and every long slow wine

Let it go slowly, let it go slow
The way it fades at the edges is the only way I know
How to hold on to something that was never meant to stay
Summer slipping through my fingers like the end of a long long day
Like the end of a long long day
Like the end of a long long day

songs.subject: gospel / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
Picked up the phone at midnight
Didn't know who'd be calling me
But the voice on the other end
Said child, I set you free
I'd been crying on my pillow
Had been lost so many days
But that midnight call came through to me
And turned my night to praise

I was sinking in the darkness
Couldn't find my way back home
But the Lord reached through the telephone
Said you're never alone
He said I see your every teardrop
I have counted every one
Hold on through the midnight hour
Your new day has begun

Oh that midnight call, that midnight call
When the world was cold and still
He called me by my name that night
The way only Jesus will
Oh that midnight call, that midnight call
From a love that never sleeps
He reached down from the heavens
Just to find one of His sheep

I told my sister in the morning
She said how did He find you there
I said grace don't need a number
It just travels through the air
He said I watched you since the cradle
I have never looked away
And tonight I needed you to know
I'm with you every day

Oh that midnight call, that midnight call
When the world was cold and still
He called me by my name that night
The way only Jesus will
Oh that midnight call, that midnight call
From a love that never sleeps
He reached down from the heavens
Just to find one of His sheep

If you're sitting in the darkness
If you're waiting all alone
Just remember that your Father
Has your number in His home
He will call you in your midnight
He will call you in your pain
Pick up when the Spirit moves you
Nothing will be the same

Oh that midnight call, that midnight call
When the world was cold and still
He called me by my name that night
The way only Jesus will
Oh that midnight call, that midnight call
From a love that never sleeps
He reached down from the heavens
Just to find one of His sheep

He reached down through the darkness
Just to find one of His sheep

songs.subject: gospel / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The streetlights hold their breath at three
The city exhales, just you and me
Every window amber gold
Every story left untold
And I am walking down this road
With your name carrying like a holy load
The pavement wet from evening rain
I would walk it all again

These empty streets are sanctuaries
These traffic lights are altars glowing red then green
I never knew that I could worship
In the spaces in between
Lord I thank You for this city
At the hour when the noise has died away
I thank You for this burning in my chest
That will not let me say
I've ever loved anything more beautiful
Than her face
And I thank You for this city
And I thank You for this grace

The bakeries are breathing warm
Into the cold before the storm
A taxi passes, headlights bright
She texted me goodnight, goodnight
And I am standing at the corner
Where I kissed her three weeks past
Some corners they become your altars
Some moments built to last

These empty streets are sanctuaries
These traffic lights are altars glowing red then green
I never knew that I could worship
In the spaces in between
Lord I thank You for this city
At the hour when the noise has died away
I thank You for this burning in my chest
That will not let me say
I've ever loved anything more beautiful
Than her face
And I thank You for this city
And I thank You for this grace

How did You make a heart so full
How did You wire me for this pull
How did You craft a feeling so enormous
That three a m on concrete feels like chorus
I am singing in the cold
I am finally coming home
Every city has a soul
She became mine I should have known

These empty streets are sanctuaries

I never knew that I could worship

Lord I thank You for this city

And I thank You for this city
And I thank You for this grace

Oh, thank You for this grace

songs.subject: rock / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
I was counting down the hours till I'd see you Saturday
Had your favorite record playing, had the whole thing planned out laid
Then my phone lit up the bedroom like a flash of lightning came
And I read it twice then three times and I swallowed down the flame

It said I think we're better off apart
It said I've made my mind up from the start
It said I hope you find what you've been looking for
Then a period and nothing anymore

So I'm standing in the wreckage of a text message tonight
Every word a little grenade that went off behind my eyes
You couldn't look at me and say it, needed pixels, needed space
Now I'm bleeding on the carpet with your font all in my face

I still love you through the static
I still love you through the screen
I still love you in the language
Of the cruelest thing I've seen
You pressed send and I fell apart at every seam
I still love you, God I love you
Through that cold and glowing screen

I'm not angry at the ending I'm just angry at the way
Twenty months and all we built got buried in a text today
Did it feel like nothing to you, did your finger barely pause
Did you edit it three times or just send it through because

It was easier than breathing through a hard and honest night
Easier than sitting with the damage in the light
I deserved to hear your voice crack, I deserved to see your eyes
Not a read receipt and silence and a hundred blinking lines

I still love you through the static
I still love you through the screen
I still love you in the language
Of the cruelest thing I've seen
You pressed send and I fell apart at every seam
I still love you, God I love you
Through that cold and glowing screen

And I could screenshot everything and show the world your words
I could blast it to the rooftops let everybody hear
But I won't do that to you baby even now I'll hold you safe
Even now I'm still protecting what we had in empty space

Because the love I have doesn't evaporate because you left
It just has nowhere to go now and it fills up every breath
And I'm not some broken record just to prove that I felt real
I'm a person standing in the dark learning how to feel

I still love you through the static
I still love you through the screen
I still love you in the language
Of the cruelest thing I've seen
You pressed send and I fell apart at every seam
I still love you, God I love you
Through that cold and glowing screen

I still love you
Damn, I still love you
Through that screen

Even now I'm still protecting what we had in empty space

Because the love I have doesn't evaporate because you left
It just has nowhere to go now and it fills up every breath
And I'm not some broken record just to prove that I felt real
I'm a person standing in the dark learning how to feel

I still love you through the static
I still love you through the screen
I still love you in the language
Of the cruelest thing I've seen
You pressed send and I fell apart at every seam
I still love you, God I love you
Through that cold and glowing screen

I still love you
Damn, I still love you
Through that screen

songs.subject: R&B / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning reaching for my phone
The way I always do before my eyes adjust to home
And there it was just waiting like it had been there all night
A little blue notification underneath the bedroom light

You said you need your freedom
You said you need to breathe
You said that you've been thinking
And you think that we should leave
You said you're sorry, hope I'm good
You said it's not about my love
Baby you wrote a paragraph
And none of it was enough

How you gonna tell me it's over
How you gonna do it in a text
Baby I deserve to see your face
When you're tearing out my chest
How you gonna write me a goodbye
Like it's something on your grocery list
After everything we built in the dark
It comes down to this
It comes down to this

I read it seven times looking for a door
Some small opening where maybe you meant something more
But the words were final, polished, clean
Like you'd been practicing alone
And I've been talking to the ceiling
In our bed, in our home

Because I'm the one who stayed up late
When you were going through it all
I'm the one who drove three hours
Just to catch you when you'd fall
And I know love ain't a ledger
And I know that ain't the point
But you could've let me hold your hand
Before you burned it to the joint

How you gonna tell me it's over
How you gonna do it in a text
Baby I deserve to see your face
When you're tearing out my chest
How you gonna write me a goodbye
Like it's something on your grocery list
After everything we built in the dark
It comes down to this
It comes down to this

I'm not even mad you left me
I'm not even mad the love is gone
What breaks me is the distance of it
One blue message and then on
Like I was something easy
Like I was something small
Baby I would've given you the grace
To let me have the fall

I would've sat across from you
I would've held your hand through it
I would've cried and told you thank you
For the time and all of it
But you gave me a screen
You gave me a cursor and a send
And now I'm here translating silence
Into something I can mend

How you gonna tell me it's over
How you gonna do it in a text
Baby I deserve to see your face
When you're tearing out my chest
How you gonna write me a goodbye
Like it's something on your grocery list
After everything we built in the dark
It comes down to this

It comes down to this
Baby, it comes down to this
After all of it
It comes down to this

songs.subject: trap / chasing a dream
songs.lyrics:
Woke up with nothing but a vision in my head
Empty pockets but I'm rich inside instead
They said slow down boy you moving way too fast
I said watch me turn this hunger into cash

Running through the city with my eyes on the prize
Every closed door just a blessing in disguise
Mama told me baby don't you ever let them win
So I put it on my soul and I do it all again

I'm chasing that dream, yeah I'm chasing that dream
Nothing gonna stop me from becoming what I see
I'm chasing that dream, yeah I'm chasing that dream
Told the doubters and the haters just to let me be
Chasing that dream, chasing that dream
Stack it to the ceiling till I'm living like a king
I'm chasing that dream

Eighteen with a notebook full of bars and plans
Sleeping on a couch with my future in my hands
Everybody round me said the odds were never right
I just turned the music up and worked into the night

Studio at midnight when the city's gone to sleep
Every sacrifice I made was mine alone to keep
Bled into the microphone and gave it everything
Now I hear my name echoing and feel the wind beneath my wings

I'm chasing that dream, yeah I'm chasing that dream
Nothing gonna stop me from becoming what I see
I'm chasing that dream, yeah I'm chasing that dream
Told the doubters and the haters just to let me be
Chasing that dream, chasing that dream
Stack it to the ceiling till I'm living like a king
I'm chasing that dream

They counted every stumble like a final defeat
They never saw me grinding on these blistered feet
Every valley that I walked through made me stronger inside
Every scar upon my body just a marker of my ride

Now the lights are getting brighter and the crowd is getting loud
I can feel my people pulling me up out the cloud
I remember every night I had to cry myself to sleep
Now I'm standing at the mountain top I climbed so steep

I'm chasing that dream, yeah I'm chasing that dream
Nothing gonna stop me from becoming what I see
I'm chasing that dream, yeah I'm chasing that dream
Told the doubters and the haters just to let me be
Chasing that dream, chasing that dream
Stack it to the ceiling till I'm living like a king
I'm chasing that dream

Yeah I made it, yeah I made it
Every tear was worth the pain
Yeah I made it, yeah I made it
And I'd do it all again

songs.subject: emo / growing up in a small town
songs.lyrics:
I packed your lunch before the sun came up today
Wrote a little note and tucked it in to say
That I love you more than words could ever hold
More than this small town and all its winter cold

We live between the grain elevator and the tracks
Where everybody knows your name and knows your back
Where the gossip travels faster than the wind
And I've been fighting just to give you more than this

I grew up here and I never got away
Your daddy left me in the middle of that May
And I promised you I'd make it worth the fight
But some nights I cry myself to sleep at night

This town is small but baby so am I
I watch you from the window every night
Wondering if I'm giving you enough
God knows I'm trying God knows it's rough
This town is small but my love is not
It stretches past the water tower and the parking lot
Past every broken dream I ever had for me
I'm pouring every one of them into you, baby

The other mothers have their husbands on their arms
Driving nicer cars and living out on farms
I drive a ninety-three that barely starts in snow
And I wait tables just to watch your garden grow

You asked me once why daddy never calls
I held it in and kept my back against the wall
I said some people aren't built for staying, hon
But I am and I'm here and you're my sun

This town is small but baby so am I
I watch you from the window every night
Wondering if I'm giving you enough
God knows I'm trying God knows it's rough
This town is small but my love is not
It stretches past the water tower and the parking lot
Past every broken dream I ever had for me
I'm pouring every one of them into you, baby

I see you at the school play in the second row
You looked back at me and God my heart just broke
In the best way that a heart can ever break
You smiled and I knew every sacrifice I make

Is worth it, every double shift is worth it
Every lonely Friday night on this torn couch
Every bill I couldn't pay and cried about
Every dream I folded up and put away
Is worth it for the look upon your face today

I never meant to raise you in a place this small
I never meant to let you watch me fall apart
But you have seen me get back up and keep on going
Baby that is something that no school is ever showing

This town is small but baby so am I
I watch you from the window every night
Wondering if I'm giving you enough
God knows I'm trying God knows it's rough
This town is small but my love is not
It stretches past the water tower and the parking lot
Past every broken dream I ever had for me
I'm pouring every one of them into you, baby

Someday you'll leave and God I hope you do
But know that every road leads back to me and you
And this small town held us close when things were thin
Baby that's where your story will begin

songs.subject: hip-hop / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning and the sky won't let it go
Raindrops on the glass moving kinda slow
Nowhere I gotta be, nowhere I gotta run
Just me and the grey light where there used to be sun

Rolled over reached for my phone before I thought
Checked the weather like I needed to be taught
Already knew what was waiting for me there
Seventy percent and not a break anywhere

Made my coffee black and let it breathe some steam
Stood up at the window living half inside a dream
The gutters running hard and the street reflects the light
Like the whole world got washed clean from Saturday night

Rainy Sunday got me in my feelings deep
Rain on the window, rain putting me to sleep
Beats in my headphones, playlist on repeat
Nothing but the rainfall and the rhythm of the street
Rainy Sunday, nowhere I gotta go
Watch the people walking slow below
This the kind of day that teaches you to feel
Everything you numbed all week is suddenly real

Called my brother just to hear a voice I trust
We don't talk enough and that's on both of us
He laughed and said man it's raining here too
I said maybe the whole country having the same mood

Put on an album that I haven't touched in years
Every track a memory that showed up with the tears
Not the heavy kind, just that bittersweet release
When the rain outside gives you unexpected peace

Ordered something warm and watched it pull up slow
Tipped the driver heavy cause I know he in the cold
Ate alone but didn't feel the lonely this time round
Something bout a rainy day that softens every sound

Rainy Sunday got me in my feelings deep
Rain on the window, rain putting me to sleep
Beats in my headphones, playlist on repeat
Nothing but the rainfall and the rhythm of the street
Rainy Sunday, nowhere I gotta go
Watch the people walking slow below
This the kind of day that teaches you to feel
Everything you numbed all week is suddenly real

I used to hate the rain, used to call it wasted time
Now I need these grey days just to clear out my mind
Week been heavy, people been a lot
Rain don't ask for nothing and it gives me what I got

Which is stillness, which is quiet, which is room to think
Which is sitting with myself right on the brink
Of something like a breakthrough or just rest
Rainy Sunday putting me up to the test

Can you just be here, can you just exist
Without the noise and the agenda and the list
Can you breathe without a purpose for a minute or two
Rainy Sunday saying yes you can and yes you do

Rainy Sunday got me in my feelings deep
Rain on the window, rain putting me to sleep
Beats in my headphones, playlist on repeat
Nothing but the rainfall and the rhythm of the street
Rainy Sunday, nowhere I gotta go
Watch the people walking slow below
This the kind of day that teaches you to feel
Everything you numbed all week is suddenly real

By the evening it was slowing to a drizzle and a mist
City lights reflecting all the things I might have missed
If I'd been running round and filling up the day
Sometimes God sends rain to make you stay

songs.subject: gospel / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
I got a hole in my shoe and a song in my chest
Got forty-seven cents and the Lord's given me rest
My children think I'm crazy when I lift my hands and sing
But a man with nothing worldly's got a better offering

I been poor before and poorer still than this
But I never missed a morning worth of gratitude and bliss
They repossessed the furniture in eighty-three
But they couldn't take the joy the good Lord planted in me

I know what it is to watch the lights go out
I know what it is to carry worry and doubt
I know what it is to have the cupboard bare
But I also know the God who always meets me there

Broke but I'm happy, hallelujah anyway
Don't got a dime to spare but I got praise
Can't explain it to the people who got plenty and still grieve
God fills up what the world could never give
Broke but I'm happy, happy in the Lord
My treasure ain't in nothing I can't afford
I wake up every morning on the winning side
Broke but I'm happy and that's no lie

Used to chase the dollar like it owed me something good
Wore myself to nothing chasing things I thought I should
Had a little more back when my Martha was alive
We weren't rich by any measure but Lord how we did thrive

Now it's just me and the Bible on this porch swing every dawn
Watching the sun come up like it has every since I was born
My neighbor said old man don't you worry bout your lack
I said son I tried that worry and it never gave me back

A single thing worth having, not a moment, not a day
So I laid my troubles down and I learned another way
When your hands are empty they are open wide to fill
I been catching mercy and I'm catching mercy still

Broke but I'm happy, hallelujah anyway
Don't got a dime to spare but I got praise
Can't explain it to the people who got plenty and still grieve
God fills up what the world could never give
Broke but I'm happy, happy in the Lord
My treasure ain't in nothing I can't afford
I wake up every morning on the winning side
Broke but I'm happy and that's no lie

My grandbaby asked me grandpa are you sad
I said child I am the richest man this family ever had
She looked at me confused at all the empty in the room
I said everything you see will pass but grace will outlive doom

I have seen the proud man stumble and the mighty take a fall
I have seen the man with everything and nothing after all
But I have never seen the righteous left without his bread
I may go to bed with little but I rise up full instead

This old body's getting tired and my time ain't far away
But I'll be singing just as loud on my very final day
Tell them at my funeral don't you weep and don't you mourn
Tell them that he left here happy as the day that he was born

Broke but I'm happy, hallelujah anyway
Don't got a dime to spare but I got praise
Can't explain it to the people who got plenty and still grieve
God fills up what the world could never give
Broke but I'm happy, happy in the Lord
My treasure ain't in nothing I can't afford
I wake up every morning on the winning side
Broke but I'm happy and that's no lie

Hallelujah, hallelujah, even now
Hallelujah, hallelujah, God has shown me how
To find the joy in empty, find the praise in need
Broke but I'm happy, happy, happy and free

Tell them at my funeral don't you weep and don't you mourn
Tell them that he left here happy as the day that he was born

Broke but I'm happy, hallelujah anyway
Don't got a dime to spare but I got praise
Can't explain it to the people who got plenty and still grieve
God fills up what the world could never give
Broke but I'm happy, happy in the Lord
My treasure ain't in nothing I can't afford
I wake up every morning on the winning side
Broke but I'm happy and that's no lie

Hallelujah, hallelujah, even now
Hallelujah, hallelujah, God has shown me how
To find the joy in empty, find the praise in need
Broke but I'm happy, happy, happy and free

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / faith
songs.lyrics:
You hold my hand like a prayer I forgot I knew
Something in the way you breathe steadies all my doubts
I used to walk these roads alone and call it freedom
But you showed me what believing is about

I never thought that I would find a congregation
In the hollow of your shoulder after dark
But here I am converting to the gospel of your patience
You are scripture written straight across my heart

So I'm laying down my armor at your doorstep
I'm trusting in the things I cannot see
If love is just a leap across an open canyon
Then darling you're the faith that carries me

I used to keep my windows locked against the weather
Convinced that every storm would find a way inside
But you walked in like sunlight after years of gray December
And taught me how to let the good things bide

I read the constellations wrong for so many seasons
Looked for signs in all the places they weren't found
And then you turned to me one night and said nothing
And everything I needed was right there in that sound

So I'm laying down my armor at your doorstep
I'm trusting in the things I cannot see
If love is just a leap across an open canyon
Then darling you're the faith that carries me

Some people pray on mountains, some pray in cathedrals
Some burn candles down to nothing in the night
I press my mouth against your name and call that holy
I close my eyes and you become my light

And I know that I'm not perfect at the practice
I stumble on the words, I lose the thread
But you never make me feel like I am broken
You just take my hand and walk me home instead

So I'm laying down my armor at your doorstep
I'm trusting in the things I cannot see
If love is just a leap across an open canyon
Then darling you're the faith that carries me
You are the faith that carries me

songs.subject: rock / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
The gutters run like rivers down the avenue
The sky has been this color since the morning broke
Nowhere I need to be and nothing I can do
Just the ceiling and the radio and the coffee's smoke

Put on something loud enough to feel it in the floorboards
Let the windows shake a little, let the thunder in
This is the kind of Sunday that was made for being restless
The kind that makes you wonder where you've always been

So turn it up, turn it up, let it rattle through the walls
Let the rain come down and drown the silence in the halls
We're soaking wet and electric, we're alive inside the grey
There's nothing like a rainy Sunday to burn the world away

Pulled out every album that I haven't touched in ages
Found a ticket stub between the records on the shelf
Some show from seven years ago, I can't recall the faces
Just that feeling of being lost inside yourself

The streets outside are empty but they're shining like a mirror
Every puddle holds a city upside down
There's something almost beautiful about this kind of nowhere
This in-between that swallows up the town

So turn it up, turn it up, let it rattle through the walls
Let the rain come down and drown the silence in the halls
We're soaking wet and electric, we're alive inside the grey
There's nothing like a rainy Sunday to burn the world away

Maybe I'll drive out past the edge of where the streetlights go
Just to feel the wipers cutting through the dark
Maybe I'll sit here till the candles burn down to the wick
And trace the water on the window with my thumb and make it art

The week is waiting somewhere past the end of all this weather
With its noise and its demands and all its weight
But right now there is nothing but the thunder and the downpour
And I swear I've never felt this kind of great

So turn it up, turn it up, let it rattle through the walls
Let the rain come down and drown the silence in the halls
We're soaking wet and electric, we're alive inside the grey
There's nothing like a rainy Sunday to burn the world away
Nothing like a rainy Sunday, nothing like today

songs.subject: indie folk / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
The phone rang at midnight and I almost didn't answer
Layla was asleep across the hall
I saw your name glow up against the darkness
And something old and tired in me stalled

But I picked it up because I always pick it up
Because some habits live below the bone
You asked me how I was and I said fine, I said fine
Standing in my kitchen all alone

And you don't know what midnight looks like from this side
You don't know how quiet gets when the baby's finally down
You don't know what it costs me just to hear your voice that easy
Like you didn't leave me holding everything in this town

I rinsed a coffee cup and kept my voice from breaking
Watched the clock above the stove turn on
You said you'd been thinking, said you missed the way things were
I said I know and then I said I had to go

Because there's a lunch to make and laundry on the couch
And a little girl who needs me in the morning light
And I have learned to love this life you didn't choose to stay for
I can't afford to come undone tonight

And you don't know what midnight looks like from this side
You don't know how quiet gets when the baby's finally down
You don't know what it costs me just to hear your voice that easy
Like you didn't leave me holding everything in this town

I stood there in the dark a while after hanging up
Let myself feel all of it for just a breath or two
Then I walked down to her doorway just to watch her chest rise slowly
And I let her be the reason I push through

She had your mouth and my stubbornness and something all her own
Some wild green thing that grew up through the stone
And I thought you called to find me but I think you called the past
And that woman doesn't live here anymore, she's gone

And you don't know what midnight looks like from this side
You don't know how quiet gets when the baby's finally down
You don't know what it costs me just to hear your voice that easy
Like you didn't leave me holding everything in this town

But I'm alright, I'm alright
I'm a little cracked but I am solid to the ground
I'm alright, I'm alright
I just had to let that midnight carry it on down

songs.subject: gospel / a first apartment
songs.lyrics:
I carried every box up four flights of stairs
With Destiny on my hip and a prayer on my lips
Lord the elevator's broke but I didn't care
When I turned that key I felt something shift

The walls were thin and the carpet was worn through
And the faucet in the kitchen had a drip
But it was mine, Lord it was mine, mine and hers
Every crack and every corner, every bit

Thank you for this roof above my baby's head
Thank you for this floor beneath our feet
Thank you for four walls and a secondhand bed
And the mercy that you tucked into this lease

I bought her a nightlight shaped like a little moon
Put her stuffed bear in the corner by the door
We ate our dinner on the floor that first night
Never had a table but we didn't need much more

I called my mama crying and she said baby
God made a way where there wasn't one to see
And I looked at Destiny just laughing at the echo
And I fell down on my knees in gratitude

Thank you for this roof above my baby's head
Thank you for this floor beneath our feet
Thank you for four walls and a secondhand bed
And the mercy that you tucked into this lease

I know it isn't much by the world's measuring
The neighbors loud, the hallway smells like rain
But I have walked through seasons that would break a body open
And I found myself still standing in the flame

So when I hung the curtains that my sister gave me
When I put her drawings up on the bedroom wall
When I made a home out of almost nothing
I heard the Lord say daughter you won't fall

Thank you for this roof above my baby's head
Thank you for this floor beneath our feet
Thank you for four walls and a secondhand bed
And the mercy that you tucked into this lease

Glory in the morning, glory at the sink
Glory in the bill I managed just to pay
Glory in my daughter sleeping sound beside me
Glory, glory, glory every day

I didn't need a mansion, I just needed solid ground
I just needed somewhere she could grow
And you gave me a beginning when I had nothing
You gave us a place to call our own

Thank you for this roof above my baby's head
Thank you for this floor beneath our feet
Thank you for four walls and a secondhand bed
And the mercy, all the mercy
All the mercy you gave me

songs.subject: alt rock / an old photograph
songs.lyrics:
Cracked along the edges, faded at the corners
Someone left you face-down in a drawer
The colors bled together, summer into winter
I don't even know what we were for

You were standing by the water
Squinting at the sun
I was standing just outside the frame
Pretending I was done

But I keep finding you in boxes
Keep finding you in walls
Keep finding you in everything that yellows
And curls
And falls
I keep finding you in margins
In the back of every room
An old photograph

Now older, turning gray

I wonder if they look like us

songs.subject: indie folk / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The bakery on Clement unlocks its door at four
Steam rising from the grates like the city's breathing more
A man walks his old dog past the bar that just closed down
Three in the morning and the quiet fills the town

A cab turns left on Market, no one flags it down
A woman reads a paperback in yellow light
The neon signs are talking to the wet and empty ground
Repeating themselves softly through the night

This is when the city lets its shoulders drop
This is when the corners hold their secrets soft
The architecture breathing in the dark
Every lit window is a world apart
Three in the morning
The city's finally alone
Three in the morning
The truest version of a home

The dispatcher on the radio reads numbers to no one

He's waiting for something, or he's letting something go
This city holds you even when you're cold

songs.lyrics:

Oklahoma at midnight, nothing but the line

The road don't ask me anything
And I don't have to answer back
Just me and the yellow lines and the dark

songs.subject: emo / moving to a new city
songs.lyrics:
Packed my life in cardboard boxes, taped them shut at 2 AM
Your voicemail still says that you love me, I keep playing it again
Signed the lease on some apartment, city I have never seen
Forty-seventh street or somewhere, somewhere lost and in between

And the skyline's just a stranger, and the streets don't know my name
Every light in every window holds a life that I can't claim
I came here to disappear but disappearing still feels wrong
Just a ghost in someone else's city trying to belong

Coffee shops with unknown faces, everyone has someone there
I've been sitting by the window watching couples everywhere
You'd have loved the architecture, you'd have loved the autumn air
But I'm learning how to love things for myself since you're not there

And the skyline's just a stranger, and the streets don't know my name
Every light in every window holds a life that I can't claim
I came here to disappear but disappearing still feels wrong
Just a ghost in someone else's city trying to belong

I ride the subway just to feel the motion underneath my feet

The tunnels swallow everything, the dark feels almost kind
A moment where the city holds the wreckage of my mind

And maybe I'll stop flinching every time my phone lights up
Maybe I'll stop ordering the thing you liked into my cup
Maybe by December I'll know which way is north from here
Maybe I'll stop missing you by the same time next year

And the skyline's just a stranger, and the streets don't know my name
Every light in every window holds a life that I can't claim
I came here to disappear but disappearing still feels wrong
Just a ghost in someone else's city trying to belong

But ghosts become familiar things
And strangers sometimes turn to friends
And cities that once swallowed you
Sometimes hold you in the end
And the skyline's still a stranger
But I'm starting not to mind
Cause everything you took from me
I'm slowly going to find

songs.subject: bluegrass / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
Well I been pouring whiskey every Friday since June
Watching folks come in and out underneath that harvest moon
I seen cowboys fall for ladies, seen ladies walk away
But nothing caught me like the moment she walked in that day

She had eyes like the river when it catches morning light
Laughing with her girlfriends by the lanterns burning bright
She ordered something simple, said she never much liked wine
And when she smiled across the bar I lost my place and time

Oh I fell hard at Miller's party on a Saturday in fall
Couldn't tell you what was in the glass I poured at all
The fiddle was still playing and the corn bread smelled like home
And I knew right then and there I didn't want to be alone
No I fell hard, Lord I fell hard
Right there behind that old oak bar
I fell hard at Miller's party
Underneath them August stars

Now I been known to keep my distance, keep things on the level side
Professional and steady with a clean and measured stride
But she leaned up on that barstool with her chin upon her hand
And asked me if I knew the name of that old fiddle band

I told her it was Henson boys from up the holler way
She said her granddaddy knew them back before his hair went gray
We talked between my pourings, talked between the rush and lull
And every time I had to walk away my chest felt heavy full

Oh I fell hard at Miller's party on a Saturday in fall
Couldn't tell you what was in the glass I poured at all
The fiddle was still playing and the corn bread smelled like home
And I knew right then and there I didn't want to be alone
No I fell hard, Lord I fell hard
Right there behind that old oak bar
I fell hard at Miller's party
Underneath them August stars

Well closing time came rolling like it always tends to do
Folks were finding jackets, finding rides and finding shoes
Her friends were pulling at her sleeve to get along the road
And I just wiped that bar top down and felt the lonesome load

But she turned back at the doorway and she hollered cross the room
Said maybe she'd come back next week if I was here come noon
I told her I was always here and she just smiled and waved
And I stood there in that empty bar half lost and half saved

Oh I fell hard at Miller's party on a Saturday in fall
Couldn't tell you what was in the glass I poured at all
The fiddle was still playing and the corn bread smelled like home
And I knew right then and there I didn't want to be alone
No I fell hard, Lord I fell hard
Right there behind that old oak bar
I fell hard at Miller's party
Underneath them August stars

She came back that next Tuesday and she sat on the same stool
And I told myself this time I wouldn't play the lovesick fool
But she ordered something simple and she smiled that river smile
And Lord I been right here behind this bar a good long while
And I fell hard again

songs.subject: blues / a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Drove out to the churchyard this cold and heavy morning
Black cars lining up the road without a single warning
Thought I knew what grief was, thought I had it figured out
But standing by that graveside I got nothing but the doubt

Reverend's talking softly bout a better place on high
But all I see is Tuesday and a man who said goodbye
Left his boots beside the doorway, left his coffee on the shelf
Left the rest of us down here to carry on ourself

Oh the ground is cold and stubborn and the sky won't shed a tear
Got a band that won't stop playing now that you ain't here
Every hymn feels like a question and the answers all ran dry
Just the blues, just the blues at the burying of a life

Wore my only good suit, Lord it's tighter than before
Last time that I wore it you were standing at my door
Said we'd get together, said we'd do it real soon
Now I'm counting out the sunsets that I spent without you

Your old hound dog won't stop howling from the yard across the way
Even animals know something holy left today
There's a table full of casseroles that no one's going to eat
And a house that's full of flowers and a silence too complete

Oh the ground is cold and stubborn and the sky won't shed a tear
Got a band that won't stop playing now that you ain't here
Every hymn feels like a question and the answers all ran dry
Just the blues, just the blues at the burying of a life

Somebody told a story bout the summer of sixty-three
How you danced all night in Mobile and you laughed until you couldn't breathe
And for just a minute standing there I almost felt you grin
Then the wind came off the hillside and I lost you back again

We don't talk enough to the ones we love I know that's true
Always think there's more time coming, always think there's more to do
Now I'm holding onto seconds like they're water in my hands
And I'm learning what it means to lose what no one understands

Oh the ground is cold and stubborn and the sky won't shed a tear
Got a band that won't stop playing now that you ain't here
Every hymn feels like a question and the answers all ran dry
Just the blues, just the blues at the burying of a life

Drive home past the diner where we always used to meet
The booth is sitting empty like it's waiting in the heat
I might go inside and sit there just to feel you near
Order black coffee and pretend that you'll appear

But you won't
No you won't
That's the blues
That's just the blues

songs.subject: ballad / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Seventy-three years of living
And I still don't know what this means
A little screen lit up this morning
With the coldest words I've ever seen
We've been walking hand in hand for decades
Through the hard times and the good
Now you're ending us in letters
Not the way I thought you would

How do you say goodbye in a text message
How do you close a door with your thumb
How do you break a heart in a blue bubble
How do you tell a man that he's done
I've written letters by candlelight
I've carved our names in an oak tree bark
But you chose a phone to end this story
Seven words that tore me apart

My hands are shaking holding this phone
These bifocals can't blur the sting
I read it six times hoping different
But it says the same goddamn thing
Fifty years I've known your laughter
Fifty years I've known your cry
And all I got this cold November morning
Was baby I think it's time goodbye

How do you say goodbye in a text message
How do you close a door with your thumb
How do you break a heart in a blue bubble
How do you tell a man that he's done
I've written letters by candlelight
I've carved our names in an oak tree bark
But you chose a phone to end this story
Seven words that tore me apart

My grandson had to show me how to answer
He said grandpa just press this little thing
I pressed it and I heard the silence after
And it was louder than a phone could ring
I walked out to the porch and sat there
Watched the neighbor walk his dog
Whole world just kept on moving forward
While I sat still like a rotting log

How do you say goodbye in a text message
How do you close a door with your thumb
How do you break a heart in a blue bubble
How do you tell a man that he's done
I've written letters by candlelight
I've carved our names in an oak tree bark
But you chose a phone to end this story
Seven words that tore me apart

Maybe you were scared to see my face fall
Maybe you were tired of explaining
Maybe seventy-three years ain't enough to learn
That love don't care about your remaining
I'll leave the phone on the kitchen table
Let the battery die and go cold
Some things deserve more than a message
Some goodbyes deserve to be told

songs.subject: bluegrass / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:
The dishes are done and the kids are in bed
And the kitchen light's humming above my head
Radio's playing some old country tune
And I got the whole floor to myself till noon
I push back the chairs and I kick off my shoes
A woman alone's got nothing to lose
I two-step with the shadow that falls on the wall
And Lord it feels good to feel anything at all

I dance alone in the kitchen tonight
No hand to hold but I'm holding on tight
The linoleum's cold but my heart's running warm
I'm the eye and the wind and the whole of the storm
Raise up my arms to nobody there
Let the fiddle run through me and fly in my hair
I dance alone in the kitchen tonight
And honey that's all right

I been working two jobs since the summer of nine
Keeping this house and this family in line
Tommy needs cleats and Gracie needs braces
And I need one minute without little faces
But the radio found me at quarter past ten
And it said hey darling come try it again
So I spun by the stove and I dipped by the sink
Lord I needed this more than I needed that drink

No hand to hold but I'm holding on tight
The linoleum's cold but my heart's running warm
I'm the eye and the wind and the whole of the storm
Raise up my arms to nobody there
Let the fiddle run through me and fly in my hair
I dance alone in the kitchen tonight
And honey that's all right

My mama used to say you find joy where you can
Don't wait on a good time and don't wait on a man
She danced in her kitchen in seventy-two
And I guess that I come by this honest and true
There's a bluegrass band playing on AM radio
And outside it's winter and cold don't you know
But in here I'm Loretta and Dolly and June
I'm dancing my heart out beneath the kitchen moon

I dance alone in the kitchen tonight
No hand to hold but I'm holding on tight
The linoleum's cold but my heart's running warm
I'm the eye and the wind and the whole of the storm
Raise up my arms to nobody there
Let the fiddle run through me and fly in my hair
I dance alone in the kitchen tonight
And honey that's all right

Some folks got ballrooms and some folks got bars
Some folks got partners underneath all the stars
But I got this kitchen and I got this tune
And I got two sleeping kids in the back room
And that's everything honey that's all that I need
One woman one radio one small victory
I'll dance till the station goes off for the night
And wake up tomorrow and I'll be all right

I'm the eye and the wind and the whole of the storm

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / chasing a dream
songs.lyrics:
You left on a Tuesday with your boxes and your cat
Said you loved me but you couldn't deal with that
Couldn't deal with all the dreaming and the late nights and the songs
Couldn't deal with barely making rent and moving along
So you took the better dishes and you took the picture frames
And I'm sitting in this empty place still answering to my name

But I got somewhere I'm going even if I go alone
Got a melody inside me that's been waiting to be known
You took the easy future and you took the plans we made
But you couldn't take the music and you couldn't take the faith
So I'm burning every bridge back to the life you wanted me to live
Got somewhere I'm going and I got something left to give

I played the coffee houses till my fingers bled
Slept in the back of a Subaru instead
Of calling home and saying that you might have had a point
That maybe I should quit and find a steadier joint
But the highway keeps on going and the songs keep coming through
And I'm finally getting somewhere without you

I got somewhere I'm going even if I go alone
Got a melody inside me that's been waiting to be known
You took the easy future and you took the plans we made
But you couldn't take the music and you couldn't take the faith
So I'm burning every bridge back to the life you wanted me to live
Got somewhere I'm going and I got something left to give

You said the dream would kill us and I said the dream's what lives
You said be practical and I said that's no way to live
And neither one of us was wrong but only one of us was right
I chose the stage and you chose comfort and I'm choosing it tonight
In this little bar in Cincinnati with twenty people here
I'm singing like it's Madison Square Garden every year

I got somewhere I'm going even if I go alone
Got a melody inside me that's been waiting to be known
You took the easy future and you took the plans we made
But you couldn't take the music and you couldn't take the faith
So I'm burning every bridge back to the life you wanted me to live
Got somewhere I'm going and I got something left to give

Maybe someday you'll hear something on the radio
And recognize the voice you used to know
And maybe you'll be proud of me or maybe you'll regret
But mostly I just hope that you're okay and I'm not bitter yet
Mostly I'm just grateful that the heartbreak came in time
Before I traded every dream I had for yours and none for mine

I got somewhere I'm going even if I go alone
Got a melody inside me that's been waiting to be known
You took the easy future and you took the plans we made
But you couldn't take the music and you couldn't take the faith
So I'm burning every bridge back to the life you wanted me to live
Got somewhere I'm going and I got something left to give
Yeah I got somewhere I'm going
And I'm going there alive

songs.subject: americana / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
The gutters are full and the clouds are low
Church bells rang an hour ago
Coffee's gone cold on the windowsill
And the whole town's quiet and the whole town's still
Dog sleeps curled at the foot of the door
Rain taps its fingers on the hardwood floor
Nowhere to be and nobody coming
Just the grey sky outside and the old pipes humming

Rainy Sunday lay me down slow
Nowhere the river don't already know
Wash the week clean off my bones
Let the thunder roll me home
The whole world's softer under all this grey
God must rest on a Sunday anyway
Rainy Sunday take your time
I ain't got nothing but this life of mine

Drove through town just to see what's there
Dollar store's closed and the lot is bare
The diner's got three trucks outside
Old farmers talking about the flooding tide
Stopped and got a piece of cherry pie
Sat and watched the rain streak down the window by
Nobody asked me and I didn't say
Just stirred my coffee and I let it all fade away

Rainy Sunday lay me down slow
Nowhere the river don't already know
Wash the week clean off my bones
Let the thunder roll me home
The whole world's softer under all this grey
God must rest on a Sunday anyway
Rainy Sunday take your time
I ain't got nothing but this life of mine

I found an old letter in a coat I hadn't worn
The handwriting familiar and the edges slightly torn
Some Sunday years ago when things were different here
Different faces on the porch and a different time of year
I held it to the window and I let the grey light in
Read it once and folded it and put it back again
Some things are like Sundays they don't need a destination
Just a little room to breathe and a little of your patience

Rainy Sunday lay me down slow
Nowhere the river don't already know
Wash the week clean off my bones
Let the thunder roll me home
The whole world's softer under all this grey
God must rest on a Sunday anyway
Rainy Sunday take your time
I ain't got nothing but this life of mine

From flat grey white to something amber-veined
A thin crack opened underneath the clouds
And lit the fields up golden and loud
Then closed again and the rain came back harder
The creek behind the barn getting fuller and darker
I brought the dog inside and locked the door
And listened to this old house breathe some more

Nowhere the river don't already know
Wash the week clean off my bones
Let the thunder roll me home
The whole world's softer under all this grey
God must rest on a Sunday anyway
Rainy Sunday take your time
I ain't got nothing but this life of mine
No I ain't got nothing but this life of mine

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
I saw you standing by the window light
A plastic cup of something red held tight
Your laugh cut through the noise like it was meant for me
I pretended not to notice two or three times

Someone introduced us on the stairs
You shook my hand and fixed a strand of your hair
I wrote the whole thing down when I got home that night
Like maybe if I held it still it wouldn't die

And I don't know what this is yet
But my heart was doing something I can't quite forget
The music was too loud and the room was too full
But there you were and everything went pull

I stayed until the lights came on inside
Helped clean the cups just so I'd have more time
You put your number in my phone with a little star
I walked home slow and counted every car

And I don't know what this is yet
But my heart was doing something I can't quite forget
The music was too loud and the room was too full
But there you were and everything went pull

I keep replaying every single word
Every half a sentence that I almost heard
The way you leaned against the wall when you were laughing hard
I think I'm writing something I can't discard

And I don't know what this is yet
But my heart was doing something I can't quite forget
The music was too loud and the room was too full
But there you were and everything went pull
And there you were and everything went pull

songs.subject: lo-fi / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
Forty dollars till the first of the month
Ramen on the stove and you hum along
The radiator knocks like it's keeping time
Baby we are broke but baby we are fine

You put your feet up on the coffee table edge
We watch the rain come sideways off the ledge
The Wi-Fi's slow and the couch has seen some years
But you're still here and that makes everything clear

And I don't need the things I thought I needed before
Got your laugh and these thin walls and nothing more
Counting quarters for the laundromat machine
This is the most alive that I have ever been

We share a plate and split the last of the bread
You fall asleep on my shoulder instead
Of going out like all our friends tonight
Sometimes staying in feels like getting it right

And I don't need the things I thought I needed before
Got your laugh and these thin walls and nothing more
Counting quarters for the laundromat machine
This is the most alive that I have ever been

The ceiling fan is missing one of its blades
The window lets the cold in anyway
But you reach over in the dark and find my hand
I swear this life is more than I had planned

Made coffee with the last of what we had
You called it fancy and it made me glad
The whole world rushing somewhere we can't afford
Baby I am rich in ways they can't record

And I don't need the things I thought I needed before
Got your laugh and these thin walls and nothing more
Counting quarters for the laundromat machine

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
Your bedroom window's dark on Clement Street
I drove by once just out of habit I think
The coffee shop where we would always sit
Has got new chairs and I still can't get used to it

I heard you found an apartment with a view
That the winters there are cold but so are you
I mean that in the kindest way I know
Some people shine brighter in the snow

And I miss you like a room that's missing furniture
Everything echoes now and the shape of things looks different here
I keep reaching for the phone to tell you something small
But the distance answers first before you get my call

Your mother says you're doing really well
You cut your hair and you've got stories to tell
I smile and nod and say I'm glad to hear
Then drive home quiet through another year

And I miss you like a room that's missing furniture
Everything echoes now and the shape of things looks different here
I keep reaching for the phone to tell you something small
But the distance answers first before you get my call

I still have the mix CD you made
The case is cracked and some of the ink has faded
Track seven skips but I don't skip it anymore
I think that scratch is something worth listening for

The city kept on going after you
New restaurants opened, same old morning dew
I'm not complaining, I just want you here
Or at least to feel like you're not disappearing

And I miss you like a room that's missing furniture
Everything echoes now and the shape of things looks different here
I keep reaching for the phone to tell you something small
But the distance answers first before you get my call
The distance answers first before you get my call

songs.subject: disco / an old photograph
songs.lyrics:
Pulled the shoebox from the closet shelf last night
Lord have mercy on the things that came to light
There you are in seventy-nine with feathered hair
Platform shoes and not a single worry anywhere

I forgot about that dress, was it purple, was it blue
The photograph has yellowed but it still looks just like you
Spinning on the floor of someone's basement flat
Oh honey I would give just about anything for that

Oh take me back, take me back to the glitter on the wall
The mirror ball was turning and we had it all
We were young and we were golden and the night was never done
Take me back, take me back, oh let me run

There's Jimmy in the corner with his collar popped up high
And Donna in the middle always catching every eye
The DJ had us moving like the world was going to end
I would dance that whole night over and over again

Oh take me back, take me back to the glitter on the wall
The mirror ball was turning and we had it all
We were young and we were golden and the night was never done
Take me back, take me back, oh let me run

I'm writing this down tonight because I want to remember
