My heart was barely twenty and it didn't know a thing
Except the way the bass line hit and how the horns would sing

There's a lipstick smudge along the corner of the frame
I don't know whose it was but I love it all the same
Evidence of something that was sweet and loud and true
Every glossy faded inch of me was pointing straight at you

Oh take me back, take me back to the glitter on the wall
The mirror ball was turning and we had it all

Take me back, take me back, oh let me run

songs.subject: soul / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
The clock strikes twelve and the silence breaks
Your number shows, my whole body shakes
Been so long since I heard your voice
Didn't know I'd be this lost for choice

Pick it up or let it ring
Either way you're everything
Three years gone and still you call
Baby catch me, I'm about to fall

Midnight on the telephone
You bring me back when I'm all alone
Every word you say cuts through the dark
Still got your number memorized by heart
Midnight on the telephone
Honey I been missing you since you've been gone
The hour don't matter when it's you on the line
Midnight, baby, and you're still mine

I can hear you breathing slow
Saying things I already know
That the distance got too wide
But you still keep me on your side

Pick it up or let it ring

Midnight on the telephone

songs.subject: R&B / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
Mama put my hair up about a hundred times
My auntie cried before we even left the driveway
And the flowers were stuck in the backseat the whole ride

I never really thought about what this day meant
Until I saw the way she looked walking down that hall
My cousin Jordan hit me like hey you crying
I said nah something's in my eye that's all

But it's real though, it's so real though
Standing here watching love become a promise kept
It's real though, yeah it's real though
Didn't know I'd feel this much I haven't even wept
It's real though

The reception got loud around nine or ten
Uncle Marcus doing way too much on the floor
Grandma Rose sat in the corner just smiling
Like she'd been waiting on this day since before

They cut the cake and fed each other slow
And everybody pulled their phones out for the kiss
I just stood there in my bridesmaid dress thinking
Someday I want somebody to look at me like this

Cause it's real though, it's so real though
Standing here watching love become a promise kept
It's real though, yeah it's real though
Didn't know I'd feel this much I haven't even wept
It's real though

And when they played that song, that old song
The one her daddy used to sing her growing up
She buried her face against his shoulder
And that man held her like he'd never get enough

I grabbed my little brother's sleeve and held it
Told him one day he'd understand what this is for
He said ew and pulled away and I just laughed
But I felt something open in me like a door

Cause it's real though, it's so real though
Standing here watching love become a promise kept
It's real though, yeah it's real though
Didn't know I'd feel this much I haven't even wept
It's real though

They drove away with cans tied to the bumper
And we all stood waving in the parking lot lights
And something about being fifteen and standing there
Made the whole world feel both small and infinite tonight

It's real though
Love is real though
Even I can see it now
It's real

songs.subject: lo-fi / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Rain hitting the window above the sink
Put on an old record, pour a little drink
The kitchen's quiet, just the needle and the hum
Been a long old life but the night ain't done

Shuffle my feet on the linoleum floor
Same floor we laid back in sixty four
She picked the pattern, yellow and cream
Lord this old kitchen's seen everything

Just me and the music and the low lamplight
Dancing alone on a Tuesday night
Nobody watching, nobody here to see
Just the ghost of who I used to be
Moving slow, that's alright
I got nowhere I need to be tonight
Just me and the music and the low lamplight
Dancing alone, and I feel alright

The song changes over, something sweet and low
Billie or Ella, I don't quite know
But it moves through my chest like it always did
When we'd dance in here back when the kids were kids

She'd dry her hands on that old dish towel
And I'd pull her close, she'd start to smile
Said Harold you're a fool, I'd say yes I am
Still the luckiest fool in all the land

Just me and the music and the low lamplight
Dancing alone on a Tuesday night
Nobody watching, nobody here to see
Just the ghost of who I used to be
Moving slow, that's alright
I got nowhere I need to be tonight
Just me and the music and the low lamplight
Dancing alone, and I feel alright

My knees ain't what they were in '68
And I hold the counter when the turning gets great
But there's something still in these old bones
That remembers joy like remembers home

The record ends and the needle just spins
I stand here breathing, then I start again
Lift the arm up, set it back at the start
Some songs you just gotta hear with your whole heart

Just me and the music and the low lamplight
Dancing alone on a Tuesday night
Nobody watching, nobody here to see
Just the ghost of who I used to be
Moving slow, that's alright
I got nowhere I need to be tonight
Just me and the music and the low lamplight
Dancing alone, and I feel alright

I feel her sometimes in the way the song bends
In the warm hum of the room before the night ends
I close my eyes and I hold my own hand
And I dance as long as I still can

songs.subject: trap / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this mornin' got my groomsmen on the phone
Told 'em pull up to the suite we ain't doin' this alone
Mama pressed my suit last night got tears up in her eyes
Said boy you found yourself a good one don't you let her slide
Jewelry on the dresser got the cufflinks lookin' right
Been waitin' on this woman gonna wife her up tonight
Pockets feeling heavy got the ring inside my coat
Every word I wrote for her been stuck up in my throat

She walkin' down the aisle now and I swear I almost broke
Reverend said some words but I ain't heard a single quote
Only see her face right now the veil blowin' in the air
God I thank you every day I found somebody there
This a diary entry yeah I'm writin' this for us
From the trap to the chapel baby built it from the dust
Told myself at seventeen I'd never see this day
Now I'm standin' at the altar got so much that I wanna say

I do I do I do
Everything I promised you
From the bottom now we rise
Baby look me in my eyes
I do I do I do
Every morning I choose you
This ain't just a pretty day
This is where we start to pray

Reception got the family going crazy in the back
Uncle Jerome already hit the bar and that's a fact
Granny on the dance floor moving better than us all
Cousins taking pictures getting videos for the hall
Wrote a speech but threw it out and spoke it from my chest
Said you make me want to be a man who gives his very best
Every scar and every struggle every night I couldn't sleep
Led me to this moment and it's mine and it's yours to keep

I do I do I do
Everything I promised you
From the bottom now we rise
Baby look me in my eyes
I do I do I do
Every morning I choose you
This ain't just a pretty day
This is where we start to pray

Midnight now the venue's closing everyone is gone
Just the two of us remaining with the slow song on
Took my jacket off and held her close against my chest
She said baby I'm so tired and I said get some rest
This a diary entry dated summer of the year
That I finally understood what it meant to have someone near
Wrote it down so when we old and when our memory fades
We can read it back together and remember all our days

I do I do I do
Everything I promised you
From the bottom now we rise
Baby look me in my eyes
I do I do I do
Every morning I choose you
This ain't just a pretty day
This is where we start to pray

Started something beautiful
Gonna see it through
Every single day I breathe
I give it all to you
Started something beautiful
Gonna see it through
This is where it all begins
Me and only you

songs.subject: reggae / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
Behind this bar I seen a thousand kinds of pain
But nothing cuts like watching people board that plane
They come in rolling suitcases and swollen eyes
Order something strong and stare up at the skies
I pour them one more drink and wipe the counter clean
And wonder 'bout the stories playing in between

This woman came in Sunday with her daughter small
Said she flying back to Kingston after all
The daughter maybe seven couldn't understand
Why mama kept on squeezing her tiny hand
I mixed them something sweet a juice for the little one
And watched them hold each other till the flight was done

Every goodbye got a story every story got a tear
I been behind this bar so long I feel them in the air
The music keep on playing but the people keep on leaving
And love don't stop for airports love don't stop for grieving
One more rum and cola and a blessing that they go
Safe across the water safe through winds that blow

A young man came in Thursday with a photograph
He showed me like he needed someone just to laugh
Or cry or something real with just a stranger's ear
Said his father living overseas for seven years
He only getting visits twice a year at most
And every time he leaves it feel like losing him the most

I told him I know something 'bout the ones who go
I had a woman once I let her board and didn't know
That goodbye at the gate was gonna be our last
Some goodbyes they become the future not the past
So hold your people tightly at the terminal door
'Cause life don't always give you what you're asking for

Every goodbye got a story every story got a tear
I been behind this bar so long I feel them in the air
The music keep on playing but the people keep on leaving
And love don't stop for airports love don't stop for grieving
One more rum and cola and a blessing that they go
Safe across the water safe through winds that blow

I seen soldiers and I seen students I seen broken wives
I seen fathers with their children trying to hold their lives
Together at the seams right here at gate fourteen
This little bar between the worlds this space in between
I give them something warm to hold before the flight
Something for the stomach when the heart don't feel right

I don't know where they going and I don't know where they been
But every soul that passes through I give them all I can
A little kindness in the pour a steady hand that's true
'Cause everybody leaving here is going somewhere new
And maybe that new somewhere holds a joy they haven't known
Maybe every goodbye is just the way we all find home

Every goodbye got a story every story got a tear
I been behind this bar so long I feel them in the air
The music keep on playing but the people keep on leaving
And love don't stop for airports love don't stop for grieving
One more rum and cola and a blessing that they go
Safe across the water safe through winds that blow

Safe across the water
Let the good wind blow
Safe across the water
Let the good love grow
Every soul departing
Carries something bright
Safe across the water
Safe into the light

One more rum and cola and a blessing that they go
Safe across the water safe through winds that blow

Safe across the water
Let the good wind blow
Safe across the water
Let the good love grow
Every soul departing
Carries something bright
Safe across the water
Safe into the light

songs.subject: soul / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
Check my pockets every morning just to see what's there
Usually find some lint and maybe bus fare
Mama said the rent is due and I already know
But something in my chest is warm despite the cold
My sneakers got a hole but I walk like I'm a king
'Cause rich ain't in the wallet it's a different kind of thing

My friends and I we met up at the park last night
Brought some chips and somebody's old guitar and we were right
There underneath the stars just singing off the key
Nobody had a dollar but we laughed until we couldn't breathe
This the kind of thing they can't take when they come collecting
This the kind of thing that's worth the most when you start reflecting

I'm broke but baby I'm alive
Got nothing in my hands but joy helping me survive
They say you need the money just to feel like you are something
But I been feeling everything with barely nothing
I'm broke but I'm on fire
Burning with a kind of love that money can't acquire
Maybe someday I'll have more and that'll be alright
But tonight I'm rich in ways you can't count up at night

My little brother asked me why I'm always laughing so
I said because the other option is to let it go
And I refuse to let the weight of what we lack
Define the forward motion or pull me looking back
We got each other and we got this neighborhood
We got the corner store lady who treats us real good

My teacher told me I got something special in my mind
Said the kind of thinking that I do is hard to find
And even though my notebook's got the cover falling off
And my lunch is half of what it should be it's enough
I carry what I got and I carry it with pride
Ain't nothing broken 'bout the fire burning inside

I'm broke but baby I'm alive
Got nothing in my hands but joy helping me survive
They say you need the money just to feel like you are something
But I been feeling everything with barely nothing
I'm broke but I'm on fire
Burning with a kind of love that money can't acquire
Maybe someday I'll have more and that'll be alright
But tonight I'm rich in ways you can't count up at night

Saturday we cooked the last of everything we had
Made a feast from rice and whatever else we grabbed
Grandma sat up at the table said a prayer so long
By the time she finished all the food was good and gone
We laughed and it echoed through the whole apartment building
And I thought to myself this right here is the feeling

Can't buy this moment with a credit card or loan
Can't manufacture what we built here in this home
I seen people with the everything who still feel hollow
I seen people with the nothing who still choose tomorrow
I choose tomorrow every single time the morning comes
Even when the bank account is barely showing ones

I'm broke but baby I'm alive
Got nothing in my hands but joy helping me survive
They say you need the money just to feel like you are something
But I been feeling everything with barely nothing
I'm broke but I'm on fire
Burning with a kind of love that money can't acquire
Maybe someday I'll have more and that'll be alright
But tonight I'm rich in ways you can't count up at night

I'm rich in ways you can't count
I'm rich in ways you can't count
Everything that matters is already in this house
I'm rich in ways you can't count
I'm rich in ways you can't count

songs.subject: R&B / insomnia
songs.lyrics:
Two forty-seven staring at the ceiling fan
Got the baby down by nine she don't understand
Why her mama lays awake and counts the hours slow
Got a mind that won't stop running nowhere left to go
Tried the tea and tried the music tried the meditation app
But the thoughts just keep on coming and they won't fall back

Bills I gotta pay on Friday sitting in my brain
Text I should have answered weeks ago still on my chain
Did I sign the permission slip did I wash her uniform
Is the car insurance current is the lease renewed
Every single question that I couldn't ask out loud
Comes and finds me in the darkness wrapped up in its shroud

I can't sleep tonight
And it ain't 'cause I ain't tired
Been running since before the sun
And still my mind's on fire
Just one hour baby just one hour is all I need
But the night don't give me nothing but this quiet that won't leave
I can't sleep I can't sleep
This silence is too loud for me

Used to be I'd have someone to pull me close and say
Baby lay your head down we'll deal with it today
But it's just me now just me and the ceiling and the dark
And the sound of her breathing coming gentle from the hall
So I get up slow and stand beside her bedroom door
Just to watch her chest rise up and fall once more

She don't know how much I carry she just knows I'm there
Every morning with the breakfast with the braided hair
She don't know I cried at three a.m. last Tuesday night
Or that sometimes I'm so scared I can't get the breathing right
She just knows her mama shows up and that's what I'll do
Even when the night takes everything I got from me and you

I can't sleep tonight
And it ain't 'cause I ain't tired
Been running since before the sun
And still my mind's on fire
Just one hour baby just one hour is all I need
But the night don't give me nothing but this quiet that won't leave
I can't sleep I can't sleep
This silence is too loud for me

Four fifteen now and the city's starting up outside
Garbage trucks and early workers passing by
In another hour I'll be up to start the whole routine
Scrambled eggs and backpack check and everything in between
I don't know how I do it but I do it every day
I guess love is just the engine that don't quit and won't decay

Sometimes I wonder what it feel like to rest
To lay my body down and not feel something pressing on my chest
To dream a dream that isn't tangled up in fear
To wake up in the morning feeling light and clear
I remember that I used to but it feels so long ago
Before the weight of raising her alone became all that I know

I can't sleep tonight
And it ain't 'cause I ain't tired
Been running since before the sun
And still my mind's on fire
Just one hour baby just one hour is all I need
But the night don't give me nothing but this quiet that won't leave
I can't sleep I can't sleep
This silence is too loud for me

But I'm still here
Through every sleepless night I'm still here
Through every worry heavy as a stone I'm still here
She's gonna wake up and I'm gonna be standing
Tired and unbroken and still understanding
That every sleepless night I gave was worth it
Every night I couldn't sleep had purpose
I can't sleep
But Lord I'm still here

songs.subject: emo / growing up in a small town
songs.lyrics:
I pour the drinks and watch the clock spin round
Another Friday night in this one-horse town
The same old faces sitting on the same old stools
We graduated together, we all played by the rules
Billy talks about the girl he never got to keep
Jenny shows her phone to anyone who'll speak
And I wipe the bar down like I've done a thousand times
Trapped between the liquor and the neon signs

This town's got a way of holding on too tight
Like the smell of cigarettes that soaks into the night
We all swore we'd leave when we turned eighteen
But here we are still haunting the same old scene
Growing up was something that just happened to our bones
But something in the water keeps us close to home
I shake the bottles empty, pour the sorrows down the drain
Small town living is a beautiful kind of pain

Tommy used to say he'd be a famous kind of man
He'd drive his daddy's pickup with a map spread on his hands
Now he tips me heavy when the loneliness gets loud
And his eyes still hold the thunder of a boy who dreamed out loud
Sarah brings her baby in before the late crowd shows
She's got her mama's tired smile and her daddy's crooked nose
And I see myself reflected in the glass behind the bar
A kid who stayed too long and now can't find where the edges are

This town's got a way of holding on too tight
Like the smell of cigarettes that soaks into the night
We all swore we'd leave when we turned eighteen
But here we are still haunting the same old scene
Growing up was something that just happened to our bones
But something in the water keeps us close to home
I shake the bottles empty, pour the sorrows down the drain
Small town living is a beautiful kind of pain

Last call is coming and the jukebox fades to low
The parking lot is empty underneath the amber glow
I lock the door behind me like I always do alone
Walk the six blocks quietly back to what I call my home
And I think about the highway and the places it could lead
About the version of myself who got to chase what he believed
But morning always finds me and I'm back behind the bar
Mixing up the same old drinks beneath the same old stars

songs.subject: bluegrass / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
Lord knows I ain't a perfect soul
I've made my bed in the bitter cold
Left my baby crying in the hall
While I was too proud to hear him call
But the creek don't stop for foolish things
And grace comes down on broken wings
I found myself on my knees one night
Begging you to make the burden light

Forgiveness flows like a mountain stream
Washing over every shattered dream
I laid my anger on the altar stone
And found I wasn't walking this alone
My boy needs a mother who can let things go
Forgiveness is the only seed I'll sow
I can't keep carrying what the past has done
Gonna lay it down beneath the morning sun

His daddy left when the leaves turned brown
Didn't even drive through this old town
Just a letter in a coffee can
Said he wasn't ready to be anyone's man
And Lord I held that bitterness like fire
Fed it every night until it grew higher
But my boy's got his daddy's way of smiling wide
And I can't let that hatred live inside

Forgiveness flows like a mountain stream
Washing over every shattered dream
I laid my anger on the altar stone
And found I wasn't walking this alone
My boy needs a mother who can let things go
Forgiveness is the only seed I'll sow
I can't keep carrying what the past has done
Gonna lay it down beneath the morning sun

Mama said you reap what you've always sown
You can't grow wildflowers in a field of stone
So I walked out to the river's edge one day
And I let that old resentment wash away
Said I forgive you even though you'll never hear
Said I forgive me for the wasted years
Said I forgive it all and I meant every word
And somewhere in the pines I swear the whole world stirred

My boy looked up at me with those big brown eyes
Said mama why you crying at the sky
I said I'm just letting something heavy go
So we can have more room to let our garden grow

Forgiveness flows like a mountain stream
Washing over every shattered dream
I laid my anger on the altar stone
And found I wasn't walking this alone
My boy needs a mother who can let things go
Forgiveness is the only seed I'll sow
I can't keep carrying what the past has done
Gonna lay it down beneath the morning sun

Gonna lay it down, gonna lay it down
Beneath the morning sun

songs.subject: rock / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
Your barstool's been empty going on six months now
I keep your usual ready out of some old habit somehow
The regulars ask about you and I say you're doing fine
But the honest truth is I haven't heard a word since the county line

You packed your life in boxes on a cold November day
Said you needed something bigger and I said okay
Now I'm here beneath these buzzing lights and the classic rock on low
Talking to a ghost of you in every glass I pour

Come back to where the bar light's warm
Come back before I lose the form
Of your face in my memory
You're just a city now to me
A dot on someone else's map
A story with a second act
I'm still stuck here in the first
Missing you is the longest thirst
I can't drink enough to drown

The new kid at your barstool doesn't know our old routine
Doesn't ask for the back booth or the pickled stuff or what your Wednesday's mean
And I smile and take his order like the professional I am
But I'm somewhere on a highway chasing taillights to the end

Come back to where the bar light's warm
Come back before I lose the form
Of your face in my memory
You're just a city now to me
A dot on someone else's map
A story with a second act
I'm still stuck here in the first
Missing you is the longest thirst
I can't drink enough to drown

I heard you got a job there and a girl with city eyes
I heard the rent's outrageous but you said it's worth the price
And I'm happy if you're happy that's the line I've learned to say
But happy people don't pour out a drink and watch it fade away

You called me once at two a.m. a little lost it seemed
Said the city's loud and bright but it ain't quite what it means
I almost said come home but I bit my tongue in two
Because the last thing that you need is me becoming something you ran from too

Come back to where the bar light's warm
Come back before I lose the form
Of your face in my memory
You're just a city now to me
A dot on someone else's map
A story with a second act
I'm still stuck here in the first
Missing you is the longest thirst
I can't drink enough to drown

I close up late and walk out in the cold alone
The street's quiet and the distance feels like it could swallow bone
I check my phone for nothing and I lock the door up tight
And somewhere you're out living and I hope it feels right

songs.subject: lo-fi / faith
songs.lyrics:
I been sitting on this porch since half past five
Watching the light come slow and feeling still alive
Seventy-eight years and a tired pair of hands
But something holds me up that I don't fully understand

I talked to God this morning like I do most every day
Not the kind of talking where you got the fancy words to say
Just a quiet conversation with the sky above the pine
He don't always answer but I know he gets it fine

It ain't loud and it ain't shining
It's just there like the wood beneath the floor
It's the thing that kept me breathing
When I didn't think I had the strength for more
Faith don't always look like mountains moving
Sometimes it's just making it to dawn
A small and quiet certainty
That says you're not alone so carry on

Lost my Margaret fourteen years ago last spring
The house went hollow quiet and I couldn't feel a thing
For a long while I was angry at the sky above this land
But something gentle found me and took hold of my old hand

The preacher says believe it and I do but not like him
My faith is worn and weathered from the years of living in
The space between the hoping and the not quite knowing why
It's a humble kind of trusting that I've carried till I die

It ain't loud and it ain't shining
It's just there like the wood beneath the floor
It's the thing that kept me breathing
When I didn't think I had the strength for more
Faith don't always look like mountains moving
Sometimes it's just making it to dawn
A small and quiet certainty
That says you're not alone so carry on

My grandson came to visit and he said grandpa don't you fear
All the things you can't control and all the end that might be near
I said son I been afraid before and fear ain't got the last
The thing about believing is it outlives all your past

I showed him how the garden comes back every single year
Even after frost and drought it finds a way to reappear
Said whatever made that happen made the stars and made your bones
And whatever holds the universe ain't gonna let you face it alone

It ain't loud and it ain't shining
It's just there like the wood beneath the floor
It's the thing that kept me breathing
When I didn't think I had the strength for more
Faith don't always look like mountains moving
Sometimes it's just making it to dawn
A small and quiet certainty
That says you're not alone so carry on

The sun is getting higher and my coffee's getting cold
And I'm just an ordinary man getting ordinary old
But I'll be on this porch tomorrow watching morning do its thing
Grateful for the quiet and whatever grace it brings

Grateful for the quiet and whatever grace it brings

songs.subject: synthpop / working a dead-end job
songs.lyrics:
Punch in at seven, punch out at nine
Fluorescent lights burning out my mind
Register beeping, same old routine
Seventeen years old and already I'm seeing
What they never told us in the classroom dreams
That life's a conveyor belt and you're stuck in between

I'm just a number on a name tag here
Counting down the minutes, watching disappear
Every single second of the life I planned
With my hands in the register, trading dreams for bland
Someone tell me this ain't all there is
Someone tell me I was born for more than this

Manager's breathing down my neck again
Telling me to smile for the customers and then
Go restock the shelves in aisle number four
My feet are killing me and my soul wants more
I drew these circuits on my notebook in class
Imagined buildings made of steel and glass

I'm just a number on a name tag here
Counting down the minutes, watching disappear
Every single second of the life I planned
With my hands in the register, trading dreams for bland
Someone tell me this ain't all there is
Someone tell me I was born for more than this

The radio's playing something I used to love
Now it's background noise from the speakers above
Old man Jerry's been here twenty years
He laughs a little too loud and his eyes hold tears
I look at him and feel the future close
Like a velvet curtain and I'm fighting the ropes

Saturday night and my friends are out
Living all the things I'm dreaming about
Parties and laughing and staying up late
I'm closing down the register, locking the gate
Wiping down counters, turning out lights
Adding up nothing for another night

I'm just a number on a name tag here
Counting down the minutes, watching disappear
Every single second of the life I planned
With my hands in the register, trading dreams for bland
Someone tell me this ain't all there is
Someone tell me I was born for more than this

I was born for more than this
I was born for more than this
Seventeen and already wondering
If the dreams I have are just splintering
Into dust beneath these fluorescent lights
Into dust, into dust, into the nights

songs.subject: trap / heartbreak
songs.lyrics:
Yeah, she left me on a Tuesday
Didn't even say why
Just a text that said I'm sorry
And I watched those three words like they multiply
Into everything I'm feeling
Crawling up inside my chest
Sixteen and I thought I knew love
Now I'm failing every single test

She was everything I posted bout
Had her name up in my phone
Now I'm staring at our pictures
In the dark just all alone
Deleted then undeleted
Must've done that forty times
She's still wearing my old hoodie
In somebody else's Insta lines

It hurt, yeah it hurt, yeah it hurt like a blade
Every promise that we made
It hurt, yeah it hurt, yeah it hurt like a war
Don't know what we were fighting for
She gone, she gone, but her ghost stay
Haunting every single day
It hurt, yeah it hurt
And I can't make it go away

My boys telling me to move on
Play the game and keep it real
But they ain't never loved somebody

Friday nights we used to drive round
Playing music way too loud
Now I'm sitting in my bedroom
Can't even face the crowd

She's already got somebody
Saw them together at the mall
She looked happy, almost waved at me
I had to stare down at the wall
Sixteen shouldn't feel this heavy
Sixteen shouldn't feel this old
But heartbreak doesn't check your ID
Just comes in and takes your soul

So nobody sees me slip

Yeah I know I'm just a teenager
Yeah I know I'll be okay

Every movie scene burns deep
I been tired since she left me

songs.subject: rock / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
I been pouring drinks since half past four
Got a hundred people crowding up this floor
White dress spinning underneath the chandelier
The groom looks like he hasn't breathed all year
Uncle Danny's already on his third
Best man's rehearsing every single word

I've seen a hundred weddings from this side of the bar
Some of them get ugly, some of them go far
Some of them end early, some of them end late
I've seen love turn cold somewhere around half past eight
But tonight feels different, I can feel it in the room
The way she's looking at him, the way he watches the bloom

Of something real between two people
Who somehow found each other in this world
I'll pour another whiskey for the father
Who's still trying to believe he's lost his girl
They're dancing to that song that everyone knows
And I'm watching from the edges as the evening glows

Pour one more, pour one more
I'm the keeper of the stories they'll forget
Pour one more, pour one more
I'm the witness that nobody's noticed yet
Behind this bar I'm watching history
All the pieces of this family come to be
Pour one more, pour one more
For the ones who made it here tonight

Bridesmaid's crying in the corner, bless her heart
Known the bride since both of them were falling apart
At nineteen in some college town in rain
Swore they'd be at every wedding, here they came
Flower girl fell asleep beneath the table
The DJ's testing if this stereo is able

Old couple in the corner, seventy years
She adjusts his tie and he pretends he doesn't tear
I watch them watch the young ones take the floor
And remember being something worth dancing for
They order ginger ales and sip them slow
And lean together like they've got nowhere else to go

Pour one more, pour one more
I'm the keeper of the stories they'll forget
Pour one more, pour one more
I'm the witness that nobody's noticed yet
Behind this bar I'm watching history
All the pieces of this family come to be
Pour one more, pour one more
For the ones who made it here tonight

The kitchen staff is betting on the speech
The mother of the groom is slightly out of reach
Of sober but she's holding herself proud
And crying just below the level of the crowd
Somebody's grandmother is dancing with a child
And the whole room stops to watch and almost smiles

I've been bartending weddings fifteen years
I've mopped up spills and I have mopped up tears
I've called the cabs and cut the angry drunks
I've listened to the lonely in their funks
But every now and then there comes a night
Where everything inside this room is right

Where the flower girl wakes up and steals the show
Where the DJ plays the one song that you know
Will make the whole room rush the floor at once
Where even the cynic in me just surrenders
To the strange and stubborn miracle of this
Two people standing still inside their bliss

Pour one more, pour one more
I'm the keeper of the stories they'll forget
Pour one more, pour one more
I'm the witness that nobody's noticed yet
Behind this bar I'm watching history
All the pieces of this family come to be
Pour one more, pour one more
For the ones who made it here tonight

Last call comes around at half past one
Half the room is dancing, half of them are done
Bride's got her heels off, groom's lost his tie
Best man's buying rounds and saying his goodbye
I'm wiping down the bar and stacking glass
Watching out the window as the limousine rolls past

And I think about my own life as I close
About the ones I loved and the ones I let go
About the way that love is its own kind of brave
The way it asks you give it everything you have
I'll be back next Saturday, another bride
Another family filling up the inside

But tonight I'll drive home quiet through the dark
With something warm and hopeful in my heart
Because I poured the drinks and no one saw
But I witnessed something beautiful and raw
Fifteen years behind this bar and still
Love finds a way to give me back my will

Pour one more, pour one more
I'm the keeper of the stories they'll forget
Pour one more, pour one more
I'm the witness that nobody's noticed yet
Behind this bar I'm watching history
All the pieces of this family come to be
Pour one more, pour one more
For the ones who made it here tonight
Pour one more
Pour one more

songs.subject: americana / the ocean
songs.lyrics:
Salt on my hands and the wind in my hair
Standing at the edge of something bigger than prayer
You came to me like the tide rolls in
Pulling me under and back out again
The gulls are crying over the harbor light
I've been thinking about you every restless night
These old boots have walked a thousand miles of shore
But I never knew what the water was for

Now I know, now I know
The ocean was just waiting to show
What it feels like when something takes hold
Deep as the current, wide as the cold
I never believed in the pull of the moon
Till you walked into my life like a June afternoon
Now I stand at the water with nowhere to go
And I know, lord I know

My grandfather fished these waters for years
Said the sea gives you wonder and the sea gives you tears
I carved your name in the salt-bleached dock
Where the old men sit and the young men talk
The boats come in with the morning catch
And I'm counting down to when our eyes will match
Across some crowded room or some open plain
I'll find my way back to you again

Now I know, now I know
The ocean was just waiting to show
What it feels like when something takes hold
Deep as the current, wide as the cold
I never believed in the pull of the moon
Till you walked into my life like a June afternoon
Now I stand at the water with nowhere to go
And I know, lord I know

There's a red-hulled boat on the horizon line
And the captain there could easily be mine
But I'd rather stay on this rocky ground
With the sound of you and the waves rolling down
The lighthouse keeper don't need to explain
Why the light keeps burning through the wind and rain
Some things are faithful and some things are true
And I've learned all of that from loving you

Now I know, now I know
The ocean was just waiting to show
What it feels like when something takes hold
Deep as the current, wide as the cold
I never believed in the pull of the moon
Till you walked into my life like a June afternoon
Now I stand at the water with nowhere to go
And I know, lord I know

Now I know

songs.subject: dream pop / faith
songs.lyrics:
There's a light behind your eyes I keep returning to
Like a dream I had before I even knew your name
The steeple's tall but I have found my church in you
And I'm not afraid to say that I will never be the same
The fog rolls in across the fields we used to walk
And I hear your voice inside the sound the willows make
I always thought that faith was just a word that people talked
But now it rises in me every morning when I wake

Carry me, carry me
Over the water and under the sky
Carry me, carry me
Like a hymn that you've known all your life
I believe, I believe
In the light that you leave on for me
Carry me, carry me home
Carry me home

The candles burn in windows I have never seen before
But somehow in the dark they feel familiar as a prayer
You told me once you loved me and I fell through every floor
And landed somewhere softer than I knew that I could bear
The world is spinning slower when I'm standing next to you
The colors bleed together in the most cathedral way
There's something ancient living in the green and gold and blue
And it says your name so gently at the turning of the day

Carry me, carry me
Over the water and under the sky
Carry me, carry me
Like a hymn that you've known all your life
I believe, I believe
In the light that you leave on for me
Carry me, carry me home
Carry me home

I used to lie awake and wonder what was worth the cost
I used to count the things I couldn't hold inside my hands
But you appeared like something I had given up as lost
Like a shore I'd always hoped for but could never understand
And now the stars feel close enough to touch above the hill
And the river runs so silver in the hour before dawn
I would follow you through anything and I believe I will
Through the darkness and the distance till the very last is gone

Carry me, carry me
Over the water and under the sky
Carry me, carry me
Like a hymn that you've known all your life
I believe, I believe
In the light that you leave on for me

Carry me home

songs.subject: americana / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
She wore her mama's dress that morning
White as the clouds out on the hill
I stood there trembling at the altar
Couldn't believe this day was real
Sixty years I've been a wandering man
Rocky roads and restless nights
But nothing ever could have told me
Love could burn this warm and bright

Oh hold my hand and don't let go
We've got our whole lives left to grow
I've seen the world from young to old
And nothing shines like watching you walk down that aisle to me
Nothing shines like this

The preacher talked about forever
Like it was something hard to find
But I looked into her eyes that morning
And forever felt like no big crime
My hands are weathered, my back is bending
But when she smiled I stood up straight
Some men search their whole lives over
I just thank God I wasn't too late

Oh hold my hand and don't let go
We've got our whole lives left to grow
I've seen the world from young to old
And nothing shines like watching you walk down that aisle to me
Nothing shines like this

My daddy told me love was fleeting
My mother said to work the land
I spent forty years just building fences
Never understanding what they planned
But there's a grace that comes with wrinkles
There's a wisdom born of loss
And standing here before our people
I finally know what living costs

Oh hold my hand and don't let go
We've got our whole lives left to grow
I've seen the world from young to old
And nothing shines like watching you walk down that aisle to me
Nothing shines like this

The fiddle played an old hymn softly
The wildflowers blew across the lawn
I kissed her slow and tasted something
Sweeter than anything I've known
If heaven's half as good as this day
Then I'll go easy when I go
But until then I'm holding on tight
To the most beautiful thing I'll ever know

Oh hold my hand and don't let go
We've got our whole lives left to grow
I've seen the world from young to old
And nothing shines like watching you walk down that aisle to me
Nothing shines like this
Nothing shines like this

songs.subject: rock / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I've been standing behind this bar since Tuesday
Pouring out the grief of strangers every night
I've heard a thousand stories of betrayal
A thousand men who swear they're in the right
There's Danny in the corner nursing whiskey
Been coming in for three months with that face
He says his brother took everything that mattered
I wipe the counter down and hold my place

But I've learned something from the broken ones
Who finally let the poison go
I've seen the ones who drank their anger dry
And the ones who let forgiveness flow
And every time a man walks out lighter
Than he stumbled through that door
I know that carrying all that weight was killing him
More than what he's angry for

So let it go, let it burn out clean
Forgiveness isn't weak it's the hardest thing
Let it go, lay it on the floor
You're not letting them win
You're just fighting the right war
Let it go

Maria comes in Fridays after closing
She sits and talks about her ex-husband's lies
I pour her something soft and listen closely
I've gotten good at reading people's eyes
She's held that stone inside her chest for seven years
You can hear it in the way she laughs
Like something sharp is always sitting sideways
Like she's only ever living half

But I've learned something from the broken ones
Who finally let the poison go
I've seen the ones who drank their anger dry
And the ones who let forgiveness flow
And every time a woman walks out lighter
Than she stumbled through that door
I know that carrying all that weight was killing her
More than what she's angry for

So let it go, let it burn out clean
Forgiveness isn't weak it's the hardest thing
Let it go, lay it on the floor
You're not letting them win
You're just fighting the right war
Let it go

I'm not preaching from behind this counter
Lord knows I've got my own unfinished debts
My father's name still catches in my throat sometimes
There are nights I'm soaked in my own sweat
But I've seen enough of what the hatred does to people
When they water it like it's something good
I've mopped up enough blood and broken glasses
To know that anger never understood

That you've learned something from the broken ones
Who finally let the poison go
You've seen the ones who drank their anger dry
Now be the ones who let forgiveness flow
And every time you walk out lighter
Than you stumbled through that door
You'll know that carrying all that weight was killing you
More than what you're angry for

So let it go, let it burn out clean
Forgiveness isn't weak it's the hardest thing
Let it go, lay it on the floor
You're not letting them win
You're just fighting the right war
Let it go
Let it go
Let it go

songs.subject: rock / summer ending
songs.lyrics:
The school bus comes on Monday
I saw the sign at the end of our street
Summer's folding up its colors
Like it never meant to stay with me
The kids were laughing in the sprinklers
Just last week I swear it was just last week
Now the fireflies are fewer
And the nights are turning bleak

And I'm standing in the yard alone again
Watching something beautiful go
The way it always does before I'm ready
The way everything I love just goes
The sun is setting earlier
The sweaters coming off the shelf
And I'm trying not to read too much into it
Trying not to think about myself

But summer's ending and so are we
The warmth we built is turning cold on me
Summer's ending in every single thing
The empty pool, the falling leaves beginning
Summer's ending, I feel it in my bones
The kids go back and I go back to being alone
Summer's ending

I worked the double shifts in June
So we could have that week down at the lake
Watched them run along the shoreline
Made myself stay awake
Every sandcastle they abandon
Every sunburn that has healed and gone
I photographed the whole damn season
Trying to find a way to hold on

And I'm standing in the yard alone again
Watching something beautiful go
The way it always does before I'm ready
The way everything I love just goes
The sun is setting earlier
The sweaters coming off the shelf
And I'm trying not to read too much into it
Trying not to think about myself

But summer's ending and so are we
The warmth we built is turning cold on me
Summer's ending in every single thing
The empty pool, the falling leaves beginning
Summer's ending, I feel it in my bones
The kids go back and I go back to being alone
Summer's ending

I don't regret a single Tuesday
I don't regret the overtime
I don't regret the sticky fingers
Or the ice cream melting down the line
I just wish that I could stop time stopping
I just wish that warm could just stay warm
I've gotten pretty good at facing forward
But I still hate watching out the storm

Every September hits me like a door
That closes on the person I was before
I swear next year I'll be more ready for this
But here I am again upon this porch
With summer burning down like a torch

Summer's ending and so are we
The warmth we built is turning cold on me
Summer's ending in every single thing
The empty pool, the falling leaves beginning
Summer's ending, I feel it in my bones
The kids go back and I go back to being alone
Summer's ending
Summer's ending
And I'm standing here alone

songs.subject: reggae / heartbreak
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning and your side was cold
Didn't even get a chance to do what I was told
Said you needed space said you needed time
Said the problem wasn't you said the problem it was mine
But the coffee cup is sitting where you left it by the door
And I keep on waiting waiting like you're coming back for more

But you're gone, gone, gone
Like the tide rolls out at dawn
Gone, gone, gone
And I'm singing this sad song
Every morning every evening every time I see your name
Every shadow in the hallway every single drop of rain
You're gone

I keep your number in my phone I haven't called it yet
Everybody says that I should try hard to forget
My neighbor play his music loud to cover up my walls
I've been walking round this island talking to the seagull calls
Three years of loving you and you said it wasn't right
And you left me with the sunrise and you walked into the night

But you're gone, gone, gone
Like the tide rolls out at dawn
Gone, gone, gone
And I'm singing this sad song
Every morning every evening every time I see your name
Every shadow in the hallway every single drop of rain
You're gone

I don't want to make you wrong
I don't want to make you small
I just want to understand
How you loved me not at all
How you smiled across the table
How you held me through the storm
And then one day just decided
That my heart was not your home

I'm walking down by the water now
The palm trees bending in the breeze
And the beauty of this whole wide world
Feels like a cruel disease
How can everything be glowing
How can everything be bright
When the one who made it matter
Packed her bags and said goodnight

But you're gone, gone, gone
Like the tide rolls out at dawn
Gone, gone, gone
And I'm singing this sad song
Every morning every evening every time I see your name
Every shadow in the hallway every single drop of rain
You're gone

And the rhythm keeps on playing
And the island keeps on turning
And the sun keeps right on blazing
And my heart keeps right on burning
And the rhythm keeps on playing
But it ain't playing for two no more
Just playing for the one man
Sitting crying on the shore

You're gone, gone, gone
Like the tide rolls out at dawn
Gone, gone, gone
And I'm singing this sad song
Every morning every evening every time I see your name
Every shadow in the hallway every single drop of rain
You're gone
You're gone
You're gone

songs.subject: rock / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
Rain's fallin' on my window pane again
Same as it did back in sixty-nine
My coffee's cold, my bones are feelin' every year
But this old chair is doing just fine
The dog is stretched across the hardwood floor
She don't mind the thunder anymore
Neither do I, been through enough of life's hard storms
To know the rain ain't keepin' score

Let it fall, let it fall on Sunday
Wash the dust off everything I've done
Let it fall, let it fall on Sunday
I've earned the right to sit here in the grey
And watch it fall on a rainy Sunday

My hands remember more than my mind does now
Callused from the work and from the cold
I built this house with timber cut from our own land
Back when thirty felt like getting old
My Margaret used to dance out in the yard
Tilting up her face to catch the drops
Lord I still can see her laughing in the grey
Some things time just never stops

Let it fall, let it fall on Sunday
Wash the dust off everything I've done
Let it fall, let it fall on Sunday
I've earned the right to sit here in the grey
And watch it fall on a rainy Sunday

The kids keep calling saying Dad are you okay
You need to get out more they say
But there's a kind of peace in being still and old and quiet
On a long and soaking Sunday in the grey
The gutters overflow, the garden drinks it up
The oak tree bends but holds its ground
I've been that oak tree most of my living life
Still standing after every storm comes down

Let it fall, let it fall on Sunday
Wash the dust off everything I've done
Let it fall, let it fall on Sunday
I've earned the right to sit here in the grey
And watch it fall on a rainy Sunday

So let it pour down on my old tin roof
Let it drum its ancient song
I got nowhere left to be and nothing left to prove
And all this rainy Sunday long
I'll sit and watch it fall

I got nowhere left to be and nothing left to prove
And all this rainy Sunday long
I'll sit and watch it fall

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
I woke up and the light was doing something strange through the curtains, that gold and watery kind that only happens in October, and I thought, this is the day, this is actually the day, and I lay there for a moment just breathing it in before anyone could knock on the door and make it real and loud

My mother did my buttons up the back and her hands were shaking and neither of us said anything because if we said anything we would cry and we had agreed the night before over lukewarm tea that we were not going to cry until at least the ceremony

The flowers smelled like every good thing I have ever hoped for

I wrote letters to you I never sent, in notebooks I kept under my bed for years, talking to a version of you I hadn't met yet, and I wonder if somehow I was writing to you all along, if the universe was kind enough to let me practice loving you before I ever knew your name

Walking down that aisle the world went quiet in a way I didn't expect, like someone turned the volume of everything down except your face, and you were trying not to cry and doing a terrible job of it, and I loved you so completely in that moment I thought my chest might actually give out

We wrote our own vows and I practiced mine forty times and forgot every word when I looked at you

My grandmother held my hand during the reception and said marry your best friend and nothing else will matter as much as you think it will, and I looked across the room at you laughing with your brother and spilling your drink a little, and I thought yes, yes I did, I really did

The cake was lemon and we fed it to each other messily on purpose and laughed with frosting on our faces and I thought this is who I want to be, someone who laughs with frosting on their face, someone who doesn't need the moment to be perfect to know that it is

I keep coming back to the way the evening light fell across the tent when we were dancing, how everyone we loved was in one place, how that never happens, how we were so lucky, how I knew even then I would spend the rest of my life trying to remember it exactly as it was

You said I do like you'd been waiting your whole life to say it to someone and meant it, and maybe you had, and maybe I had too, and maybe that's the whole story, maybe that's everything

Later when everyone was gone and we were alone in the room at last I took off my shoes and sat on the edge of the bed and you sat beside me and we didn't say anything for a long time and it was the most married I have ever felt, just that silence, just that we made it, just that

I want to remember the shoes on the floor and your jacket over the chair and the noise of the street outside and how ordinary and how sacred everything was all at once

This was the day

I will keep this day inside me like a stone in my pocket, smooth from being held

songs.subject: hip-hop / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I used to hold it like a weapon in my chest
Every wrong you did to me catalogued and pressed
Between the pages of a case I built for years
Exhibit A the night you left me, Exhibit B the tears
But somewhere in the middle of my righteous bitter war
I looked across the room and couldn't remember what it was for
You were just a person standing there afraid
And I was just a person who forgot that people make mistakes

I'm letting go, I'm letting it go
Not because you deserve it but because I need the room
I'm letting go, watch it go
Like smoke into the ceiling, like a tide into the blue
I forgive you, I forgive you
Not as a gift I'm handing over with a bow
I forgive you, I forgive you
Because I love you and I'm ready to let go

See I read somewhere that holding onto grudges is a weight
You carry for the other person while they living life great
And I looked in the mirror and I saw what I'd become
A man so full of keeping score he forgot how to love someone
You reached across the table with your sorry in your eyes
And I felt the whole architecture of my anger start to die
Not all at once, it wasn't like the movies made it seem
More like winter turning slowly into something like a spring

I'm letting go, I'm letting it go
Not because you deserve it but because I need the room
I'm letting go, watch it go
Like smoke into the ceiling, like a tide into the blue
I forgive you, I forgive you
Not as a gift I'm handing over with a bow
I forgive you, I forgive you
Because I love you and I'm ready to let go

Now let me be honest cause this ain't a fairytale track
Forgiveness doesn't mean I'm taking all of it back
Don't mean I'm acting like the hurt was never there
Don't mean I'm handing you the keys and saying take the stairs
It means I looked at you and looked at me and made a choice
That love was louder than the anger and I'm giving it a voice
It means some mornings I'ma have to choose it all again
Forgiveness isn't something that you do once and it ends

It's a practice it's a posture it's a daily kind of work
Getting up and choosing us above the memory of hurt
I have loved you through my pride and loved you through my pain
And I would rather learn to forgive you than to lose you to my blame

I'm letting go, I'm letting it go
Not because you deserve it but because I need the room
I'm letting go, watch it go
Like smoke into the ceiling, like a tide into the blue
I forgive you, I forgive you
Not as a gift I'm handing over with a bow
I forgive you, I forgive you
Because I love you and I'm ready to let go

I'm ready to let go
I'm ready to let go
Come here, I'm ready to let go

songs.subject: indie folk / growing up in a small town
songs.lyrics:
Sixty years of dust on these boots I wear
The same cracked sidewalks, the same thin air
I remember when the hardware store
Had old man Hemmings sitting by the door
He'd hand out candy to the summer kids
Never asked for nothing, never really did

These streets grew smaller as I grew tall
Or maybe I just started seeing all
The chips of paint on every front porch rail
The way the old ones told their worn-out tales
Like water following the same creek bed
You stay because you never leave, they said

I watched them build the stoplight at the square
Thought it was the future standing there
Now it blinks on yellow after ten
The diner closed and opened up again
With different owners but the same cracked booth
Where I first kissed Mary, told her half the truth

There's a boy who cuts across my lawn each day
The same path I wore in 1958
He doesn't know he's walking where I walked
He doesn't hear the ghosts of how we talked
On summer nights when everything was wide
And the world was just beyond the county line

We used to swear we'd shake this red clay loose
Catch a Greyhound, have something to prove
Billy went to Cleveland, came back gray
Tommy made it somewhere far away
I stayed and watched the elm trees grow and fall
Turns out the small town was the world after all

There's comfort in a thing that knows your name
The way the morning comes in just the same
Through the curtains that my mother sewed
Past the window facing Cantor Road
I've got no need for all that open sky
I found my whole life in this four-mile wide

songs.subject: americana / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
She left her reading glasses on the table
Next to a coffee cup she'll never fill
I keep expecting her to call me down for breakfast
But the kitchen just sits quiet and still
I don't know what to do with all this Sunday
Don't know how to make the hours move
Dad keeps saying she would want us laughing
But I can't find a single thing to prove

Her old car is still sitting in the driveway
Nobody wants to be the one to move it
I traced her name into the passenger window
In the frost last week just so I could see it

I'm sixteen and I don't know how to do this
Nobody taught me how to watch someone go
She held my hand through every hard beginning
But she couldn't stay to help me through this road
I keep her voicemail saved inside my phone still
Press play sometimes when I forget to grieve
Just to hear her say she loves me, call her later
It's the cruelest kindest thing I'll ever keep

The preacher said she's somewhere green and golden
The relatives brought casseroles and cried
My little brother drew her in his notebook
Wings too big and a halo slightly wide
I stuck it to the mirror in my bedroom
Next to her handwriting on a birthday card
Funny how the people that we're made of
Don't leave easy, don't leave whole, don't leave hard

I'm sixteen and I don't know how to do this
Nobody taught me how to watch someone go
She held my hand through every hard beginning
But she couldn't stay to help me through this road
I keep her voicemail saved inside my phone still
Press play sometimes when I forget to grieve
Just to hear her say she loves me, call her later
It's the cruelest kindest thing I'll ever keep

Spring came around and I planted what she planted
Tomatoes in the beds along the fence
My hands in dirt she touched felt like a letter
Written in a language almost dense
Almost clear enough to understand it
Almost like she answered something back
I'm learning how to carry what she gave me
Learning how to live along the crack

songs.subject: rock / a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Black dress black tie the organ starts to moan
Folding chairs in rows and flowers no one's taking home
Somebody's reading from a book they barely know
While the rest of us just stare into the vertical and low

The parking lot was full of cars I've never seen
The people wearing faces sewn to somewhere in between
We shook some hands and nodded at the things we recognized
Watched the light come through the window fall and shift and die

We all go down the same road
Just different shoes on different days
We all come to this building
Where the music tries to say
The things we couldn't manage
Through all the ordinary time
And we hold each other up until we can't
Then we drive home through the night

There's a woman in the second row who knew him first of all
She's got a tissue twisted in her hands into a ball
There's a man who laughs too loud at the wrong thing the pastor says
I think he's just been holding it so long he lost the brakes

The casket's real expensive for a man who drove a Ford
Someone picked a song he probably never would've heard before
But it doesn't matter now what any single choice becomes
We're all just trying hard to make the nothing into something

We all go down the same road
Just different shoes on different days
We all come to this building
Where the music tries to say
The things we couldn't manage
Through all the ordinary time
And we hold each other up until we can't
Then we drive home through the night

Outside by the hedges two old friends smoke cigarettes
They're talking about something far away from all of this
Maybe baseball or a bar or someone's daughter's name
Death makes people either go real deep or go real plain

I stood beside the photo board and looked at who he was at twenty-three
Some face I never met, some version no one living gets to see
We only know the ending and the middle chapter stuff
The beginning's always photographs and someone saying that was us

The reception smelled like coffee cake and something like relief
Not the bad kind, just the kind that comes at the end of grief's first peak
Somebody told a story and the whole room started in
And for a second it felt warm and almost like a thing called living

We all go down the same road
Just different shoes on different days
We all come to this building
Where the music tries to say
The things we couldn't manage
Through all the ordinary time
And we hold each other up until we can't
Then we drive home through the night

Then we drive home through the night
Through the night
Through the long and ordinary night

songs.subject: trap / a childhood home
songs.lyrics:
Pulled up to the old block, 3 AM and I'm faded
She left me on read and my whole chest feel weighted
Used to run these streets when I ain't know what heartbreak was
Now I'm parked outside the house just 'cause

Momma's flower pots still sitting on the porch
Same cracked sidewalk where I learned what I was worth
Before the girls, before the pain, before I knew the cost
Standing in the driveway counting everything I lost

Trap money couldn't fix it, designer couldn't dress it
All these years of grinding and I still can't suppress it
She took the last of something I had stored away
Now I'm back at the beginning trying not to break

Used to catch lightning bugs right there in the yard
Didn't know that growing up was gonna leave these scars
She said I never let her in, said I keep the walls up high
Maybe she was right, I built them brick by brick right here inside

This is where it started, this is where the damage lives
Where my daddy taught me don't you ever give
But giving was the one thing she kept asking me to do
Now I'm sitting here without her, nothing left to prove

Trap money couldn't fix it, designer couldn't dress it
All these years of grinding and I still can't suppress it
She took the last of something I had stored away
Now I'm back at the beginning trying not to break

Old bedroom window got a light on, someone new lives here
They don't know this wood absorbed a hundred thousand tears
They don't know I kissed my first girl on that second stair
They don't know I held my breath the night my father disappeared

So I'm just a ghost out here floating through the dark
Empty passenger seat where she used to park her heart
Ain't got nowhere else to go when everything collapse
So I come back to the beginning and I just sit back

Trap money couldn't fix it, designer couldn't dress it
All these years of grinding and I still can't suppress it
She took the last of something I had stored away
Now I'm back at the beginning trying not to break

Back at the beginning trying not to break
Back at the beginning, yeah

Now I'm back at the beginning trying not to break

Back at the beginning, yeah

songs.subject: emo / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
Last call at a quarter to two
I'm wiping down the bar top thinking of you
Not you like you used to be at the end
But you like you were way back when

You used to come in Thursdays, order something cheap
Tell me all your stories while the whole town fell asleep
I never had the words back then to tell you what you meant
Now I'm pouring drinks for strangers and I'm trying to relent

And I've served a thousand sorrows in a glass
Watched a hundred broken people let the hours pass
But nothing that they're drowning in comes close to what I feel
When the bar goes dark and quiet and the grief gets real

They say bartenders are therapists without the degree
I know how to hold the weight of everyone but me
I know how to keep it steady when the other people cry
But I keep it in the bottle and I don't ask myself why

It's been two years since the phone call in the parking lot
Two years since the floor fell out from under everything I got
I drove straight to work and I tied on my apron strings
Shook the drinks and smiled all night like nothing

And I've served a thousand sorrows in a glass
Watched a hundred broken people let the hours pass
But nothing that they're drowning in comes close to what I feel
When the bar goes dark and quiet and the grief gets real

You raised me in between the chaos and the quiet
Taught me when the world gets loud the best thing is to try it
So I tried to keep on breathing, tried to keep on showing up
Every night I pour these glasses out for you and fill the cup

Do you see me here still standing when the music dies
Do you see me locking up alone beneath these neon signs
I talk about my regulars like you would know their names
I catch myself mid-sentence and it never feels the same

And I've served a thousand sorrows in a glass
Watched a hundred broken people let the hours pass
But nothing that they're drowning in comes close to what I feel
When the bar goes dark and quiet and the grief gets real

The last chair on the counter is the one I save for you
The lights are low and it is late and I don't know what to do
So I pour one final glass out, set it down, and let it breathe
The only drink I've ever poured that wasn't meant for me

And I've served a thousand sorrows in a glass
Watched a hundred broken people let the hours pass
But nothing that they're drowning in comes close to what I feel
When the bar goes dark and quiet and the grief gets real

When the bar goes dark and quiet
When the grief gets real

songs.subject: R&B / chasing a dream
songs.lyrics:
I woke up with the city in my lungs
A seventeen-year-old hunger on my tongue
Every window in the skyline told me something
Every flicker of a light said keep on coming

My mama said the odds were not in favor
My daddy said to find a job with labor
But something in my bloodstream kept on singing
A frequency that no one else was hearing

So I chased it through the night and through the morning
Through the doors they slammed and through the warnings
Every no just made the yes taste sweeter
Every setback pulled the dream in closer, deeper

I'm running toward the light that keeps on moving
I'm climbing up the walls that keep on proving
That everything they said that I could never be
Is standing right in front of me and breathing
I can feel it in my fingers, in my feet
That burning in my chest won't let me sleep
I'm chasing every version of the dream
Until the dream starts chasing me

Years went by like pages I was turning
Every stage and studio still burning
I picked up all the pieces when they scattered
Learned the only voice that really mattered

Was the one that spoke at 4 AM in silence
When the doubt came in like something like a siren
And I had to make a choice to keep believing
Or lay down all my wanting and stop dreaming

I'm running toward the light that keeps on moving
I'm climbing up the walls that keep on proving
That everything they said that I could never be
Is standing right in front of me and breathing
I can feel it in my fingers, in my feet
That burning in my chest won't let me sleep
I'm chasing every version of the dream
Until the dream starts chasing me

You don't understand the price of this obsession
Every dollar spent on faith instead of lessons
Every relationship that bent beneath the pressure
Every sacrifice I made without a measure

But I would do it all again from the beginning
Even all the losing was a kind of winning
'Cause I know myself in ways they never taught me
And the dream worth chasing is the one that caught me

I'm running toward the light that keeps on moving
I'm climbing up the walls that keep on proving
That everything they said that I could never be
Is standing right in front of me and breathing
I can feel it in my fingers, in my feet
That burning in my chest won't let me sleep
I'm chasing every version of the dream
Until the dream starts chasing me

Until the dream starts chasing me
Until the dream starts chasing me
Yeah

songs.subject: hip-hop / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
It's three a.m. and the phone rings loud
Cuts right through my sleep like a siren in a crowd
I reach across the nightstand, knock my glasses to the floor
My old bones crackin' as I rise, ain't as young as I was before
Hello, who is this, what you calling for
Don't you know the night ain't meant for this no more
But something in the silence on the other end
Tells me this ain't wrong number, this is something else, my friend

Used to get these calls back in sixty-nine
When trouble found your people you'd be first in line
Now my children grown and scattered cross this tired land
And I'm sitting in the dark just trying to understand
The voice comes through all broken, crackling, small
Sounds like my grandson and I press close to the wall
He says grandpa I don't know what I'm doing wrong
I said boy, you called the right man, pull up a chair, so long

I been around the mountain more than you know
Seen the winter come and watched the summer go
Ain't nothing new beneath this worn out sun
Every battle that you fighting, son, I fought every one
So talk to me slow, let the words fall free
Three a.m. is sacred time between you and me
The whole world sleepin' and it's just us two
And old man got all night, got nothing else to do

I remember when your daddy called me just like this
Must've been nineteen eighty something, something he had missed
Standing at the crossroads didn't know which way to turn
Had to tell him what my daddy told me, lessons hard to learn
You cannot run from what is already inside
Cannot hide the storm by just swallowing your pride
Gotta face the mirror even when the mirror's mean
Gotta clean the wound before you cover what you seen

I been around the mountain more than you know
Seen the winter come and watched the summer go
Ain't nothing new beneath this worn out sun
Every battle that you fighting, son, I fought every one
So talk to me slow, let the words fall free
Three a.m. is sacred time between you and me
The whole world sleepin' and it's just us two
And old man got all night, got nothing else to do

My wife, God rest her, she would answer every call
Said the phone was just a rope between the people in the hall
Of this life, this long and winding corridor we walk
Said the most important thing a person does is talk
I think about her now in this quiet room
Her side of the bed still holding her perfume
And I hold this phone a little tighter than I should
Cause connection in the darkness, boy, is always good

We talked till the birds started warming up their song
Talked till the purple sky said night was almost gone
He said grandpa how you always know just what to say
I said baby I was young and lost just yesterday
Don't let nobody tell you that the old don't understand
We just been a little longer walking on this land
So call me any hour, any dark and hollow night
Old man keeps his phone charged up and the ringer bright

I been around the mountain more than you know
Seen the winter come and watched the summer go
Ain't nothing new beneath this worn out sun
Every battle that you fighting, son, I fought every one
So talk to me slow, let the words fall free
Three a.m. is sacred time between you and me
The whole world sleepin' and it's just us two
And old man got all night, got nothing else to do

Yeah the whole world sleeping but we still got things to say
Old man got all night, gonna talk till break of day

3. A indie folk song about an old photograph.

songs.subject: alt rock / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:
The refrigerator hums a steady indifferent note
And I'm in my socks on the tile with your jacket and my throat
Is doing that thing again where it forgets how to breathe
And the radio is playing something I don't want to believe

So I start moving, I don't know why
Just my arms and the counter and the low kitchen light
Nobody watching, nobody here to say my name
Just me and the linoleum and this stupid, stupid pain

I spin around the island like I'm seventeen
Like heartbreak is a thing I haven't yet seen
I knock the dish towel off the oven door
Catch my reflection in the window and I hit the floor
No I pull myself back up and keep on turning
While everything inside of me is churning, churning
Dancing alone in the kitchen again
Dancing alone, don't know where else to begin

You said I was too much and never quite enough
Said loving me was some kind of an endurance stuff
Left your mug here on the counter with the broken handle still
And I can't throw it out and probably never will

So I start moving like the motion is a cure
Like if I spin around enough maybe I'll feel sure
About something, anything, the ground beneath my feet
The radio keeps playing and I follow every beat

I spin around the island like I'm seventeen
Like heartbreak is a thing I haven't yet seen
I knock the dish towel off the oven door
Catch my reflection in the window and I hit the floor
No I pull myself back up and keep on turning
While everything inside of me is churning, churning
Dancing alone in the kitchen again
Dancing alone, don't know where else to begin

There's a grief that lives specifically in ordinary rooms
In the places where the two of you made space for afternoons
I keep reaching for the second cup before I catch myself
Keep your photograph face down on the shelf

But right now I'm dancing, right now I'm moving
Right now the only thing I've got is this
And it's not healing and it's not beautiful
It's just surviving, it's just this

I spin around the island like I'm seventeen
Like heartbreak is a thing I haven't yet seen
I knock the dish towel off the oven door

The refrigerator hums
I keep on dancing
The refrigerator hums
And I keep dancing

songs.subject: indie folk / an old photograph
songs.lyrics:
Inside a cardboard box behind the attic stairs
Between a broken clock and your grandmother's prayers
I found you there among the folded yellow things
A moment locked in silver and the silence that it brings

Two people on a porch in nineteen sixty-three
One of them a stranger and one of them is me
Or close enough to me that I can feel the August heat
The dust along the dirt road and the grass beneath their feet

Oh what do you remember, what do you keep
What gets stored in paper and what wanders off to sleep
Every face a whole world that has come and nearly gone
And the photograph just holds you and quietly carries on

The woman there is laughing at something off to the left
Somebody just outside the frame still holding all their breath
I wonder what was funny, wonder what was said
Wonder how much longer till that laughing woman's dead
These are dark thoughts for a Tuesday afternoon I know
But the photograph just makes me want to understand the flow
Of time and how it carries us and where we finally land
And how we're all just standing there with something in our hand

Oh what do you remember, what do you keep
What gets stored in paper and what wanders off to sleep
Every face a whole world that has come and nearly gone
And the photograph just holds you and quietly carries on

My father used to say that photographs were lies
They only catch the outside, never catch behind the eyes
I used to think him cynical but maybe he was right
That the truest parts of people live just outside of the light
Still I keep this one and press it to my chest
Like somehow I can feel them, like my wanting is a quest
To reach back through the paper to the world that they were in
When everything was forward and had not yet grown thin

I set it on the windowsill beside the afternoon
Let the light come through the curtain like a quiet kind of tune
Whoever you were, woman with the laugh I'll never hear
Thank you for existing, thank you for being here
Thank you for the August and the porch and the unknown joke
Thank you for the evidence that someone loved and spoke
And stood in the sunlight before the world moved on
These are the small eternities we carry when they're gone

Oh what do you remember, what do you keep
What gets stored in paper and what wanders off to sleep
Every face a whole world that has come and nearly gone
And the photograph just holds you and quietly carries on

Quietly carries on
Just carries on
