songs.subject: rock / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
Your porch light's dark and there's a for sale sign in the yard
And the neighborhood looks smaller now and getting here was hard
Not the distance, not the highway, not the miles I had to burn
Just the knowing when I got here there was nothing to return

I drove past your window at a quarter after ten
Like some kind of fool who thought that looking helped and then
I sat out in my car until the cold came through the glass
Watching your old curtains in a window that you've passed

Come back, the town is quieter than it's got a right to be
Come back, I know you had your reasons but come back to me
There's a space where your laugh used to live
And the whole street feels it, we don't know how to give
That back to each other, we've been trying since June
Come back, we're all just howling at a secondhand moon

Your mother told me Portland's treating you alright
Said you've got an apartment with good windows and good light
Said you seem like you are happy and I swallowed that whole hard
Smiled and said I'm glad for you and walked back to my car

I should be glad for you and mostly I believe I am
But the selfish part won't quiet down, it don't give a damn
About your windows or your good light or the life you're building there
It just wants you back on this side of everywhere

Come back, the town is quieter than it's got a right to be
Come back, I know you had your reasons but come back to me
There's a space where your laugh used to live
And the whole street feels it, we don't know how to give
That back to each other, we've been trying since June
Come back, we're all just howling at a secondhand moon

Maybe this is just the thing that growing up requires
Everybody scattering like sparks from separate fires
Maybe I'll get used to it, maybe I'll move on
Maybe I'll stop waking up and reaching for the phone

But not tonight, tonight it's just the cold and the dark drive
And the strange exhausting labor of just staying alive
In a town that used to fit us like a house fits all its noise
And now just echoes quietly with all its missing voice

Come back, the town is quieter than it's got a right to be
Come back, I know you had your reasons but come back to me
There's a space where your laugh used to live

And the neighborhood looks smaller now and getting here was hard
Not the distance, not the highway, not the miles I had to burn
Just the knowing when I got here there was nothing to return

Like some kind of fool who thought that looking helped and then
I sat out in my car until the cold came through the glass

I should be glad for you and mostly I believe I am
But the selfish part won't quiet down, it don't give a damn
About your windows or your good light or the life you're building there
It just wants you back on this side of everywhere

songs.subject: hip-hop / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Flour on my hands and the radio's on
It's two in the morning and everybody's gone
The grandkids are sleeping, my wife's in the bed
But something 'bout this beat got into my head
I push back the table, I slide in my socks
Seventy-three years old but I still got the clock
The linoleum's shining like a Saturday night
And I'm cutting a rug in the kitchen light

I don't need a partner, I don't need a crowd
Just me and this music playing a little too loud
My hips remember what my knees forgot
I'm dancing alone in the kitchen and I'm giving it all I got
No one's watching, no one's judging, just the refrigerator hum
I'm dancing alone in the kitchen till the morning comes

Used to take Eleanor out to the hall
Nineteen sixty-something, Lord we had it all
My collar was starched and her dress was blue
I still got the moves even if they're overdue
The doctor said easy, said take it real slow
But the doctor ain't here and the bass is too low
I'm rolling my shoulders like I used to do
Pretending that the mop is dancing too

I don't need a partner, I don't need a crowd
Just me and this music playing a little too loud
My hips remember what my knees forgot
I'm dancing alone in the kitchen and I'm giving it all I got
No one's watching, no one's judging, just the refrigerator hum
I'm dancing alone in the kitchen till the morning comes

My daddy danced at his brother's wake
Said son there's always a reason to shake
Said grief and joy are the same two feet
And you gotta keep moving to stay off the street
So I learned it early and I kept it close
When I needed it most, that's when I'd go
To the kitchen alone with the good Lord above
Dancing is prayer when it's full of love

I don't need a partner, I don't need a crowd
Just me and this music playing a little too loud
My hips remember what my knees forgot
I'm dancing alone in the kitchen and I'm giving it all I got
No one's watching, no one's judging, just the refrigerator hum
I'm dancing alone in the kitchen till the morning comes

Eleanor's picture is up on the wall
She's smiling like she smiled at the old dance hall
I swear sometimes I can feel her hand
Resting on my shoulder like she understands
The song fades out and I'm breathing hard
Standing in the kitchen in my own backyard
Seventy-three years old and I feel like twenty
This old life of mine's been more than plenty

I don't need a partner, I don't need a crowd
Just me and this music playing a little too loud
My hips remember what my knees forgot
I'm dancing alone in the kitchen and I'm giving it all I got

3. A disco song about a friend who changed. from a teenager's point of view
4. A country song about forgiveness. from someone who just got dumped

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / quitting your job
songs.lyrics:
I've been folding myself into a shape that don't fit
Smiling at a man who don't care one single bit
Punching somebody else's clock to pay somebody else's rent
Every morning on the highway wondering where my life went
Maggie needs new shoes and the electric's almost due
But I've been doing math in margins and I think I can get through

So I'm walking in at eight and I'm walking out for good
I've been afraid to do this longer than I should
There's a letter on his desk, it's got my name signed at the bottom
I've been carrying this courage since the last leaves fell in autumn
I don't know exactly how the next six months will go
But I know I can't keep dying somewhere no one sees the show

Maggie drew a picture of me crying at the table
She said mama you look tired and I said honey I'm just able
To carry a little more than most and I smiled so she'd believe it
But a child always knows the truth no matter how you try to weave it
So this is for the drawing and the way she held my hand
And every woman working twice as hard for half the plans

So I'm walking in at eight and I'm walking out for good
I've been afraid to do this longer than I should
There's a letter on his desk, it's got my name signed at the bottom
I've been carrying this courage since the last leaves fell in autumn
I don't know exactly how the next six months will go
But I know I can't keep dying somewhere no one sees the show

I've got a sister in Columbus and a plan inside my head
I've got a daughter who needs a mother who gets out of bed
Not because she has to but because she wants to rise
I've been so close to the ceiling I forgot about the sky
There's a farmer's market three blocks down I've always passed on Saturday
Maybe I'll be selling something there by end of May

I cried in the parking lot before I went inside
Sat there for twenty minutes with my feelings and my pride
Then I thought about my mother and the things she never chased
And how she looked at sixty like a woman half-erased
I said not me, not Maggie, not the life we're gonna make
Some things you do for survival, some things you do for the escape

So I'm walking in at eight and I'm walking out for good
I've been afraid to do this longer than I should
There's a letter on his desk, it's got my name signed at the bottom
I've been carrying this courage since the last leaves fell in autumn
I don't know exactly how the next six months will go
But I know I can't keep dying somewhere no one sees the show

The elevator opened and I didn't even shake
Walked past the water cooler and I didn't even break
Said goodbye to Carmen at the front desk by the door
She said girl I always knew that you were meant for something more
And the sunlight hit the sidewalk and I stood there in the bright
For the first time in forever it was the middle of the day and it felt like night

Turning into morning
Turning into mine
Turning into something that I'd buried a long long time
Maggie I am coming
Maggie I am new
Everything I'm walking toward I'm walking toward for you

songs.subject: disco / a friend who changed
songs.lyrics:
Friday night and the gym is packed
Everybody's here and the bass is stacked
Remember when we used to come together
Best friends forever through any kind of weather
But now you're standing on the other side
Got a new crowd and a brand new stride
What happened to the girl I used to know
We were inseparable just a year ago

Donna, Donna, who are you now
Standing in the mirror you forgot somehow
We used to share our secrets in the back of the bus
Now you don't even save a dance for us
Donna, Donna, spinning away
Every time I reach you there's nothing left to say
The mirror ball is turning, turning overhead
But the friend I used to love might already be dead

Remember sophomore summer by the pool
We said we'd never try so hard to be cool
You borrowed all my records, I borrowed your shoes
We stayed up until morning talking about boys and the blues
Now you're laughing at a table I can't sit at
Tossing all your hair and doing this and that
I walk by and you look right through me
Like the history between us is ancient news to you, see

Donna, Donna, who are you now
Standing in the mirror you forgot somehow
We used to share our secrets in the back of the bus
Now you don't even save a dance for us
Donna, Donna, spinning away
Every time I reach you there's nothing left to say
The mirror ball is turning, turning overhead
But the friend I used to love might already be dead

I heard you talking last week in the hall
Said you never really liked me much at all
Did you mean that or was it just the show
Performing for the people that you desperately want to know
I'm not gonna cry about it on the floor
I'm dancing by myself right next to the door
And the music's good and the lights are bright
And I'm gonna be alright by the end of tonight

But Donna don't you miss it even a little
Don't you feel the answer hiding in the middle
Of all the things you're trying hard to be
Don't you ever think about just being free
Like we were before the world told you the rules
Before the hierarchy of the popular at school
You could come back, you know, you could come back easy
This life you're living now must make you queasy

Donna, Donna, who are you now
Standing in the mirror you forgot somehow
We used to share our secrets in the back of the bus
Now you don't even save a dance for us
Donna, Donna, spinning away
Every time I reach you there's nothing left to say
The mirror ball is turning, turning overhead
But the friend I used to love might already be dead

The DJ plays our song from seventh grade
And I watch your face and see you start to fade
Just for half a second you were almost there
Standing in the gym light with your old self bare
Then someone called your name from across the room
And just like that you disappeared into the costume
I'm leaving now, I'm getting my coat
But I want you to know before I go

Donna, Donna, I hope you find your way back
I hope you realize what you lost in the cracks
I hope someday the mirror shows you something real
I hope you still remember how it used to feel
Donna, Donna, I'm not waiting around
But somewhere in my heart there's still a piece of that sound
Two girls on a bus with the whole world ahead
Donna I still love who you were before
Donna I still love who you were

songs.subject: country / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I found your note on a Tuesday
Next to the coffee pot, plain as the day
Six lines was all that you needed
To tell me everything love couldn't say
I stood there in yesterday's t-shirt
Reading it over two, maybe three times
And somewhere between the first and the last word
I lost my grip on the thing I called mine

I'm not gonna hate you for leaving
Even though every bone wants to break
Even though the bedroom still smells like your shampoo
And I don't know what to do with Sunday mornings for my own sake
I'm not gonna curse your name to the highway
Or drink to the wreck of what we had
Forgiveness ain't saying it didn't hurt me
It's just saying the hurting won't drive me mad

My mama said love is a lesson
And every hard lesson leaves a mark
She said baby the ones who hurt us the deepest
Are teaching us something about our own heart
I didn't want to hear it that evening
I was parked in her driveway for over an hour
But somewhere in all that Tennessee darkness
I started to feel the first edge of the power

I'm not gonna hate you for leaving
Even though every bone wants to break
Even though the bedroom still smells like your shampoo
And I don't know what to do with Sunday mornings for my own sake
I'm not gonna curse your name to the highway
Or drink to the wreck of what we had
Forgiveness ain't saying it didn't hurt me
It's just saying the hurting won't drive me mad

There's a version of me that wants to be angry
That wants to tell everyone what you did
Put it out like a fire that warms while it's burning
Till there's nothing left standing, just ash on the grid
But anger is renting a room in my future
And I got too much living I still need to do
Too many mornings I haven't seen yet
Too many good things that don't involve you

So here's what I'm choosing out here in the open
Standing in a field where the fence used to be
I'm signing your name to the part of this story
That I'm finally willing to go ahead and set free
You were real, you were something, this wasn't a nothing
Three years don't just vanish into the air
But I'm bigger than the ending you handed me, darling
And there's life on the other side of this prayer

I'm not gonna hate you for leaving
Even though every bone wants to break
Even though the bedroom still smells like your shampoo
And I don't know what to do with Sunday mornings for my own sake
I'm not gonna curse your name to the highway
Or drink to the wreck of what we had
Forgiveness ain't saying it didn't hurt me
It's just saying the hurting won't drive me mad

I'll probably cry at the next county fair
Hear some old song and feel you standing there
That's alright, that's alright, that's just how it goes
The heart holds on to what the mind already knows
But I forgive you for leaving
I forgive you for going
I forgive the version of myself that let it go this long
I forgive the winter
I forgive the morning
I forgive the whole damn thing and I'm moving on

songs.subject: americana / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Saw your name light up my screen at half past nine
Seven words that cut me like a knife down my spine
"I think we should go our separate ways"
Couldn't even call me, just typed it out that day

We had Sunday mornings on the back porch swing
You said you loved the way I wore that old worn ring
Now you're ending us between your coffee and your lunch
Baby, I deserved at least the dignity of your touch

Did you even hesitate before you hit send
Did your hands shake at all knowing this was the end
I've been staring at this phone out in the parking lot
Trying to find the words to say for something that I've got

Nothing left to type back, nothing left to say
You delivered all our history in a digital grave
Seven words, no period, not even a goodbye
Just a blinking cursor where our love went to die

I drove past the diner where we had our first date
Sat in the gravel lot till it was getting late
The waitress saw me through the glass and gave a knowing nod
Like she'd seen a hundred broken hearts like mine out in that fog

I thought about the camping trip we took up past the ridge
How you said forever standing on that old covered bridge
Now forever's got an expiration date it seems
And it arrived between your lunch break and your evening dreams

Did you even hesitate before you hit send
Did your hands shake at all knowing this was the end
I've been staring at this phone out in the parking lot
Trying to find the words to say for something that I've got

Nothing left to type back, nothing left to say
You delivered all our history in a digital grave
Seven words, no period, not even a goodbye
Just a blinking cursor where our love went to die

Mama always said that love was worth the fight
Said you look a person in the eyes when something isn't right
I guess we moved so fast we lost the old way things were done
Traded conversations for the blue light of a phone

Maybe I'll delete the thread tonight or maybe I'll save it all
Screenshot it and wonder how we managed this long fall
From whiskey and wildflowers out behind the county line
To seven words at half past nine

Did you even hesitate before you hit send
Did your hands shake at all knowing this was the end
I've been staring at this phone out in the parking lot
Trying to find the words to say for something that I've got

Nothing left to type back, nothing left to say
You delivered all our history in a digital grave
Seven words, no period, not even a goodbye
Just a blinking cursor where our love went to die

Write 4 completely different full-length songs, one per request below. Each song must be complete, like a real radio song: at least three verses and a repeated chorus. For each, write only the song lyrics -- no titles, no section labels like Verse or Chorus, no commentary. Separate each song with a line that is exactly:
@@@NEXT-SONG@@@

1. A americana song about a breakup text.
2. A punk song about growing up in a small town. from a teenager's point of view
3. A R&B song about growing up in a small town. from a bartender's point of view
4. A blues song about the ocean. from an old man's point of view

songs.subject: R&B / growing up in a small town
songs.lyrics:
I been pouring drinks in this same spot for seventeen years
Watching people walk through that door dragging all their fears
The jukebox plays the same fifteen songs every single night
And the neon Budweiser sign flickers like it might go out

I grew up four blocks over on the corner of First and Vine
My daddy drank in here before it ever was mine
Now I own the bar stools and the sticky countertop
And every dream I had at twenty somehow made me stop

But I don't regret it, no I don't regret it
This town poured itself into my hands
And I became the keeper of its stories and its pain
Pouring out the whiskey while the rain comes down again
I don't regret it, no I don't regret it
Though sometimes late at night I wonder what I missed
But then somebody sits down needing someone just to listen
And baby this right here, this is what home is

Young Darnell comes in after his shift at the plant
Talks about his daughter like she hung the moon herself
Says she's leaving come September, got a scholarship out west
I top his glass off slowly and I tell him that's the best

I see myself in him, the way his eyes go somewhere far
Half proud, half broken sitting at my bar
I had a scholarship to play ball up at State
But Mama got sick in August and I stayed

But I don't regret it, no I don't regret it
This town poured itself into my hands
And I became the keeper of its stories and its pain
Pouring out the whiskey while the rain comes down again
I don't regret it, no I don't regret it
Though sometimes late at night I wonder what I missed
But then somebody sits down needing someone just to listen
And baby this right here, this is what home is

Old Miss Clara always takes the stool on the far end
Orders one sweet tea and nurses it till ten
She lost her husband back in ninety-four
But she comes in for company not the liquor anymore

The high school kids park out back on summer nights
I pretend I don't see them though they're in my sight
I remember being them, the world felt like a door
A mouth wide open ready to give you something more

Saturday a couple came in, said they'd moved away at eighteen
Now retired, drove back through just to see
Said the town looked smaller than they kept it in their hearts
I dried a glass and told them that's how memory works

But I don't regret it, no I don't regret it
This town poured itself into my hands
And I became the keeper of its stories and its pain
Pouring out the whiskey while the rain comes down again
I don't regret it, no I don't regret it
Though sometimes late at night I wonder what I missed
But then somebody sits down needing someone just to listen
And baby this right here, this is what home is

Last call comes and I wipe the bar down slow
Chairs up on the tables, time for everyone to go
I lock the door and stand out in the parking lot a while
Look up at those same stars I've been looking at my whole life

And they don't look like a small town
They look like everything

songs.subject: blues / the ocean
songs.lyrics:
I been coming to this shoreline since before your daddy breathed
Used to run these dunes like water, had good legs beneath
Now I walk with something heavy living in my knee
But the ocean doesn't notice and that suits me fine you see

She don't care how old I'm getting, don't care what I've lost
Don't care about the woman that I loved and what it cost
Just keeps rolling in and pulling out like breath inside a chest
And an old man on the shoreline gets to lay his troubles to rest

Oh the ocean, she's been here since before the world had names
And she'll be here long after they forget that I came
I've cried into her waters and she swallowed every tear
The ocean don't need nothing from me and that's why I come here

My boy thinks I shouldn't drive this far out to the coast
Says the roads are getting harder and he worries the most
But I tell him if I'm going to go, Lord let it be this way
Windows down and salt air coming in off of the bay

I buried my wife's ashes out beyond the second break
That was seven years ago or eight, I lose the days
Sometimes I think I see her in the way the water turns
Silver in the evening light, that particular burn

Oh the ocean, she's been here since before the world had names
And she'll be here long after they forget that I came
I've cried into her waters and she swallowed every tear
The ocean don't need nothing from me and that's why I come here

I fished these waters as a younger man, caught more than I could carry
Had a boat named after Mama, wasn't nothing ordinary
But time takes boats and it takes mamas and it takes the strength to haul
And now I just sit watching, and I don't mind it at all

There's a pelican that lands on the same post every dawn
I've been watching him for three years now, we got an understanding going on
He don't ask me where I've been or how I'm holding up these days
Just tucks his big old head back and we sit there in the haze

The young folks walk the beach with their machines and earbuds in
I don't begrudge them nothing, I was young and deaf to it
But if they'd stop a minute and just listen to that sound
The oldest voice there is would come on up from underground

Oh the ocean, she's been here since before the world had names
And she'll be here long after they forget that I came
I've cried into her waters and she swallowed every tear
The ocean don't need nothing from me and that's why I come here

I ain't scared of dying, had too long a life for that
Just want to see one more red evening, watch the light go flat
Out beyond the water where the sky becomes the sea
And something in that nothing feels a whole lot like free

Oh the ocean rolls on, rolls on, rolls on
She was rolling before I got here and she'll roll when I'm gone
She don't need my name and she don't need my prayer
But Lord I need her something awful and she's always right there

Oh the ocean, she's been here since before the world had names
And she'll be here long after they forget that I came
I've cried into her waters and she swallowed every tear
The ocean don't need nothing from me and that's why I come here

That's why I come here
Lord that's why I come here

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a new baby
songs.lyrics:
You came into the world at 6 AM
The light was grey and soft and everything was new
I hadn't slept in thirty hours but I didn't care
I just kept counting all your fingers, counting all your toes

I called your grandma crying, couldn't get the words out
She already knew somehow, she said she felt you coming
Your dad was pacing circles in the hallway like a fool
The nurses laughed and brought us coffee, brought us blankets, brought us you

And I don't know what I'm doing
I've never done this before
But I swear I'll learn your language
I'll be standing at your door
Every morning, every midnight
Every fever, every fall
I had no idea what love was
Till you showed me in that hall

I wrote this in my notebook while you're sleeping on my chest
Your breath is like a secret that I'm terrified to break
The window shows the parking lot but I could swear it's heaven
Everything is rearranged and I am rearranged

Your name sounds like a prayer now
I've said it forty times
Just to hear it in the room with us
Just to make it mine
Just to feel how real this is
Just to know I'm here
I've been waiting for you, darling
For what feels like a thousand years

And I don't know what I'm doing
I've never done this before
But I swear I'll learn your language
I'll be standing at your door
Every morning, every midnight
Every fever, every fall
I had no idea what love was
Till you showed me in that hall

Someday you'll want answers I won't have
Someday you'll outgrow everything I give you
But today you're three pounds seven ounces in a blanket
And today I am the whole world that you know

So I'll stay right here and breathe with you
And memorize your face
And write it down so I don't lose
A single stolen grace
You are my greatest sentence
And I'm just finding words
You are already everything
Before you've said a word

And I don't know what I'm doing
I've never done this before
But I swear I'll learn your language
I'll be standing at your door
Every morning, every midnight
Every fever, every fall
I had no idea what love was
Till you showed me in that hall

I had no idea what love was
Till the morning you were born

songs.subject: emo / the ocean
songs.lyrics:
I drove four hours just to see you
Stood at the edge where the land gives out
You were grey and you were massive
And you didn't care that I was here

I don't know why I needed this
To stand somewhere that swallows me
She's got eyes like the Atlantic
And I'm just learning how to drown

Take me under, take me under
Let the salt work through my bones
I've been landlocked in my longing
And I've never felt so alone
Every wave is like a sentence
That I don't know how to read
Take me under, take me under
Cause she's all I need

The seagulls screamed above me
Like they knew what I was thinking
Like they'd seen a thousand fools
Come down here with their hearts wide open

I took my shoes off in November
Let the water hit my feet
It was cold enough to hurt me
And that felt like honesty

Take me under, take me under
Let the salt work through my bones
I've been landlocked in my longing
And I've never felt so alone
Every wave is like a sentence
That I don't know how to read
Take me under, take me under
Cause she's all I need

She doesn't know I'm standing here
She doesn't know I drove all night
She doesn't know the things she does to me
Just laughing in the morning light

I press my face into the wind
And I can almost hear her name
The ocean doesn't love me back
But neither does she
And I love them both the same

I am ruined by the beautiful
I am ruined by the deep
Everything I reach for pulls away
Everything I want is out to sea

Take me under, take me under
Let the salt work through my bones
I've been landlocked in my longing
And I've never felt so alone
Every wave is like a sentence
That I don't know how to read
Take me under, take me under
Cause she's all I need

The tide went out and took the light
I stood there till my hands went numb
I thought about the way she laughs
And how I'm wrecked by all of it

I drove back home as dawn came in
Didn't fix a single thing
But the ocean held my sadness for a minute
And I think that's everything

Take me under, take me under
Let the salt work through my bones
I've been landlocked in my longing
And I've never felt so alone
Every wave is like a sentence
That I don't know how to read
Take me under, take me under
Cause she's all I need

She's all I need
She's all I need

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I held it like a stone inside my chest
For three years, maybe four
Every time I saw your face across a room
I picked the stone up more

I rehearsed the things I'd say to you
Wrote them down and burned the page
I was righteous and I was exhausted
And I was drowning in my own cage

But you called me on a Tuesday
Said you didn't know what for
Said you'd been sitting with your sorry
And you couldn't hold it anymore

And I want to let it go now
I want to set down every wall
I want to love you past the damage
I want to catch you when you fall
It won't be easy, I'm not easy
And the wound is still a wound
But I choose you in the morning
I choose you in the afternoon

I didn't say forgiveness comes for free
I didn't say the scar goes away
But I've been carrying your worst moment
At the cost of all my days

You sat across my kitchen table
Looking smaller than I'd known
I remembered when I loved you easily
Before I learned to be alone

And something moved inside me, shifting
Like a window letting sun
Not because you earned it fully
But because I'm tired of being done

songs.subject: synthpop / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning, got the call at nine
Your voice was static on the line
I was standing in the kitchen light
And the whole world changed its shape that night

I drove the highway, city blurred
The radio played, I didn't hear a word
Just the hum of something ending
Just the sound of me pretending I was fine

I keep the light on, keep the light on
In the room where you used to stay
I know it's empty, I know it's empty
But I can't bring myself to look away
Every frequency I'm scanning
Every signal lost in space
I keep the light on, keep the light on
Like you're coming back someday

Your coat is hanging by the door
I rearranged the room and then restored
Everything exactly like you left it
Like some part of me contested what I heard

Your coffee mug is on the counter
I washed it once and then I wept for an hour
Put it back just where you'd left it
All these small coordinates of you

I keep the light on, keep the light on
In the room where you used to stay
I know it's empty, I know it's empty
But I can't bring myself to look away
Every frequency I'm scanning
Every signal lost in space
I keep the light on, keep the light on
Like you're coming back someday

The synthesizer plays your favorite song
The one we used to drive to all night long
Windows down and you were laughing
Now the melody is all I have of it

I built a playlist, forty seven songs
Everything that ever made you sing along
Hit play and closed my eyes and for a second
For a second you were standing right beside me

I keep the light on, keep the light on
In the room where you used to stay
I know it's empty, I know it's empty
But I can't bring myself to look away
Every frequency I'm scanning
Every signal lost in space
I keep the light on, keep the light on
Like you're coming back someday

Tell me where you are in all this static
Tell me if the other side is vast
Tell me that you knew before you left me
Tell me that the love I gave you lasts

Cause I'm standing in the neon dark without you
And the city keeps on moving, doesn't care
And the lights of every building make a signal
But I can't find you anywhere

I keep the light on, keep the light on
In the room where you used to stay
I know it's empty, I know it's empty
But I can't bring myself to look away
Every frequency I'm scanning
Every signal lost in space
I keep the light on, keep the light on
Like you're coming back someday

Like you're coming back
Like you're coming back
Like you're coming back someday

songs.subject: soul / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
Empty pockets, full of laughter
That's the life I chose to lead
Pouring drinks for dreams and heartaches
Giving everyone what they need
The tips don't always cover Tuesday
But I got music on the floor
And when the last call light stops shining
I still walk out wanting more

I may be broke but honey I am rich
In all the ways that dollar bills can't fix
I got callused hands and a open heart
And I wouldn't trade this life right from the start
No I may be broke but I am not poor
Every night I find what I came here for
Standing behind this worn out bar
Counting blessings not the scars

Saturday night the whole place humming
People leaning close to talk
A woman laughing at the corner
A old man doing his evening walk
I slide him his usual without asking
He nods like I'm the only one who cares
And for a moment all the world stops spinning
And I remember why I'm here

I may be broke but honey I am rich
In all the ways that dollar bills can't fix
I got callused hands and a open heart
And I wouldn't trade this life right from the start
No I may be broke but I am not poor
Every night I find what I came here for
Standing behind this worn out bar
Counting blessings not the scars

My feet are tired and my back is aching
But there's something singing in my bones
This old building been here since my daddy
Used to carry me here when he closed up alone
I learned the names before I learned the numbers
I learned that love shows up in little things
A cold glass of water a listened story
And whatever small comfort kindness brings

I may be broke but honey I am rich
In all the ways that dollar bills can't fix
I got callused hands and a open heart
And I wouldn't trade this life right from the start
No I may be broke but I am not poor
Every night I find what I came here for
Standing behind this worn out bar
Counting blessings not the scars

Count my blessings not the scars
Yeah I'm counting every single star
Broke and beautiful as we are

songs.subject: emo / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
The fluorescent lights are killing me again
Gate C fourteen and you're checking bags
I wiped down the bar at the international terminal
Three years of watching people turn to photographs
I memorized your jacket from the distance
The way you drag your suitcase like a question mark
I poured myself a shot of something useless
And watched you disappear into the dark

This is where the leaving starts
This is where they rip apart
Everything that meant the world to me
Goodbye tastes like recycled air
And cheap perfume and I don't care
Anymore if anyone can see me bleed
I'm bleeding right here at the gate
I'm bleeding right here a minute too late

You never ordered anything but water
You sat three stools down every layover night
We talked about the cities you were flying toward
And all the ones that never felt quite right
I never said the things I should have said to you
I just refilled your glass and kept my mouth shut clean
Now they're calling final boarding over speakers
And you're the saddest thing I've ever seen

This is where the leaving starts
This is where they rip apart
Everything that meant the world to me
Goodbye tastes like recycled air
And cheap perfume and I don't care
Anymore if anyone can see me bleed
I'm bleeding right here at the gate
I'm bleeding right here a minute too late

The strangest thing about this job is watching
The way that people hold each other near the door
Like they already know that something's ending
Like they're trying to memorize the shore
I never cry I never let them see it
A bartender's supposed to be the strong one here
But tonight I pressed my forehead to the window
And let the runway lights dissolve through tears

This is where the leaving starts
This is where they rip apart
Everything that meant the world to me
Goodbye tastes like recycled air
And cheap perfume and I don't care
Anymore if anyone can see me bleed
I'm bleeding right here at the gate
I'm bleeding right here a minute too late

I watched the plane until it was a pinhole
I watched the pinhole until it was gone
I stacked the glasses wiped the bar in circles
And started telling myself to move along
But airports never really empty do they
There's always someone pressing hands to glass
Looking at the sky like it owes them something
Looking for someone who's already passed

This is where the leaving starts
This is where they rip apart
Everything that meant the world to me
Goodbye tastes like recycled air
And cheap perfume and I don't care
Anymore if anyone can see me bleed
I'm bleeding right here at the gate
I'm bleeding right here a minute too late
I'm bleeding and the planes just fly away

songs.subject: indie folk / starting over
songs.lyrics:
I left my name back in that town
Somewhere between the leaving and the ground
The highway opened up like something new
And every mile I drove I thought of you
The mountains didn't know my history
They just stood there beautiful and free
And I pulled over just to breathe the air
And felt for the first time like I could be there

I'm starting over with you beside me
The old life folded up and left behind
I'm starting over with your hand to guide me
Through all the unbecoming and the finding
We burned the map we didn't need the road
We just needed each other and the open
I'm starting over and I'm not afraid
When you're the reason and the ground I've laid

We found a little house with crooked windows
The landlord said it had some history
We laughed about the ghosts inside the hallways
And decided we would let them be
You put your records on the wooden shelves
I hung the curtains that your mother made
We made a life from all our gathered pieces
And something in me finally wasn't afraid

I'm starting over with you beside me
The old life folded up and left behind
I'm starting over with your hand to guide me
Through all the unbecoming and the finding
We burned the map we didn't need the road
We just needed each other and the open
I'm starting over and I'm not afraid
When you're the reason and the ground I've laid

There's a garden that we haven't planted yet
There's a future running just ahead
There are mornings we haven't woken into
And a thousand words we haven't said
I used to think that starting over meant
That something beautiful had to break and end
But I'm standing here with you in all this newness
And I think beginning is where I begin

I'm starting over with you beside me
The old life folded up and left behind
I'm starting over with your hand to guide me
Through all the unbecoming and the finding
We burned the map we didn't need the road
We just needed each other and the open
I'm starting over and I'm not afraid
When you're the reason and the ground I've laid

Some people fear the empty page before them
Some people can't let go of who they were
But every book I've loved began with nothing
And every song starts somewhere in a blur
So here we are two people soft and willing
Standing at the edge of something true
I'm not starting over from an ending
I'm starting over because I found you

I'm starting over
Yeah I'm starting over
And the world looks new
The world looks new
When I'm starting over with you

songs.subject: alt rock / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
She's walking down the aisle in white
I remember when she took her first steps in the light
Forty years of mornings now they blur and fade
But nothing ever touched me like this day

My hands are shaking holding onto this old pew
My wife is squeezing tight the way she used to do
I see my daughter's eyes across the room
She's got her mother's smile and all her mother's bloom

Don't let them see me crying in the front row
I swore I'd hold it together for the show
But something in the music broke me open wide
I'm just an old man trying to hold his pride

The pastor's saying words I've heard before
Back in 1979 outside that chapel door
I was the nervous one in someone's borrowed tie
Wondering how a man gets lucky enough to find

A love that stretches forty years and counting still
A love that climbs and breaks you and rebuilds
And now my little girl is standing where I stood
Lord I hope he loves her half as good

Don't let them see me crying in the front row
I swore I'd hold it together for the show
But something in the music broke me open wide
I'm just an old man trying to hold his pride

My knees don't work the way they worked back then
My hair is gone and I won't see sixty-five again
But standing here I swear I'm twenty-three
Dancing in a kitchen she is dancing next to me

She was three years old and chasing butterflies
She was seventeen and rolling tired eyes
She was twenty-two and calling late at night
And now she's beautiful and standing in the light

Don't let them see me crying in the front row
I swore I'd hold it together for the show
But something in the music broke me open wide
I'm just an old man trying to hold his pride

So go on baby girl and build your life
Go on and be somebody's world somebody's wife
I'll be in the garden if you need me I'll be there
An old man in his roses saying a quiet prayer

songs.subject: dream pop / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
The terminal is cold and bright
You're pulling at my sleeve beneath the fluorescent light
Your backpack's got your favorite dinosaur
I'm trying not to fall apart on this linoleum floor

Security is just beyond those doors
And everything that mattered stands on this side of the corridor
I count the coins inside my pocket just to breathe
I am already missing you before you leave

And the PA calls another flight
And the strangers drift like paper through the white
I am standing here with you inside this in-between
You are going somewhere I have never been

Kiss me one more time before you go through
Tell me that you'll call me when you land at two
I have packed your medicines and written down the time
You are so much braver than this mother of mine

The escalator rises and you wave
You look so small beneath the sign that says departures gate
I watch until the crowd has swallowed you away
Then I stand a little longer just in case

And the PA calls another flight
And the strangers drift like paper through the white
I am standing here with you inside this in-between
You are going somewhere I have never been

I drive home on the freeway in the rain
The radio plays something soft and I feel everything again
Your cereal bowl is sitting by the sink
It's funny what becomes unbearable when you stop to think

I remember being young and leaving too
I remember how my mother stood the way I'm standing now for you
Maybe every love is practice for the door
Maybe goodbye teaches us what we are here for

And the PA calls another flight
And the strangers drift like paper through the white
I was standing there with you inside that in-between
You are going somewhere you have never been

And the clouds will hold you when I cannot reach
And the distance is a lesson I keep trying hard to teach
Myself to bear it better every time
You are half of me and wholly yours and that is fine

And the PA calls another flight
And the strangers drift like paper through the white
I was standing there with you inside that in-between
You're out there somewhere and I'm somewhere in between

songs.subject: hip-hop / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Woke up Monday and my phone was blowing up
But it wasn't you it was just my cousin showing up
In the group chat talking nonsense like he always does
Then I saw the notification and I felt my stomach drop because

You sent a text
Three lines long
Didn't even call
Just gone
You sent a text
Lowercase letters and a period at the end
Like I was anybody
Like I was nothing like a friend

I read it seven times beside my bedroom wall
Between my soccer posters and the photos in the hall
My mom was downstairs cooking something on the stove
The whole world smelled like breakfast but I couldn't even go

You sent a text
Three lines long
Didn't even call
Just gone
You sent a text
Lowercase letters and a period at the end
Like I was anybody
Like I was nothing like a friend

I showed it to Marcus in third period math
He said bro delete her and I tried to laugh
But I kept the message and I read it in the hall
Between geometry and English when I hit the bathroom stall

And I'm sixteen and I know that sounds like nothing much
I know the grown-ups say it's puppy love and such
But I swear to every version of whatever God is real
That I have never in my life felt something this for real

You sent a text
Three lines long
Didn't even call
Just gone
You sent a text
Lowercase letters and a period at the end
Like I was anybody
Like I was nothing like a friend

So I'm typing and deleting and I'm typing it again
Trying to say something back that doesn't sound like pain
Trying to play it cool the way the older kids do
But every single word I write is just the word you

You sent a text
Three lines long
Didn't even call
Just gone
You sent a text
Lowercase letters and a period at the end
Like I was anybody

I'm gonna leave it on delivered
I'm gonna leave it on delivered
I'm gonna leave it on delivered
And act like I'm fine
And act like I'm fine

songs.subject: hip-hop / a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Shadows gather 'round the churchyard gate
Everybody dressed in black, nobody running late
Flowers on the casket, tears on every face
Trying to make sense of an empty space
Remember how he laughed, remember how he'd say
Don't you cry for me when I finally fade away
But here we are crying anyway

Pastor reading scripture, organ playing low
Grandma in the front row putting on a show
Strong for everybody else but her hands won't stop shaking
Every heart in this room is breaking
Photos on the program, young face frozen still
Never thought we'd be here but here we are and we will
Find a way to carry what he left us

We carry you in every step we take
In every breath and every choice we make
You gone but you ain't gone you understand
Still feel you every time I raise my hand
The eulogy they're reading got it right
You burned so bright you had to leave the night
Rest easy now we got it from here
Rest easy now we got it from here

Pallbearers lifting slow like they could keep him
From the ground from the cold from the leaving
Children in the back don't understand
Why we putting daddy in the land
Somebody's phone goes off and it's his ringtone
Everybody freeze up, everybody stone
For a second he was here and then he wasn't

Reception at the house with the potato salad
Everybody trading stories like a ballad
He was this he was that he was everything
Old heads laughing just to feel something
Tables full of food nobody's touching
Grief and potato salad ain't quite matching
But we here because that's what you do

We carry you in every step we take
In every breath and every choice we make
You gone but you ain't gone you understand
Still feel you every time I raise my hand
The eulogy they're reading got it right
You burned so bright you had to leave the night
Rest easy now we got it from here
Rest easy now we got it from here

Drove past his block on the way home tonight
Porch light on like somebody left the light
Force of habit maybe or somebody forgot
To turn it off or maybe they did not
Want to turn it off because the dark's too real
And a porch light is the last familiar feel
Of a man who always left the light on

His kids gon grow up and tell his story
Pass it down pass it down in all its glory
Twenty years from now somebody's gonna say
I heard about your granddad what a way
He lived his life like every day was chosen
Every door he found he left it open
That's a life right there that's a whole life

We carry you in every step we take
In every breath and every choice we make
You gone but you ain't gone you understand
Still feel you every time I raise my hand
The eulogy they're reading got it right
You burned so bright you had to leave the night
Rest easy now we got it from here
Rest easy now we got it from here

Yeah we got it from here
Yeah we got it from here
Sleep well
We got it

songs.subject: R&B / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Three dots appeared and then they went away
Came back again at the start of the day
I was making coffee thinking everything was fine
Then my phone lit up with a single line
Said I think we need to talk and I knew
That kind of talk don't come from someone who
Still wants you the way they used to

No phone call no sitting down face to face
Just a rectangle of light taking your place
Baby couldn't you have looked me in my eyes
Or was it easier to text me your goodbye
I read it seven times like it might change
Like autocorrect might rearrange
Into something that don't mean you're leaving

So this is how it ends on a little screen
The shortest sentence carrying the most it's ever mean
No punctuation where you took my heart away
Just lowercase letters on a Saturday
I zoomed in like the font might tell me more
What exactly were you looking for
When you typed the words and hit the arrow

I sat there in my kitchen on the floor
Still in my socks still holding what we were
The coffee getting cold the room too still
Baby breakups used to have a feel
Of presence of a voice of something real
Now it's just a glow and a little bell
That rings and takes your whole world with it

So this is how it ends on a little screen
The shortest sentence carrying the most it's ever mean
No punctuation where you took my heart away
Just lowercase letters on a Saturday
I zoomed in like the font might tell me more
What exactly were you looking for
When you typed the words and hit the arrow

I screenshot it I don't know why
Maybe so I have proof that we did die
Maybe so I can read it when I doubt myself
Pull it from the graveyard of my shelf
And say see there it was never really real
If it was real you would have let me feel
The weight of it the way it should be carried

Deleted the thread and then restored it twice
Some kind of painful is its own kind of nice
When you got nothing left you hold the hurt
Like a worn-out patch on a favorite shirt
I knew your voice I knew your hands I knew your smell
Now I know the font that you chose to say farewell
Helvetica or whatever it don't matter

Something with a lesson something with a hence
But right now it's just me and what you wrote
Three sentences and that was it that was our whole note
That was the whole closing of our book

songs.subject: bluegrass / a new baby
songs.lyrics:
Well I woke up this morning at a quarter to four
Never slept so little never loved so much before
Mama said it hits you like a freight train in the night
Reckon she was telling me the honest truth alright

Little fingers curled up like a fiddlehead fern
Got so much ahead of you so much still to learn
Your mama sleeping finally after all that fight she gave
Lord I never knew that I had this much love to save

You came into this world at seventeen and nine
Seven pounds and two ounces and you're already mine
Looked into your eyes before your eyes could even see
And every single thing I thought I was changed on me

I sat beside the window with you wrapped up on my chest
Wrote this down in lamplight while your mama got her rest
Tried to find the words for it and words keep coming up short
You're something that the language hasn't caught up to yet sport

They let us leave the hospital on Sunday afternoon
Strapped you in so careful had you home before the moon
Every bump and back road had me gripping white and tight
Your mama laughing telling me we'd figure out alright

You came into this world at seventeen and nine
Seven pounds and two ounces and you're already mine
Looked into your eyes before your eyes could even see
And every single thing I thought I was changed on me

Your granddaddy came over with his old beat-up guitar
Sat on the back porch and he picked beneath the stars
Said boy that baby's gonna hear this all her life
Music in the bloodline cuts deeper than a knife
Your great-grandmother's name is what we gave to you
Figured all that history ought to carry someone through

Wrote down all your measurements and stuck them in this book
First day first hour the first long loving look
Someday when you're grown and think we don't understand
You can read these pages and see where you began

You came into this world at seventeen and nine
Seven pounds and two ounces and you're already mine
Looked into your eyes before your eyes could even see
And every single thing I thought I was changed on me

It's three weeks in now and I'm still not right
Still get up four times and stare at you all night
Making sure your breathing making sure you're warm
Never thought I'd be the kind to turn into this form

Of a man who cries at nearly anything at all
Tiny little socks can bring me to a fall
Your mama caught me weeping at a commercial on TV
Said welcome to the rest of your whole life honey

You came into this world at seventeen and nine
Seven pounds and two ounces and you're already mine
Looked into your eyes before your eyes could even see
And every single thing I thought I was changed on me

Sleep now little darlin let the night roll past
Everything worth having was worth waiting for at last
I'll be right here writing till the morning comes on in
Just your daddy and his notebook and the sound of the wind

songs.subject: reggae / driving with the windows down
songs.lyrics:
The sun is risin' over the mountain road
I got nowhere to be and I'm droppin' my load
Roll down the glass and let the warm wind in
Feel the rhythm of the road beneath my skin

Salt and jasmine flowin' through my hair
Every green light feelin' like a answered prayer
The speakers bumpin' low, the bass is deep
This kinda freedom ain't the kind you keep

Roll the windows down, let the breeze blow through
Nothin' in this world gonna make me blue
Roll the windows down, feel the sun on your face
Every open road is a holy place
Roll the windows down, let your troubles fly
Watch 'em disappear into the open sky
Roll the windows down, yeah roll 'em down
This is the sweetest feelin' I have found

Coconut and diesel mixed into the air
The highway stretching long without a single care
My elbow on the sill, my fingers catch the wind
Like a bird that's finally free to begin

Children on the sidewalk waving as I pass
Old man on his porch sippin' something from a glass
The world is movin' slow and honey that's alright
Everything looks golden in this afternoon light

Roll the windows down, let the breeze blow through
Nothin' in this world gonna make me blue
Roll the windows down, feel the sun on your face
Every open road is a holy place
Roll the windows down, let your troubles fly
Watch 'em disappear into the open sky
Roll the windows down, yeah roll 'em down
This is the sweetest feelin' I have found

You could have your aircon blowin' cold and clean
You could have your tinted glass and your leather gleam
But I want the honeysuckle and the Georgia pine
Rolling through my lungs like a cup of wine

I want the thunder comin' up from miles away
I want to smell the rain before the end of day
I want the evening cooling down my sunburned arm
This old battered car is my lucky charm

Roll the windows down, let the breeze blow through

Roll the windows down, feel the sun on your face

songs.subject: synthpop / faith
songs.lyrics:
Static in the dark before the signal comes through
I've been standing at the edge of what I thought I knew
The numbers on the clock don't mean a thing tonight
I'm reaching through the frequency searching for your light

Every atom in my body wants a reason to believe

I still believe, I still believe
Even when the data fails and I cannot see
I still believe, through the digital cold
Something larger than the story I've been told
I still believe, I still believe

I still believe, in the space between
Every known and unknown thing

Neon rivers running under city glass

Every face a constellation, every voice a coded song
Maybe what I've been searching for was here the whole along

The synthesizers hum with something close to prayer
Frequencies vibrating at the edges of the air
I've run the calculations and they all come up the same
There's a mathematics to the mercy, there's a logic to the flame

I've stood inside the void and watched it watch me back

But falling is a kind of faith I've started to embrace
Every ending is a doorway, every darkness holds a face

songs.subject: lo-fi / a friend who changed
songs.lyrics:
You used to call me every night just to say nothing
We'd laugh about the teachers and the stupid things
I've got your old mix CD on the shelf collecting dust
I keep it there but I don't know why I must

You got a new jacket and a new group now
Walk right past me in the hall with your eyebrows up
I used to know every single thing inside your head
Now I'm reading strangers' faces trying to find you instead

It's weird how people change, yeah I know they always do
But nobody told me I'd be standing here
Holding onto someone that I barely knew
Maybe it was gradual, maybe it was fast
I just didn't notice until the version of you I loved
Became the past

We used to skip fourth period to sit behind the gym
Talk about the futures like they were made for us to win
You had a plan, I had a dream, the world was full of space
Now you look right through me like I've got no face

I don't hate you, that would be too easy
I just miss the way things were before the shift
Something happened somewhere in the in-between
And I'm too young to know what it means

It's weird how people change, yeah I know they always do
But nobody told me I'd be standing here
Holding onto someone that I barely knew
Maybe it was gradual, maybe it was fast
I just didn't notice until the version of you I loved
Became the past

I found a picture on my phone from October two years back
You were laughing with your head thrown all the way back
Eyes all squinted up, my thumb half in the frame
I almost texted it to you before I remembered things ain't the same

I wonder if you ever think about it
I wonder if you even know that something broke
I wonder if the person you are now
Would laugh at who we were or feel the smoke

It's weird how people change, yeah I know they always do
But nobody told me I'd be standing here
Holding onto someone that I barely knew
Maybe it was gradual, maybe it was fast
I just didn't notice until the version of you I loved
Became the past

I keep the CD on the shelf
I keep the picture on my phone
I keep the memories for myself
And try to find a way to let them go

songs.subject: disco / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:
We packed the car on a Tuesday morning
Five suitcases, no room for warning
The map was old, the tank was full
The open highway had a pull

Mama said put on something funky
Daddy's dancing, looking kind of spunky
Grandma in the back with her window cracked
We are never ever turning back

Oh we're burning down the highway
Burning down the line
A thousand miles of asphalt and we're feeling so fine
Burning down the highway, the disco balls are spinning
Somewhere out ahead of us the good times are beginning
Yeah we're burning, burning, burning down the road

Nevada desert turning orange and gold
Every radio station playing something bold
We're singing out of key and we don't even care
The music lifting up our hands into the summer air

Truck stop diner at the state line
Pie and coffee tasting so divine
Strangers waving from the parking lot
We got everything we need and we need what we got

Oh we're burning down the highway
Burning down the line
A thousand miles of asphalt and we're feeling so fine
Burning down the highway, the disco balls are spinning
Somewhere out ahead of us the good times are beginning
Yeah we're burning, burning, burning down the road

Ooh the miles roll by like a fever dream
Nothing is as far away as it might seem
Got the bass turned up and the windows shaking
Got a road trip memory in the making

Ooh we stop to see the world's largest ball of twine
Take a photograph and get back in line
Every little detour is a story to tell
Every long road trip is casting its spell

The sun going down over the mountains wide
Everybody quiet, watching from inside
Then somebody laughs and somebody sings
And we remember what a journey brings

Oh we're burning down the highway
Burning down the line
A thousand miles of asphalt and we're feeling so fine
Burning down the highway, the disco balls are spinning
Somewhere out ahead of us the good times are beginning
Yeah we're burning, burning, burning down the road

We're almost there but baby don't you rush it
The best part of arriving is the way you approach it
Let the highway hold us just a little more
We'll be home soon but we're not keeping score

Oh we're burning down the highway
Burning down the line
A thousand miles of asphalt and we're feeling so fine

Somewhere out ahead of us the good times are beginning

songs.subject: pop / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:
Counting miles like counting stars tonight
The highway pulls us through the fading light
Your playlist spinning songs we used to know
The dashboard glows as all the exit signs blow

We packed our bags before the morning came
Left everything behind except our names
The radio predicts a storm out west
But darling we were always up for tests

Miles and miles of open sky
Watch the world go blurring by
Singing loud with nowhere to be
Just the road and you and me
Miles and miles we're burning through
Every mile brings me closer to you
This asphalt river pulling us along
Miles and miles this is our song

The diner coffee tastes like everything
That freedom feels like on a golden string
We split the last of what we had to eat
And laughed until the tears ran down our cheeks

The corn fields turn to desert turn to hills
I never knew the road could give such thrills
You fell asleep somewhere past Abilene
Your hair across the window like a dream

Miles and miles of open sky
Watch the world go blurring by
Singing loud with nowhere to be
Just the road and you and me
Miles and miles we're burning through
Every mile brings me closer to you
This asphalt river pulling us along
Miles and miles this is our song

The mountains came up blue before the dawn
We counted every peak and drove headlong
Into the purple valley spreading wide
I reached across and took your hand inside

And somewhere on this ribbon we got lost
And found ourselves and never paid the cost
Of staying still and letting life grow thin
The road just keeps on giving all it's in

Miles and miles of open sky
Watch the world go blurring by
Singing loud with nowhere to be
Just the road and you and me
Miles and miles we're burning through
Every mile brings me closer to you
This asphalt river pulling us along
Miles and miles this is our song

Miles and miles and miles and miles
You make everything worthwhile
Miles and miles till the road runs out
That's what this love is all about

songs.subject: soul / driving with the windows down
songs.lyrics:
Baby let me tell you something about today
I rolled all four of them windows down
And the summer wind just carried me away
Down every back road in this town

And I thought about your fingers in my hand
How you look when you don't know I'm watching
Every beautiful thing about this land
Felt like something you'd been promising

Oh the wind is warm and your love is warmer
And I'm flying down this avenue
Every breath of air just keeps reminding
Every bit of good I know is you
Windows down and soul wide open
Riding free on what we've been building
Honey I'm so gone on you it shows
Everywhere this summer highway goes

I passed by where we had our first slow dance
That old pavilion by the river bend
And I sat there in a grateful kind of trance
Knowing this is where my lonely ends

The Spanish moss was hanging in the breeze
The way your laughter hangs inside my chest
I pulled off just to feel it if you please
Cause loving you has made me all the rest

Oh the wind is warm and your love is warmer
And I'm flying down this avenue
Every breath of air just keeps reminding
Every bit of good I know is you
Windows down and soul wide open
Riding free on what we've been building
Honey I'm so gone on you it shows
Everywhere this summer highway goes

Lord I used to drive these roads so hollow
Like a man who lost something precious young
And now I got so much more than I can swallow
Got more joy than can fit under my tongue

So I let it out the window as I'm going
Watch it rise up like a gospel choir
Every mile of this old county glowing
With the warmth of your undying fire

Oh the wind is warm and your love is warmer
And I'm flying down this avenue
Every breath of air just keeps reminding
Every bit of good I know is you
Windows down and soul wide open
Riding free on what we've been building
Honey I'm so gone on you it shows
Everywhere this summer highway goes

Baby I'm coming home to you
With all four windows down
And a heart so full it's breaking through
The most beautiful sound

songs.subject: rock / getting sober
songs.lyrics:
I poured the last bottle down the drain at dawn
While you were sleeping in the other room
I watched the amber spiral round and gone
And felt the ghost of it cut through me soon

You were four years old and didn't know
Your mama was a war she couldn't win
But something in your sleeping face said go
Back to who you were before the spin

I picked myself up off the bathroom floor
I taped your drawings back onto my wall
I dialed the number I'd been dreading for
A year of watching myself fall

I'm getting clean for you and getting clean for me
Burning through the wreckage to get free
Shaking through the nights and sweating through the days
Fighting through the hunger and the haze
I'm getting clean and it's the hardest thing I've known
But you deserve a mother not a ghost
So I'm climbing up this mountain on my own
I'm getting clean because I love you most

The meetings smell like coffee and regret
And every face in there could be my face
And I stood up and said what I said yet
And someone grabbed my hand and held their grace

You started kindergarten in the fall
I was sixty days and barely standing
But I was there and I was present through it all
And you ran to me not understanding

How much that moment cost me and rebuilt me
How your little arms undid the chains
How your voice saying mama nearly killed me
With the beauty cutting through the pain

I'm getting clean for you and getting clean for me
Burning through the wreckage to get free
Shaking through the nights and sweating through the days
Fighting through the hunger and the haze
I'm getting clean and it's the hardest thing I've known
But you deserve a mother not a ghost
So I'm climbing up this mountain on my own
I'm getting clean because I love you most

They told me it gets easier with time
And I called that a lie for half a year
But something starts to shift across the line
When survival starts to slowly feel like here

I bought you new shoes with a paycheck clean
Made your lunch and braided back your hair
Watched you sleeping again at seventeen
Months and I was finally really there

Yeah I was there I was your mother
Not the hollow thing I'd been before
I was learning how to love you and recover
How to open up my own front door

I'm getting clean for you and getting clean for me
Burning through the wreckage to get free
Shaking through the nights and sweating through the days
Fighting through the hunger and the haze
I'm getting clean and it's the hardest thing I've known
But you deserve a mother not a ghost
So I'm climbing up this mountain on my own
I'm getting clean because I love you most

There's a woman in the mirror I'm just meeting
She's been waiting underneath all of the noise
And every scar I carry is just teaching
Me the fullness of this fragile precious choice

I'm getting clean I'm getting clean
I'm getting clean I'm getting clean
For you
songs.lyrics_en:
I poured the last bottle down the drain at dawn
While you were sleeping in the other room
I watched the amber spiral round and gone
And felt the ghost of it cut through me soon

songs.subject: indie folk / driving with the windows down
songs.lyrics:
There's a road that bends behind the old mill town
Where the willows drag their fingers to the ground
And I've been driving it with both our windows down
Letting everything that loves us in

Your hair is catching all the late light of July
And you're singing something soft beneath the sky
And I keep stealing looks when you're not watching
Like a man who can't believe his life

The clover smell comes rushing through the car
And you point out every hawk and shooting star
And I think about how most things fall apart
But somehow we just keep on opening

Roll the windows down let the whole world in
Every road we're on is where we've always been
Heading somewhere slow with nowhere that we're going
Just the trees and you and everything we're growing
Roll them all the way down till the wind says our names
Till the meadow and the evening mean the same
As your hand across the console finding mine
Roll the windows down into the light

I never used to notice much before
The way the light goes golden at the four
O'clock hour when the fields have had their fill of sun
And the whole earth seems to let out what it's held

But now I see it all through knowing you
The muscadine the kudzu and the dew
Still hanging on the morning glories long past noon
I see it because you taught me how to look

The gravel road gives way to pavement black
And you lean your elbow out and don't look back
At the town we came from only forward out
Into whatever soft thing comes for us

Roll the windows down let the whole world in
Every road we're on is where we've always been
Heading somewhere slow with nowhere that we're going
Just the trees and you and everything we're growing
Roll them all the way down till the wind says our names
Till the meadow and the evening mean the same
As your hand across the console finding mine
Roll the windows down into the light

We could stop here by the sycamore a while
Watch the creek go silver for another mile
You'd say something quiet I'd pretend to hear
Over all the beautiful noise of living

And I wouldn't trade a single bit of this
Not the ordinary not the almost missed
Not the back road or the cracked and faded map
Not the small and unimportant going back

To who we were before we were each other
This car this road this ordinary wonder
Of two people just alive and barely speaking
With the whole wide open summer leaking in

Roll the windows down let the whole world in
Every road we're on is where we've always been
Heading somewhere slow with nowhere that we're going
Just the trees and you and everything we're growing

Roll the windows down into the light

Into the light into the light

songs.subject: trap / a friend who changed
songs.lyrics:
Used to call me every night, cryin' on the phone
Now you too good for us, actin' like you grown
Remember when we starved together, lights got cut off too
Now you got a little money and forgot what we been through
My baby askin' where you at, I don't know what to say
Can't explain to a five-year-old why auntie moved away
We used to split our last twenty at the dollar store
Now you walk right past me like you never seen me before

I see you changed, I see you changed
Ain't the same girl that was holdin' my hand through the rain
I see you changed, I see you changed
Got new friends, new life, but girl you still got our name
I see you changed

Pulled myself up with no help, workin' double shifts
You was right there when I had my son, you brought the baby gifts
Now your number going straight to voicemail every time I call
And I heard through everybody else you don't remember the struggle at all
I'm a mother first so I keep my feelings locked inside
But at night when the kids asleep I let myself cry
Cause I lost my best friend to a version I don't know
And the hardest part of all is watchin' someone you love go

I see you changed, I see you changed
Ain't the same girl that was holdin' my hand through the rain
I see you changed, I see you changed
Got new friends, new life, but girl you still got our name
I see you changed

My kids still love you, they still draw your name in crayon on the wall
I kept every picture of us two hangin' in the hall
I don't need your money, never needed nothing fake
I just needed my sister and you left me at the gate
These streets been talkin' and they say you doin' well
But doin' well without your people is its own kind of hell
I forgive you cause I have to, for my peace and for my kids
But I mourn you like you dyin' cause that's really what this is

I see you changed, I see you changed
Ain't the same girl that was holdin' my hand through the rain
I see you changed, I see you changed
Got new friends, new life, but girl you still got our name
I see you changed, yeah you changed
And I'm still here in the same place cryin' in the rain
I see you changed, I see you changed
Lost my best friend and the world don't know my pain
I see you changed

songs.subject: hip-hop / heartbreak
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this mornin' reachin' for your side of the bed
Got your voicemail saved just to hear the things you said
Your hoodie still hangin' on the back of the door
I keep tellin' myself I don't love you no more
But the lies I tell myself ain't workin' like they should
And I'm drivin' through our neighborhood misunderstood
Passin' every restaurant we used to close down
And this whole city feel like it's gonna swallow me now

How do you move on from someone who was everything
How do you find your breath when the silence starts to sting
I gave you every broken piece and you made me feel whole
And now I'm standin' in the wreckage tryna find my soul
How do you move on, how do you move on
When half of who you are is already gone

Called my brothers up but I ain't say what was wrong
Told em I was good, told em I was holdin' on strong
But strong is just a word that men use when they bleed alone
And I been leakin' out for weeks just starin' at my phone
You posted up like everything was perfectly fine
Smilin' for the camera while I'm losin' my mind
How you do all that while I can't get off this floor
I thought I knew you but I never knew what you kept in store

How do you move on from someone who was everything
How do you find your breath when the silence starts to sting
I gave you every broken piece and you made me feel whole
And now I'm standin' in the wreckage tryna find my soul
How do you move on, how do you move on
When half of who you are is already gone

I ain't write this for you, I wrote this for me
Gotta get it out my chest so I can finally breathe
Cause the version of myself I lost along the way
Been buried underneath the things I never got to say
I loved you past the point that loving made sense
I stayed through every storm and every argument tense
And maybe that's on me for givin' more than I had
But I refuse to let this pain be the best thing I've had

I'm gonna move on even though it's everything
Gonna find my breath even when the silence stings
I gave my broken pieces but I'm taking them back
Gonna find the version of myself that got lost in the cracks
I'm gonna move on, yeah I'm gonna move on
Even if it takes me years to feel like I belong
How do you move on from someone who was everything
You fall apart completely and you learn to grow your wings

songs.subject: ballad / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
These old hands are shakin' as I hold your face
Eighty-one years old and I still can't keep my grace
You got your mother's eyes and your grandfather's chin
And every time I look at you I see where I've been
The terminal is crowded but the world feels still
I practiced what to say but I never got the skill
For goodbyes, never could, not since your grandma went
Now here I am again not knowin' what God meant

Don't cry for me at the gate, save your tears for something real
There's a whole wide world out there waitin' for you to feel
I've had my time, I've seen my seasons come and go
Just promise me you'll write and let this old man know
That you're alright, that you're livin', that you're runnin' free
And when you're old like me, you'll understand what this costs me
Don't cry for me at the gate

Your grandmother and I stood here in 'sixty-three
She was wearin' that blue dress and she was leavin' me
Three months she was gone and felt like thirty years
I wrote her every day and soaked the pages with my tears
But she came back to me and Lord we built a life
Raised four kids and buried two and weathered every strife
And now she's gone for good and you're the one that stays
When you visit on the holidays, you brighten up my days

Don't cry for me at the gate, save your tears for something real
There's a whole wide world out there waitin' for you to feel
I've had my time, I've seen my seasons come and go
Just promise me you'll write and let this old man know
That you're alright, that you're livin', that you're runnin' free
And when you're old like me, you'll understand what this costs me
Don't cry for me at the gate

I know the house gets quiet when your car pulls away
And I sit in my armchair and I let the evening grey
I make my tea for one and watch the news alone
But I made my peace with lonely a long time ago
What I never made my peace with is the speed of time
How you were just a baby and now look at you shine
Go on and change the world, don't you worry about me
Just carry what I told you and go be what I couldn't be

The gate is callin' your name and I'm still standin' here
Holdin' on a second cause I need the moment clear
Your eyes are full of water and I'm pretendin' mine are dry
I've said a thousand goodbyes and this one still cuts like the first time
So go on, go on now, don't look back at me
I'll be alright, I've survived every hard thing life could bring
Go be the reason that I made it through the years
Go live the life I dreamed when I was young and full of fire and fear

Don't cry for me at the gate, save your tears for something real
There's a whole wide world out there waitin' for you to feel
I've had my time, I've seen my seasons come and go
Just promise me you'll write and let this old man know
That you're alright, that you're livin', that you're runnin' free
And when you're old like me, you'll understand what this costs me
Don't cry for me at the gate
Don't cry, don't cry
Just go and live for me
