songs.subject: trap / summer ending
songs.lyrics:
Ain't gotta check the calendar I feel it in the air
Everybody movin' slower, something heavy everywhere
The pool closed down on Sunday and the cookouts gettin' thin
And that feeling in my stomach keep on creeping back again
August got a different light, you can see it in the sky
Like the sun already know that it's about to say goodbye
Nights are comin' faster and the mornings comin' cold
And everything we lived this summer turnin' grey and old

Summer's dyin' on us, summer's dyin' slow
All the things we said we'd do we never got to go
Summer's dyin' on us, feel it in my bones
Sweatin' through the night but now I'm reachin' for my coat
Summer's dyin', summer's dyin'
All those lazy perfect days they don't know how to stay
Summer's dyin' on us and we gotta face the grey

Remember back in June when everything felt wide
We had windows down and music up and nowhere we had to hide
Spent three weeks straight not thinkin' bout a single thing
Just livin' in the light and lettin' summertime just sing
Now the kids are gettin' fitted for they back to school shoes
And the news is talkin' autumn and I'm singin' the blues
Barbecue smell fading, front porches gettin' bare
Crickets still out late at night but they got sadness in their air

Summer's dyin' on us, summer's dyin' slow
All the things we said we'd do we never got to go
Summer's dyin' on us, feel it in my bones

Summer's dyin', summer's dyin'
All those lazy perfect days they don't know how to stay
Summer's dyin' on us and we gotta face the grey

Last bonfire of the season and we let it burn till four
Everybody knew it but ain't nobody said it no more
Took a picture with the whole crew cause we needed to hold on
To the version of ourselves before the summer gone
The lake is gettin' darker and the campgrounds closin' up
And we traded out the lemonade for coffee in a cup
Back to alarm clocks, back to schedules, back to all of that
Man I'd give up everything to just get summer back

But she don't wait, no she never waits for no one
She just packs up all her golden light and runs
Leaves you standin' in September with your memories
Wishin' you had held her tighter while she let you breathe
So I pour one out for summer, yeah I pour it slow
For the nights we danced barefoot and the places that we'd go
For the freedom and the fire and the heat upon the street
Summer I will miss you till you find your way back to me

Summer's dyin' on us, summer's dyin' slow
All the things we said we'd do we never got to go
Summer's dyin' on us, feel it in my bones
Sweatin' through the night but now I'm reachin' for my coat
Summer's dyin', summer's dyin'
All those lazy perfect days they don't know how to stay
Summer's dyin' on us and we gotta face the grey
Summer's dyin', yeah she dyin'
Goodbye summer, see you on the other side

All those lazy perfect days they don't know how to stay
Summer's dyin' on us and we gotta face the grey

Remember back in June when everything felt wide
We had windows down and music up and nowhere we had to hide
Spent three weeks straight not thinkin' bout a single thing
Just livin' in the light and lettin' summertime just sing
Now the kids are gettin' fitted for they back to school shoes
And the news is talkin' autumn and I'm singin' the blues
Barbecue smell fading, front porches gettin' bare
Crickets still out late at night but they got sadness in their air

Summer's dyin' on us, summer's dyin' slow
All the things we said we'd do we never got to go
Summer's dyin' on us, feel it in my bones
Sweatin' through the night but now I'm reachin' for my coat
Summer's dyin', summer's dyin'
All those lazy perfect days they don't know how to stay
Summer's dyin' on us and we gotta face the grey

Last bonfire of the season and we let it burn till four

Back to alarm clocks, back to schedules, back to all of that

songs.subject: emo / starting over
songs.lyrics:
Broken crayons still color, that's what they say
I wrote it on a napkin on my worst day
The bills were stacked like monuments to fear
And you were sleeping soundly, didn't hear me disappear
Into the bathroom, sitting on the floor
Asking God or anyone what I was here for

I'm starting over, starting over again
Picking up the pieces with these shaking hands
I'm starting over, starting over tonight
Learning how to stand back up and fight
For you, for me, for something I believe
I'm starting over, watch me finally breathe

The daycare smells like bleach and broken dreams
And I'm holding it together at the seams
My mother says I made my bed, lie in it
I'm done with all her wisdom, every bit
I paint my nails alone on Friday nights
And practice being brave before I kill the lights

I'm starting over, starting over again
Picking up the pieces with these shaking hands
I'm starting over, starting over tonight
Learning how to stand back up and fight
For you, for me, for something I believe
I'm starting over, watch me finally breathe

You asked me once if mommy ever cries
I told you only when I watch the sunrise
Because that part was true, I cry but then
I watch the whole world get to start again
And baby so do we, we always do
There's nothing in this world I'd trade for you

So I'm starting over, starting over again
No longer scared of these unsteady hands
I'm starting over, starting over tonight
I finally learned the difference between wrong and right
It's you, it's me, it's something I believe
We're starting over, God just let us breathe
Let us breathe
Let us breathe

I threw away the photos of that life
Cut off the dead ends with a kitchen knife
The apartment's small but every wall is ours
I hung your drawings up among the stars
Glow-in-the-dark constellations on the ceiling
Baby this right here is what it means to be healing

I'm starting over, starting over again
Turned all of my damage into medicine
I'm starting over, starting over tonight
And yeah it's hard but hard don't mean it isn't right
For you, for me, for all I still believe
We're starting over, watch us finally breathe
Watch us finally breathe
Watch us finally breathe

songs.subject: hip-hop / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning and the city don't sleep
Got secrets in the gutters running forty feet deep
Streetlights flickering like they trying to confess
Cabs running red lights through the emptiness
Steam coming up from the underground veins
The city got a heartbeat, yeah it pumping through pain

Corner store neon painting red on the block
Old man behind the glass not watching the clock
Every transaction got a story attached
Two brothers in the alley getting back on track
Or maybe falling off, hard to tell at this hour
When the night got this much weight and this much power

This is three AM, three AM
When the city strips down to her bones again
Three AM, three AM
When the wolves and the angels share the same amen
Three AM, three AM
When the truth comes out and you can't pretend
Three AM, this is where we live
Three AM, this is all we give

Nurse walking home in her scrubs looking tired
Another shift of keeping strangers' hearts rewired
She pass the club where the last ones spill out
Laughing too loud, got nothing to shout about
DJ packing up his crates in a van
Once had a record deal, now running his plan

Homeless prophet on the steps of the bank
Got a cardboard sign and a mind that's a tank
Full of everything this city threw away
Visions that would terrify the light of day
He nod at the nurse, she nod right back
Two survivors on the same midnight track

This is three AM, three AM
When the city strips down to her bones again
Three AM, three AM
When the wolves and the angels share the same amen
Three AM, three AM
When the truth comes out and you can't pretend
Three AM, this is where we live
Three AM, this is all we give

I been up since yesterday running these routes
Seen the best of this city and I've seen the drought
Seen the kids on the corner making minimum wage
Seen the artists in their lofts burning up the page
Seen the lovers and the fighters and the ones in between
This city got more faces than you ever have seen

Restaurant workers counting tips in the lot
Laughing about something, giving all that they got
Line cook got flour on his neck like a ghost
He sending half his check to the ones who need it most
Back home where the family got his picture on the wall
Hero of the block, and they don't know the half at all

This is three AM, three AM
When the city strips down to her bones again
Three AM, three AM
When the wolves and the angels share the same amen
Three AM, three AM
When the truth comes out and you can't pretend
Three AM, this is where we live
Three AM, this is all we give

And when the sun finally drag itself up over the east
The night shift disappear like some kind of beast
Back into the walls, back into the ground
The city put her daytime face back on without a sound
But we know what she is, we know what she hides
We the three AM people, we ride with her tides

Three AM, three AM
Yeah the city strips down to her bones again
Three AM, three AM
When the wolves and the angels share the same amen
Three AM, this is where we live
Three AM, everything we give
Three AM

songs.subject: synthpop / heartbreak
songs.lyrics:
I polish glasses clean at the end of every night
Watch the liquid amber catch the neon light
Another stranger came and sat down at my bar
Told me all about the one who broke him like a star
Dropped from the sky and shattered on the floor
I mixed his drink and nodded, heard it all before

Because I know that look, I've worn that look myself
Keep the good stuff and the heartbreak on the same shelf
Every night I pour the medicine and smile
Been standing in this same fluorescent isle
For years now, watching people fall apart
I'm the last confession booth for every broken heart

Neon and static and the sound of the machines
I'm still replaying all our old routines
Neon and static and the cold between
I can feel you everywhere and nowhere in between
Neon and static, I'm still here behind the glass
Pouring out the present while I'm living in the past

Last call and the stools are upside down
The cleaning crew is moving through the empty underground
I wipe the bar until it shines like something new
Can't wipe away the memory of you
You used to sit right there on number four
Order something dark and leave me wanting more

You never told me what you did before you came to me
Just said that everyone you loved had let you leave
And I thought I understood, thought I could break that chain
Learned the hard way that the broken ones bring rain
You disappeared like everyone you'd known
And now I'm just the bartender alone

Neon and static and the sound of the machines
I'm still replaying all our old routines
Neon and static and the cold between
I can feel you everywhere and nowhere in between
Neon and static, I'm still here behind the glass
Pouring out the present while I'm living in the past

I hear it in the synthesizers late at night
That humming in the walls when I'm the one who kills the light
Sounds like something you would say when you were half asleep
All those tiny words I swore that I would keep
Funny how a sound can pull you back so fast
Into something beautiful that didn't last

My regulars don't know they're sitting in your seat
Don't know the whole geography of your defeat
Don't know I built this whole career on staying numb
On pouring drinks and acting like I'm not undone
But every night the neon bleeds into the floor
And I am twenty-seven and you're walking through my door

Neon and static and the sound of the machines
I'm still replaying all our old routines
Neon and static and the cold between
I can feel you everywhere and nowhere in between
Neon and static, I'm still here behind the glass
Pouring out the present while I'm living in the past

I serve the heartbroken their bitter remedies
Never mention that I'm drowning by degrees
They tip me well and stumble out into the rain
And every night I lock the door and do it all again
The synthesizers hum, the neon breathes
And somewhere you are living like you never needed me

Neon and static and the sound of the machines
God I'm still replaying all our old routines
Neon and static and the cold between
I can feel you everywhere and nowhere in between
Neon and static, I'm still here behind the glass
Some people pour the future, I just pour the past
I just pour the past
I just pour the past

songs.subject: synthpop / an ex you still think about
songs.lyrics:
My hands don't look like mine when I hold photographs
They're older now, the knuckles cracked like epitaphs
To all the years I spent not saying what I meant
And you were there and then one day you went
I found your number in an old coat, faded through
Still memorized it though, what does that tell you

My daughter says I need to let the dead things go
She doesn't understand what dead things she don't know
She never saw the way you laughed before a storm
Or how you made a house feel more than just a form
She never knew the version of me that you kept
The one I've been looking for since you left

Somewhere in the circuitry of who I used to be
You're still there pulsing like a frequency
Somewhere in the static of these years between
You're the signal, always the clearest thing I've seen
Somewhere in the circuitry I can't decode
You became the dream that never let me go

The television sounds like nothing anymore
I eat my dinner watching light move on the floor
My wife was good to me, I want you to know that
I built a decent life, I'm not ashamed of that
But decent isn't everything a young man swore
He'd find the night he stood outside your door

You moved away and married someone I never knew
I heard it from a friend who heard it from you
I told myself I was relieved, I told myself I'd heal
Funny how a lie can almost start to feel real
Fifty years of almost feeling fine
And still I wake at four and think of you and nine

Nineteen seventy-nine, that summer in the heat
The way the pavement shimmered underneath our feet
We thought we had all the time that anyone could need
Turned out time was just the fastest thing to bleed

Somewhere in the circuitry of who I used to be
You're still there pulsing like a frequency
Somewhere in the static of these years between
You're the signal, always the clearest thing I've seen
Somewhere in the circuitry I can't decode
You became the dream that never let me go

I don't want you back, I want to make that plain
I'm not a fool, I know you can't go back again
But I want back the person standing next to you
That kid who thought that anything could still come true
He's in there somewhere under all these years
Behind the medication and the morning mirror

My grandson plays those synthesizer songs
The ones with all that feeling and the shining tones
I heard one and I sat down in his room
And told him once I knew a girl could fill that kind of room
With something you can't name but always recognize
He laughed and said, granddad you're kind of a surprise

Somewhere in the circuitry of who I used to be
You're still there pulsing like a frequency
Somewhere in the static of these years between
You're the signal, always the clearest thing I've seen
Somewhere in the circuitry I can't decode
You became the dream that never let me go

I hope you're warm wherever evening finds you now
I hope someone has loved you and has shown you how
I hope the years were kinder than the ones we knew
I hope you sometimes hear a song and think of me too
I'll fold this thought back up and put it with the rest
The ones I keep inside the left side of my chest

Somewhere in the circuitry
Somewhere in the frequency
Somewhere in the years between
You're still the clearest thing I've seen
Somewhere I can't decode
You never let me go
You never let me go
You never let me go

songs.subject: americana / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:
The radio's playing something soft and low
Mama's out and the whole house is mine to know
I push the chairs back, feel the cool linoleum
The ceiling fan's my spotlight, I'm the only one

I don't need nobody telling me to move
I found the beat between the refrigerator's hum
Dancing in the kitchen like I'm seventeen
The most alive that I have ever been

I spin around the counter where she rolls the dough
I two-step past the window where the sunflowers grow
My socks are sliding on the old cracked floor
I knock the pepper off the shelf and dance some more

I don't need nobody telling me to move
I found the beat between the refrigerator's hum
Dancing in the kitchen like I'm seventeen
The most alive that I have ever been

Someday they'll tell me that I gotta grow up straight
Walk a proper line and hold my feelings to a gate
But right now the kettle's singing and the light is gold
And I'm dancing in the kitchen before I get too old

The neighbors prob'ly see me through the window glass
A silly girl who doesn't know these good years pass
But I know better than they think I do
I'm holding on to something wild and true

I don't need nobody telling me to move
I found the beat between the refrigerator's hum
Dancing in the kitchen like I'm seventeen
The most alive that I have ever been

Dancing in the kitchen like I'm seventeen
The most alive that I have ever been

I push the chairs back, feel the cool linoleum

songs.subject: americana / working a dead-end job
songs.lyrics:
Clock punched in at seven, clock punched out at four
Fluorescent lights been humming since 1984
Manager's got a clipboard and a brand new car
I'm mopping up the back lot underneath the same old stars

Fourteen months of Fridays all the same
Coffee from the break room, learn to forget your name
The radio plays something that you used to love
Now it's just the soundtrack to the waiting room above

I keep my head down, I keep my boots worn through
I keep on showing up the way my daddy taught me to
The highway takes me home along the faded yellow line
Working a dead end job and running out of time

Bobby from the loading dock got married in the spring
Cashed his check at seven and still can't buy a ring
Linda's been here twenty years, she knows every crack
In the plaster of this building and she ain't looking back

They say that honest work is something to be proud
I write that on my mirror when the doubting gets too loud
But somewhere past the exit ramp there's something in the air
A different kind of life that feels like breathing clean out there

I keep my head down, I keep my boots worn through
I keep on showing up the way my daddy taught me to
The highway takes me home along the faded yellow line
Working a dead end job and running out of time

Come September I was gonna go back to school
Fill out all the papers sitting on the stool
But September came and went like all the rest
And I folded up my dreaming with my Sunday best

There's something in the stubborn way I tie my boots each dawn
Something in the quiet of a man who just keeps on
I don't know if it's dignity or if it's just the rut
Either way I'm here at seven with the door propped up

I keep my head down, I keep my boots worn through
I keep on showing up the way my daddy taught me to
The highway takes me home along the faded yellow line
Working a dead end job and running out of time

songs.subject: trap / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
Woke up in the penthouse suite it's the big day
White dress on the hanger and the skies grey
Mama crying in the corner doing her makeup right
Said she been waiting on this morning her whole life

Rolls Royce pulling up it's the arrival
Everybody dressed up for my survival
Diamonds on my neck and the veil on right
Bout to walk into the best night

It's the wedding day, yeah we going all out
Flowers on the altar roses all about
Ice sculpture melting in the summer heat
Everybody standing when I take my feet
Wedding day, said it's the wedding day
Somebody better capture every single frame
Photographer moving like he know my name
It's the wedding day

Pastor at the podium with the good word
Groomsmen looking clean every tie preferred
My daddy holding my arm at the door
Never seen him cry like that before
Guests in the pews everybody standing tall
String quartet playing in the marble hall
Tears in my eyes but the makeup held
Walked to the altar like a miracle

It's the wedding day, yeah we going all out
Flowers on the altar roses all about
Ice sculpture melting in the summer heat
Everybody standing when I take my feet
Wedding day, said it's the wedding day
Somebody better capture every single frame
Photographer moving like he know my name
It's the wedding day

Reception got the DJ on the ones and twos
Open bar flowing everybody lose
First dance slow then the beat drop hard
Whole venue going crazy in the courtyard
Aunties on the floor doing the old school
Groomsmen getting loose breaking every rule
Cake cutting cameras flashing everywhere
Flower petals falling through the chandelier air

Said I do and I meant every single word
In a room full of people you the only one I heard
Slip the ring on slow like a ceremony
Every moment feeling like it's only
Ours to keep now sealed and spoken
Every vow a promise never to be broken
Drive away with the cans behind the car
Disappearing underneath the falling stars

Everybody standing when I take my feet

songs.subject: synthpop / a new baby
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. and the world is glowing blue
Monitor light and the sound of you breathing
I never knew silence could feel this loud
I never knew my hands could shake this gently

Something electric is moving through the walls
Something digital turning into something real
I wrote it down so I wouldn't forget
The way you smelled like beginning

I'm a diary entry, ink still wet
Timestamp blinking, haven't slept yet
Every second feels like a photograph
Every heartbeat writes the paragraph
Of everything I didn't know I'd be
Until you opened your eyes and looked at me

The nurses hummed like fluorescent lights
The city kept on spinning outside the window
But in this room we invented a new time
Where clocks don't mean what clocks used to mean

I charged my phone just to hold something familiar
Then put it down because nothing compares
To the analog fact of your fingers curling
Around the edge of my whole existence

I'm a diary entry, ink still wet
Timestamp blinking, haven't slept yet
Every second feels like a photograph
Every heartbeat writes the paragraph
Of everything I didn't know I'd be
Until you opened your eyes and looked at me

Day one, page one, the cursor blinking
I've been composing this song my whole life
And every lyric I thought I'd already written
Got deleted when you arrived

Now I'm starting over, starting clean
Learning a language I've never spoken
You're the signal cutting through the static
You're the frequency I've been searching

Timestamp blinking, haven't slept yet
Every second feels like a photograph
Every heartbeat writes the paragraph
Of everything I didn't know I'd be
Until you opened your eyes and looked at me

Until you opened your eyes

4. A americana song about getting sober.

songs.subject: ballad / an old photograph
songs.lyrics:
I found you in a shoebox underneath the stairs
Between a broken watch and letters no one reads
You're faded now, the edges soft as winter air
But Lord you bring me to my knees

She's standing by the Chevy in the summer light
A dress the color of the Gulf of Mexico
Her hand is raised against the sun, she's squinting right
At someone I used to know

That someone was a young man, full of fire and noise
He thought that time was something he could spend
He didn't know how quiet swallows up the boys
And turns them into old men

Oh the photograph don't lie but it don't tell it all
It can't hold the sound of her laughing in the yard
It can't keep the way she caught me every time I'd fall
Or how loving someone easy can be hard

Fifty years we built a life from borrowed land
Raised our children, buried both our mothers
I memorized the particular shape of her hand
The way no two people memorize another

And here she is, nineteen and unconquered
Standing in a July that's long since gone
I turn you over, see my own handwriting scrawled
Martha, summer '61

Oh the photograph don't lie but it don't tell it all
It can't hold the sound of her laughing in the yard
It can't keep the way she caught me every time I'd fall
Or how loving someone easy can be hard

The house is quiet in a way it never used to be
The kind of quiet that has weight and weather
I make my coffee, set one cup out of habit, see
Then put the second cup away together

Some mornings I forget for just a half a breath
Then the kitchen tells me what I already know
There are worse things in this world than loving until death
And worse griefs than watching someone go

Oh the photograph don't lie but it don't tell it all
It can't hold the sound of her laughing in the yard
It can't keep the way she caught me every time I'd fall
Or how loving someone easy can be hard

So I'll put you on the mantle where the good light falls
Where I can see you when I drink my morning coffee
Nineteen forever, standing pretty, knowing nothing at all
About how long I'd love you
How desperately I'd love you
How I'd still be loving you

songs.subject: hip-hop / a new baby
songs.lyrics:
Six in the morning, he's crying again
I count on my fingers how long since I slept
The formula's low and the rent's due Friday
And my mama can't come till the end of the week
But I look at his face and I pull myself up
'Cause somebody in this apartment gotta be strong
And that somebody's me, been that somebody
Every single day since the day he was born

They don't show you this part in the movies
They don't rap about milk stains and no sleep
They don't talk about signing the papers alone
Or walking out the hospital just you and me
But we made it home, baby, we made it home
Put your car seat in a taxi and we made it home

This is for my son, this is for my son
I am the moon and he is my sun
Doing everything under it just to see him run
Single, yeah, but never alone, never undone
This is for my son, this is for my son

I had dreams before you, let me be honest
I had plans and a passport and places to go
And I won't lie and say I didn't cry in the bathtub
When the test came back telling me what I didn't know
But dreams don't die, baby, they just get redirected
And you became the reason behind every one
I'm going back to school in the spring semester
I'm building something real for both of us, son

They gonna underestimate us, let them
They don't know what a woman alone can do
They never watched me stretch two hundred dollars
Into a whole week of something close to new
We got hand-me-down onesies and secondhand grace
And more love in this apartment than money can buy

This is for my son, this is for my son
I am the moon and he is my sun
Doing everything under it just to see him run
Single, yeah, but never alone, never undone
This is for my son, this is for my son

Some nights I call my girl and I cry on the phone
Some nights I'm laughing because he made a face
Some nights I sit in the rocker while he's sleeping
And I thank God for giving me somebody to raise
My mama said it takes a village, I believe her
But sometimes the village don't show up on time
So you become the village, you become the anchor
You become every single thing your baby needs tonight

And I need him just as much as he needs me
That's the part nobody tells you either
That the love comes back a hundred times the terror
That the baby saves you same as you save him
I was drowning in a life I didn't recognize
And he pulled me to a shore I'd never seen
Now I'm standing in the morning making oatmeal
And for the first time in my life I know what living means

This is for my son, this is for my son
I am the moon and he is my sun
Doing everything under it just to see him run
Single, yeah, but never alone, never undone
This is for my son, this is for my son

Yeah this is for my son
My beautiful, complicated, perfect son
We gon' be alright
We already are

For each, write only the song lyrics -- no titles, no section labels like Verse or Chorus, no commentary. Separate each song with a line that is exactly:
@@@NEXT-SONG@@@

1. A synthpop song about a new baby. like a diary entry
2. A ballad song about an old photograph. from an old man's point of view
3. A hip-hop song about a new baby. from a single mother's point of view
4. A americana song about getting sober.

songs.subject: americana / getting sober
songs.lyrics:
I used to know a back road that could swallow a man whole
Gravel and darkness and the bottle on the seat
I drove it every Friday with a purpose and a hole
And I called that hole my freedom, called that recklessness relief

The neon signs of Clarksville knew my name and knew my order
Jim Beam and a Budweiser, keep 'em coming son
I thought I was a cowboy living life without a border
'Til I woke up in a ditch somewhere east of everyone

I'm learning how to stand up in the morning without armor
Learning how to feel the cold and not reach for the fire
Trading in the liar that I loved for something harder
Trading in the hunger for a truth that don't expire

Gonna lay this bottle down by the side of the road
Gonna let the river wash away what I can't carry alone
There's a long way still to go and Lord I already know
But I'm walking sober now through the wreckage of my old home

My daddy was a drinking man, his daddy was the same
I thought it was inheritance, I thought it was my blood
Like some kind of heirloom I was handed with his name
Like the land and the stubbornness and the tendency toward mud

My ex-wife took the children to her sister's place in Memphis
Left a note that said she'd pray for me but couldn't stay
I held that note for three days before I finally finished
The last bottle in the house and called my brother Ray

He drove four hours without asking any questions
Sat with me through Sunday, made me eggs and black coffee
Said sometimes the bravest thing ain't fighting, it's surrendering
Said the river's always running, always willing to be free

Gonna lay this bottle down by the side of the road
Gonna let the river wash away what I can't carry alone
There's a long way still to go and Lord I already know
But I'm walking sober now through the wreckage of my old home

Three hundred and twelve days since the last one touched my lips
I counted every sunrise like a prisoner counts walls
Some days are just a white-knuckle grinding of the fists
Some days are almost easy and the light comes down the hall

I called my daughter on her birthday, first time in two years
She didn't hang up, that's the thing, she stayed on the line
We talked about her school play and I swallowed back the tears
And she said daddy, are you better, and I said I'm trying to be fine

I'm trying, that's the truest thing that I have ever spoken
More true than all the promises I made and couldn't keep
The man I was is shattered and the man I am is broken
But broken things can mend themselves if you give them time to breathe

Gonna lay this bottle down by the side of the road
Gonna let the river wash away what I can't carry alone
There's a long way still to go and Lord I already know
But I'm walking sober now through the wreckage of my old home

The wreckage of my old home
Finding something worth saving in the wreckage of my old home

songs.subject: americana / driving with the windows down
songs.lyrics:
Cracked the windows on the highway
Let the August heat blow through
Turned the radio up louder
So I couldn't think of you
These white lines keep on coming
Like promises you broke
And I'm burning through the county
On a tank of grief and smoke

I used to know where I was going
Now I'm just going fast
Corn fields bleeding into twilight
And I'm leaving you at last
Or maybe I'm just running
Same damn thing some days
Down these two-lane back roads
Watching everything turn haze

Roll on, roll on, roll on down
Wind in my hair and a heart on the ground
Fields keep flying and the sun keeps dropping low
I don't need a destination
I just need to go
Roll on, roll on, roll on down

Somewhere back in that town you're sleeping
Or maybe you're not alone
I got one hand on the wheel now
And I'm too far gone to moan
There's a diner in the next county
Where nobody knows my name
I'll drink black coffee till the morning
Try to feel something again

These old roads don't ask you nothing
They just take you where you go
I've been crying since the county line
But you'd never even know
The stars are coming out now
Like they always will
Long after you forget me
Long after I go still

Roll on, roll on, roll on down
Wind in my hair and a heart on the ground
Fields keep flying and the sun keeps dropping low
I don't need a destination
I just need to go
Roll on, roll on, roll on down

And I swear I'm not looking backward
Though your exit's in my rearview still
I'm just driving, just breathing
Got a long night left to fill
Maybe somewhere past the morning
I'll remember how to be
Till then I'll let this warm wind
Do its damage over me

Roll on, roll on, roll on down
Wind in my hair and a heart on the ground
Fields keep flying and the sun keeps dropping low
I don't need a destination
I just need to go
Roll on, roll on, roll on down
Just roll on down

songs.subject: reggae / working a dead-end job
songs.lyrics:
Clock on the wall say half past seven
Same clock always running slow
Boss man hollering from the back room
Telling me things I already know
Stacking boxes, mopping hallways
Pulling double shifts for change
But when I think about my darling
Even this dead end feels like fame

She called me this morning, say she love me
Her voice like sunshine on the sea
I put the phone back in my pocket
Carry her words all day with me
So let them cut the hours, cut the wages
Let them take away my break
Long as I got someone waiting
There's a reason I can wake

Cause I'm rich, I'm rich, I'm so rich inside
Got love burning like a fire I cannot hide
Could be broke and bent and buried in the rain
But she smiles at me and I forget the pain
I'm rich, I'm rich, walking on the tide
With her love burning me up inside

Monday comes like a bad neighbor
Knocking early, staying long
Fluorescent light above the register
Flickering like a tired song
Customers come and go like seasons
Never look me in the eye
But in my mind I see her dancing
Underneath a Kingston sky

They don't know what I'm carrying
They don't see the gold I hold
Every hour I'm getting richer
In a way they've never been told
Let them laugh about my paycheck
Let them talk about my shoes
When you're loved the way she loves me
Brother, you got nothing left to lose

Cause I'm rich, I'm rich, I'm so rich inside
Got love burning like a fire I cannot hide
Could be broke and bent and buried in the rain
But she smiles at me and I forget the pain
I'm rich, I'm rich, walking on the tide
With her love burning me up inside

End of the night I'm counting nothing
Pockets empty heading home
But the gate opens and she's standing there
And brother I am not alone
She hands me tea and asks me how I feel
Rubs the tired from my hands
And in that moment I'm a king again
Standing on my promised land

So let the dead end stay a dead end
Let the wages stay the same
I'll be whistling through the morning shift
Whispering her name
Cause some men chase after fortune
Some men chase the rising tide
I got a woman who loves me freely
And that is all I need tonight

Cause I'm rich, I'm rich, I'm so rich inside
Got love burning like a fire I cannot hide
Could be broke and bent and buried in the rain
But she smiles at me and I forget the pain
I'm rich, I'm rich, walking on the tide
With her love burning me up inside
So rich inside

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a first apartment
songs.lyrics:
The key stuck in the lock for a minute
Like even the door wasn't sure I should stay
Fourteen boxes and a mattress on the floor
And the landlord's footsteps fading away
The walls were white like an empty notebook
Water stain above the kitchen sink
I stood there in the middle of the quiet
Taking longer than I meant to think

There was nobody to call and say I made it
Nobody's name on the other side of the bed
Just me and all these rooms I had to learn
And every thought I'd left unsaid

But I bought a plant at the corner market
Talked to it those first two weeks like it could hear
Put a record on to fill the corners
Learned the way the radiator knocked each night like fear
Made a list of what I'd hang on every wall
Then never got around to buying frames
Ate cereal for dinner more than once
And wrote down all my passwords and my names

There was nobody to call and say I made it
Nobody's name on the other side of the bed
Just me and all these rooms I had to learn
And every thought I'd left unsaid

Somewhere around the third or fourth week
I stopped waiting for the quiet to end
Found myself just sitting in it easy
Like an old silence becoming a friend
And I'd watch the streetlight come through the window
Fall across the secondhand wooden floor
And something in my chest kept getting quieter
Something I'd been carrying before

There was nobody to call and say I made it
Nobody's name on the other side of the bed
Just me and all these rooms I had to learn
And every thought I'd left unsaid

The plant is still alive, which surprises me
The frames went up eventually
I know now which pipe knocks when the heat kicks in
And what the fourth stair sounds like under me
First apartment, first address that's only mine
First door I deadbolt just for me
Took me months to call it home out loud
But quietly, quietly, quietly

There was nobody to call and say I made it
Nobody's name on the other side of the bed
Just me and all these rooms I finally learned
And every thought I've already said

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / starting over
songs.lyrics:
She's asleep now in the other room
The nightlight shapes a moon across the wall
I'm sitting at this table we moved ourselves
In a city where nobody knows my name at all
Couldn't stay where all the damage lived
Couldn't teach her broken was the way
So I signed the lease and packed the car at midnight
And I drove until the dark turned back to gray

They don't tell you starting over feels like failing
Even when you know leaving was right
They don't tell you how you'll miss what hurt you
Somewhere in the middle of the night
But I look at her and something straightens in me
Some wire in my spine won't let me fall
Cause she's watching how I do this
She's learning from it all

So I'm going to hold my head up in the grocery store
Going to learn these streets until they're mine
Going to cry in the car before I pick her up from school
Then fix my eyes and walk the line
Going to let myself be scared and do it anyway
Going to let myself be lost and still be found
I have burned down to the bottom of myself
And I am building from the ground

I don't have it figured out, Lord knows I don't
Some nights beans and rice is all we've got
Some mornings I call my mother just to hear her voice
And thank whatever grace I haven't forgot
But she laughs at breakfast like the world is hers
She draws me pictures that she tapes beside the door
And I don't recognize the woman in the mirror always
But I'm starting to want to know her more

They don't tell you starting over feels like failing
Even when you know leaving was right
They don't tell you how you'll miss what hurt you
Somewhere in the middle of the night
But I look at her and something straightens in me
Some wire in my spine won't let me fall
Cause she's watching how I do this
She's learning from it all

So I'm going to hold my head up in the grocery store
Going to learn these streets until they're mine
Going to cry in the car before I pick her up from school
Then fix my eyes and walk the line
Going to let myself be scared and do it anyway
Going to let myself be lost and still be found
I have burned down to the bottom of myself
And I am building from the ground

There will be a morning, I believe it
When this city starts to feel like mine
When she runs inside from school and says I love it here
And I say baby me too, we're doing fine
Not the life I planned but look at what we've made here
Out of nothing, out of nerve, out of need
I am more than what I left behind me
I am more than someone else's leaving
I am a woman and a mother and a seed

So I'm going to hold my head up in the grocery store
Going to learn these streets until they're mine
Going to cry in the car before I pick her up from school
Then fix my eyes and walk the line
Going to let myself be scared and do it anyway
Going to let myself be lost and still be found
I have burned down to the bottom of myself
And I am building
Still building
From the ground

songs.subject: reggae / moving to a new city
songs.lyrics:
Packed up my life in a cardboard box
Left behind the old familiar docks
The morning sun was rising on a brand new day
I threw my bags in the car and I drove away

New streets calling out my name
Nothing here is ever gonna be the same
But I can feel the rhythm in my bones
Even in a city I can make my home

Move your feet, let the music play
Every new beginning finds a way
Roots run deep but the branches grow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow

First week walking down these crowded streets
Strangers turning into faces that I meet
Found a little corner shop that feels like grace
Slowly learning all the contours of this place

The landlord said the pipes are kind of old
But the walls keep out the winter and the cold
I hung my grandmother's picture by the door
Brought a piece of home with me to this new shore

Move your feet, let the music play
Every new beginning finds a way
Roots run deep but the branches grow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow

Sometimes in the evening I get lonely
Wishing for the faces and the ones who know me
Pick up the phone and hear a familiar voice
Remind myself I made this move by choice

But there's something in the struggle that is sweet
The way a new horizon and an old soul meet
I'm building something here brick by brick
Learning all the rhythms and the neighborhood tricks

Move your feet, let the music play
Every new beginning finds a way
Roots run deep but the branches grow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow

Gonna make this city love me back
Find the little alleyways and get on track
Every city has a heartbeat, has a song
Gonna find the place where I belong

So if you're standing at the crossroads feeling fear
Know that something beautiful is waiting here
Take that leap of faith into the unknown wide
Let the music carry you on the rising tide

Move your feet, let the music play
Every new beginning finds a way
Roots run deep but the branches grow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow
songs.lyrics_en:
Packed up my life in a cardboard box
Left behind the old familiar docks
The morning sun was rising on a brand new day
I threw my bags in the car and I drove away

New streets calling out my name
Nothing here is ever gonna be the same
But I can feel the rhythm in my bones
Even in a city I can make my home

Move your feet, let the music play
Every new beginning finds a way
Roots run deep but the branches grow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow

First week walking down these crowded streets
Strangers turning into faces that I meet
Found a little corner shop that feels like grace
Slowly learning all the contours of this place

The landlord said the pipes are kind of old
But the walls keep out the winter and the cold
I hung my grandmother's picture by the door
Brought a piece of home with me to this new shore

Move your feet, let the music play
Every new beginning finds a way
Roots run deep but the branches grow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow

Sometimes in the evening I get lonely
Wishing for the faces and the ones who know me
Pick up the phone and hear a familiar voice
Remind myself I made this move by choice

But there's something in the struggle that is sweet
The way a new horizon and an old soul meet
I'm building something here brick by brick
Learning all the rhythms and the neighborhood tricks

Move your feet, let the music play
Every new beginning finds a way
Roots run deep but the branches grow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow

Gonna make this city love me back
Find the little alleyways and get on track
Every city has a heartbeat, has a song
Gonna find the place where I belong

So if you're standing at the crossroads feeling fear
Know that something beautiful is waiting here
Take that leap of faith into the unknown wide
Let the music carry you on the rising tide

Move your feet, let the music play
Every new beginning finds a way
Roots run deep but the branches grow
Plant your seeds wherever the wind may blow

songs.subject: R&B / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I still remember riding bikes on Jefferson Street
The way the summer pavement burned beneath my feet
Mama on the porch calling out our names
The whole neighborhood was playing evening games

Corner store on Fifth where Mr. Davis smiled
Gave me candy every time I was just a child
Church bells ringing out on Sunday morning clear
Some things live inside you even when you're not there

You are the soil that made me grow
The first heartbreak and the first hello
Every scar and every beautiful thing
You are the song my whole life sings
You are the song my whole life sings

Left you for the city lights at twenty-two
Thought I needed something more than what I knew
Chased a dream across a thousand miles of road
Carried you inside me like a precious load

Called my cousin late at night from far away
Talked about the people and the price we pay
For leaving what we love to find ourselves
Like pulling all the good books off the shelves

You are the soil that made me grow
The first heartbreak and the first hello
Every scar and every beautiful thing
You are the song my whole life sings
You are the song my whole life sings

I've been to places full of glass and gold
But nothing fills the hollow in my soul
Like the smell of rain on that red Georgia clay
Or the way my grandmother would start her day

She'd hum a tune while the coffee percolated slow
A sound I didn't know I'd need so bad to know
Now I hum it in my kitchen far from home
A thread connecting me across the miles of foam

You are the soil that made me grow
The first heartbreak and the first hello
Every scar and every beautiful thing
You are the song my whole life sings
You are the song my whole life sings

I'm coming back to walk those streets again
To put flowers on the graves of all my kin
To stand beneath that old oak in the yard
And thank the place that made me strong and scarred

Because you gave me language, gave me pride
Gave me something fierce and warm inside
No matter where my wandering feet may go
You're the undertow, you're the undertow

You are the soil that made me grow
The first heartbreak and the first hello
Every scar and every beautiful thing
You are the song my whole life sings
You are the song my whole life sings
You are the song my whole life sings

songs.subject: disco / moving to a new city
songs.lyrics:
I loaded up the moving truck at half past nine
My little girl was sleeping, everything was fine
The city skyline waiting like a disco ball of light
We were gonna dance our way into a brand new life

Single mama, two suitcases, and a dream to chase
Gonna find us something better, gonna find our place
The DJ on the radio was playing all night long
I drove five hundred miles, baby, on the power of a song

Get up, get up, feel the beat
New city underneath my feet
Gonna shine, gonna shine
Me and mine, gonna shine
New horizon, new sunrise
Watch me dance into my life
Gonna shine, gonna shine
Me and mine, gonna shine

Found an apartment on the fourth floor of a walk-up
Kindergarten down the block, I could walk her up
The neighbors brought us casseroles the very first week
Found a little community of souls I needed to seek

Working double shifts at the restaurant on Main
Dancing through my tiredness like dancing through the rain
Every Friday paycheck was a small bright victory
I was writing a new story for my daughter and for me

Get up, get up, feel the beat
New city underneath my feet
Gonna shine, gonna shine
Me and mine, gonna shine
New horizon, new sunrise
Watch me dance into my life
Gonna shine, gonna shine
Me and mine, gonna shine

I won't pretend the hard nights didn't bring me down
When the babysitter cancelled and the car broke down in town
When I cried into the pillow so she couldn't hear me cry
When I questioned every choice beneath that unfamiliar sky

But then she'd come in singing some song she learned at school
Twirling in her nightgown like a little dancing fool
And all of that exhaustion melted into something bright
I put on the music loud and we danced all night

Get up, get up, feel the beat
New city underneath my feet
Gonna shine, gonna shine
Me and mine, gonna shine
New horizon, new sunrise
Watch me dance into my life
Gonna shine, gonna shine
Me and mine, gonna shine

Now the years have turned the struggle into something sweet
I know every shop owner on our little street
My daughter calls this city the only home she's known
Look at everything we built here, look at how we've grown

If you're a mama standing scared at some new door
Carrying your children and not knowing what's in store
Put your dancing shoes on, honey, feel the music rise
The disco ball is spinning and it's lighting up your eyes

Get up, get up, feel the beat
New city underneath my feet
Gonna shine, gonna shine
Me and mine, gonna shine
New horizon, new sunrise
Watch me dance into my life
Gonna shine, gonna shine
Me and mine, gonna shine
Gonna shine, gonna shine
Me and mine, gonna shine

songs.subject: indie folk / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I kept your letters in a shoebox by my bed
Read them in the quiet after she was fed
The things you said to me I couldn't quite release
I was holding onto anger like it held some peace

She looked so much like you around the eyes
I'd catch myself and have to look away sometimes
Carrying the weight of what I couldn't say
The bitterness was eating me from day to day

Let it go like water from my hands
I'm learning now to finally understand
That holding on only hurts the one who holds
Forgiveness is the story I choose to be told
Forgiveness is the story I choose to be told

She asked me once why you were never there
I chose my words so carefully with love and care
Told her people sometimes lose their way
That it wasn't about her, not one single day

But in my heart I was still a burning field
A wound that years and years had failed to heal
Until the morning came when I just let it fall
Sat down in the kitchen, back against the wall

Let it go like water from my hands
I'm learning now to finally understand
That holding on only hurts the one who holds
Forgiveness is the story I choose to be told
Forgiveness is the story I choose to be told

It wasn't for you, I want to make that plain
It wasn't to say that you caused me no pain
It was for the girl asleep down the hall
Who needs a mother who isn't against the wall

It was for the woman I was meant to be
Not the monument to someone else's leaving me
I lit a candle, let the smoke carry it out
Breathed in something quieter than doubt

Let it go like water from my hands
I'm learning now to finally understand
That holding on only hurts the one who holds
Forgiveness is the story I choose to be told
Forgiveness is the story I choose to be told

Now the shoebox sits in the closet, still and closed
I don't need to read those words, God only knows
I've got a daughter who comes laughing through the door
With mud on both her knees asking for something more

And I am present, finally, I am here
Not haunted by the ghost of a long-gone year
I am the home she runs to, I'm the light
I chose to put down everything to get that right

Let it go like water from my hands
I'm learning now to finally understand
That holding on only hurts the one who holds
Forgiveness is the story I choose to be told

Let it go, let it go
Like winter into spring
Forgiveness isn't weakness
It's the truest, hardest thing
Forgiveness is the story I choose to be told
Forgiveness is the story I choose to be told

songs.subject: indie folk / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
I was watching leaves come down outside my window pane
When the phone lit up your name
And I thought you might be calling just to hear my voice
But you gave me no such choice

Seven sentences, a period at the end
That's how you chose to let it all begin
To fall apart in the palm of my hand
Seven sentences, I'm trying to understand

You said you'd been unhappy for a while
You said you'd lost your smile
You said it wasn't something that I did
You said goodbye and closed the lid

Seven sentences, a period at the end
That's how you chose to let it all begin
To fall apart in the palm of my hand
Seven sentences, I'm trying to understand

I sat there in the quiet of my room
The afternoon turned to blue
Read it again like the words might rearrange
Into something less strange

There's a photograph still pinned above my desk
Your hand across my chest
And I don't know if I should take it down
Or leave it there, leave it there

Seven sentences, a period at the end
That's how you chose to let it all begin
To fall apart in the palm of my hand
Seven sentences, I'm trying to understand

I know that hard things don't come easy to say
Maybe you needed it that way
Maybe your hands were shaking when you typed
Maybe you cried when you hit send goodnight

I keep telling myself that story when I can
Trying to be soft, trying to understand
That people do the best they know to do
I'm just learning how to miss you

Seven sentences, a period at the end
Watched everything we were begin
To fall apart in the palm of my hand
Seven sentences
Seven sentences
I'm still trying to understand

@@@NEXT-SONG@@@

1. A blues song about being broke but happy.
2. A rock song about dancing alone in the kitchen. like a diary entry
3. A indie folk song about a breakup text.
4. A emo song about missing someone who moved away. from a bartender's point of view

songs.subject: emo / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
Tuesday night and the bar is almost dead
Just a few sad regulars and the thoughts inside my head
I'm wiping down the counter in the same old way
Since the week you packed your boxes and you drove away

You used to sit right there on the end stool by the door
Now someone else sits there but it's never what it was before
I pour their drink and smile and ask them how they've been
But I'm looking past their shoulder at the ghost of where you've been

And I keep pouring out these drinks for other broken hearts
Listening to their stories while mine tears apart
Every night I close this place and walk out to my car
And the city feels so heavy without you and your laugh

She moved away in October with a U-Haul and a plan
Said the city never loved her back the way she thought it can
Left a tab she never closed, I cleared it out myself
And her name's still in my phone between the others on the shelf

And I keep pouring out these drinks for other broken hearts
Listening to their stories while mine tears apart
Every night I close this place and walk out to my car
And the city feels so heavy without you and your laugh

Friday nights are hardest cause you always came in late
After your shift across the street you'd slide up to my gate
Order something cheap and talk until I turned the lights down low
And I'd walk you to the corner and we'd both pretend I wasn't slow

To say the things I should have said before you made that move
Before you needed something that this city couldn't prove
I had two years of Tuesday nights and never said a word
Now I talk to strangers every night and that's the most absurd

And I keep pouring out these drinks for other broken hearts
Listening to their stories while mine tears apart
Every night I close this place and walk out to my car
And the city feels so heavy without you and your laugh

Got a postcard that you sent me from wherever you are now
Little coffee shop, a new job, said you're figuring it out somehow
Said you missed my well drinks and you missed the neon glow
I wrote back three different times but never let them go

They're in the drawer behind the bar between the tape and rubber bands
Right next to all the courage I could never fit inside my hands
And the bar keeps filling up with people aching just like me
Paying someone else to hold the weight of what they couldn't be

And I keep pouring out these drinks for other broken hearts
Listening to their stories while mine tears apart
Every night I close this place and walk out to my car
And the city feels so heavy
God the city feels so heavy
Without you and your laugh

songs.subject: lo-fi / chasing a dream
songs.lyrics:
Last call was twenty minutes ago
I'm wiping down the bar real slow
The chairs are upside down on every table
And I'm still here, same as always

I got this notebook underneath the register
Full of chord progressions, half-filled pages
My manager says I pour the best old fashioned
But my hands were made for something else

Maybe tonight's the night I finish that last verse
Maybe I'll stop talking to myself about it
The neon sign is buzzing out front
And I keep chasing something I can't quite touch

Regulars come in and tell me
Son, you got a real gift, a real gift
Then they tip me four dollars on a forty
And they stumble out into the cold

I recorded something on my phone last Tuesday
Played it back and thought it wasn't bad
Then the morning shift came and I forgot it
In the noise of someone else's bad day

Maybe tonight's the night I finish that last verse
Maybe I'll stop talking to myself about it
The neon sign is buzzing out front
And I keep chasing something I can't quite touch

The dishwasher's been broken since October
My lower back is something else entirely
But there's a melody that lives behind my sternum
That I can't drink away and can't ignore

I know a guy who knew a guy who made it
Playing rooms no bigger than this bar
And I think about him when I lock up
Walking to my car beneath these sleepy stars

Maybe tonight's the night I finish that last verse
Maybe I'll stop talking to myself about it
The neon sign is buzzing out front
And I keep chasing something I can't quite touch

I'll keep chasing, I'll keep chasing
Even when my feet are aching
Even when it doesn't make sense anymore
I'll keep chasing, I'll keep chasing
There's a song inside me waiting
And I'll find it when I walk out that door
songs.lyrics_en:
Last call was twenty minutes ago
I'm wiping down the bar real slow
The chairs are upside down on every table
And I'm still here, same as always

I got this notebook underneath the register

And I keep chasing something I can't quite touch

That I can't drink away and can't ignore

And I think about him when I lock up
Walking to my car beneath these sleepy stars

Maybe tonight's the night I finish that last verse
Maybe I'll stop talking to myself about it

And I keep chasing something I can't quite touch

I'll keep chasing, I'll keep chasing
Even when my feet are aching
Even when it doesn't make sense anymore
I'll keep chasing, I'll keep chasing
There's a song inside me waiting
And I'll find it when I walk out that door

songs.lyrics:

songs.subject: hip-hop / an old photograph
songs.lyrics:
Found it in a shoebox under Grandma's bed
Nineteen seventy-something, everybody's dead
Or moved away to places that forgot the old address
A faded square of paper holding everything I missed

Look at you, so young, you didn't know yet
What the years had waiting around that corner
Standing in the yard in somebody's Easter clothes
Before the family scattered like a dandelion blown

Who are you in this photograph
Smiling like the world ain't gonna tax you
Standing tall before the decade cracked you
I wish I could step in and warn you
But you look so free, so free
Before you became me
Before you became me

Flip it over, someone wrote in cursive
Summertime, nineteen and seventy-nine
And I'm sitting on the floor just doing the math here
Thirty years before I even crossed your mind

My grandfather with hair, that stops me cold
My mother looking sixteen, barely grown
And somewhere in that backyard is a version
Of this family before the stories went untold

Who are you in this photograph
Smiling like the world ain't gonna tax you
Standing tall before the decade cracked you
I wish I could step in and warn you
But you look so free, so free
Before you became me
Before you became me

I got your nose, I got your hands, I got your eyes
I got your stubbornness and probably your blind spots
I got the stories that survived through somebody's mouth
And this photograph that's all I got, that's all I got

There's a whole world living in a three by five
People I will never get to know
Cousins that became strangers, uncles in the ground
And Grandma keeping all of it in a box below

I sat there on the hardwood for an hour
Just holding all that history in my palm
Wondering if they knew they'd be remembered
Or if they thought they'd just fade out and be gone

Who are you in this photograph
Smiling like the world ain't gonna tax you
Standing tall before the decade cracked you
I wish I could step in and warn you
But you look so free, so free
Before you became me
Before you became me

Rest easy, rest easy
Every face inside that shoebox
You were here, you were here
I see you, I see you
Even faded at the edges
You were real, you were real
And I carry you with me
I carry you with me
I carry you with me still

songs.subject: americana / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Dust on the welcome sign, faded paint on the mill
Water tower's leaning like it always has and always will
Mama used to drive me down the old two-lane road
Past the church where grandaddy carried every heavy load

The barbershop is empty now, the chairs are cold and still
Johnny Ray sold everything and moved to Jacksonville
But I still smell the sawdust and the river in the air
Some things leave a fingerprint that just won't go from there

I was seventeen and swore I'd never look back
Left my name in wet cement behind the railroad tracks
Now I'm forty years and weathered, standing in the yard
Finding out that leaving home is not near as hard
As trying to come back to something that's moved on
Like a photograph that's yellowed with the people nearly gone
But the hills are still the same hills, and the creek still finds its way
And something in my blood remembers how to stay

The diner on the corner kept the same cracked vinyl seats
Same pie behind the glass case, same tired summer heat
Loretta still takes orders like she's done for thirty years
And she looked at me like no time passed and almost came to tears

We sat and talked till closing while the coffee turned to stone
About the ones who made it out and the ones who stayed at home
Neither one of us was certain who had done the wiser thing
She refilled my cup in silence and we listened to the spring

I was seventeen and swore I'd never look back
Left my name in wet cement behind the railroad tracks
Now I'm forty years and weathered, standing in the yard
Finding out that leaving home is not near as hard
As trying to come back to something that's moved on
Like a photograph that's yellowed with the people nearly gone
But the hills are still the same hills, and the creek still finds its way
And something in my blood remembers how to stay

Drive past daddy's old house, new family on the porch
Kids I'll never know the names of carrying his torch
The oak tree in the front yard grew another twenty rings
Holding all its quiet secrets, holding everything

I pulled over in the gravel, let the engine breathe
Watched the light go amber through the old oak's leaves
Maybe home is not a place that waits for you
Maybe home is just the wound that never quite broke through

songs.subject: emo / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
Last call was twenty minutes ago but I'm still here
Wiping down the mahogany, rinsing out the fear
She called at half past seven, said it didn't look right
And I poured a hundred drinks between that call and tonight

The regulars could feel it, they stayed a little long
Mick left me an extra twenty, Carol hummed a song
They didn't say a word about the phone call that I'd had
Bartenders don't get to fall apart so I just held it back

And I'm pouring from the bottom of a bottle I can't name
Talking to the empty room like you're still here just the same
The glasses all need washing and the stools need putting up
But I keep one glass out for you and I can't put it up
Dad, I should have called more, I should have driven down
Instead I'm standing in this bar in this nothing little town
Mixing drinks for strangers while the monitors went flat
I keep one glass out for you and I can't put it back

He used to come on Thursdays, always ordered what I'd pour
Said he trusted my opinion, never asked for anything more
He'd sit until the crowd thinned out and talk to me about
The farm and how the summers used to be without a doubt

The best years of his living, before the knees went bad
Before my mother left him to the loneliest he had
I'd listen while I worked the tap, not looking in his eyes
Bartenders learn to hear the grief beneath the otherwise

And I'm pouring from the bottom of a bottle I can't name
Talking to the empty room like you're still here just the same
The glasses all need washing and the stools need putting up
But I keep one glass out for you and I can't put it up
Dad, I should have called more, I should have driven down
Instead I'm standing in this bar in this nothing little town
Mixing drinks for strangers while the monitors went flat
I keep one glass out for you and I can't put it back

I learned to hold the weight of everyone who sat across
I learned the practiced smile you wear to minimize their loss
But now that it's my loss and now that it's my hands
Shaking on the bar top I don't think I understand

How do you console yourself when you're the one who pours
When you're the one who's always listened through the others' wars
The hospital left a voicemail that I played out in the lot
And I stood there in the cold air falling apart on the spot

And I'm pouring from the bottom of a bottle I can't name
Talking to the empty room like you're still here just the same
The glasses all need washing and the stools need putting up
But I keep one glass out for you and I can't put it up
Dad, I should have called more, I should have driven down
Instead I'm standing in this bar in this nothing little town
Mixing drinks for strangers while the monitors went flat
I keep one glass out for you and I can't put it back

The neon sign is buzzing and the parking lot is clear
I'll lock the door behind me, I'll be back again next year
Next week I mean, next Tuesday, I'll be back behind this wood
Living like the grief is something I learned to make good

But tonight I'll leave one glass out by the rail
Tonight I'll let the closing duties fail
Tonight you get the barstool and I'll pour
One last round for my father, nothing more

songs.subject: country / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
There's a grain elevator standing like a saint
And a water tower covered in old peeling paint
That says the home of champions, class of eighty-nine
And I grew up in the shadow of that faded sign

There's a feed store on the corner, open since before
My daddy's daddy tied his horse outside that door
Friday nights in October when the whole town bled
School colors on the bleachers, the whole county said

This is where I'm from, this red dirt and this clay
These crooked little back roads that all wind the same way
Back to somebody's kitchen, back to somebody's field
Back to all the things that time and leaving can't quite heal
Where everybody knows the name your grandmother had
And they'll tell you that you've got the eyes of your dad
Where the summers are brutal and the winters are thin
And I've been gone so long but I still call this home when I'm in

The Methodist and Baptist share a parking lot
And somehow everybody seems to know which one you got
Married in or christened in or buried from in spring
Sunday morning steeples make the same bells ring

I worked the counter at the Dixie Cup Café
Pouring coffee for the farmers starting every day
Listening to the weather and the price of corn
Learning what it meant to be from somewhere you were born

This is where I'm from, this red dirt and this clay
These crooked little back roads that all wind the same way

And they'll tell you that you've got the eyes of your dad
Where the summers are brutal and the winters are thin
And I've been gone so long but I still call this home when I'm in

I know every pothole on the road to Miller's Creek
And I know which house puts lights up every Christmas week
And which neighbor keeps a garden and which one keeps a grudge
And which families have been friendly since before the flood

Some people say there's nothing in a town this size
But I watched a thousand sunsets light a thousand skies
Over nothing, over everything, over all I've known
There's a kind of grace in knowing every stone

When I'm in

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / driving with the windows down
songs.lyrics:
Somewhere between the city and wherever this road ends
I rolled all four of them down, let the whole world in
The radio found something old, something with a steel guitar
And the wind hit my face like it wanted to remind me who we are

Miles of yellow centerline and nothing on my mind
Nothing on my mind for the first time in a long time
The fields are going amber and the sky is going wide
And I feel something loosen up somewhere deep inside

This is the only church I know, this highway and this speed
This wind that takes the names of everything I don't need
Out here I'm not behind or lost or running out of years
Just moving through the summer light with nothing in my ears
But everything, but all of it, the real unfiltered world
Windows down and hair undone and every flag unfurled
This is where the living lives, between the town and town
Sixty miles an hour with every window down

I pulled off at a gas station somewhere past the state line
Got a cold thing from the cooler, sat out on the hood a while
A couple kids were playing in the gravel cross the road
And I thought about how light I felt without my usual load

The attendant asked me where I'm headed, I said I don't know
She smiled like she'd heard that once about a hundred years ago
Said the best rides are the ones without a place to be
And I believed her like I hadn't believed anything

This is the only church I know, this highway and this speed
This wind that takes the names of everything I don't need
Out here I'm not behind or lost or running out of years
Just moving through the summer light with nothing in my ears
But everything, but all of it, the real unfiltered world
Windows down and hair undone and every flag unfurled
This is where the living lives, between the town and town
Sixty miles an hour with every window down

There's a particular kind of quiet that only motion makes
When the noise inside you finally gives the noise outside a break
And the trees come fast and faster and then suddenly they're gone
And you're out into the open and you just keep going on

I used to think that stillness was the only way to rest
But speed with windows open puts that theory to the test
Because something about moving through the thick and living air
Reminds you that your body and your soul are both still there

The sun is going sideways now and painting everything
That gold that only happens in the early harvesting
I turn the radio up loud, I turn my worries loose
There's nothing like an open road to cut you free and let you move

This is the only church I know, this highway and this speed
This wind that takes the names of everything I don't need
Out here I'm not behind or lost or running out of years
Just moving through the summer light with nothing in my ears
But everything, but all of it, the real unfiltered world
Windows down and hair undone and every flag unfurled
This is where the living lives, between the town and town
Sixty miles an hour with every window down

Sixty miles an hour with every window down
Every window down

songs.subject: synthpop / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
Neon lights are bleeding through the smoke
I wasn't looking for a reason, wasn't looking for hope
Just another Friday spinning out of control
Then I saw you standing there and felt something in my soul

The bass line dropped and everything went slow
Your eyes caught mine like electricity and I couldn't let go
I reached across the static in the air
And suddenly the whole room disappeared

Falling into you like a signal in the night
Every frequency is turning to a brilliant white
I never thought a stranger could rewrite my code
But here we are and baby I'm about to overload
Falling into you, the satellites align
Every pulse and every heartbeat falling into mine
I never thought a stranger could rewrite my code
But here we are and baby I'm about to overload

We talked until the speakers went to sleep
Trading all our secrets like they weren't ours to keep
You laughed and I could feel it in my chest
Some kind of digital and analog request

The crowd dissolved to background noise and haze
I memorized the way you moved through the ultraviolet glaze
Your number glowing on my phone screen bright
A constellation written just for tonight

Falling into you like a signal in the night
Every frequency is turning to a brilliant white
I never thought a stranger could rewrite my code
But here we are and baby I'm about to overload
Falling into you, the satellites align
Every pulse and every heartbeat falling into mine
I never thought a stranger could rewrite my code
But here we are and baby I'm about to overload

Outside the city hums its endless song
I never knew a single night could feel this long
The streetlights paint you gold against the dawn
And I'm terrified of blinking and you being gone

Falling into you like a signal in the night
Every frequency is turning to a brilliant white
I never thought a stranger could rewrite my code
But here we are and baby I'm about to overload
Falling into you, the satellites align
Every pulse and every heartbeat falling into mine
I never thought a stranger could rewrite my code
But here we are and baby I'm about to overload

Stay until the morning cuts the dark
Let this be the beginning, let this be the start
I was just a signal searching for a home
And then I found you and I'm never going back alone

songs.subject: R&B / starting over
songs.lyrics:
Cleaned out my locker on a Tuesday afternoon
Threw away the pictures, threw away the proof
Sixteen and I'm standing in the wreckage of a year
Everybody's watching but I act like I don't care

I used to think that growing up meant having it all figured out
But I'm learning that the falling down is what it's really all about
My mama said the hardest roads lead somewhere worth the climb
So I'm lacing up my sneakers one more time

I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
Gonna write my name in ink that hasn't dried
I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
Gonna be the girl I never thought I'd find
Every broken thing is just a lesson in disguise
I'm starting over, I'm starting over
Watch me rise

My friends don't really know me like they think they do
I've been wearing all these faces just to make it through
But I'm done pretending that the mirror lies
There's someone worth believing in behind these eyes

I deleted every message, every memory, every thread
All the things I kept on replay living in my head
My journal's full of futures that I haven't chased yet
And I'm seventeen and honey I ain't finished yet

I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
Gonna write my name in ink that hasn't dried
I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
Gonna be the girl I never thought I'd find
Every broken thing is just a lesson in disguise
I'm starting over, I'm starting over
Watch me rise

People always tell me I'm too young to feel this deep
Too young to have a heartbreak, too young to lose my sleep
But pain don't check your age before it knocks down at your door
And healing doesn't either so I'm walking through it more

I sat alone at lunch the day my whole world caved
Thought about the version of myself I hadn't saved
But she was always in there waiting patient, waiting strong
Just needed me to stop and tell her she belonged

I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
Gonna write my name in ink that hasn't dried
I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
Gonna be the girl I never thought I'd find
Every broken thing is just a lesson in disguise
I'm starting over, I'm starting over
Watch me rise

So here I am with new eyes and an open hand
Eighteen coming faster than I understand
But the girl I was last autumn taught me how to fly
And the girl I'm becoming isn't scared to try

I'm starting over, yeah I'm starting over
And this time I'm doing it my way
I'm starting over, finally starting over
And there's nothing left standing in my way
