songs.subject: rock / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Three little dots appeared and then they disappeared
Just like you always did when things were getting too sincere
I was standing in the kitchen, coffee going cold
Never thought the end of us would happen on my phone

A notification and my whole world cracked in two
Seven letters, two words, nothing left of me and you
You couldn't even dial the numbers, couldn't hear my voice
Like I was just a contact, like I was just a choice

So this is how it ends
A backlit screen and a blinking cursor
This is how it ends
Not even worth the words to say it worse
I gave you everything, I gave you all my broken open heart
And you gave me a text message tearing us apart
So this is how it ends

I read it fourteen times before it started making sense
Tried to find the subtext, tried to find the tense
Was this written quick or did you draft it, did you wait
Did you hold your finger over send before it was too late

I deserved a conversation, I deserved your eyes
Not a paragraph of almost truth and half recycled lies
You could've called me, hell you could've knocked upon my door
But you couldn't even give me that, no you couldn't give me more

So this is how it ends
A backlit screen and a blinking cursor
This is how it ends
Not even worth the words to say it worse
I gave you everything, I gave you all my broken open heart
And you gave me a text message tearing us apart
So this is how it ends

I still love the way you laughed at nothing in the car
I still love the constellations that you named from the yard
I still love the version of you that used to hold my hand
And I'm trying to understand how you became this man

Because the person that I fell for would have looked me in the face
Would have let me have my moment, would have given me my space
To cry and ask my questions and to finally say goodbye
Not a screen glow in a dark room, not a cold and hollow night

So this is how it ends
A backlit screen and a blinking cursor
This is how it ends
Not even worth the words to say it worse
I gave you everything, I gave you all my broken open heart
And you gave me a text message tearing us apart
So this is how it ends

I'm gonna put my phone down on the counter
And I'm gonna let myself fall apart
Because you owed me more than digital surrender
You owed me at least the sound of your breaking heart

So this is how it ends
I never thought it'd be like this
So this is how it ends
One final punctuated dismissal
I gave you every single piece of everything I had inside
And you couldn't even call me, couldn't face me, couldn't try
So this is how it ends
God this is how it ends

songs.subject: country / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:
The house is dark and everyone is sleeping sound
Got the radio turned low so it don't wake them now
Tile's cold against my feet but I don't care tonight
'Cause I got you on my mind and everything feels right

I pull you close in empty air and sway beside the stove
The kind of dancing that nobody ever needs to know
Just me and all this longing in the four a.m. glow
Dancing alone in the kitchen missing you like whoa

Dancing alone in the kitchen, turning slow
Your name on my lips and the radio low
The moonlight coming through the window, falling on the floor
And I'm dancing alone in the kitchen wanting more
Wanting more

I remember how you held me at your cousin's wedding night
You whispered something stupid and it made me laugh despite
The fancy shoes that killed my feet, the crowd, the noise, the heat
You always had this way of making any moment sweet

So I sway beside the refrigerator, one hand in the air
Pretending that your fingers are tangled up in my hair
I know it sounds like crazy but this lonesome heart don't care
Dancing alone in the kitchen 'cause you're not here

Dancing alone in the kitchen, turning slow
Your name on my lips and the radio low
The moonlight coming through the window, falling on the floor
And I'm dancing alone in the kitchen wanting more
Wanting more

Three more weeks until you're home and Lord it feels like years
I've talked to all your pictures and I've cried my share of tears
But tonight feels like a love song, tonight feels like a prayer
Just me and this old kitchen and your ghost dancing there

I know the neighbors think I'm odd, I know the dog thinks so too
He's watching from his corner like I've lost a screw or two
But love makes fools of all of us and I wear that crown proud
Loving you so loud and quiet, soft and hard, and wow

Dancing alone in the kitchen, turning slow
Your name on my lips and the radio low
The moonlight coming through the window, falling on the floor
And I'm dancing alone in the kitchen wanting more
Wanting more

When you get home I'll tell you every silly midnight spin
About the songs I danced to and the state that I was in
And you'll laugh that laugh of yours and pull me off the ground
And we'll dance right here together, Lord the best thing I have found

But till then I'll keep on turning in this dim and amber light
With my arms around the feeling of you holding me tight
Three more weeks is thirty days is not so long from now
And I'll be dancing in this kitchen with my love somehow

Dancing alone in the kitchen, turning slow
Your name on my lips and the radio low
The moonlight coming through the window, falling on the floor
And I'm dancing alone in the kitchen wanting more

Dancing alone, dancing alone
But never really alone with you in my bones
Dancing alone in the kitchen coming home to you
There's nothing this old heart won't do

songs.subject: americana / a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Wore my daddy's old black tie, the one he bought in '89
Stood beside the pine box thinking Lord I'm running out of time
Mama held a crumpled tissue, wouldn't look me in the eye
Just the wind across the cornfield telling everybody's lies

I wrote it down this morning, tried to get the feeling right
How the sun came through the curtains on the coldest kind of light
How the preacher said your name wrong, how nobody seemed to care
How I counted all the ceiling tiles to keep from going there

We sang the songs you hated, sang them low and out of tune
Aunt Carol brought a casserole, we ate it by noon
I kept your pocket watch wound up, it's ticking in my hand
Thought if I could keep the seconds going you might understand

I drove home through the back roads, stopped the car beside the creek
You used to fish that water every single early week
I left a stone on top the bank the way you showed me how
Can't tell if I was leaving it for you or for me now

The paper says arrangements by the family, that's just us
Four kids and a tired woman and a whole lot of mistrust
But somehow we kept standing, kept the candles burning clean
Wrote your name in the guest book like you were just passing through a dream

I'll read this back in October when the leaves go brown and fall
Maybe I'll have better words by then or no words left at all
For now I've got this Tuesday and this dirt beneath my feet
And one less person in the world who knew me when I was complete
songs.lyrics_en:
Wore my daddy's old black tie, the one he bought in '89
Stood beside the pine box thinking Lord I'm running out of time
Mama held a crumpled tissue, wouldn't look me in the eye
Just the wind across the cornfield telling everybody's lies

I wrote it down this morning, tried to get the feeling right
How the sun came through the curtains on the coldest kind of light
How the preacher said your name wrong, how nobody seemed to care
How I counted all the ceiling tiles to keep from going there

We sang the songs you hated, sang them low and out of tune
Aunt Carol brought a casserole, we ate it by noon
I kept your pocket watch wound up, it's ticking in my hand
Thought if I could keep the seconds going you might understand

I drove home through the back roads, stopped the car beside the creek
You used to fish that water every single early week
I left a stone on top the bank the way you showed me how
Can't tell if I was leaving it for you or for me now

The paper says arrangements by the family, that's just us
Four kids and a tired woman and a whole lot of mistrust
But somehow we kept standing, kept the candles burning clean
Wrote your name in the guest book like you were just passing through a dream

I'll read this back in October when the leaves go brown and fall
Maybe I'll have better words by then or no words left at all
For now I've got this Tuesday and this dirt beneath my feet
And one less person in the world who knew me when I was complete

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
The clock said twelve-oh-seven when my phone lit up the room
Your name across the screen like something pulling out of tune
I almost let it ring, I almost rolled back into sleep
But something in my stomach said this call was yours to keep

Your voice came through all gravel like you hadn't slept in days
Said you're sitting in your car outside a house inside a haze
Said you don't know why you called me, said you almost didn't try
And the silence after that was long enough to hold a life

So I just stayed on the line with you
Midnight and the whole world quiet through
I didn't have the words to make it right
But I had time, I had tonight
And the streetlights bleeding orange through your glass
And every year that brought us to the past
Was sitting in that call somehow
I just stayed on the line with you
I just stayed

You told me about the evening, told me what was said
Told me how the walls felt closer when you went to bed
Told me you'd been carrying something heavy, hadn't named it yet
That it lived behind your shoulder like a thing you can't forget

I told you I'd been there once, that long December year
When everything went sideways and the ground would disappear
Said I didn't fix it clean, said it didn't leave a bow
But I woke up the next morning and I thought I'd let you know

So I just stayed on the line with you
Midnight and the whole world quiet through
I didn't have the words to make it right
But I had time, I had tonight
And the streetlights bleeding orange through your glass
And every year that brought us to the past
Was sitting in that call somehow
I just stayed on the line with you
I just stayed

Sometimes the kindest thing is just to be a voice
When everything is burning down there ain't always a choice
Between the fix and the witness, sometimes witness is the gift
Sometimes staying on the phone at midnight is enough to lift

The hour turned to one and then to something after two
Your breathing got a little slower and the shaking wasn't through
But something had been said out loud that couldn't be unsaid
And you told me you were going in and going up to bed

So I just stayed on the line with you
Midnight and the whole world quiet through
I didn't have the words to make it right
But I had time, I had tonight
And the streetlights bleeding orange through your glass
And every year that brought us to the past
Was sitting in that call somehow
I just stayed on the line with you
I just stayed on the line
I just stayed

songs.subject: hip-hop / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Yeah, I grew up where the corner store knew your government name
Where the summer hit like verdict and the winters did the same
Where the bus ran forty minutes late but everybody rode
And somehow we still laughed about it, that was just the code

Where Miss Patterson would holler out her second-story screen
And every block had somebody's uncle living in between
The liquor store and the laundromat and the church with the drum
I swear that city made me, made me everything I've become

I'm in love with every crack in every sidewalk
Every late night on the stoop and the real talk
Every face I grew up knowing like a second skin
I carry every street in me wherever I been
I've been across the country seen a thousand different towns
But nothing ever hit the same as those familiar sounds
Of home calling me back, pulling like a tide
That city running through me, that's the love I can't hide
That's the love I can't hide

We lost some people young, lost them to the statistics and the streets
Lost them to the choices that were never really choices they could beat
And I won't dress it up or make it shine for nobody's comfort now
But I'll say we held each other and we still don't know exactly how

The parks where we played ball until the floodlights all went dark
The teacher who told me that I had a little something, that small spark
The girl I swear I loved at sixteen underneath the overpass
How everything felt permanent and everything moves fast

I'm in love with every crack in every sidewalk
Every late night on the stoop and the real talk
Every face I grew up knowing like a second skin
I carry every street in me wherever I been
I've been across the country seen a thousand different towns
But nothing ever hit the same as those familiar sounds
Of home calling me back, pulling like a tide
That city running through me, that's the love I can't hide
That's the love I can't hide

People ask me why I rep it when the rep don't always flatter
When the news shows up for the wrong reasons and the rest don't matter
I say because the place that shaped your pain still shaped your hands
And the people standing in it still deserve somebody who understands

I know the city's changing, got the cranes above the skyline
Got the new spots where the old spots used to be and that's fine
But somewhere in the texture of the concrete and the heat
Are the fingerprints of everyone who taught me how to speak

I'm in love with every crack in every sidewalk
Every late night on the stoop and the real talk
Every face I grew up knowing like a second skin
I carry every street in me wherever I been
I've been across the country seen a thousand different towns
But nothing ever hit the same as those familiar sounds
Of home calling me back, pulling like a tide
That city running through me, that's the love I can't hide

Yeah I'm coming home, I'm always coming home
No matter where I wander I ain't doing it alone
Got the whole city walking right beside me in my chest
Gave me everything I needed and I gave it all the rest
That's the love I can't hide

songs.subject: blues / moving to a new city
songs.lyrics:
Packed up my old life in a cardboard box
Said goodbye to everything I owned on that block
Threw my bags in the back of a borrowed car
Drove a thousand miles just to find who we are

New city lights don't know my name
Concrete strangers all look the same
Got a pocket full of hope and an empty room
Trying to outrun myself, trying to outrun this gloom

Walked down the boulevard in the morning rain
Every neon sign just reflecting my pain
Coffee shop on the corner gave me one kind smile
Thought maybe I could make this place worthwhile

New city lights don't know my name
Concrete strangers all look the same
Got a pocket full of hope and an empty room
Trying to outrun myself, trying to outrun this gloom

Well the blues followed me all seven hundred miles
Sat down in my new apartment and stayed a while
But there's something in the wind tonight that smells like change
Maybe this old dog can still learn a brand new range

Gonna put my photographs up on the wall
Gonna learn the names of the neighbors down the hall
Gonna find my favorite bar stool, gonna find my street
Gonna plant these two tired feet

New city lights don't know my name
Concrete strangers all look the same
Got a pocket full of hope and an empty room
Trying to outrun myself, trying to outrun this gloom

But maybe new city lights will learn my name
Maybe one day soon nothing will feel the same
Still got this pocket full of hope, and that's enough
Love is where you make it, and making it is tough

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
Someone put a record on from 1975
And the room was full of people but I never felt so alive
You were standing by the window with a drink you didn't want
Laughing at a joke you'd heard before like a polite savant

And I don't know how to do this
I just know I couldn't leave
Your hair was catching lamplight
And I forgot to breathe

So I crossed the room like crossing an ocean
Every step a wave I couldn't control
You turned around and something like devotion
Landed quiet in the corner of my soul

We talked until the party thinned to nothing
Until the host started putting out the lights
You told me about the book you never finished writing
And I told you all my ordinary nights

And I don't know how to do this
I've been wrong so many times
But your voice was like a doorway
Into something I can't quite define

So I crossed the room like crossing an ocean
Every step a wave I couldn't control
You turned around and something like devotion
Landed quiet in the corner of my soul

Outside on the steps the city hummed below us
You pulled your jacket tighter in the cold
I was trying to find a way to say it without breaking
All the careful careful careful ways I hold

Maybe this is foolish, maybe this is nothing
Maybe I'm a shipwreck reading into signs
But you laughed and leaned your shoulder into my shoulder
And the whole dark city glittered and aligned

So I crossed the room like crossing an ocean
Every step a wave I couldn't control
You turned around and something like devotion
Landed quiet in the corner of my soul

I crossed the room, I crossed the room
I crossed the room and found you there
I crossed the room like crossing an ocean
Into the rarest kind of air

songs.subject: synthpop / a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Black cars in a line on a Tuesday afternoon
Everybody wearing what they thought he'd want them to
The organ played a song that nobody really knew
And I kept thinking how the light came through

We are dancing through the static
Through the silence and the years
Neon underneath the heartache
Synthesized and full of tears
We are holding onto something
That the darkness tried to take
We are dancing, we are dancing
For the beautiful mistake

Standing in the rain outside a church in November
Trying to find the words to say to someone's mother
The catering was wrong, he would have hated every thing
But somewhere in the room I heard his laugh in everything

We are dancing through the static
Through the silence and the years
Neon underneath the heartache
Synthesized and full of tears
We are holding onto something
That the darkness tried to take
We are dancing, we are dancing
For the beautiful mistake

Driving home at midnight with the radio on low
Every song a signal from a place I'll never know
Your coat still on my passenger seat, your number in my phone
I can't delete the evidence, I can't go home alone

And the grid lights up in patterns
Like a pulse that won't give in
Every ending is a frequency
That starts somewhere again
We are digital and broken
We are blinking in the dark
We are more than what they bury
We are more than what departs

We are dancing through the static
Through the silence and the years
Neon underneath the heartache
Synthesized and full of tears
We are holding onto something
That the darkness tried to take
We are dancing, we are dancing
We are dancing in your wake

We are dancing, we are dancing
For the beautiful mistake

songs.subject: alt rock / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The dishes are done and the kids are asleep
The apartment breathes in the dark
I pull on my coat and I count to ten
And I carry my tired heart

Down the stairs and into the street
Where the city hums at three
Every bus stop lamp is a little sun
And for a minute I am free

This is my city, this is my hour
When the bars close down and the sirens flower
Streetlight psalms on the avenue
A whole world moving and nobody knew
Nobody knew about me

The diner on Fifth has the good bad coffee
The cook knows how I take it
He never asks about the rings under my eyes
Or why the mornings seem to break me

I sit in the booth with my hands around the cup
And I watch the night shift walk
Nurses and drivers and the man with the dog
Who never seems to talk

This is my city, this is my hour
When the bars close down and the sirens flower
Streetlight psalms on the avenue
A whole world moving and nobody knew
Nobody knew about me

I think about Marcus when he was small enough
To sleep inside the crook of my arm
I think about all of the versions of myself
I left in other towns

I think about calling someone who loves me
But it's three in the morning now
And love is a thing with a bedtime, I guess
And I've forgotten how

This is my city, this is my hour
When the bars close down and the sirens flower
Streetlight psalms on the avenue
A whole world moving and nobody knew
Nobody knew about me

Walking back home as the sky turns gray
Past the bodega opening slow
I buy a coffee and a candy bar
For a boy who doesn't know

How many three a.m. streets I walked for him
How many quiet skies
How many times I found myself again
Between the dark and sunrise

This is my city, this is my hour
This is the wolf and this is the flower
Streetlight psalms on the avenue
A whole world moving but I'm moving too
I'm moving too
I'm moving through

songs.subject: synthpop / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
The clock reads twelve and the blue light glows
My old hands shake but nobody knows
The telephone rings like a voice from the past
I pick it up slow, I pray it will last

Is that you calling through the static and years
Your voice on the wire dissolves all my fears
The midnight is cold but the sound of you warm
Like a fire I built to get through the storm

I still remember your number by heart
Every digit a scar where we fell apart
The midnight phone call is all that remains
Two old souls talking through joy and through pain

I pour a glass and I sit in the dark
Your laugh on the line is a match to a spark
We talk about nothing and talk about all
The years in between and the rise and the fall

I still remember your number by heart
Every digit a scar where we fell apart
The midnight phone call is all that remains
Two old souls talking through joy and through pain

My knees don't work like they did back then
My eyes aren't sharp but I see you again
In every word that you send through the wire
In every silence between us, a fire

The world outside has gone cold and to sleep
But here in the dark there are promises kept
You say you're tired and I say me too
But neither one hangs up, neither one moves

I still remember your number by heart
Every digit a scar where we fell apart
The midnight phone call is all that remains
Two old souls talking through joy and through pain

Don't say goodbye, not yet, not tonight
The clock can keep moving, that's perfectly fine
I've got all the time that a man at my age
Can pour into one phone call, one final page

I still remember your number by heart
Every digit a scar where we fell apart
The midnight phone call is all that remains
Two old souls talking till the darkness fades
songs.lyrics_en:
The clock reads twelve and the blue light glows
My old hands shake but nobody knows
The telephone rings like a voice from the past
I pick it up slow, I pray it will last

Is that you calling through the static and years
Your voice on the wire dissolves all my fears
The midnight is cold but the sound of you warm
Like a fire I built to get through the storm

I still remember your number by heart
Every digit a scar where we fell apart
The midnight phone call is all that remains
Two old souls talking through joy and through pain

I pour a glass and I sit in the dark
Your laugh on the line is a match to a spark
We talk about nothing and talk about all
The years in between and the rise and the fall

I still remember your number by heart
Every digit a scar where we fell apart
The midnight phone call is all that remains
Two old souls talking through joy and through pain

My knees don't work like they did back then
My eyes aren't sharp but I see you again
In every word that you send through the wire
In every silence between us, a fire

The world outside has gone cold and to sleep
But here in the dark there are promises kept
You say you're tired and I say me too
But neither one hangs up, neither one moves

I still remember your number by heart
Every digit a scar where we fell apart
The midnight phone call is all that remains
Two old souls talking through joy and through pain

Don't say goodbye, not yet, not tonight
The clock can keep moving, that's perfectly fine
I've got all the time that a man at my age
Can pour into one phone call, one final page

I still remember your number by heart
Every digit a scar where we fell apart
The midnight phone call is all that remains
Two old souls talking till the darkness fades

songs.subject: trap / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
Back in my day we danced with some class
Now I'm at this party and Lord it goes fast
My grandson he dragged me out here tonight
The music so loud that my hearing aid's tight

But then I seen her standing over by the wall
She got silver in her hair and she standing up tall
My chest got to thumping like back in sixty-three
I said Lord have mercy, what is happening to me

I'm falling, I'm falling, I'm falling for real
At my age I didn't think I still could feel
But she walked through that party like the whole world slowed down
And this old heart started losing its ground

I shuffled on over, my back giving grief
She looked up and smiled Lord I couldn't breathe
I said excuse me darling I know I'm no kid
But I ain't felt like this since my late wife did

My palms getting sweaty, my face getting hot
These young folks around us don't know what we got
Seventy years of living and I'm standing here shook
From one silver-haired woman and one single look

I'm falling, I'm falling, I'm falling for real
At my age I didn't think I still could feel
But she walked through that party like the whole world slowed down
And this old heart started losing its ground

We stepped out the back where the music died low
She said she likes jazz and I said baby let's go
I asked for her number she said write this down slow
I told her these fingers still remember, you know

My grandson came looking, he stopped and he stared
He said granddad you out here, I said son I'm prepared
Been around long enough I know love when it lands
Even at a trap party with a cup in my hands

I'm falling, I'm falling, I'm falling for real
At my age I didn't think I still could feel
But she walked through that party like the whole world slowed down
And this old heart started losing its ground

Call me a fool but a fool who is blessed
I drove her home slow, she leaned on my chest
Said the music was too loud, said the night was too cold
Said she hadn't felt butterflies since she was twenty years old

I'm falling, I'm falling, Lord I'm falling again
Didn't know love had no limit on when
She walked through that party and she changed everything
And this old man's heart learned it still had some spring

songs.subject: pop / heartbreak
songs.lyrics:
Last call and the lights come up slow
I wipe down the bar, watching everyone go
You've been here all night on that same barstool seat
Staring at the bottom like it holds what you need

You told me his name around number drink three
Said you gave him everything, said he let you bleed
I poured and I listened like I always do
Trying to keep my face neutral while your whole world came through

I have seen a hundred hearts shatter right here
Dissolve in the whiskey and drown in the beer
I know all the faces that heartbreak can wear
But yours is the one that I cannot stop staring at

You pushed the glass over and asked for one more
I said take some water, you said what's that for
You laughed but it broke somewhere in the middle
And I thought how love makes a wreck of us all

I have seen a hundred hearts shatter right here
Dissolve in the whiskey and drown in the beer
I know all the faces that heartbreak can wear
But yours is the one that I cannot stop staring at

I'm not supposed to do anything but pour
Keep the counter between us and hold to that law
But something about the way you looked up at me
Made me feel like maybe I was drowning with thee

I said hey it gets better, I know how that sounds
Said I've watched enough broken come back from the ground
You said does it really, I said I don't know
I said I just know that you should probably go home

I have seen a hundred hearts shatter right here
Dissolve in the whiskey and drown in the beer
I know all the faces that heartbreak can wear
But yours is the one that I cannot stop staring at

I locked up at two and I sat in the dark
And I thought about you and the look and the bar
How every night someone loses their way
And I'm the one handing them something to say

Maybe tomorrow you come back in here
Maybe you don't and you're already clear
Either way something shifted in me tonight
Behind the bar under the low neon light

I have seen a hundred hearts shatter right here
Dissolve in the whiskey and drown in the beer
I know all the faces that heartbreak can wear
But yours is the one I'll be thinking about
Long after closing, long after the lights go out

songs.subject: pop / a friend who changed
songs.lyrics:
October third, I don't know what to write
I just keep reading our old texts at night
You used to send me stupid things that made me laugh
Now your profile picture is someone I don't recognize in the photograph

You came back through town and we sat at the place
Where we used to go every Saturday, remember that space
You ordered something different, talked about brands
You had new rings on every single finger of both your hands

I don't know who you are anymore
You walked right through the same door
But something about the way you talked and the way you smiled
Made me feel like I was talking to a stranger and I haven't cried like that in a while

I know people change, I know that's the deal
I know I've changed too but this doesn't feel
Like growing up or growing out or finding your feet
This feels like you left and just sent someone back in your seat

November something, skipped a few days
I keep almost texting you things from our phase
A song came on that we used to scream in my car
I started to send it and then stopped, remembering where we are

You posted a photo of people I don't know
All laughing around you in some city I've never been to though
And I hit the like button and felt myself disappear
Because liking a stranger's photo is somehow worse than the tears

I don't know who you are anymore
You walked right through the same door
But something about the way you talked and the way you smiled
Made me feel like I was talking to a stranger and I haven't cried like that in a while

Was I supposed to change with you, did I miss that memo
Did we just grow in opposite directions, is this just the tempo
Of two lives that started out tangled and then slowly came loose
I just keep asking questions this journal can't use

December, holidays, everyone's home
You didn't reach out and I spent it alone
Well not alone exactly but alone in the way
That you feel alone in a room full of people okay

I wrote you a message I never once sent
I said I miss who we were and I think what I meant
Was I miss who I was when you still knew my name
Before you became someone who just looks the same

I don't know who you are anymore
You walked right through the same door
But something about the way you talked and the way you smiled
Made me feel like I was talking to a stranger and I haven't cried like that in a while

January first, new year, supposed to feel new
I made a resolution to stop writing about you
But here I am two minutes in, pen to the page
Grief looks funny when it's missing someone who's still alive on your screen everyday

I hope wherever you are that you're happy and full
I hope the new version of you gets everything that you pull
Toward yourself and I mean that, I really do
I'm just still learning how to miss the old you

songs.subject: country / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
Mama kept a garden out behind the house
Row of sunflowers tall beside the fence
She'd be out there every morning without a sound
Making beauty out of Georgia red clay and dirt

I still drive past the old place now and then
Park the truck and just sit watching through the rain
The new folks put a swimming pool out back
Poured concrete where her roses used to lay

I'm holding on to everything you left me
Your old Bible and your apron worn to thread
I hear your voice in every thunderstorm that rolls in
And I talk to you like you ain't really dead

Daddy said the hardest thing he ever did
Was learn to make his coffee black each day
Said he always used to let you pour the cream
And somehow that small thing won't go away

My kids never got the chance to know your hands
The ones that held me through my worst of nights
I try to tell them stories after supper
But stories ain't the same as having you alive

I'm holding on to everything you left me
Your old Bible and your apron worn to thread
I hear your voice in every thunderstorm that rolls in
And I talk to you like you ain't really dead

They say that time will soften all this sorrow
They say the grief will someday lose its teeth
But three years gone and every autumn morning
Still drops me straight down to my knees

I planted sunflowers out behind my house
Put the row of them right up against the fence
And I stand out there each morning in the quiet
Making beauty out of Tennessee red clay and dirt

I'm holding on to everything you left me
Your old Bible and your apron worn to thread
I hear your voice in every thunderstorm that rolls in
And I talk to you like you ain't really dead

I talk to you like you ain't really dead

songs.subject: bluegrass / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
I been standing on this sawdust floor since half past four
My boots got holes and my truck needs a new right door
I got eleven dollars and some change inside my coat
But friend, I'm rich as any king, I'll tell you that for sure

I pulled these taps for going on eleven years
Watched the whole town come and cry and laugh and bend their ears
I know every name and every sorrow on this strip
And every story poured out here's become a part of me

I may be broke but Lord I'm free
Got cold creek water and good company
These strings are ringing through the mountain air tonight
I may be broke but Lord I'm free

Old Clarence comes in every Thursday, drinks his one
Tells me bout his late wife Ruth and how she used to run
Their farm up on the ridge before the cedar years went thin
I pour him slow and let him talk till closing time again

Bobby brings his banjo on the last Friday of each month
We push the barstools back and brother let me tell you once
This hollow fills with something money couldn't buy or hold
The rafters shake and everybody's young and everybody's whole

I may be broke but Lord I'm free
Got cold creek water and good company
These strings are ringing through the mountain air tonight
I may be broke but Lord I'm free

My landlord's patient, mostly, and my tab at the feed store
Is something of a local joke they've written on the door
But when I lock up late and walk out underneath those stars
I breathe in deep and brother I ain't poor, not by far

I watched a man come in one night, suit pressed without a crease
He sat alone and ordered doubles, couldn't find his peace
I thought about my worn-out boots and thought about his shoes
And I wouldn't trade this sawdust floor for all his mountain views

I may be broke but Lord I'm free
Got cold creek water and good company
These strings are ringing through the mountain air tonight
I may be broke but Lord I'm free

Yeah these strings are ringing and I'm right where I should be
I may be broke but Lord I'm free

songs.subject: bluegrass / heartbreak
songs.lyrics:
She left on a Sunday while the church bells still were ringing
Took the blue suitcase and left the grey one on the stair
I found her coffee cup still warm beside the window
And a note that didn't say near enough to bear

The mockingbird outside keeps singing like she's somewhere
Like she's hanging laundry out beneath the elm
But there ain't nothing hanging and the yard is cold and empty
And these hands of mine have lost the thing they held

Oh darling how'd you leave so clean
Not a door slam not a single angry word
Just a quiet like the valley after snowfall
The most lonesome quiet I have ever heard

My buddy Ray says time's the only doctor
Brought his mandolin and sat with me till three
We played all the old songs till our fingers got to bleeding
But the music only made me miss her more, not less

I keep the radio off now in the mornings
Can't afford to hear a love song come around
Found her earring in the cushion of the sofa
Set it on the sill and couldn't put it down

Oh darling how'd you leave so clean
Not a door slam not a single angry word
Just a quiet like the valley after snowfall
The most lonesome quiet I have ever heard

I went back to where we danced the night I met her
That old barn outside of town above the creek
The band was playing and the lanterns all were swinging
And every woman's face was hers I couldn't speak

I walked back out and leaned against the sycamore
Let the music drift on out across the dark
And I thought about the way she laughed at nothing
And how that laugh had lived inside my heart

Oh darling how'd you leave so clean
Not a door slam not a single angry word
Just a quiet like the valley after snowfall
The most lonesome quiet I have ever heard

The most lonesome quiet I have ever heard

For each, write only the song lyrics -- no titles, no section labels like Verse or Chorus, no commentary. Separate each song with a line that is exactly:
@@@NEXT-SONG@@@

1. A country song about losing a parent.
2. A bluegrass song about being broke but happy. from a bartender's point of view
3. A bluegrass song about heartbreak.
4. A ballad song about moving to a new city. from a bartender's point of view

songs.subject: alt rock / insomnia
songs.lyrics:
Last call was at two and I killed all the lights
Stacked up the glasses and wiped down the bar
Drove home through the empty arterial nights
Parked in the driveway and sat in the car

The ceiling's familiar, the sheets are the same
The dark has a texture I've memorized now
Four years of this habit, I forget how it came
This body that won't sleep, this restless somehow

My hands still smell like bourbon and lime
My ears are still ringing from somebody's song
I pour myself water and watch the clock climb
And wonder where sleeping went, what I did wrong

I know every hour by how the street sounds
Three is the garbage truck two blocks away
Four is the birds doing their pre-morning rounds
Five is the light going gray

I used to think night shift would wreck me for good
Now I think maybe I was always this way
Built for the hours that nobody else would
Built for the gap between night and the day

I've heard every secret a barstool can hold
Men crying softly into their last beer
Women who married someone they were told
Was good for them, whispering into my ear

I pour it and nod and I do my job right
And I carry it home like a coat full of stones
I lay in the dark of another dead night
And add their insomnia onto my own

I know every hour by how the street sounds
Three is the garbage truck two blocks away
Four is the birds doing their pre-morning rounds
Five is the light going gray

Six and the alarm goes off in the next room
Neighbor heading out to his regular life
I hear the whole building come out of its tomb
The husband, the children, the early-rising wife

And something in me that can't touch all of that
Just watches the ceiling start catching the dawn
I've read every word on this mattress tag
I've counted the cracks till the cracks are all gone

Sometimes I drive back to the bar in my head
Walk through the motions, the ice and the pour
It's easier than lying here acting like bed
Is something my body still knows what it's for

I know every hour by how the street sounds
Three is the garbage truck two blocks away
Four is the birds doing their pre-morning rounds
Five is the light going gray

Maybe tomorrow I'll sleep like a stone
Maybe tomorrow it all clicks in place
But I've said that before to this room and this bone
This wide-open, restless, exhausted face

The bar will be dark for another six hours
I'll go in at four and start cutting the fruit
And somewhere between the lemons and flowers
Of lime peel I'll wonder if sleep is the truth

Or if I was made for the space between things
The gap in the city when nobody's there
The hum of the streetlight and whatever it brings
The dark and the quiet and nobody's prayer

I know every hour by how the street sounds
Three is the garbage truck two blocks away
Four is the birds doing their pre-morning rounds
Five is the light going gray

songs.prompt:
Write 4 completely different full-length songs, one per request below. Each song must be complete, like a real radio song: at least three verses and a repeated chorus. For each, write only the song lyrics -- no titles, no section labels like Verse or Chorus, no commentary. Separate each song with a line that is exactly:
@@@NEXT-SONG@@@

1. A indie folk song about missing someone who moved away. from a teenager's point of view
2. A alt rock song about insomnia. from a bartender's point of view
3. A punk song about driving with the windows down. from someone in love
4. A disco song about a new baby. from an old man's point of view

songs.subject: disco / a new baby
songs.lyrics:
Seventy-three years I been walking this earth
Seen my share of the joy and the sorrow
But nothing prepared me for this kind of worth
This brand new light that arrived here this morning

She's got fingers like questions she's asking the air
She's got lungs that could rattle the rafters
Her mama is sleeping with roses in her hair
And the whole world just started a chapter

Get on up get on down this baby arrived
Put your hands in the air for the miracle
Everything old in this moment's alive
The impossible made perfectly lyrical

I called all the numbers I still have by heart
The ones who've known me since Nixon
I said get yourself ready to fall apart
Because something brand new has been given

She looks like my daughter who looks like her mother
Who looked like her mother before her
This chain of faces going back through the thunder
And somehow she's more than the sum of her

Get on up get on down this baby arrived
Put your hands in the air for the miracle
Everything old in this moment's alive
The impossible made perfectly lyrical

I remember when her mother came home from the hospital
That drive, thirty-two miles, doing thirty-five
Every car on the road felt like an obstacle
I never felt so responsible to be alive

Now I'm the old man sitting in the plastic chair
With a cup of bad coffee and nowhere to be
And the nurse says visiting hours but I don't care
This baby's got something she needs to show me

She opened her eyes for a half of a second
And looked at my face with that ancient confusion
That look that every new person has reckoned
The world and the light and the strange intrusion

Get on up get on down this baby arrived
Put your hands in the air for the miracle
Everything old in this moment's alive
The impossible made perfectly lyrical

I have buried people I thought I could not survive
I have stood at the edge of things and looked down
But here comes this girl, brand new and alive
To pull up the anchor and run me aground

I got a feeling in my chest like a Sunday in June
Like the first warm day after a long hard season
I'm humming to her some half-remembered tune
And she wrinkles her nose and I'm crying for no reason

For every reason
For every single reason there has ever been

Get on up get on down this baby arrived
Put your hands in the air for the miracle
Everything old in this moment's alive
The impossible made perfectly lyrical

They'll take her home in a car seat I already checked
Three times at least and once more after dinner
And the world will keep spinning slightly wrecked
But tonight there's a winner

Tonight there's a winner
Oh honey tonight we're all winners

Get on up get on down
Get on up

songs.subject: blues / quitting your job
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning, coffee cold and black
Boss man called my name and I ain't ever going back
She walked out on Tuesday, took the sunshine too
Now I'm cleaning out my locker and I'm finally through

I quit, I quit, I quit this place today
Can't punch a clock for heartache, gonna throw these keys away
The fluorescent lights been lying and the carpet's turning grey
I quit, I quit, I quit this place today

Foreman says I'm crazy, maybe that's the truth
Left my hard hat on the table and I headed for the roof
She said I worked too many hours, said I missed her needs
Now the only thing I'm punching is the autumn-colored leaves

I quit, I quit, I quit this place today
Can't punch a clock for heartache, gonna throw these keys away
The fluorescent lights been lying and the carpet's turning grey
I quit, I quit, I quit this place today

Got a little savings and a whole lot of blues
Got a broken heart to carry and some worn-out shoes
Maybe losing her and losing this was all the same damn thing
Maybe quitting everything I know might teach me how to sing

Lord I quit, I quit, I quit this place today
Can't punch a clock for heartache, gonna throw these keys away
The fluorescent lights been lying and the carpet's turning grey
I quit, I quit, Lord I quit this place today

Drive down highway nothing, windows open wide
Every mile a memory slowly dying on the vine
Two weeks notice sitting crumpled on the floor
Baby took her laughter and I don't work there no more

songs.subject: reggae / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:
Sun is rising slow and the engine purring sweet
Got my sandals on the dashboard and the road beneath my feet
Packed a bag on Friday when the spirit said to go
Windows down and nowhere urgent, taking it real slow

Miles and miles, the white lines rolling by
Miles and miles beneath a wide open sky
Every town we pass through got a story of its own
Miles and miles and miles away from home

Stopped in a little diner somewhere past the hills
Had some eggs and conversation, paid a couple bills
Woman behind the counter said the sunrise here is fine
Said she never left this county and she said that's doing fine

Miles and miles, the white lines rolling by
Miles and miles beneath a wide open sky
Every town we pass through got a story of its own
Miles and miles and miles away from home

The radio is playing something I don't know
Tune my ears to the rhythm of the way the rivers flow
Mountain pass at sunset turn the whole world burning red
No appointment waiting on me, no alarm inside my head

Feel the road, feel the road, feel the road beneath your wheels
Let the asphalt and the distance help you process how you feel
Ain't no hurry in the heartbeat of the wandering man
Just the highway and the horizon and the warmth upon your hand

Miles and miles, the white lines rolling by
Miles and miles beneath a wide open sky
Every town we pass through got a story of its own
Miles and miles and miles away from home

Pull over at the overlook and watch the valley breathe
Fireflies are waking up among the silver trees
Tomorrow we keep driving just to see where driving leads
Tonight we camp beside the river, that is all we need

Miles and miles, the white lines rolling by
Miles and miles beneath a wide open sky
Every town we pass through got a story of its own
Miles and miles and miles and miles and miles from home

songs.subject: disco / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
October fourteen, the rain won't quit
I'm in my robe at noon, I have to admit
The mascara from last night still rings my eyes
And the weather outside matches something inside

Ooh, rainy Sunday, stay with me
Ooh, rainy Sunday, let me be
Dancing alone in my kitchen light
Everything sparkling and nothing quite right
Ooh, rainy Sunday, you understand me

Made myself some breakfast around half past two
Burned the toast a little, ate it anyway too
Put on a record that I haven't played in years
Poured a glass of something, let myself feel the tears

Ooh, rainy Sunday, stay with me
Ooh, rainy Sunday, let me be
Dancing alone in my kitchen light
Everything sparkling and nothing quite right
Ooh, rainy Sunday, you understand me

The window is steaming from the kettle on the stove
I wrote three pages in a notebook that nobody knows
Told myself I'd call my mother, told myself I'd clean
Got lost inside a movie I had already seen

But I don't mind, no I don't mind
There's something gorgeous in the ordinary grey
There's something honest about wasting a day
Let the whole world rush, I'm staying right here
In my robe and my records and my beautiful fear

Ooh, rainy Sunday, stay with me
Ooh, rainy Sunday, let me be
Dancing alone in my kitchen light
Everything sparkling and nothing quite right
Ooh, rainy Sunday, you understand me

The streetlights are glowing on the wet pavement now
I lit a candle and I don't quite know how
But I feel a little better than I did at noon
Just me and the rain and this small quiet room

Ooh, rainy Sunday, stay with me
Ooh, rainy Sunday, never leave
Dancing alone in my kitchen light
Everything's broken and everything's right
Ooh, rainy Sunday, you understand me

songs.subject: soul / faith
songs.lyrics:
I seen a thousand faces come through that door at night
Some of them are running, some of them are looking for a light
I pour the whiskey steady and I listen while they talk
About the things they've carried and the roads too hard to walk

But I believe, yeah I believe
Seen too much sorrow not to know there's something more
I believe, Lord I believe
Every soul that walks in broken walks back out that door

Old man Thomas comes in every Friday night
Lost his wife in springtime and he's still not feeling right
He orders just the one drink and he talks to her out loud
I don't say a word, I just pour and feel proud

That I believe, yeah I believe
Seen too much sorrow not to know there's something more
I believe, Lord I believe
Every soul that walks in broken walks back out that door

Young girl at the bar stool with the mascara-stained face
Said she didn't know if anything could fill up the space
I slid a glass of water and I sat down for a while
And by the time the night was done she managed half a smile

Because I believe, yeah I believe
Seen too much sorrow not to know there's something more
I believe, Lord I believe
Every soul that walks in broken walks back out that door

I ain't a preacher, I ain't got a robe or a stage
I just got my rag and my bottles and my well-worn age
But this bar is my cathedral, every stool is a pew
And the gospel that I'm living is in what I do

When I believe, yeah I believe
You don't need an altar for the holy to appear
I believe, Lord I believe
Sometimes grace looks like a stranger lending you an ear

So I'll keep on pouring, keep the dim lights low
Keep on bearing witness to the ebb and the flow
This old world is heavy but it's also full of grace
I see it every evening in the lines on every face

And I believe, yeah I believe
Seen too much sorrow not to know there's something more
I believe, Lord I believe
Every soul that walks in broken walks back out that door
Walks back out that door
Walks back out that door

songs.subject: punk / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Cracked sidewalks talking underneath my boots
The neon signs are bleeding through the rain
Been walking these same streets since '74
And every goddamn corner looks the same
The kids are out there screaming at the walls
Like walls are gonna listen, walls are gonna care
I used to scream myself back in the day
Now I just watch and breathe the poisoned air

This city never sleeps it just collapses
The garbage trucks are gods at 3am
The drunks are philosophers with bottles
And nobody remembers where they've been
I'm too old for this noise but here I am
Too old for this noise but here I am

The bar on Fifth been closed for twenty years
That's where I kissed a girl whose name was Lou
The building's now a parking structure, grey
And Lou is someone's grandmother in Duluth
The young ones think they own these broken hours
They think the night invented itself for them
But I was here before their parents kissed
I'll be here when they're ghosts like all my friends

This city never sleeps it just collapses
The garbage trucks are gods at 3am
The drunks are philosophers with bottles
And nobody remembers where they've been
I'm too old for this noise but here I am
Too old for this noise but here I am

My knees are bad, my hearing's mostly gone
The doctor says I ought to stay inside
But something pulls me out into the dark
Some hunger that refuses to subside
Maybe it's the echo of my younger self
Still bouncing off these buildings, off these stones
Maybe it's just stubbornness and spite
Maybe no one wants to die alone

This city never sleeps it just collapses
The garbage trucks are gods at 3am
The drunks are philosophers with bottles
And nobody remembers where they've been
I'm too old for this noise but here I am
Too old for this noise but here I am

So let it be loud
Let it be filthy and cracked
Let the sirens keep wailing
Let the sky stay black
I earned every scar on every street I've known
And 3am's the only time I feel at home
At home
At home
songs.lyrics_en:
Cracked sidewalks talking underneath my boots
The neon signs are bleeding through the rain
Been walking these same streets since '74
And every goddamn corner looks the same
The kids are out there screaming at the walls
Like walls are gonna listen, walls are gonna care
I used to scream myself back in the day
Now I just watch and breathe the poisoned air

This city never sleeps it just collapses
The garbage trucks are gods at 3am
The drunks are philosophers with bottles
And nobody remembers where they've been
I'm too old for this noise but here I am
Too old for this noise but here I am

The bar on Fifth been closed for twenty years
That's where I kissed a girl whose name was Lou
The building's now a parking structure, grey
And Lou is someone's grandmother in Duluth
The young ones think they own these broken hours
They think the night invented itself for them
But I was here before their parents kissed
I'll be here when they're ghosts like all my friends

This city never sleeps it just collapses
The garbage trucks are gods at 3am
The drunks are philosophers with bottles
And nobody remembers where they've been
I'm too old for this noise but here I am
Too old for this noise but here I am

My knees are bad, my hearing's mostly gone
The doctor says I ought to stay inside
But something pulls me out into the dark
Some hunger that refuses to subside
Maybe it's the echo of my younger self
Still bouncing off these buildings, off these stones
Maybe it's just stubbornness and spite
Maybe no one wants to die alone

This city never sleeps it just collapses
The garbage trucks are gods at 3am
The drunks are philosophers with bottles
And nobody remembers where they've been
I'm too old for this noise but here I am
Too old for this noise but here I am

At home
At home

songs.subject: soul / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
I still reach for the phone on Sunday mornings
Before I remember you won't answer anymore
The coffee's on, the light is thin and golden
And your voice is just an echo on the floor
I wore your coat last winter through December
The wool still smelled a little bit like you
And I stood there in the hallway barely breathing
Holding onto everything we never got to do

Mama, there's a hole where you were standing
Daddy, there's a silence in your chair
And I keep on living, keep on moving
But I carry all this grief like it's air
Like it's the very air
The very air I breathe

I sorted through your dresser in the autumn
Found the letters that you wrote and never sent
Found the photograph of you at seventeen
Before the years arrived and all of them were spent
You were laughing at somebody out of frame
Your whole life still ahead of you like light
I held that photograph against my chest and cried
For the person that you were before you were mine

Mama, there's a hole where you were standing
Daddy, there's a silence in your chair
And I keep on living, keep on moving
But I carry all this grief like it's air
Like it's the very air
The very air I breathe

People say it gets easier with time
People say a lot of things that aren't quite true
Time don't heal, it just gives you more distance
And distance is a lonesome thing to move through
I learned to cook your recipes by heart
I burn the edges just the way you did
And at the table set for one I whisper
All the thank yous that I saved up since I was a kid

Thank you for the patience, thank you for the fighting
Thank you for the nights you stayed up by my side
Thank you for the stubborn way you loved me
Thank you for the dignity you showed me how to find

Mama, there's a hole where you were standing
Daddy, there's a silence in your chair
And I keep on living, keep on moving
But I carry all this grief like it's air
Like it's the very air
The very air I breathe

And when my time comes, I hope you'll be there waiting
With that laugh I used to know before all this
With your hands the way I memorized them, warm now
And we'll start again somewhere beyond the mist
Beyond the mist
Beyond the mist

songs.subject: reggae / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Every evening when the sun drops in the harbor
I set up the bottles and I wait for the crowd
The fishermen come in with their salt and their stories
The tourists find their stools and they start getting loud
Twenty years I've been behind this same counter
Watching this town breathe in and breathe out
I know every face that comes through that door at midnight
I know what they're drinking and I know what they're about

This is my hometown, irie and sweet
The ocean at the bottom of every street
The mango trees leaning over the wall
I've seen this place in summer I've seen this place in fall
And every night I pour the rum and I smile
Because I know this place is worth every mile
Worth every mile

Old Mr. Cornelius drinks his stout at seven
Tells me about the hurricane of '88
Says the water came up past the second window
Says he and his wife danced till morning anyway
Miss Gloria brings plantains on a Thursday
Sets them on the bar without a word to say
I put them out for everyone who's hungry
And that's been our agreement now for fifteen years today

This is my hometown, irie and sweet
The ocean at the bottom of every street
The mango trees leaning over the wall
I've seen this place in summer I've seen this place in fall
And every night I pour the rum and I smile
Because I know this place is worth every mile
Worth every mile

The young ones want to leave for the city
They want the concrete and the cold and the rush
I understand it, I almost left myself once
Had a bag packed and a ticket and a crush
But I stood out on the road in the early morning
Heard the roosters and the waves coming in
Smelled the salt air and the smoke from someone's kitchen
And I unpacked every dream I kept within

Because this town held me when I had nothing
When my father left and money wasn't there
The neighbors fed me, the old women blessed me
And a stranger fixed my shoes without a care
So now I'm here behind this bar most evenings
Giving back the only way I know
A cold drink, a warm word, a little patience
And a place for weary people just to go

This is my hometown, irie and sweet
The ocean at the bottom of every street
The mango trees leaning over the wall
I've seen this place in summer I've seen this place in fall
And every night I pour the rum and I smile
Because I know this place is worth every mile

Come gather at the bar, let the night come slow
Let the music find your feet and let the good times flow

songs.subject: gospel / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
I was holding your hand when you crossed over
The morning light was coming through the blinds
And the one I love was standing at the window
Holding me together with their eyes
I don't know how a heart can break and open
At the exact same moment in one breath
But I felt your spirit lift right through the ceiling
And I felt love catch me just beneath your death

God you are so good, even in my sorrow
God you are so good, even in my grief
The one you gave me holds me through the hollow
And somehow that's enough, somehow that's enough
That's enough for me

We drove home through the city in the evening
The streetlights all came on like answered prayers
My love reached over, found my hand in darkness
And we didn't need a single word to share
I thought about the years ahead without you
The holidays and ordinary days
But then I felt that hand squeeze mine a little tighter
And I understood that love finds all its ways

God you are so good, even in my sorrow
God you are so good, even in my grief
The one you gave me holds me through the hollow
And somehow that's enough, somehow that's enough
That's enough for me

You raised me to believe that love was action
Not just words we sing on Sunday in our pew
And I see you in the way my love treats strangers
In the kindness and the patience they move through
Maybe that's how you stay here among the living
In the love that you poured into everyone you touched
And it multiplied and grew beyond you
And now I'm wrapped in it and that is more than much

Lord I know you sit with the one I've lost now
I know you dry the tears I cannot see
But you also gave me someone here beside me
A living testament to your mercy
To your mercy

God you are so good, even in my sorrow
God you are so good, even in my grief
The one you gave me holds me through the hollow
And somehow that's enough, somehow that's enough

When the night is long and the bed is cold beside me
When the grief rolls in like weather off the sea
I reach out in the dark and find them breathing
And I thank you, Lord, I thank you, Lord
For leaving love with me
For leaving love with me
For leaving love
With me

songs.subject: pop / moving to a new city
songs.lyrics:
Packed the boxes, said goodbye to everything I knew
Told my daughter, baby, we're gonna start brand new
She asked me, mama, will we be okay out there
I kissed her forehead, said I'd always be right there

We drove through the night with the radio on
Singing every word to every single song
The skyline rose up like a promise in the dark
I felt it burning like a fire in my heart

We're starting over, yeah we're starting over
In a city where nobody knows our names
We're starting over, yeah we're starting over
And I will love you through the growing pains
Through the growing pains

Found an apartment on the third floor, east side street
Neighbors brought us cookies, said welcome, take a seat
I cried a little when she went to her first day
But when she came home laughing, all my fears just fell away

Learned the subway lines and where to find the good bread
Working double shifts but we are warm and we are fed
I never knew that I could be this kind of strong
Turns out we were headed somewhere we belong

We're starting over, yeah we're starting over
In a city where nobody knows our names
We're starting over, yeah we're starting over
And I will love you through the growing pains
Through the growing pains

Some nights I miss the faces and the familiar roads
But this was never giving up, I was letting go
I left behind the version of myself that played it small
And now I'm standing in the light of it all

Watched her make a friend outside beneath the oak tree
Laughed and thought, my God, is this what free could be
I put the key into the lock and called it home
Two hearts in a new place, but we're not alone

We're starting over, yeah we're starting over
In a city where nobody knows our names
We're starting over, yeah we're starting over
And I will love you through the growing pains

We're gonna make it, baby, we're gonna make it here
We've already come so far to let go of the fear
Starting over, starting over
Through the growing pains

songs.subject: alt rock / a new baby
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning and the hospital white
I've been awake through every minute of the night
And then they put you there against my chest and damn
I don't think I'll ever be the same again

Your mother looked at me like I was made of glass
Like she could see the future and the buried past
And everything I thought I knew about this world
Just cracked wide open when they told me, it's a girl

And I am wrecked, I am ruined in the best way
I am burning up from something I can't say
Never thought that love could break me down like this
I am wrecked, I am wrecked, I am wrecked by this

I used to think that I was hollow at the core
Used to drag myself across the barroom floor
Used to think that I was built without the wiring
For something real, for something worth admiring

But you showed up with your wrinkled, furious face
And screamed your way into an unfillable space
And now I'm walking through a different kind of fire
And for the first time in my life I don't want to be a liar

And I am wrecked, I am ruined in the best way
I am burning up from something I can't say
Never thought that love could break me down like this
I am wrecked, I am wrecked, I am wrecked by this

They say you change when you become a father
But I didn't think it'd hit me like a car
Didn't know that someone smaller than my forearm
Could rearrange the bones beneath my armor

I've been writing songs for fifteen years or more
About the kind of love that leaves you on the floor
I never wrote one that came close to true
Until the night I wrote one just for you

And I am wrecked, I am ruined in the best way
I am burning up from something I can't say
Never thought that love could break me down like this
I am wrecked, I am wrecked

You're sleeping now, the room is finally still
I swear I'll spend my life against my will
Being better than I was the day before
Because you deserve that and so much more

I am ruined, I am ruined, I am ruined
In the best way I have ever known
You wrecked me good, you wrecked me good
And now I'm home

songs.subject: punk / chasing a dream
songs.lyrics:
She left on a Tuesday with a text and a box
Left my record collection and took all the locks
Said I was going nowhere, said I was a mess
Said my dreams were just excuses for the rest

Well fine, okay, then watch me go
I've got nothing left to lose and nowhere left to be slow
You wanted stable, wanted safe, wanted sure
I wanted stages and I wanted more

So I'm running, I'm running toward the edge of something real
I don't care if it destroys me, I just want to feel
I'm done apologizing for the fire in my chest
Done making myself smaller so that you could feel the best
I'm chasing it, I'm chasing it, I'm chasing every burning thing
Watch me go, watch me go, watch me chase this dream

Loaded up the van with broken amps and hope
The guys showed up at seven and we laughed and choked
On bad coffee and the glory of a stupid plan
Driving somewhere we'd only ever seen in bands

She said you'll never make it, said grow up, get real
Said music's not a life, it's just a way to feel
Well yeah, it is, and I will feel it till I'm dead
I'd rather die alive than live like what she said

So I'm running, I'm running toward the edge of something real
I don't care if it destroys me, I just want to feel
I'm done apologizing for the fire in my chest
Done making myself smaller so that you could feel the best
I'm chasing it, I'm chasing it, I'm chasing every burning thing
Watch me go, watch me go, watch me chase this dream

Yeah it hurts, okay, it hurts like breaking glass
I'm not made of stone, I know that didn't have to crash
But she wanted someone finished and I'm barely even started
And you can't make a home with someone already departed

Played a show in Akron to eleven people, man
Sweat through every shirt I owned and fell into the van
One girl up front knew every word I'd ever wrote
And I forgot her name by morning but I can't forget that note

She was singing back what I'd been trying to say
That the mess and the hunger and the cost of it's okay
That the people who don't get it never had to anyway
That you keep going, you keep going, you keep going, yeah

So I'm running, I'm running toward the edge of something real
I don't care if it destroys me, I just want to feel
I'm done apologizing for the fire in my chest
Done making myself smaller so that you could feel the best
I'm chasing it, I'm chasing it, I'm chasing every burning thing
Watch me go, watch me go, watch me chase this dream

Watch me go
I'm gone
Watch me chase this dream

Said I was going nowhere, said I was a mess

Well fine, okay, then watch me go

songs.subject: alt rock / starting over
songs.lyrics:
Fifty years of building walls I tore down in a day
Packed a single suitcase, drove the rust and doubt away
The house still smells like her perfume and twenty years of wrong
But I'm seventy-one years old and finally moving on

My hands are slow, my back is bent, my eyes have started to fade
But somewhere in this tired chest a younger man's afraid
Afraid to start, afraid to fail, afraid of being free
Well fear's a luxury I wasted most my life believing

I'm starting over with these crooked hands
Learning how to be a brand new man
Every scar I carry got me here
Starting over at the end instead of beginning
Lord knows it took me long enough to hear
That it's never too late to disappear into something new
I'm starting over, yes I am, and I'm starting over true

My children call and worry that their father's lost his mind
I tell them I found pieces of myself I left behind
A coffee shop in Denver where I don't know anyone
Just a old man with a notebook and a life just begun

The jukebox plays a song I knew from nineteen seventy-three
And I remember who I was before the world got into me
Before the mortgages and meetings, before the grinding gray
Before I forgot completely that I had something to say

I'm starting over with these crooked hands
Learning how to be a brand new man
Every scar I carry got me here
Starting over at the end instead of beginning
Lord knows it took me long enough to hear
That it's never too late to disappear into something new
I'm starting over, yes I am, and I'm starting over true

Some men go out quietly
Rocking on a porch somewhere
Counting all the years behind
Instead of the ones ahead
But I got nothing left to lose
And everything to find
And a motel room in Colorado clearing out my mind

I'm starting over with these crooked hands
Learning how to be a brand new man
Every scar I carry got me here
Starting over at the end instead of beginning
Lord knows it took me long enough to hear
That it's never too late to disappear into something new
I'm starting over, yes I am, and I'm starting over true

So let the young men have their certainty
And let the old songs play
I'll be the fool who learned to live
On some improbable day
Seventy-one years old and Lord
I finally feel okay

songs.subject: emo / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
Gate seventeen and I'm already breaking
Trying not to let you see my hands are shaking
You've got your backpack and your father's eyes
And baby I rehearsed this but I'm done with all my lies

I told you it was temporary
I told you months go fast
I told myself that I could do this
I told myself it'd pass
But you're seven years old and you don't understand
Why Mommy lets you go and holds a stranger's hand

Don't look back, don't look back at me
I need you to walk through that gate so I can fall apart freely
I've been strong for so long but the seams are coming undone
Don't look back, don't look back, just run
Please just run

Your grandmother will braid your hair the way I never learned
And I'll be in our apartment where every corner burns
With pictures that I taped up just to have your face around
Making dinner for one person in a too-quiet sound

I signed the papers with a steady hand
I said the words I practiced standing straight
I smiled for the mediator's questions
Now I'm just a woman dying at a gate

Don't look back, don't look back at me
I need you to walk through that gate so I can fall apart freely
I've been strong for so long but the seams are coming undone
Don't look back, don't look back, just run
Please just run

And there it is, you turned around
And now your face is what I'll drown in
In every sleepless Tuesday night
Every shower where I'm crying right
Into the tile so you never have to know
How much it costs me letting go

They said that this was best for you
They said that I'd find peace somehow
Six weeks until I see you and
Six weeks is a lifetime now

Don't look back, don't look back at me
I need you to walk through that gate so I can fall apart freely
I've been strong for so long but the seams are coming undone
Don't look back, don't look back at me
My darling you just run
Baby please just run

The terminal is emptying
And I'm still standing here
Watching planes take off into a gray November sky
Holding everything we are in a carry-on of fear
And all I know is loving you is the hardest way to say goodbye

songs.subject: rock / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
Last call is two and I'm still wiping glasses down
Same four walls that heard me tell my regulars she's gone
Third shift Tuesday when my cousin walked inside
And all I saw was the way he held the door like something died

I smiled through forty people asking for another round
I poured the whiskey neat and let the music cover sound
I laughed at Jimmy's joke about the horses at the track
While in the back of everything my mother's not coming back

Pour another one for somebody's pain
Mine can wait, it's always been that way
Everybody's got a story at the bar on Friday night
And I'm the one who listens till the neon signs go quiet
But nobody pours for me, nobody pours for me
I go home when the bottles all go dark
And the breaking always happens after everyone departs
Nobody pours for me when I need it most
I just pour for everybody else and pour myself a ghost

She used to sit right there on the end, the corner stool I saved
She'd nurse a ginger ale and tell me I was throwing life away
Behind a bar, behind a bar, she'd say, you're too damn smart
And I'd say Mama I'm exactly where I want to be, don't start

She'd wave at all the locals and she knew them all by name
She'd say that boy looks lonely and that woman's got some pain
She read the room the way she read her Bible, word by word
And I learned everything I know about people from what she observed

Pour another one for somebody's pain
Mine can wait, it's always been that way
Everybody's got a story at the bar on Friday night
And I'm the one who listens till the neon signs go quiet
But nobody pours for me, nobody pours for me
I go home when the bottles all go dark
And the breaking always happens after everyone departs
Nobody pours for me when I need it most
I just pour for everybody else and pour myself a ghost

Saturday I opened up at noon and worked till four
And swept the whole place twice and polished up the floor
Because if I go home I have to feel it
If I go home I have to deal with it
And this bar has always been the only church I trust

Someone at the counter said you look like you could use a drink
And I laughed and said you have no idea, brother, what you think
He said I lost my father in the spring and nights are hard to hold
I said I know exactly what you mean and poured us both one cold

Pour another one for somebody's pain
Mine can wait, it's always been that way
Everybody's got a story at the bar on Friday night
And I'm the one who listens till the neon signs go quiet
But nobody pours for me, nobody pours for me
I go home when the bottles all go dark
And the breaking always happens after everyone departs
Nobody pours for me when I need it most
I just pour for everybody else and pour myself a ghost

And on the corner stool I put a ginger ale
And told her everything I should have said before the curtain fell
And the neon buzzed and no one saw me talking to the air
But I swear she waved at all the locals
I swear she was still there

songs.subject: trap / a childhood home
songs.lyrics:
Cracked linoleum in the kitchen, mama's slippers on the floor
Radiator hissing in the winter, daddy's coat hung by the door
Used to count the water stains on ceilings, map 'em like a sky
Little me just lying on that carpet watching years go by

Back porch wood was rotting through the middle, still we stood out there
Caught the lightning bugs in mason jars and let 'em in our hair
Neighborhood was never what they called it on the news
We was rich in everything that money never could produce

That house, that house, still got me
Peeling paint and broken locks still got me
Every crack in every wall still got me
Four rooms and a hallway, yeah they got me, got me
That house, that house, still got me
Middle of the night I'm driving back to see it
Don't know why I need it
That house still got me

Basement had a leak we never fixed right, bucket caught the rain
Wrote my name in permanent marker on the wall behind the frame
Wondered if whoever lives there now has painted over it
Or if my name still breathing in that dark, refusing to quit

Summer heat would turn the door to swelling, couldn't hardly close
Every season had a smell I'd recognize if heaven chose
Fall was wood smoke, spring was mud and something blooming wild
Winter was the oil heat and something that smelled like a child

That house, that house, still got me
Peeling paint and broken locks still got me
Every crack in every wall still got me
Four rooms and a hallway, yeah they got me, got me
That house, that house, still got me
Middle of the night I'm driving back to see it
Don't know why I need it
That house still got me

I drive past slow with my window down just breathing in that street
Same old oak tree split that sidewalk, heaving up concrete
Different family, different curtains, different car out front
But the bones of it still mine, still everything I want

Tried to leave it, couldn't leave it
Tried to bury it, can't bury it
Tried to sell it out my memory, nobody buying it
Tried to trade it for the future, future gave it back
Everything I am is just that house and this map

That house, that house, still got me
Peeling paint and broken locks still got me
Every crack in every wall still got me
Four rooms and a hallway, yeah they got me, got me
That house, that house, still got me
Middle of the night I'm driving back to see it
Don't know why I need it
That house still got me
That house still got me
Yeah that house, that house
