songs.subject: pop / getting sober
songs.lyrics:
I've been standing at this bar since I was twenty-two
Learned to read a person's pain the way they order their first drink
Watched a thousand people drown in something I kept pouring through
And I kept on smiling honey, never stopped to think

But I started counting glasses at the end of every night
Started seeing you come in on Mondays looking worse
And I'm the one who filled 'em up and told you you'd be alright
Maybe I was just as lost and maybe I was just as cursed

I put the bottle down on a Tuesday
Didn't tell a single soul till Wednesday came
The shaking hands, the cold sweat, the new day
I learned that sober living ain't the one that's ashamed
No, I put the bottle down, I put the bottle down
And the whole world got quieter and louder all at once somehow
I put the bottle down

Standing on the other side of something that I served for years
Funny how the clarity can feel like a kind of grief
I mopped the floors and memorized the sound of people's fears
Poured the remedy that robbed everybody of relief

Now I watch them differently, I know what I know
I see the way they hold the glass like it's the last good thing
Used to be I'd let it go, let the register and tips just flow
But something in me finally broke and something else took wing

I put the bottle down on a Tuesday
Didn't tell a single soul till Wednesday came
The shaking hands, the cold sweat, the new day
I learned that sober living ain't the one that's ashamed
No, I put the bottle down, I put the bottle down
And the whole world got quieter and louder all at once somehow
I put the bottle down

Ninety days felt like a foreign country I'd never been to
Hundred eighty, started learning how to use my hands
Phone numbers in my pocket of the people who pulled through
Finally started getting something that I couldn't understand

Standing behind this bar is different now I'm clear
I see you and I see me six months ago tonight
I can't save you, but I'll give you water, I'll be here
And maybe watching me walk through it gives you the right

To put the bottle down on a Tuesday
You don't have to tell a single soul till you're ready
The shaking hands, the cold sweat, the new day
I'll be behind this bar and I'll be steady
You put the bottle down, you put the bottle down
And the whole world gets quieter and louder all at once somehow
Put the bottle down
Put the bottle down
You don't have to do it perfect, only do it now
Put the bottle down

songs.subject: bluegrass / falling in love at a party
songs.lyrics:
Well I never much cared for these Saturday nights
Too many lanterns and too many lights
Too many strangers all hollering loud
Me and my tap handles buried in crowd

She come up asking for something cold
Smiled like a story that never gets old
Said she don't usually stop into bars
But her friends dragged her out underneath all them stars

And Lord have mercy I felt something move
Like a fiddle just found its long-lost groove
She laughed at something I said, Lord I don't recall what
I just know every door in me swung open that had been long shut

Pour me another reason to stay right here
The night's still young and you're still near
I been behind this bar for going on nine years
And nothing's ever made me want to close up and disappear
Pour me another reason to stay right here

Well the band kicked up a red-dirt reel
And the whole room bent to that cypress feel
She stayed and talked while the whiskey flowed
And I forgot every name in my tip jar owed

Asked her where she come from, she said down the valley
Her people farmed tobacco near an old river galley
Her daddy plays banjo, her mama sings shape note
And something about that just stuck in my throat

Pour me another reason to stay right here
The night's still young and you're still near
I been behind this bar for going on nine years
And nothing's ever made me want to close up and disappear
Pour me another reason to stay right here

Now closing time crept like it always will do
Last call hollered out, thinned the room to a few
I was wiping the counter pretending I wasn't
Watching to see if she'd leave like the others that wouldn't

She folded her napkin and tucked it away
Said well bartender I'm glad that I came today
I said me too and I meant it more than I could say
She wrote something down and she smiled and she walked away

That paper said Sunday and a number and a name
Magnolia Raines, Lord I'll never be the same
I locked up the bottles and turned out the back light
Stood in the parking lot long in the cool of the night

Pour me another reason to stay right here
But the bar's gone dark and you ain't near
I been behind this bar for going on nine years
But I'm driving down the valley, honey, that much is clear
Lord pour me another reason to stay right here
But Magnolia Raines has got me leaving here tonight

songs.subject: alt rock / driving with the windows down
songs.lyrics:
Highway opens up past the edge of town
Got your hand out in the rushing sound
Every mile marker blurring soft
The kind of afternoon that holds you up aloft

Radio finds something we both know
You sing it wrong but God I love it so
Dashboard warm from three hours of sun
Don't care where we're going, only that we run

Windows down and your hair going everywhere
I am so in love it's almost hard to bear
The whole world smells like cut grass and the coming rain
I would drive this road with you again and again and again
Windows down, you're laughing at something I can't hear
The wind takes it away but I want it known right here
That I have never felt this easy or this found
As I do with you and the windows down

Semis blow us sideways, you grab my arm
Your fingernails are chipped and perfect and they're calm
Exit signs for places neither of us knows
You say let's take that one and see where it goes

So we peel off into somewhere small and green
A water tower and a gas station in between
We stretch our legs and you look up at the sky
I take a picture of you squinting in my mind

Windows down and your hair going everywhere
I am so in love it's almost hard to bear
The whole world smells like cut grass and the coming rain
I would drive this road with you again and again and again
Windows down, you're laughing at something I can't hear
The wind takes it away but I want it known right here
That I have never felt this easy or this found
As I do with you and the windows down

There's something about motion that confesses everything
Can't look at you too long but I feel you like a second skin
The speed strips off whatever we were trying hard to be
And what's left is just you next to me

I've been looking for the word for what this afternoon has been
Not a word exactly, more a frequency within
The hum of engine, sixty miles an hour through the gold
The kind of ordinary day I hope I'm always told

I was happy, I was here, and I was yours
I had everything I needed, nothing more
You turned the music up and closed your eyes
And I just drove us underneath the open skies

Windows down and your hair going everywhere
I am so in love it's almost hard to bear
The whole world smells like cut grass and the coming rain
I would drive this road with you again and again and again
Windows down, you're laughing at something I can't hear
The wind takes it away but I want it known right here
That I have never felt this easy or this found
As I do with you and the windows down
As I do with you and the windows down

songs.subject: punk / a friend who changed
songs.lyrics:
You used to call me up at midnight just to laugh
We'd ride around on nothing, living for the crash
You had a fire in your eyes that no one could contain
Now you're wearing khakis and you forgot my name

What happened to the kid who spray-painted the overpass
Who told the whole world where to shove it, made it all an art
I don't recognize the face behind that collared shirt
Did you trade your ammunition just to stop the hurt

You changed, you changed
And I'm the only one who noticed you were gone
You changed, you changed
You left the rest of us to carry something on
We were burning down the fences just to feel alive
Now you're shaking hands with people that you used to despise
You changed, you changed
And I don't know who I'm looking at anymore

Remember when we swore that we would never sell the dream
That the moment that we settled we would cease to even breathe
You laughed at all the zombies in their nine to five parade
Now you're first in line and brother you forgot what we once said

We broke windows not because we had to but we could
We made noise for all the people who were misunderstood
I still got the scar from when we jumped the county fence
You got a mortgage and a handshake and you called it common sense

You changed, you changed
And I'm the only one who noticed you were gone
You changed, you changed
You left the rest of us to carry something on
We were burning down the fences just to feel alive
Now you're shaking hands with people that you used to despise
You changed, you changed
And I don't know who I'm looking at anymore

Maybe I'm the one who's living wrong
Maybe holding on is just a stupid song
Maybe you were right to walk away from all of this
But I'd rather burn out swinging than die living like this

You changed, you changed
And I'm the only one who noticed you were gone
You changed, you changed
You left the rest of us to carry something on
We were burning down the fences just to feel alive
Now you're shaking hands with people that you used to despise
You changed, you changed
And I don't know who I'm looking at anymore

You changed
And I miss you every single stupid day
You changed
I just needed you to know before you walked away

songs.subject: disco / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
Rain is knocking on the window, Sunday's moving slow
Umbrellas at the door and nowhere anybody's gotta go
I been polishing these glasses since the morning light came in
And the first one walked in soaking just to wash away the week again

She said pour me something golden, make it last a little while
Outside the whole world's crying but in here we got our style
The jukebox in the corner playing something from seventy-three
And honey on a rainy Sunday, this is right where I should be

Oh Sunday rain, Sunday rain
Everybody finding shelter from the storm
Sunday rain, Sunday rain
This little bar is keeping everybody warm
The candles burning low and the conversation flows like wine
On a rainy Sunday, sugar, everybody's doing fine
Sunday rain, come on inside

Old Marcus takes his corner stool at half past two
He orders up his usual and tells me something new
Says the rain reminds him of a woman in Savannah long ago
She danced barefoot in a thunderstorm and lord he loved her so

The young couple in the window booth are laughing over drinks
She's drawing on a cocktail napkin something pink
The whole world got cancelled and they ended up right here
And I can see they found each other somewhere in the atmosphere

Oh Sunday rain, Sunday rain
Everybody finding shelter from the storm
Sunday rain, Sunday rain
This little bar is keeping everybody warm
The candles burning low and the conversation flows like wine
On a rainy Sunday, sugar, everybody's doing fine
Sunday rain, come on inside

I love this job the most on days like this
When the neon's all reflecting in the wet and liquid mist
When the whole wide world slows down and people let their shoulders fall
When strangers share an hour and they don't feel small at all

I'll pour another round before the evening fades away
Nobody really wants to leave and honey that's okay
The rain can keep on falling, let it fall until it's done
We'll keep this party going darling till we see the sun

Oh Sunday rain, Sunday rain
Everybody finding shelter from the storm
Sunday rain, Sunday rain
This little bar is keeping everybody warm
The candles burning low and the conversation flows like wine
On a rainy Sunday, sugar, everybody's doing fine
Sunday rain, come on inside

Sunday rain, come on in from the cold
Sunday rain, got a story to be told
Sunday rain, everybody's got a friend
Sunday rain, let it fall, let it fall again

songs.subject: synthpop / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:
Miles of white lines pulling underneath the wheels
Dashboard lit up orange, I can barely feel
My hands on the wheel, the static on the dial
Been driving since the morning, adding up the miles

Somewhere past the last town on the map I know
Somewhere where the signal finally lets me go
The sky is getting bigger as the houses disappear
And everything I'm running from is fading in the rear

Take me down the long road, let the engine breathe
Take me past the places where I used to believe
The synthesizer's humming and the headlights cut the black
I'm somewhere in the middle and I'm never going back
Long road, long road
I'm alive inside this motion, I can finally feel
Long road, long road
There's only me and darkness and the spinning wheel

Two a.m. at a gas station in the middle of the plains
The fluorescent lights are buzzing and it looks like it might rain
I buy a cup of coffee and I stare at my own face
And wonder what I'm looking for in all this empty space

The man behind the counter says where you headed friend
I tell him I don't really know, just driving to the end
He laughs and says he used to have that feeling once before
He found his destination but he missed the open road

Take me down the long road, let the engine breathe
Take me past the places where I used to believe
The synthesizer's humming and the headlights cut the black
I'm somewhere in the middle and I'm never going back
Long road, long road
I'm alive inside this motion, I can finally feel
Long road, long road
There's only me and darkness and the spinning wheel

The mountains came at sunrise like a curtain being drawn
A purple wall of silence in the pale and breaking dawn
I turned the music louder till it filled up every cell
And cried a little somewhere between nowhere and somewhere else

There's something about motion that the body understands
When everything is broken and it's slipping through your hands
You aim yourself at distance and you let the road decide
And somewhere in the traveling you find what's still inside

Take me down the long road, let the engine breathe
Take me past the places where I used to believe
The synthesizer's humming and the headlights cut the black
I'm somewhere in the middle and I'm never going back
Long road, long road
I'm alive inside this motion, I can finally feel
Long road, long road
There's only me and darkness and the spinning wheel

I don't need a destination
I just need the white lines and the dark
I don't need an explanation
I just need the engine and the spark
Long road, just keep on going
Long road, there's no returning now
Long road, just keep on going
Long road

songs.subject: hip-hop / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning, seven days since you walked out that door
Rain on the window talking to me like it has before
I got your voicemail greeting saved and that's a sorry thing
Couldn't sleep till four a.m., been staring at my ring

Got a blanket on the couch that smells a little like your hair
I know I should've washed it but I can't bring myself to care
The coffee maker's dripping and the television's on
But everything is background noise to you being gone

Rain on a Sunday, whole sky crying with me now
Rain on a Sunday, universe is showing me somehow
That even heaven's going through it, even clouds can't hold it in
Rain on a Sunday morning, let me feel this once again
Yeah, rain on a Sunday, wash me clean or wash me out
Rain on a Sunday, I got nothing but this doubt
Sitting in the wreckage of a thing I thought was real
Rain on a Sunday, tell me when I'm gonna heal

I went back through the messages to find where it went wrong
Read the whole thing backwards like the meaning of a song
Seen the little moments where the ice began to crack
Funny how you see the road more clearly looking back

Called my brother, couldn't talk, just let the silence breathe
He stayed on for twenty minutes, that was all I need
Sometimes people love you in the spaces they don't fill
And the rain kept right on falling and it's raining still

Rain on a Sunday, whole sky crying with me now
Rain on a Sunday, universe is showing me somehow
That even heaven's going through it, even clouds can't hold it in
Rain on a Sunday morning, let me feel this once again
Yeah, rain on a Sunday, wash me clean or wash me out
Rain on a Sunday, I got nothing but this doubt
Sitting in the wreckage of a thing I thought was real
Rain on a Sunday, tell me when I'm gonna heal

I know you had your reasons and I know I had my part
I ain't here to villainize you, I'm just here with a broken heart
And that's the realest thing I've said in longer than I know
Sometimes love just runs its course and then you let it go

But today I'm not that bigger man, today I'm on the floor
Today the rain is everything and I can't ask for more
Than just to feel it fully before I pick myself back up
Cause the only way to empty out is first to fill the cup

Rain on a Sunday, whole sky crying with me now
Rain on a Sunday, universe is showing me somehow
That even heaven's going through it, even clouds can't hold it in
Rain on a Sunday morning, let me feel this once again
Yeah, rain on a Sunday, wash me clean or wash me out
Rain on a Sunday, I got nothing but this doubt
Sitting in the wreckage of a thing I thought was real
Rain on a Sunday, tell me when I'm gonna heal

Tomorrow I'll be moving, tomorrow I'll get up and go
Tomorrow I'll be stronger than the man you used to know
But today I'm giving in to it, today the rain is mine
Rain on a Sunday, give me one more Sunday, I'll be fine

Rain on a Sunday, I got nothing but this doubt
Sitting in the wreckage of a thing I thought was real
Rain on a Sunday, tell me when I'm gonna heal

I know you had your reasons and I know I had my part
I ain't here to villainize you, I'm just here with a broken heart
And that's the realest thing I've said in longer than I know
Sometimes love just runs its course and then you let it go

But today I'm not that bigger man, today I'm on the floor
Today the rain is everything and I can't ask for more
Than just to feel it fully before I pick myself back up
Cause the only way to empty out is first to fill the cup

Rain on a Sunday, whole sky crying with me now
Rain on a Sunday, universe is showing me somehow
That even heaven's going through it, even clouds can't hold it in
Rain on a Sunday morning, let me feel this once again
Yeah, rain on a Sunday, wash me clean or wash me out
Rain on a Sunday, I got nothing but this doubt
Sitting in the wreckage of a thing I thought was real
Rain on a Sunday, tell me when I'm gonna heal

Tomorrow I'll be moving, tomorrow I'll get up and go
Tomorrow I'll be stronger than the man you used to know
But today I'm giving in to it, today the rain is mine
Rain on a Sunday, give me one more Sunday, I'll be fine

songs.subject: americana / summer ending
songs.lyrics:
The screen door's been swinging all of July
And the lightning bugs are thinning out at night
The corn is standing golden, nearly done
And I can feel the leaving in the sun

Spent the mornings on the porch with bitter coffee
Watched the neighbor kids chase nothing through the yard
Now the school bus route is painted on the asphalt
And the summer's folding up like playing cards

Hold on, hold on to the fading of the light
The way the August air goes heavy before nine
Everything we were in June feels further now
Like a radio station losing ground
Hold on, hold on, the season's turning down

My mother used to say the fields could feel it
Before the almanac could name the change
Something in the way the starlings gather early
And the thunderstorms start rolling in from the plains

The tomatoes on the vine are past their sweetness
The lawn chair fabric's bleaching in the heat
We left our names carved somewhere in this summer
Now the bark is growing slow across the creak

Hold on, hold on to the fading of the light
The way the August air goes heavy before nine
Everything we were in June feels further now
Like a radio station losing ground
Hold on, hold on, the season's turning down

There's a bonfire smell already in the evening
And the lake has lost its shimmer and its pull
The children are already somebody different
By the time the maples start to change their color

I drove the old highway out past the orchard
Where we used to stop and buy the cider jugs
The stand was boarded over, someone moved on
But I swear I smelled the apples in the dust

Hold on, hold on to the fading of the light
The way the August air goes heavy before nine
Everything we were in June feels further now
Like a radio station losing ground
Hold on, hold on, the season's turning down

Hold on, hold on
The goldenrod is burning by the road
Hold on, hold on
Lord, I'm not ready to let summer go

songs.subject: bluegrass / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
Well my boots got holes and my truck won't start
Got sixty-three cents and a grateful heart
The bank man called but I let it ring
Cause I was out back listening to the robins sing

My daughter says daddy you ought to worry more
I say honey I been poor since 1954
Worry never planted nothing in the ground
Worry never made a single sweet note sound

But I'm richer than the river in the spring
Richer than the church bells when they ring
My pockets might be empty as a promise in the wind
But there's cornbread on the stove and I got kin
Lord I'm broke and I am happy and I'd do it all again

Now the Wilson boy he drives a fancy car
Works himself to nothing for them things that are
I see him sometimes looking at the sky
Like he forgot there ever was a reason why

I remember when his daddy used to fish
Down at Miller's Creek without a single wish
Just a cane pole and a August afternoon
And a boy who thought his daddy hung the moon

But I'm richer than the river in the spring
Richer than the church bells when they ring
My pockets might be empty as a promise in the wind
But there's cornbread on the stove and I got kin
Lord I'm broke and I am happy and I'd do it all again

I played the fiddle at my Rosie's grave last fall
Played the tune she loved the most of all
Old Sweet Sunny South beneath the oak
And I swear she heard it in the way the cold wind spoke

Seventy-one years and I have slept on hard floors
Eaten beans three weeks and still asked Lord for more
Not more money not more land not more acclaim
Just more mornings just to wake and know my name

Cause I'm richer than the river in the spring
Richer than the church bells when they ring
My pockets might be empty as a promise in the wind
But there's cornbread on the stove and I got kin
Lord I'm broke and I am happy and I'd do it all again

So pick it up boys let that old banjo ring
Let that high lonesome find its way to sing
We ain't got nothing that the tax man wants tonight
But we got the porch and Lord we got the moonlight

I'm richer than the river in the spring
Richer than the church bells when they ring
My pockets might be empty as a promise in the wind
But there's cornbread on the stove and I got kin
Lord I'm broke and I am happy and I'd do it all again
Lord I'm broke and I am happy and I'd do it all again

songs.subject: lo-fi / a first apartment
songs.lyrics:
move-in day was a saturday in october
borrowed my cousin's van and a stranger helped with the couch
third floor walkup, one window faces the alley
but i made it mine with a lamp i found on somebody's curb

learned the pipes knock at two in the morning
learned the neighbor to my left plays guitar badly but sweet
burned my first dinner, set off the smoke alarm laughing
ate cereal on the floor cause the table hadn't come yet

this is mine, even the drafty corner
even the stain i covered with a rug
even the nights that felt a little too quiet
this four-hundred square feet is enough
this is mine

november came and the radiator sputtered
i wore my coat inside the first two weeks
taped the window with the plastic from the hardware store
and lit a candle just to feel a little more like me

wrote bad poetry at my secondhand kitchen table
hung a postcard where a painting ought to be
called my mom on sundays, didn't say i was lonely
just said the neighborhood was nice and i was learning how to sleep

this is mine, even the drafty corner
even the stain i covered with a rug
even the nights that felt a little too quiet
this four-hundred square feet is enough
this is mine

february made it harder than i figured
the grey came in and settled like a guest
but i bought a plant and named it something stupid
and watered it like proof that i could tend to something yet

started leaving notes to myself on the mirror
small things like you paid the light bill, that was good
and somewhere in the smallness of those gestures
i started feeling like i understood

that this is mine, even the drafty corner
even the stain i covered with a rug
even the nights that felt a little too quiet
this four-hundred square feet is enough
this is mine

by april i had learned the building's rhythms
the mailman comes at eleven, not before
the woman on the second floor gets roses every friday
and the super only fixes things if you knock twice on his door

i know which stair will creak and wake me coming home late
i know the way the sunset hits the alley just in june
i know this life is small by someone else's measure
but everything that's mine feels like a room

this is mine, even the drafty corner
even the stain i covered with a rug
even the nights that felt a little too quiet
this four-hundred square feet is enough
this is mine
yeah this is mine

songs.subject: hip-hop / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
Rain on the window before the alarm went off
Six fifty-eight and my baby's still soft
Curled up sleeping, she don't know it yet
Another Sunday where I'm already in my head

No church today cause the car needs a belt
No brunch, no plans, just the hand I been dealt
Cartoons on low while I make the eggs
Count the last of what I got in my head

This is my Sunday, rain coming down
My daughter's laughing at the TV sound
Coffee going cold cause I forgot to drink it
Bills on the counter but I try not to think it
Sunday, just trying to hold it together
Sunday, in this grey and quiet weather
Just me and my baby and the rain

She asked me can we get the good syrup today
I said we got syrup, she said yeah but the other way
I laughed, she laughed, and I put on more toast
Made it special, made it close as I could get to most

Called my sister but she's working double shifts
My mama's far and the distance is a gift I miss
Turned the music on and danced her around the floor
She said mama you're silly, I said that's what mamas are for

This is my Sunday, rain coming down
My daughter's laughing at the TV sound
Coffee going cold cause I forgot to drink it
Bills on the counter but I try not to think it
Sunday, just trying to hold it together
Sunday, in this grey and quiet weather
Just me and my baby and the rain

Noon came slow like it does when you don't go nowhere
Braided her hair while she picked what to wear
Like it matters, but it matters, cause she matters to me
We put on our coats walked to the corner just to see

The rain let up for about a half a block
We jumped the puddles till my boots were soaked
Bought one hot chocolate split it half and half
Lord I don't know the last time I laughed like that

This is my Sunday, rain coming down
My daughter's laughing at the TV sound
Coffee going cold cause I forgot to drink it
Bills on the counter but I try not to think it
Sunday, just trying to hold it together
Sunday, in this grey and quiet weather
Just me and my baby and the rain

After bedtime I sat in the quiet
Didn't turn the TV on, I wasn't ready to fight it
Just the rain and the hum of the refrigerator low
And something in my chest that I don't usually show

I'm tired in a way that sleep don't always fix
And proud in a way that don't come with applause or gifts
And I love this little life we're building slow
Even when it's hard I want her to know

That every Sunday, rain coming down
Her mama was here, wasn't nowhere to be found
Except right here, yeah right here in this kitchen
With a love so full it don't need no permission
Sunday, yeah we made it through together
Sunday, my heart heavy but light as a feather
Just me and my baby and the rain
Just me and my baby and the rain

songs.subject: emo / a friend who changed
songs.lyrics:
Broken picture frames still hang on your wall
I pass your house and I don't recognize it at all
You used to answer when I called at two in the morning
Now I'm just a name you forgot without warning

She left me last Tuesday with nothing to say
And I reached for my phone like I always did in those days
But the number I dialed just rang out to silence
You disappeared long before her, I just kept denying

Where did you go, where did the person I knew disappear
You're standing right there but you're somehow never here
I lost her last week but I lost you long before
I keep grieving people who are still breathing
And I can't do it anymore

Remember the winter we slept on your floor
Watching bad movies and swearing we'd never get bored
You knew all my shadows before I could name them
Now you shake my hand like a stranger and I can't explain it

I called you tonight cause I had nowhere to fall
Left a voicemail that said nothing, said nothing at all
Because how do you tell someone they already left you
How do you mourn someone who never said they were leaving

Where did you go, where did the person I knew disappear
You're standing right there but you're somehow never here
I lost her last week but I lost you long before
I keep grieving people who are still breathing
And I can't do it anymore

Maybe you changed and I was supposed to follow
Maybe I held too tight to something already hollow
Maybe I'm the ghost here, haunting our old places
Carrying photos with slowly fading faces

But she was just one and you were something different
A decade of knowing reduced to indifferent
And I'm sitting alone in the rubble of everything
Wondering how I lost two people in the same week

Where did you go, where did the person I knew disappear
You're standing right there but you're somehow never here
I lost her last week but I lost you long before
I keep grieving people who are still breathing
And I can't do it anymore

I can't do it anymore
I can't do it anymore

songs.subject: bluegrass / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:
The sun went down behind the old pine ridge
And the supper dishes soaked beneath the bridge
Of my two tired hands and the faucet's cold drip
I dried my palms on the hem of my slip

And the radio hummed something from years ago
A fiddle and a steel guitar, soft and low
I pushed the kitchen table up against the wall
And I let the music take me down the hall

Oh I'm dancing alone on the old hardwood floor
With the moonlight coming under the back screen door
My stockings sliding on the linoleum tile
I haven't felt this free in a long, long while
Just me and the music and the hum of the fridge
Dancing alone by the old pine ridge

My grandmother learned on a sawdust floor
In a barn out past the county road store
She said a woman needs a place to be free
To move her bones from how God made her be

I thought of her then as I spun myself round
My hair coming loose and my feet leaving ground
Just barely just briefly just holy and strange
A woman alone and completely unchained

Oh I'm dancing alone on the old hardwood floor
With the moonlight coming under the back screen door
My stockings sliding on the linoleum tile
I haven't felt this free in a long, long while
Just me and the music and the hum of the fridge
Dancing alone by the old pine ridge

The children are sleeping, my husband's away
Working the harvest some seventeen days
And I don't feel lonely, I feel something more
Like I found a room I forgot was my door

My arms opened wide to an invisible hand
Some partner made purely of music and land
Of soil and seasons and fiddle and pine
Every woman who danced here before she was mine

Oh I'm dancing alone on the old hardwood floor
With the moonlight coming under the back screen door
My stockings sliding on the linoleum tile
I haven't felt this free in a long, long while
Just me and the music and the hum of the fridge
Dancing alone by the old pine ridge

When the song ended I stood in the still
With the warm in my chest and the sweet in the chill
I pushed back the table and I finished the dishes
And I carried myself off to bed full of wishes

But something had changed in the kitchen that night
Some small resurrection in the ordinary light
A woman remembered she lived in a body
And danced herself back to herself, sweet and gaudy

Oh I'm dancing alone on the old hardwood floor
With the moonlight coming under the back screen door
My stockings sliding on the linoleum tile
I haven't felt this free in a long, long while
Just me and the music and the hum of the fridge
Dancing alone by the old pine ridge
Dancing alone by the old pine ridge

And the supper dishes soaked beneath the bridge
Of my two tired hands and the faucet's cold drip
I dried my palms on the hem of my slip

And the radio hummed something from years ago
A fiddle and a steel guitar, soft and low
I pushed the kitchen table up against the wall
And I let the music take me down the hall

Oh I'm dancing alone on the old hardwood floor
With the moonlight coming under the back screen door
My stockings sliding on the linoleum tile
I haven't felt this free in a long, long while
Just me and the music and the hum of the fridge
Dancing alone by the old pine ridge

My grandmother learned on a sawdust floor
In a barn out past the county road store
She said a woman needs a place to be free
To move her bones from how God made her be

I thought of her then as I spun myself round
My hair coming loose and my feet leaving ground
Just barely just briefly just holy and strange
A woman alone and completely unchained

My stockings sliding on the linoleum tile
I haven't felt this free in a long, long while
Just me and the music and the hum of the fridge
Dancing alone by the old pine ridge

Working the harvest some seventeen days
And I don't feel lonely, I feel something more
Like I found a room I forgot was my door

My arms opened wide to an invisible hand
Some partner made purely of music and land
Of soil and seasons and fiddle and pine
Every woman who danced here before she was mine

With the warm in my chest and the sweet in the chill
I pushed back the table and I finished the dishes
And I carried myself off to bed full of wishes

But something had changed in the kitchen that night
Some small resurrection in the ordinary light
A woman remembered she lived in a body
And danced herself back to herself, sweet and gaudy

songs.subject: indie folk / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Last call was an hour ago but he's still nursing it slow
The man in the corner with the collar turned against the cold
I've been wiping down the mahogany since half past one
Watching this city unravel now that the night is done

The streets out there are orange-lit and mostly empty now
A cab rolls past, a couple fights, a dog walks by somehow
I know this city better than the people who own it outright
I know her in the hours when she finally stops performing right

The city breathes at three in the morning
Slow and deep and a little broken
She lets her makeup run and her good shoes hurt
And she's beautiful in ways she doesn't work at
I've poured a thousand drinks to reach this hour
Stacking glasses in the blue and quiet
The city breathes at three in the morning
And I'm the only one still watching over her

There's a woman who comes in on the last Thursday of every month
She orders gin and waters it and never tells me much
But last night she told me she was leaving for the coast
Said this city took the best years and gave her almost

I've heard more truth across this bar than most priests ever do
Confessions paid in whiskey tabs and things they can't undo
The loneliness comes out around the second or the third
When people stop performing and start meaning every word

The city breathes at three in the morning
Slow and deep and a little broken
She lets her makeup run and her good shoes hurt
And she's beautiful in ways she doesn't work at
I've poured a thousand drinks to reach this hour
Stacking glasses in the blue and quiet
The city breathes at three in the morning
And I'm the only one still watching over her

I step outside to pull the chairs in off the sidewalk stone
The air is cold and smells like rain and something sweet and old
A light is on above the dry cleaner across the way
Someone else awake and working through the middle of the day

We never wave but there's a kind of knowing between us still
The ones who keep the world alive while everyone else is still
I've learned to love this city in her most unglamorous hour
When all the noise dies off and leaves the silence and the power

The city breathes at three in the morning
Slow and deep and a little broken
She lets her makeup run and her good shoes hurt
And she's beautiful in ways she doesn't work at
I've poured a thousand drinks to reach this hour
Stacking glasses in the blue and quiet
The city breathes at three in the morning
And I'm the only one still watching over her

I'll lock the door and count the drawer and kill the neon sign
Walk home through streets that know my name beneath the amber light
And somewhere in a high-rise or a basement someone's waking
Starting coffee for a world that doesn't know the night is breaking

But I'll know it, like I always do, I'll carry it home inside
That soft specific loneliness of being on the other side
Of all the hours people sleep through, warm and safe in beds
While I walk home with other people's stories in my head

The city breathes at three in the morning
Slow and deep and a little broken
She lets her makeup run and her good shoes hurt
And she's beautiful in ways she doesn't work at
I've poured a thousand drinks to reach this hour
Stacking glasses in the blue and quiet
The city breathes at three in the morning
And I'm the only one still watching over her
I'm the only one still watching over her

songs.subject: lo-fi / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
Your jacket's in the closet and I haven't moved it yet
It's been three months and fourteen days since that last cigarette
We smoked out on the fire escape with all your boxes packed
I told you I was happy for you and I almost meant that

The apartment sounds different now, there's too much room in the air
I keep buying the same amount of groceries when you're not there
I eat half and let the rest go soft inside the fridge
Some kind of ritual mourning on a small domestic ridge

I miss you like a low hum that I can't quite hear
Not sharp enough to cry over, just always there
In the background of the coffee and the morning light
In the space beside me in the blue of night
You moved a thousand miles away and still you fill the room
I miss you like the aftertaste of something good consumed

We call on Sunday evenings and the signal always breaks
Your new city sounds warmer, you sound lighter, and it aches
In the best kind of way I guess, the kind that means you're fine
But fine feels very far away from sitting here on mine

I learned to sleep in silence, took me almost six whole weeks
Stopped reaching for the lamp before I heard you brush your teeth
These small adjustments pile up like sediment or snow
The shape of someone missing is a thing you slowly know

I miss you like a low hum that I can't quite hear
Not sharp enough to cry over, just always there
In the background of the coffee and the morning light
In the space beside me in the blue of night
You moved a thousand miles away and still you fill the room
I miss you like the aftertaste of something good consumed

I found a note you left inside a library book I own
Just a dumb drawing of the two of us eating ice cream cones
The paper soft and yellow at the edges where you folded it
I stood there in the hallway for a while just holding it

Isn't it a strange thing how a person leaves in layers
First the physical and then the habits and the flavors
Of having someone know you in the ordinary way
Just existing in the background of your ordinary days

I miss you like a low hum that I can't quite hear
Not sharp enough to cry over, just always there
In the background of the coffee and the morning light
In the space beside me in the blue of night
You moved a thousand miles away and still you fill the room
I miss you like the aftertaste of something good consumed

Maybe I'll come visit when the weather gets less mean
Sleep on your new floor and meet the people you've been seeing
And maybe it'll feel like nothing ever really changed
Or maybe it'll feel like visiting a country with your name

Either way I'll take it, I'll take any version of you
Any city, any distance, any patchy signal through
I keep your jacket in the closet, keep your number saved at the top
Some things you just don't put away
Some things you just don't stop

I miss you like a low hum that I can't quite hear
Not sharp enough to cry over, just always there
In the background of the coffee and the morning light
In the space beside me in the blue of night
You moved a thousand miles away and still you fill the room
I miss you like the aftertaste of something good consumed
I miss you like the aftertaste of something good consumed

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
The phone rings out at midnight
And I already know it's you
Something in the silence before your voice
Tells me what you're about to do

You say you've been driving
Past the places where we used to go
The diner on the corner of Fifth and Vine
And that old movie theater with the broken window glow

And I'm sitting in my kitchen
In the dark with a cold cup of tea
Listening to your voice come through the wire
Like it's the only thing that's real to me

Don't hang up, don't say goodnight
Stay here with me a little longer
Midnight has a way of making feelings
Loud and louder and even stronger
So stay on the line, just breathe, just stay
I'll hold the quiet with you
There's nothing I need you to say
I just need to hear you, I just need to hear you

You tell me that the stars are out
And you pulled over just to look
You laugh a little, say you sound like someone
Straight out of a paperback book

But I don't think you sound crazy
I think you sound like someone true
Someone who finally stopped pretending
That they don't feel what they feel for you

And I'm turning on the lamp now
Pulling my knees up to my chest
Thinking how this one midnight phone call
Is worth more than all the daylight rest

Don't hang up, don't say goodnight
Stay here with me a little longer
Midnight has a way of making feelings
Loud and louder and even stronger
So stay on the line, just breathe, just stay
I'll hold the quiet with you
There's nothing I need you to say
I just need to hear you, I just need to hear you

The clock is moving slow now
Like it's giving us a gift
Every second stretching out like taffy
Every word a tiny lift

And I know the morning's coming
And the world will want us back
Coffees and commutes and conversations
That never get beneath the surface cracks

But right now we're somewhere different
Right now we're somewhere soft and wide
Right now there's nothing but your voice in darkness
And everything I feel inside

Don't hang up, don't say goodnight
Stay here with me a little longer
Midnight has a way of making feelings
Loud and louder and even stronger
So stay on the line, just breathe, just stay
I'll hold the quiet with you
There's nothing I need you to say
I just need to hear you, I just need to hear you

So don't hang up
Just stay
Don't hang up
Just stay with me this way

songs.subject: gospel / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
Lord it's Tuesday and the lights got cut
Sitting in the candle glow with the windows shut
Thirty-two dollars in a mason jar
Can't fill the tank but I can see the stars
And I know, yeah I know, I know I should be scared
But something in my chest is lifted and the weight's not there
So I'm singing

I got nothing but I got it good
Praising Your name in this neighborhood
Empty pockets but my hands are raised
Broke and blessed and lost in praise
You said You'd never leave and Lord I feel You here
In the middle of the nothing You are crystal clear
I got nothing but I got it good

Called my mama, she said baby hold on
I told her Mama I ain't even sad, I'm strong
She got quiet then she said I raised you right
I said the Lord's been keeping candles lit all night
And she started humming that old hymn we know
The one her mama sang through every high and low
So we sang it

I got nothing but I got it good
Praising Your name in this neighborhood
Empty pockets but my hands are raised
Broke and blessed and lost in praise
You said You'd never leave and Lord I feel You here
In the middle of the nothing You are crystal clear
I got nothing but I got it good

I wrote it down so I won't forget
October ninth, the most I ever felt
No television, no distraction left
Just me and You and one long grateful breath
The refrigerator's empty and the rent is late
But joy don't come from anything that's on that plate
No it don't

Because I remember when I had it all
And felt so hollow I could hear my echo in the hall
So maybe this is what it took to see
That You were always everything I'd ever need
Yeah maybe empty was the door
That finally let You fill me to the core
And I'm full, Lord I'm full

I got nothing but I got it good
Praising Your name in this neighborhood
Empty pockets but my hands are raised
Broke and blessed and lost in praise
You said You'd never leave and Lord I feel You here
In the middle of the nothing You are crystal clear
I got nothing but I got it good

So go ahead and take it, take it all away
Long as You are with me I can face the day
I got nothing but I got it good
Lord I got nothing but I got it good

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a wedding day
songs.lyrics:
She tried on seventeen dresses in the mirror last night
I only saw her in one, the way she held back the light
She doesn't know I watched her through the bedroom door
The way a mother watches what she's always prayed for

Today she walks down the aisle with her daddy's ghost
In the space beside her where she'll feel him most
I'll be the one with carnations shaking in my hands
Trying to hold my whole heart up, trying to stand

Oh my darling, oh my girl
You were my first love, you were my whole world
Before I knew what grace was, you showed me how
I was somebody's mother before I knew how
And look at you, look at you now

I think about the apartment with the leaking sink
The nights I fed you cereal because I couldn't think
The way you'd fall asleep on my chest like a stone
Two people keeping each other from being alone

I memorized the weight of you at two years old
The way your laugh was something no one could be told
You had to hear it echoing off the kitchen wall
To understand why I never minded when you'd call

Oh my darling, oh my girl
You were my first love, you were my whole world
Before I knew what grace was, you showed me how
I was somebody's mother before I knew how
And look at you, look at you now

I practiced what I'd say in the car on the way here
Something wise and something brief and perfectly clear
But wisdom left me standing when you took his hand
And all I had was all I've always had

Just love, bare and simple like a February yard
Love that comes out easy, love that came out hard
Love that stayed up late and love that rose at dawn
Love that is still here now, love that will carry on

Oh my darling, oh my girl
You were my first love, you were my whole world
Before I knew what grace was, you showed me how
I was somebody's mother before I knew how

And I was not enough and I was more than enough
And I was falling down and I was getting up
And I was everything that I could be for you
And here you are, here you are, here you are

Look at you, look at you now

And all I had was all I've always had

Just love, bare and simple like a February yard
Love that comes out easy, love that came out hard
Love that stayed up late and love that rose at dawn
Love that is still here now, love that will carry on

Oh my darling, oh my girl
You were my first love, you were my whole world
Before I knew what grace was, you showed me how
I was somebody's mother before I knew how

And I was not enough and I was more than enough
And I was falling down and I was getting up
And I was everything that I could be for you
And here you are, here you are, here you are

Look at you, look at you now

songs.subject: rock / quitting your job
songs.lyrics:
I walked into his office at a quarter to nine
Said I quit, slid my badge across the line
He said son you've got a future in this place
I said mister I've got sunlight on her face

And I don't need your parking spot
I don't need this fluorescent light
I don't need the forty floors between me
And everything that's right
She's waiting at the bottom with the engine running loud
I'm coming down, I'm coming down
I'm burning through the crowd

I've been staring at this spreadsheet for three years straight
Trading every good thing in for something that can wait
But she called me on a Thursday in the rain
And told me that she loved me and I lost my brain

And I don't need your parking spot
I don't need this fluorescent light
I don't need the forty floors between me
And everything that's right
She's waiting at the bottom with the engine running loud
I'm coming down, I'm coming down
I'm burning through the crowd

My desk is clean, my voicemail is full
There are seventeen emails about a conference call
And I am in the elevator going down
And the city is enormous and we're leaving town

We're gonna drive until we hit the sea
We're gonna roll the windows down and let it be
We're gonna eat bad food at some forgotten stop
And she's gonna laugh at me and I'm not gonna stop
Just staring at her like a fool
Like a living breathing fool

And I don't need your parking spot
I don't need this fluorescent light
I don't need the forty floors between me
And everything that's right
She's waiting at the bottom with the engine running loud
I'm coming down, I'm coming down
I'm burning through the crowd

The lobby doors blow open and the wind hits my chest
I see her leaning on the hood in her old leather vest
She says took you long enough, I said I know
She says where you wanna go

I said anywhere, anywhere, anywhere you are
Anywhere, anywhere, anywhere you are
Anywhere, anywhere, anywhere
Anywhere you are

songs.subject: americana / heartbreak
songs.lyrics:
She orders whiskey neat and stares into the glass
I've seen that look a thousand times, I know it's gonna last
The kind of hurt that settles in behind a person's eyes
When someone promised forever and forever turned to lies

I wipe the counter down and let her have her space
Another soul came stumbling in to find this hollow place
The neon sign outside blinks red against the rain
And everybody drowning here is drowning just the same

I pour the drinks and pocket tips and watch the hours bleed
I've heard a hundred stories of the things that people need
She says his name like it's a stone she's carrying in her chest
I nod and fill her glass again, sometimes that's what's best

The bottles on the mirror shelf reflect a world in two
Everything looks backwards here and everyone's see-through
I've got my own collection of the names I don't speak out
The ones that left without a reason, ones that left with doubt

She finally puts her coat on somewhere after two
Says thank you like I saved her life, I didn't, but I knew
That sometimes all a person needs is somebody near
Who asks no questions, keeps it clean, and keeps the bourbon clear

I lock the door and kill the lights and sit a minute down
I'm everybody's shoulder but I'm no one in this town
I wipe my eyes the same way that I wipe the bar each night
And drive home through the darkness hoping morning brings some light

The bottles on the mirror shelf reflect a world in two
Everything looks backwards here and everyone's see-through
I've got my own collection of the names I don't speak out
The ones that left without a reason, ones that left with doubt

She'll probably come back Thursday and she'll probably heal in time
And I'll be standing right here ready, back behind this line
Between the living and the leaving, between the lost and found
Mixing drinks for broken people in this broken little town

The bottles on the mirror shelf reflect a world in two
Everything looks backwards here and everyone's see-through
I've got my own collection of the names I don't speak out
The ones that left without a reason, ones that left with doubt
Every night I'm pouring out what I can't talk about

songs.subject: americana / a first apartment
songs.lyrics:
The key stuck in the lock the way it always does
I jiggled it the way the landlord showed me, just because
The carpet was the color of a decision made in haste
But it was mine, mine and hers, our own little place

She was sleeping on my shoulder with her sneakers in my hand
Thirty-seven boxes and a secondhand futon plan
The walls were thin as paper and the pipes sang every night
But I stood in that kitchen and I started feeling right

This is my ground, these are my floors
These are my windows, these are my doors
Nobody's leaving, nobody's taking this away
We built a life here out of not much at all
Finger-paint on drywall, crayon marks down the hall
This cracked and crooked place became our sacred space

I hung her drawings up with tape beside the kitchen sink
Made her a bedroom out of curtains, purple, just to think
Of something she could love and call her own inside these walls
While I ate cereal for dinner listening to her breathe down the hall

The radiator clanked at three and kept me half awake
I learned the sound of every floorboard, learned which ones to take
When I carried her to bed at night without waking her up
I was so young and so tired and I never gave that up

This is my ground, these are my floors
These are my windows, these are my doors
Nobody's leaving, nobody's taking this away
We built a life here out of not much at all
Finger-paint on drywall, crayon marks down the hall
This cracked and crooked place became our sacred space

Some nights I sat outside on the stoop after she'd sleep
Listening to the neighborhood and feeling something deep
Not sadness exactly and not quite pride I guess
Just a woman sitting still inside her own smallness

Inside her own bigness, inside her own fear
Knowing that she built this from one good decision here
To stay, to try, to plant something even in this clay
To make a home from nothing in the most old-fashioned way

This is my ground, these are my floors
These are my windows, these are my doors
Nobody's leaving, nobody's taking this away
We built a life here out of not much at all
Finger-paint on drywall, crayon marks down the hall
This cracked and crooked place became our sacred space

We moved away eventually to somewhere with a yard
But I still dream about that kitchen, and it don't feel hard
It feels like where I learned that I was stronger than I thought
That home is not a thing you find, home is a thing you've got

songs.subject: reggae / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Everybody's out tonight but I don't really mind
Got the whole house to myself, the kitchen, every kind
Of music coming from my phone up on the counter there
And I'm moving to the rhythm with the moonlight in my hair

Mom won't be home until eleven, dad don't live here anymore
And my sister's at her boyfriend's so I've got this whole dance floor
Just me and the refrigerator humming in the back
And a bassline rolling through me keeping everything on track

I'm dancing in the kitchen, yeah I'm dancing all alone
No one here to tell me that I'm doing something wrong
I'm spinning by the stove and I'm sliding on the tile
I've been practicing this feeling for a really long while
Dancing in the kitchen just for me

The music's kind of loud but hey the neighbors never say
A single thing because they know that we'll be fine today
I put a pot on just to look like I'm doing something real
If someone comes home early but I'm only here to feel

The song drops and I drop with it, my knees go to the ground
I'm laughing by myself and there is nobody around
And that right there is something I'm still learning how to hold
That being by yourself can feel like pure and solid gold

I'm dancing in the kitchen, yeah I'm dancing all alone
No one here to tell me that I'm doing something wrong
I'm spinning by the stove and I'm sliding on the tile
I've been practicing this feeling for a really long while
Dancing in the kitchen just for me

At school I'm always watching how I walk and how I stand
Always worried who is looking, always secondhand
But here between the counter and the kitchen table edge
I'm not performing anything, I'm right here on the ledge

Of something I can't name yet but I feel it in my chest
Like the person that I'm going to be is maybe one of the best
Or maybe not, and either way I'm good with what I've got
Just a kid alone on Friday turning music up a lot

I'm dancing in the kitchen, yeah I'm dancing all alone
No one here to tell me that I'm doing something wrong
I'm spinning by the stove and I'm sliding on the tile
I've been practicing this feeling for a really long while
Dancing in the kitchen just for me

One day I'll have my own place and I'll dance there too
In the morning or the evening with a better sound system, true
But right now this is plenty, right now this is enough
Being sixteen in a kitchen learning not to be so tough

Learning just to be a body, learning just to be a song
Jah know the world is heavy but tonight I can't go wrong
The beat drops one more time and I surrender to the floor
And I am every version of myself I've ever been before

I'm dancing in the kitchen, yeah I'm dancing all alone
No one here to tell me that I'm doing something wrong
I'm spinning by the stove and I'm sliding on the tile
I've been practicing this feeling for a really long while
Dancing in the kitchen, dancing in the kitchen
Dancing in the kitchen just for me

songs.subject: rock / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The traffic lights go green to red and no one's there to see
The city breathes a different kind of air at half past three
Steam rising from the grates below like something underground
Is sighing after midnight in a low and steady sound

A cab rolls through the intersection, headlights cut the rain
The driver knows these empty streets the way you know your name
The storefronts shuttered, iron-gated, menus in the dark
And every neon open sign still burning like a spark

This is the city at three in the morning
The city stripped down to its bones
This is the city at three in the morning
And baby it is never quite alone
Something's always burning somewhere
Someone's always wide awake
The city at three in the morning
Has a pulse nobody's paid to fake

A woman in a yellow coat is walking really fast
Heels hitting concrete like a drumbeat built to last
She knows exactly where she's going, doesn't look around
Just cutting through the city with that purposeful sound

The kitchen of a restaurant is still alive with heat
The last of the dishwashers pulling off the final sheet
Someone plays guitar out on a stoop two floors above
And the music falls like rainfall over everything we love

This is the city at three in the morning
The city stripped down to its bones
This is the city at three in the morning
And baby it is never quite alone
Something's always burning somewhere
Someone's always wide awake
The city at three in the morning
Has a pulse nobody's paid to fake

Two kids are laughing on a corner over something dumb
Their voices echoing off buildings till the echo comes
Back to them a half second later, doubled, strange and sweet
Like the city is laughing too out there beneath their feet

The hospital two blocks away is lit on every floor
Somebody being born right now and somebody no more
And both of those things happening inside the same stone wall
While the avenue just stretches out, indifferent to it all

This is the city at three in the morning
The city stripped down to its bones
This is the city at three in the morning
And baby it is never quite alone
Something's always burning somewhere
Someone's always wide awake
The city at three in the morning
Has a pulse nobody's paid to fake

I've been standing at this window with a cold cup in my hand
Watching all of this and trying hard to understand
Why the city at its emptiest feels fuller than the day
When the crowds and noise have swallowed every quieter thing away

There's something in the skeleton, there's something in the frame
When all the decoration fades and everything's the same
Gray and silver, black and amber, hollow and alive
This city at three in the morning is the city in its drive

Its raw and ancient hunger just to be and to persist
To push its concrete shoulders up against the winter mist
To say I am still standing, I am still here, I am still real
To hum beneath the silence with that low electric feel

This is the city at three in the morning
The city stripped down to its bones
This is the city at three in the morning
And baby it is never quite alone
Something's always burning somewhere
Someone's always wide awake
The city at three in the morning
Has a pulse nobody's paid to fake
Has a pulse nobody's paid to fake

songs.subject: rock / working a dead-end job
songs.lyrics:
I been punching this clock since before you were born
My hands look like leather, my boots are all worn
The foreman don't know me, he just knows my number
I count down the hours through the heat and the thunder

Same fluorescent flicker on the same cracked wall
The same busted locker at the end of the hall
Forty years of Mondays and they all taste the same
I forgotten the feeling of learning my name

I used to have fire, I used to have plans
I was gonna build something with these two bare hands
Now I'm building somebody else's dream instead
Trading all my hours just to keep myself fed

The younger ones ask me what it's like to stay
I tell them run fast while you still can today
They laugh like I'm joking, Lord I wish I were
The exit doors locked on the day I turned thirty

My back makes a sound like a warranty's expired
My doctor says rest but I can't afford tired
The pension they promised got restructured away
And restructured means stolen but nobody'll say

I used to have fire, I used to have plans
I was gonna build something with these two bare hands
Now I'm building somebody else's dream instead
Trading all my hours just to keep myself fed

The whistle blows loud at the end of the shift
I don't feel relief and I don't feel the lift
I just feel the weight of tomorrow already
The same road home, same coffee, go steady

My grandson drew pictures of what I do here
I didn't correct him, his version's more clear
In his drawing I'm smiling and standing up tall
In his drawing there's windows and light in the hall

I used to have fire, I used to have plans
I was gonna build something with these two bare hands
Now I'm building somebody else's dream instead
Trading all my hours just to keep myself fed

But I'll get up at five when that alarm starts to ring

Because what else is left for a man such as me

My hands look like leather, my boots are all worn

songs.lyrics:
The city breathes so soft at three a.m.
I've got your hand inside my hand again
The streetlamps make a gold of everything
I swear I hear the quiet start to sing

We left the party when the music slowed

Past shuttered shops with lights still in the glass
The whole wide world asleep except for us

And I don't need the daylight when I've got
The way you lean into me on this block
The taxicabs go sliding through the wet
I want to live inside this moment yet

A bakery somewhere is making bread
The smell of it is rising to my head
You say you love the city when it sleeps
And I love you for finding what it keeps

And I don't need the daylight when I've got
The way you lean into me on this block
The taxicabs go sliding through the wet
I want to live inside this moment yet

This city's never felt so incomplete

songs.subject: blues / getting sober
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning, wrote the date down on the page
Day thirty-one of fighting with this cage
My hands were shaking when I poured my coffee out
My hands been shaking, that's what this is all about

I used to start the morning with a different kind of pour
Tell myself just one and then I'd always want one more
The bottle was my calendar, my clock, my closest friend
I told myself it wasn't till I couldn't tell the end

Lord knows I tried to quit a hundred times before
Wrote it in a notebook, threw the notebook on the floor
This time I got a reason and I wrote her name down too
My daughter sent a letter and I cried the whole way through

She said daddy I remember when you used to be
The man who fixed my bicycle and sang me off to sleep
I don't know where you went to but I hope you find your way
I folded up that letter and I look at it each day

Day forty and the shaking's mostly left my hands
I'm starting to remember what it means to have some plans
The coffee tastes like something now, the food does too
I forgot that hunger was a feeling that I knew

My sponsor says take it slow, don't look too far ahead
I keep that in my jacket pocket somewhere near my chest
Some days the want comes crawling back like an old dog
And I just write it down and keep it in this log

Day fifty-seven, had a dream I was inside that bar
Woke up reaching for a glass and found my mouth was dry
Sat up in the dark a long time listening to the street
Found my daughter's letter and I read it on repeat

Day sixty, called her on the phone for the very first time
She didn't hang up on me and I stumbled through my lines
I told her I was sorry about every year I lost
She said she knew and daddy do you know how much this costs

I said I'm learning, baby, what a sober morning feels
She said she'd think about a visit when the summer heals
I held the phone long after she had said goodbye
And wrote it all down so I never let this lie

Day seventy-five, I'm sleeping through the night now mostly
The old me comes around sometimes and sits there closely
Whispers that I'm nothing and it always felt so good
I write him down and ask him if he thinks I understood

I think I'm starting to, I think I'm getting near
The kind of man my daughter wrote about in that letter here
My hands don't look like mine yet but they're getting there
Day seventy-five and I'm still breathing this cold air

Still breathing this cold air
Still here, still breathing this cold air

songs.subject: disco / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Neon lights are breathing low
The city hums a rhythm only we can know
Taxi cabs like shooting stars
Glide across the asphalt underneath the bars
And I'm walking in the glow of you
Every sidewalk crack a shade of something new
Three a.m. and I'm alive
With your name inside my chest I feel like I could fly

This city never sleeps but I'm the one who's dreaming
Every corner store and lamppost softly gleaming
Oh the night is ours, baby can't you feel it
Three a.m. and love is at the wheel it
Got me spinning, got me moving, got me grooving through the dark
You're the disco ball that's turning inside my heart

Steam is rising from the grates
The saxophone is leaking out from somewhere great
I can hear the bass line low

And I think about your hands in mine
How the whole dang world looked like it turned to valentine
Three a.m. the city sings
And I'm dancing through the streets on invisible wings

This city never sleeps but I'm the one who's dreaming
Every corner store and lamppost softly gleaming
Oh the night is ours, baby can't you feel it
Three a.m. and love is at the wheel it

You're the disco ball that's turning inside my heart

Your love made the invisible finally seen
So I shimmy down Fifth Avenue
Every sequin on the sidewalk sending me to you
The bakeries are waking up
The whole sweet world is rising like it can't get enough

This city never sleeps but I'm the one who's dreaming

Oh the night is ours, baby can't you feel it

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a childhood home
songs.lyrics:
The porch is rotting at the third board in
My mother always said don't step on that
The maple tree we measured every spring
Is taller than a thing should be allowed to get
I stood out on the sidewalk for a while
Before I let myself go up the path
A family I don't know lives here now
I don't know why I thought that I could ask

But I grew up in the shadow of that roof
I learned to lie and I learned the truth
I lost my first tooth in the kitchen sink
I learned how love could be a complicated thing
Every crack in every wall I memorized
Every way the winter light would fall inside
This is where I came from, this is where I learned my name
Nothing holds a person quite the same

My father's workshop was behind the house
He smelled like sawdust every single night
He'd let me sit and watch him work the wood
And I believed that he could fix anything right
My bedroom ceiling had a water stain
That looked like France or maybe a sleeping bear
I'd lie awake and trace it with my eyes
Not knowing yet how much I'd learn from staring there

But I grew up in the shadow of that roof
I learned to lie and I learned the truth
I lost my first tooth in the kitchen sink
I learned how love could be a complicated thing
Every crack in every wall I memorized
Every way the winter light would fall inside
This is where I came from, this is where I learned my name
Nothing holds a person quite the same

I don't begrudge the strangers in those rooms
They hung a different color on the door
The garden where my grandmother grew roses
Is a patch of gravel now and nothing more
But somewhere in the wood beneath their floors
My childhood is still running through the halls
I left something I can't take back with me
Pressed like a hand into the plaster walls

But I grew up in the shadow of that roof
I learned to lie and I learned the truth
I lost my first tooth in the kitchen sink
I learned how love could be a complicated thing

songs.subject: americana / starting over
songs.lyrics:
Sold what was left of the furniture
Loaded my truck in the rain
Said goodbye to the town that remembers
Every single one of my mistakes and shames
Highway 9 going northward
Nothing but pines and the broken white line
I got a little bit of money and a whole lot of nothing
And that's about the best place to begin

Lord I'm starting over, starting over
Laying my troubles on down by the river
Starting over, starting over
Something like dying and something like living again
I'm starting over

Got a cousin in a mountain town
Says she's got a room I can rent
Three hundred dollars a month and some company
In a place where nobody knows what I meant
I burned a decade like a brushfire
I watched the smoke curl up into the sky
Started walking when the ashes stopped smoldering
A man can't live forever in the fire

Lord I'm starting over, starting over
Laying my troubles on down by the river
Starting over, starting over
Something like dying and something like living again
I'm starting over

There's a diner outside of Clarkesville
Where a woman brings coffee at six
She looked at me like she saw my whole history
But was kind enough not to mention it
You meet a lot of people in transit
Everybody running from or running toward
I left my wedding ring on the dresser
And my old life in a pile on the floor

Lord I'm starting over, starting over
Laying my troubles on down by the river
Starting over, starting over
Something like dying and something like living again
I'm starting over

And maybe I won't find whatever I'm looking for
Maybe I'll sleep on the wrong side of grace
But I'd rather live hungry and free in the open
Than slow-dying back in that familiar place
The radio's playing an old Merle Haggard
And the mountains are coming up dark on the right
I don't know what's waiting on the other side of them
But I'm going to meet it before losing the light

Lord I'm starting over, starting over
Laying my troubles on down by the river
Starting over, starting over
Something like dying and something like living again
I'm starting over, starting over
I'm starting over
